activities.why: Use It is said that + clause or Subject is said to + infinitive, matching the infinitive tense to the fact and choosing the reporting verb to signal how strongly the claim is backed.

learn.title: Subject vs object questions
learn.html: When the wh-word is the subject, we use no auxiliary and normal word order: Who called you? When it's the object, we need do/does/did (or another auxiliary) and inversion: Who(m) did you call? Mixing these up is a persistent error.

learn.title: who vs whom
learn.html: whom is the object form. It's obligatory after a preposition (To whom should I write?) and standard in formal writing (the candidate whom we selected). In speech and informal writing, who usually replaces it: Who did you meet? Choose by register.

learn.title: which vs what; whose
learn.html: which asks about a limited, known set (Which of these is yours?); what is open-ended (What would you like?). whose asks about possession: Whose car is that? · Whose is this?

learn.title: how + adjective/adverb/much/many
learn.html: how combines to ask about degree and quantity: How long …? How often …? How far …? How much/many …? and manner (How did you do it?). Word order follows the object-question pattern with an auxiliary.

learn.title: Prepositions: fronted vs stranded
learn.html: The preposition can go to the front (formal, with whom/which) or stay at the end (‘stranded’, informal, with who/what): With whom did you travel? (formal) vs Who did you travel with? (everyday). Don't front the preposition and then also strand it.

learn.title: Advanced learner errors
learn.html:
✗ Who did call you? (subject question) → ✓ Who called you?

✗ To who did you give it? → ✓ To whom …? / Who did you give it to?

✗ What of these do you prefer? → ✓ Which of these …?

learn.title: In a nutshell
learn.html:
• Subject question = no auxiliary (Who called?); object question = auxiliary + inversion (Who did you call?).
  • whom = object form; obligatory after a fronted preposition; formal.
  • which = closed set; what = open; whose = possession.
  • Front the preposition (formal, whom/which) or strand it (informal, who/what) — not both.

ru.title: Вопросы к подлежащему vs дополнению
ru.html: Когда wh-слово — подлежащее, используем без вспомогательного, обычный порядок: Who called you? Когда дополнение — нужен do/does/did (или другой вспомогательный) и инверсия: Who(m) did you call? Путаница здесь — устойчивая ошибка.

kk.title: Бастауыш vs толықтауыш сұрақтары
kk.html: Wh-сөз бастауыш болса, көмекші жоқ, қалыпты сөз реті: Who called you? Толықтауыш болса, do/does/did (не басқа көмекші) және инверсия керек: Who(m) did you call? Бұларды шатастыру — тұрақты қате.

ru.title: who vs whom
ru.html: whom — форма дополнения. Обязательна после предлога (To whom should I write?) и стандартна в формальном письме (the candidate whom we selected). В речи и неформально who обычно заменяет её: Who did you meet? Выбирай по регистру.

kk.title: who vs whom
kk.html: whom — толықтауыш формасы. Предлогтан кейін міндетті (To whom should I write?) әрі ресми жазуда стандарт (the candidate whom we selected). Тілде және бейресми жазуда who оны алмастырады: Who did you meet? Регистрмен таңда.

ru.title: which vs what; whose
ru.html: which спрашивает об ограниченном, известном наборе (Which of these is yours?); what — открытый (What would you like?). whose спрашивает о принадлежности: Whose car is that? Whose is this?

kk.title: which vs what; whose
kk.html: which шектеулі, белгілі жиын туралы сұрайды (Which of these is yours?); what ашық (What would you like?). whose иеленуді сұрайды: Whose car is that? Whose is this?

ru.title: how + прилагательное/наречие/much/many
ru.html: how сочетается для вопроса о степени и количестве: How long …? How often …? How far …? How much/many …? и способе (How did you do it?). Порядок слов следует модели вопроса к дополнению со вспомогательным.

kk.title: how + сын есім/үстеу/much/many
kk.html: how дәреже мен санды сұрауға бірігеді: How long …? How often …? How far …? How much/many …? және тәсіл (How did you do it?). Сөз реті көмекшімен толықтауыш-сұрақ үлгісін ұстанады.

ru.title: Предлоги: вынос вперёд vs в конце
ru.html: Предлог может идти вперёд (формально, с whom/which) или оставаться в конце (‘stranded’, неформально, с who/what): With whom did you travel? (формально) / Who did you travel with? (обиходно). Не выноси предлог вперёд и одновременно не оставляй в конце.

kk.title: Предлогтар: алға шығару vs соңында қалдыру
kk.html: Предлог алға шыға алады (ресми, whom/which-пен) не соңында қала алады (‘stranded’, бейресми, who/what-пен): With whom did you travel? (ресми) / Who did you travel with? (күнделікті). Предлогты алға шығарып, сосын соңында да қалдырма.

ru.title: Ошибки продвинутого уровня
ru.html: ✗ Who did call you? (вопрос к подлежащему) → ✓ Who called you? ✗ To who did you give it? → ✓ To whom …? / Who did you give it to? ✗ What of these do you prefer? → ✓ Which of these …?

kk.title: Жоғары деңгей қателері
kk.html: ✗ Who did call you? (бастауыш сұрақ) → ✓ Who called you? ✗ To who did you give it? → ✓ To whom …? / Who did you give it to? ✗ What of these do you prefer? → ✓ Which of these …?

ru.title: Кратко
ru.html:
• Вопрос к подлежащему = без вспомогательного (Who called?); к дополнению = вспом. + инверсия (Who did you call?).
  • whom = форма дополнения; обязательна после вынесенного предлога; формально.
  • which = закрытый набор; what = открытый; whose = принадлежность.
  • Выноси предлог вперёд (формально, whom/which) или оставляй в конце (неформ., who/what) — не оба.

kk.title: Қысқаша
kk.html:
• Бастауыш сұрақ = көмекшісіз (Who called?); толықтауыш сұрақ = көмекші + инверсия (Who did you call?).
  • whom = толықтауыш формасы; алға шыққан предлогтан кейін міндетті; ресми.
  • which = жабық жиын; what = ашық; whose = иелену.
  • Предлогты алға шығар (ресми, whom/which) не соңында қалдыр (бейресми, who/what) — екеуін бірге емес.

activities.prompt: Sort each wh-question.
activities.why: When the wh-word is the subject, no auxiliary is used; when it's the object, an auxiliary and inversion are needed.

items.s: To whom should I address the letter?
Whose is this umbrella?
Who did break the vase?
With whom did you go with?

activities.why: a subject question needs no ‘did’ (Who broke…?), and you can't both front and strand the preposition.
the wh-word is the subject, so no auxiliary → Who left…?
after a fronted preposition, use the object form → to whom.

activities.after: of the two candidates impressed you more? (which/what)
activities.why: choosing from a known, limited set → Which.
how + long + auxiliary inversion for an object-type question.

activities.q: You're offered a choice between tea and coffee. The natural question is:
why: ‘what’ is open-ended; here the set is limited to two.
Correct — a choice from a known, limited set → which.
‘whose’ asks about possession, not choice.

activities.q: Which question is most appropriate in a formal legal document?
options: Who did you give the documents to?
To whom did you give the documents?
Who'd you hand the docs to?
why: correct but informal (stranded preposition, ‘who’).
Correct — fronted preposition + ‘whom’ suits formal register.
very informal (contraction, ‘docs’).

activities.prompt: Sort these equivalent questions by register.

items.t: About which project is he asking?

activities.why: Fronted preposition + whom/which = formal; stranded preposition + who/contractions = informal.
this is a subject question, so no ‘did’ → “Who won the chess tournament?”

activities.typeLabel: Rewrite in a more formal style
activities.instruction: Rewrite with a fronted preposition and whom:
activities.prompt: Who were you speaking to?
alts: To whom were you speaking?
to whom were you speaking?
activities.why: front the preposition and use the object form → To whom were you speaking?

activities.scene: Choose the correctly formed and register-appropriate question.

steps.npc: I'd like to lodge a complaint about my treatment.

options.t: Certainly. To whom did you speak when you first called?
options.why: formal context, object of a preposition → to whom, fronted.

options.t: Certainly. Who did you speak to who when you first called?
options.why: the question is garbled; either ‘Who did you speak to?’ or ‘To whom did you speak?’

steps.npc: And there were two advisers — does it matter which?

options.t: It does. Which of them handled your case?
options.why: a choice from a known set of two → which of them.

options.t: It does. What of them handled your case?
options.why: choosing from a limited, known set requires ‘which’, not ‘what’.

activities.why: Distinguish subject questions (no auxiliary) from object questions, choose whom/which for formal/closed-set contexts, and front or strand the preposition consistently by register.

learn.title: Negative questions: not really asking
learn.html: A negative question (Isn't it lovely? · Didn't you know?) rarely seeks information. It usually expresses surprise, seeks agreement/confirmation, or conveys criticism/persuasion: Aren't you ashamed? · Wouldn't it be better to wait?

learn.title: The ‘yes/no’ trap
learn.html: Answering negative questions differs from many languages. “Didn't you call her?” — Yes (= I did call) / No (= I didn't call). English answers the fact, not the negative word. This mismatch causes frequent confusion.

learn.title: Echo questions: surprise & clarification
learn.html: Echo questions repeat part of what was said, often with rising intonation and stress, to show surprise or ask for repetition: “I'm quitting.” “You're what?” · “He paid £5,000.” “He paid how much?” The wh-word usually stays in situ (not fronted).

learn.title: Questions with that-clauses (embedded questions)
learn.html: When a question is reported/embedded, it loses inversion and takes statement word order: Do you know where the station is? (not ‘where is the station’). With yes/no questions, use if/whether: I wonder whether it's true.

learn.title: Tags, ‘do you think’ & politeness
learn.html: Embedding makes questions more polite/indirect: Could you tell me what time it opens? With think/suppose/imagine, the wh-word fronts the whole question: What do you think it means? (not ‘Do you think what it means?’).

learn.title: Advanced learner errors
learn.html:
✗ Do you know where is the toilet? → ✓ … where the toilet is?

✗ “I won.” “You won what?” for surprise → ✓ “You what?”

✗ Answering ‘Didn't you see it?’ with the wrong polarity → answer the fact (Yes, I did / No, I didn't).

learn.title: In a nutshell
learn.html:
• Negative questions express surprise, seek agreement, or persuade — answer the fact (Yes = did).
  • Echo questions repeat with stress to show surprise or ask for repetition; wh-word stays in place.
  • Embedded/indirect questions use statement word order and if/whether for yes/no.
  • With ‘do you think’, front the wh-word across the whole question.

ru.title: Отрицательные вопросы: не совсем вопрос
ru.html: Отрицательный вопрос (Isn't it lovely? Didn't you know?) редко ищет информацию. Обычно он выражает удивление, ищет согласия/подтверждения или несёт критику/убеждение: Aren't you ashamed? Wouldn't it be better to wait?

kk.title: Болымсыз сұрақтар: шын сұрамайды
kk.html: Болымсыз сұрақ (Isn't it lovely? Didn't you know?) сирек ақпарат іздейді. Ол әдетте таңдануды білдіреді, келісім/растауды іздейді, не сын/иландыруды береді: Aren't you ashamed? Wouldn't it be better to wait?

ru.title: Ловушка ‘yes/no’
ru.html: Ответ на отрицательный вопрос отличается от многих языков. “Didn't you call her?” — Yes (= позвонил) / No (= не позвонил). Английский отвечает на факт, а не на отрицательное слово. Это несовпадение часто вызывает путаницу.

kk.title: ‘Yes/no’ тұзағы
kk.html: Болымсыз сұраққа жауап көп тілден өзгеше. “Didn't you call her?” — Yes (= қоңырау шалдым) / No (= шалмадым). Ағылшын болымсыз сөзге емес, фактіге жауап береді. Бұл сәйкессіздік жиі шатасу тудырады.

ru.title: Эхо-вопросы: удивление и уточнение
ru.html: Эхо-вопросы повторяют часть сказанного, часто с восходящей интонацией и ударением, чтобы показать удивление или попросить повтор: “I'm quitting.” “You're what?” “He paid £5,000.” “He paid how much?” Wh-слово обычно остаётся на месте (не выносится).

kk.title: Эхо-сұрақтар: таңдану мен нақтылау
kk.html: Эхо-сұрақтар айтылғанның бір бөлігін қайталайды, жиі көтеріңкі интонация мен екпінмен, таңдануды көрсету не қайталауды сұрау үшін: “I'm quitting.” “You're what?” “He paid £5,000.” “He paid how much?” Wh-сөз әдетте орнында қалады (алға шықпайды).

ru.title: Вопросы с that-clause (встроенные)
ru.html: Когда вопрос сообщается/встраивается, он теряет инверсию и берёт порядок утверждения: Do you know where the station is? (не ‘where is the station’). Для yes/no используй if/whether: I wonder whether it's true.

kk.title: that-сөйлемді сұрақтар (енгізілген)
kk.html: Сұрақ баяндалғанда/енгізілгенде, инверсияны жоғалтып, сөйлем сөз ретін алады: Do you know where the station is? (‘where is the station’ емес). Yes/no сұрақтарда if/whether қолдан: I wonder whether it's true.

ru.title: Теги, ‘do you think’ и вежливость
ru.html: Встраивание делает вопрос вежливее/косвеннее: Could you tell me what time it opens? С think/suppose/imagine wh-слово выносится в начало всего вопроса: What do you think it means? (не ‘Do you think what it means?’).

kk.title: Тэгтер, ‘do you think’ және сыпайылық
kk.html: Енгізу сұрақты сыпайы/жанама етеді: Could you tell me what time it opens? think/suppose/imagine-мен wh-сөз бүкіл сұрақтың алдына шығады: What do you think it means? (‘Do you think what it means?’ емес).

ru.title: Ошибки продвинутого уровня
ru.html: ✗ Do you know where is the toilet? → ✓ … where the toilet is? ✗ “I won.” “You won what?” для удивления → ✓ “You what?” ✗ Ответ на ‘Didn't you see it?’ с неверной полярностью → отвечай на факт (Yes, I did / No, I didn't).

kk.title: Жоғары деңгей қателері
kk.html: ✗ Do you know where is the toilet? → ✓ … where the toilet is? ✗ “I won.” “You won what?” (таңдану) → ✓ “You what?” ✗ ‘Didn't you see it?’-ке қате полярлықпен жауап → фактіге жауап бер (Yes, I did / No, I didn't).

ru.title: Кратко
ru.html:
• Отрицательные вопросы выражают удивление/согласие/убеждение — отвечай на факт (Yes = сделал).
  • Эхо-вопросы повторяют с ударением; wh-слово остаётся на месте.
  • Встроенные/косвенные вопросы используют порядок утверждения и if/whether для yes/no.
  • С ‘do you think’ выноси wh-слово в начало всего вопроса.

kk.title: Қысқаша
kk.html:
• Болымсыз сұрақтар таңдану/келісім/иландыру білдіреді — фактіге жауап бер (Yes = істедім).
  • Эхо-сұрақтар екпінмен қайталайды; wh-сөз орнында қалады.
  • Енгізілген/жанама сұрақтар сөйлем сөз ретін және yes/no-ға if/whether қолданады.
  • ‘do you think’-пен wh-сөзді бүкіл сұрақтың алдына шығар.

activities.prompt: Sort each question by its function.

items.t: Could you tell me where she lives?

activities.why: Negative questions seek agreement/express surprise; echo questions repeat with stress; embedded questions are indirect with statement order.

items.s: Do you know what time it starts?
I wonder whether they'll come.
Do you know where is the exit?
What do you think happened?

activities.why: embedded questions use statement order: ‘where the exit is’, not ‘where is the exit’.

activities.before: Could you tell me what time
alts: the museum shuts
activities.why: an indirect question keeps statement word order → the museum closes.

alts: if
activities.why: an embedded yes/no question uses whether/if.

activities.after: do you think she meant by that? (what)
activities.why: with ‘do you think’, the wh-word fronts the whole question → What do you think…?
embedded question → statement word order (why he said that).

activities.q: “Didn't you lock the door?” You DID lock it. The correct short answer is:
why: ‘No’ would mean you didn't lock it.
Correct — English answers the fact: you locked it, so ‘Yes, I did.’
self-contradictory (‘No, I did’).

activities.q: Your friend says, “I'm emigrating to Iceland.” Which reply echoes to show surprise?
options: Do you know where Iceland is?
why: a normal information question, no surprise.
Correct — the wh-word stays in place, stressed, echoing the statement in surprise.
an embedded question, not an echo.

activities.prompt: Sort each by its structure.
activities.why: Direct questions invert subject and auxiliary; embedded questions keep statement word order.
an embedded question drops the auxiliary and inverts back → “how much it costs.”

activities.instruction: Rewrite as a polite indirect question starting ‘Could you tell me…’:
activities.prompt: Where is the nearest pharmacy?
activities.answer: could you tell me where the nearest pharmacy is
alts: Could you tell me where the nearest pharmacy is?
could you tell me where the nearest pharmacy is?
activities.why: embed with statement order → where the nearest pharmacy is.

steps.npc: I've handed in my notice — I'm going travelling for a year.

options.t: You're doing what?! That's so sudden.
options.why: an echo question with the wh-word in place shows surprise.

options.t: What are you doing? That's so sudden.
options.why: to echo the shock of the specific statement, keep the wh-word in situ: ‘You're doing what?!’

steps.npc: Any tips on where to start?

options.t: Well, do you know whether you'll need visas in advance?
options.why: an embedded yes/no question uses whether + statement order.

options.t: Well, do you know will you need visas in advance?
options.why: an embedded question can't keep inversion; use ‘whether you'll need visas’.

activities.why: Use negative questions for surprise/agreement (answering the fact), echo questions to signal surprise (wh-word in place), and embedded questions with statement order and if/whether for indirectness.

learn.title: What can follow a verb
learn.html: Verbs differ in what they allow after them. A transitive verb takes an object (She read the book); an intransitive verb takes none (He arrived). Some verbs also take an object complement — a word describing the object: They elected her chair. · We painted it red.

learn.title: Transitive, intransitive & both
learn.html: Many verbs work both ways, sometimes with a meaning shift: The door opened (intransitive) / She opened the door (transitive). Some are only intransitive (arrive, fall, happen, exist) — these can't be passive. Others are always transitive (make, want, enjoy) and feel incomplete without an object.

learn.title: Object + complement patterns
learn.html: Verbs like make, find, consider, call, name, keep, leave, appoint, declare take object + complement (noun or adjective): I find him tiresome. · The jury found her guilty. · They appointed him manager. The complement describes or renames the object.

learn.title: consider (to be), think … to be
learn.html: Some object-complement verbs allow an optional (to be): I consider him (to be) the best. · We believe the plan to be flawed. With more formal verbs (believe, know, suppose) the to be is often required; with find/make it isn't used.

learn.title: Prepositional & phrasal patterns
learn.html: Many verbs need a preposition before their object (listen to, depend on, refer to) or work as phrasal verbs (look after). Learning the verb with its pattern — [V + obj], [V + prep + obj], [V + obj + complement] — is the C1 goal, not the verb alone.

learn.title: Advanced learner errors
learn.html:
✗ He explained me the rule. → ✓ He explained the rule to me.

✗ They elected her as chair (many prefer no ‘as’) → ✓ They elected her chair.

✗ The accident was happened. → ✓ The accident happened (intransitive — no object/passive).

learn.title: In a nutshell
learn.html:
• Transitive verbs take an object; intransitive verbs don't (and can't be passive).
  • Object-complement verbs (make/find/consider/elect) take object + noun/adjective.
  • Formal verbs (believe/know) may need (to be) before the complement.
  • Learn each verb with its pattern, including any required preposition.

ru.title: Что может следовать за глаголом
ru.html: Глаголы различаются тем, что допускают после себя. Переходный берёт дополнение (She read the book); непереходный — нет (He arrived). Некоторые берут и объектный комплемент — слово, описывающее дополнение: They elected her chair. We painted it red.

kk.title: Етістіктен кейін не келе алады
kk.html: Етістіктер өзінен кейін не рұқсат ететінімен ерекшеленеді. Өтпелі етістік объект алады (She read the book); өтпесіз алмайды (He arrived). Кейбірі объект толықтауышын да алады — объектіні сипаттайтын сөз: They elected her chair. We painted it red.

ru.title: Переходные, непереходные и оба
ru.html: Многие глаголы работают обоими способами, иногда со сдвигом значения: The door opened (неперех.) / She opened the door (перех.). Некоторые только непереходные (arrive, fall, happen, exist) — не могут быть пассивом. Другие всегда переходны (make, want, enjoy) и без дополнения неполны.

kk.title: Өтпелі, өтпесіз және екеуі
kk.html: Көп етістік екі жолмен де істейді, кейде мағына ауысады: The door opened (өтпесіз) / She opened the door (өтпелі). Кейбірі тек өтпесіз (arrive, fall, happen, exist) — пассив болмайды. Басқалары әрдайым өтпелі (make, want, enjoy) және объектсіз толымсыз сезіледі.

ru.title: Модели объект + комплемент
ru.html: Глаголы make, find, consider, call, name, keep, leave, appoint, declare берут объект + комплемент (сущ./прил.): I find him tiresome. The jury found her guilty. They appointed him manager. Комплемент описывает или переименовывает объект.

kk.title: Объект + толықтауыш үлгілері
kk.html: make, find, consider, call, name, keep, leave, appoint, declare сияқты етістіктер объект + толықтауыш (зат/сын есім) алады: I find him tiresome. The jury found her guilty. They appointed him manager. Толықтауыш объектіні сипаттайды не атайды.

ru.title: consider (to be), think … to be
ru.html: Некоторые объектно-комплементные глаголы допускают необязательное (to be): I consider him (to be) the best. We believe the plan to be flawed. С более формальными (believe, know, suppose) to be часто обязательно; с find/make не используется.

kk.title: consider (to be), think … to be
kk.html: Кейбір объект-толықтауыш етістіктер қосымша (to be)-ға рұқсат етеді: I consider him (to be) the best. We believe the plan to be flawed. Ресмилеу етістіктермен (believe, know, suppose) to be жиі міндетті; find/make-пен қолданылмайды.

ru.title: Предложные и фразовые модели
ru.html: Многие глаголы требуют предлог перед дополнением (listen to, depend on, refer to) или работают как фразовые (look after). Учить глагол с его моделью — [V + obj], [V + prep + obj], [V + obj + complement] — цель C1, а не глагол сам по себе.

kk.title: Предлогтық және фразалық үлгілер
kk.html: Көп етістік объект алдында предлог қажет етеді (listen to, depend on, refer to) не фразалық етістік ретінде істейді (look after). Етістікті үлгісімен үйрену — [V + obj], [V + prep + obj], [V + obj + complement] — C1 мақсаты, етістіктің өзін ғана емес.

ru.title: Ошибки продвинутого уровня
ru.html: ✗ He explained me the rule → ✓ He explained the rule to me. ✗ They elected her as chair (многие предпочитают без ‘as’) → ✓ They elected her chair. ✗ The accident was happened → ✓ The accident happened (непереходный — нет объекта/пассива).

kk.title: Жоғары деңгей қателері
kk.html: ✗ He explained me the rule → ✓ He explained the rule to me. ✗ They elected her as chair (көбі ‘as’-сыз қалайды) → ✓ They elected her chair. ✗ The accident was happened → ✓ The accident happened (өтпесіз — объект/пассив жоқ).

ru.title: Кратко
ru.html:
• Переходные глаголы берут дополнение; непереходные — нет (и не пассивируются).
  • Объектно-комплементные глаголы (make/find/consider/elect) берут объект + сущ./прил.
  • Формальные глаголы (believe/know) могут требовать (to be) перед комплементом.
  • Учи каждый глагол с его моделью, включая нужный предлог.

kk.title: Қысқаша
kk.html:
• Өтпелі етістіктер объект алады; өтпесіз алмайды (пассив та болмайды).
  • Объект-толықтауыш етістіктер (make/find/consider/elect) объект + зат/сын есім алады.
  • Ресми етістіктер (believe/know) толықтауыш алдында (to be) қажет етуі мүмкін.
  • Әр етістікті үлгісімен, қажет предлогымен қоса үйрен.

activities.prompt: Sort each verb use.
activities.why: Transitive verbs take an object; intransitive verbs take none; object-complement verbs add a word describing the object.

items.s: The jury found the defendant guilty.
He explained the situation to us.
She explained us the situation.
The meeting was happened yesterday.

activities.why: explain takes ‘the situation to us’ (not ‘us the situation’); happen is intransitive, so no passive.

activities.after: her treasurer for a second term. (appoint)
activities.why: appoint + object + complement (a role noun) → appointed her treasurer.

alts: depend upon
activities.why: depend requires the preposition ‘on/upon’ before its object → depend on.

alts: consider
activities.why: believe + object + to be + complement → believe the manuscript to be genuine.
keep + object + adjective complement (awake).

activities.q: Which sentence uses a correct object + complement structure?
options: They named to the baby Aisha.
why: grammatical but lacks the naming complement the context needs.
Correct — name + object + complement (the name).
‘to the baby’ inserts a wrong preposition.

activities.q: Which verb CANNOT be made passive?
why: transitive (a bridge was built).
Correct — arrive is intransitive, so it has no passive.
transitive (the logo was designed).

activities.typeLabel: Does the verb need a preposition?
activities.prompt: Sort by whether a preposition precedes the object.
activities.why: Some verbs take a direct object (discuss/answer); others need a preposition (listen to, refer to).
describe takes ‘the man to me’, not ‘me the man’ → “describe the man to me.”

activities.instruction: Rewrite using find + object + adjective:
activities.prompt: In my opinion, the lectures are dull.
alts: I find the lectures dull.
I find them dull
activities.why: find + object + adjective complement → I find the lectures dull.

activities.typeLabel: Extended context: after a committee meeting

steps.npc: So, is it official now — the new role?

options.t: Yes — they've appointed Dana head of research.
options.why: appoint + object + role complement, no preposition.

options.t: Yes — they've appointed Dana as head of research to her.
options.why: the pattern is ‘appointed Dana head of research’; ‘to her’ and extra words don't belong.

steps.npc: And will it all work out, do you think?

options.t: It depends on how much support she gets.
options.why: depend requires the preposition ‘on’ before its object.

options.t: It depends how much support she gets, on it.
options.why: depend on takes the clause directly: ‘depends on how much support…’, without a stray ‘on it’.

activities.why: Match each verb to its pattern: transitive vs intransitive, object + complement for verbs like appoint/find, and the required preposition for verbs like depend on.

learn.title: Ditransitive verbs: two objects
learn.html: Some verbs take an indirect object (usually a person, the receiver) and a direct object (the thing): She gave [me] [the keys]. Two orders are possible: V + IO + DO (gave me the keys) or V + DO + to/for + IO (gave the keys to me).

learn.title: to or for?
learn.html: When the indirect object moves after the direct object, most verbs take to (transfer to a recipient: send, give, lend, offer, show, tell, pass) but some take for (do something on someone's behalf: buy, make, cook, get, find, save): I made a cake for her. · He sent it to her.

learn.title: Which order to choose
learn.html: Word order follows end-weight and focus. Put long or new information last: I gave the keys to the man in the grey coat (long IO → to-phrase last); I gave him the keys (short IO first). A pronoun IO usually comes first: Give me it / more commonly Give it to me.

learn.title: Passive with two objects
learn.html: Either object can become the passive subject, but English usually prefers the person: They offered Sam the job → Sam was offered the job (common) / The job was offered to Sam. Some verbs (explain, describe, suggest, say) resist the IO-first pattern: ✗ She explained me it → She explained it to me.

learn.title: Verbs that don't take IO + DO
learn.html: A key C1 point: verbs like explain, describe, suggest, mention, report, announce, introduce, demonstrate take only V + DO + to + IO: Explain the rules to us. · She suggested a plan to me. ✗ explain us the rules.

learn.title: Advanced learner errors
learn.html:
✗ He suggested me a good hotel. → ✓ He suggested a good hotel to me.

✗ I bought to my sister a gift. → ✓ I bought my sister a gift / a gift for my sister.

✗ Say me the truth. → ✓ Tell me the truth / Say it to me.

learn.title: In a nutshell
learn.html:
• Ditransitive verbs allow V + IO + DO or V + DO + to/for + IO.
  • to = transfer to a recipient; for = on someone's behalf.
  • Order by end-weight/focus; put long or new information last.
  • explain/describe/suggest take only DO + to + IO — never IO first.

ru.title: Дитранзитивные глаголы: два дополнения
ru.html: Некоторые глаголы берут косвенное дополнение (обычно лицо, получатель) и прямое дополнение (вещь): She gave [me] [the keys]. Два порядка: V + IO + DO (gave me the keys) или V + DO + to/for + IO (gave the keys to me).

kk.title: Дитранзитивті етістіктер: екі объект
kk.html: Кейбір етістіктер жанама объект (әдетте адам, алушы) және тура объект (зат) алады: She gave [me] [the keys]. Екі рет мүмкін: V + IO + DO (gave me the keys) не V + DO + to/for + IO (gave the keys to me).

ru.title: to или for?
ru.html: Когда косвенное дополнение идёт после прямого, большинство глаголов берут to (передача получателю: send, give, lend, offer, show, tell, pass), но некоторые — for (сделать для кого-то: buy, make, cook, get, find, save): I made a cake for her. He sent it to her.

kk.title: to не for?
kk.html: Жанама объект тура объектіден кейін жылжығанда, көп етістік to алады (алушыға беру: send, give, lend, offer, show, tell, pass), бірақ кейбірі for (біреу үшін істеу: buy, make, cook, get, find, save): I made a cake for her. He sent it to her.

ru.title: Какой порядок выбрать
ru.html: Порядок следует конечному весу и фокусу. Длинную или новую информацию — в конец: I gave the keys to the man in the grey coat (длинное IO → to-фраза в конце); I gave him the keys (короткое IO вперёд). Местоименное IO обычно вперёд: Give me it / чаще Give it to me.

kk.title: Қай ретті таңдау
kk.html: Сөз реті соңғы салмақ пен фокусқа сай. Ұзын не жаңа ақпаратты соңында қой: I gave the keys to the man in the grey coat (ұзын IO → to-тіркес соңында); I gave him the keys (қысқа IO алдында). Есімдік IO әдетте алдында: Give me it / жиірек Give it to me.

ru.title: Пассив с двумя дополнениями
ru.html: Любое дополнение может стать подлежащим пассива, но английский обычно предпочитает лицо: They offered Sam the job → Sam was offered the job (часто) / The job was offered to Sam. Некоторые глаголы (explain, describe, suggest, say) не берут IO первым: ✗ She explained me it → She explained it to me.

kk.title: Екі объектімен пассив
kk.html: Кез келген объект пассив бастауышы бола алады, бірақ ағылшын әдетте адамды қалайды: They offered Sam the job → Sam was offered the job (жиі) / The job was offered to Sam. Кейбір етістіктер (explain, describe, suggest, say) IO-алды үлгісіне қарсы: ✗ She explained me it → She explained it to me.

ru.title: Глаголы без IO + DO
ru.html: Важный момент C1: глаголы explain, describe, suggest, mention, report, announce, introduce, demonstrate берут только V + DO + to + IO: Explain the rules to us. She suggested a plan to me. ✗ explain us the rules.

kk.title: IO + DO алмайтын етістіктер
kk.html: Маңызды C1 тұсы: explain, describe, suggest, mention, report, announce, introduce, demonstrate сияқты етістіктер тек V + DO + to + IO алады: Explain the rules to us. She suggested a plan to me. ✗ explain us the rules.

ru.title: Ошибки продвинутого уровня
ru.html: ✗ He suggested me a good hotel → ✓ He suggested a good hotel to me. ✗ I bought to my sister a gift → ✓ I bought my sister a gift / a gift for my sister. ✗ Say me the truth → ✓ Tell me the truth / Say it to me.

kk.title: Жоғары деңгей қателері
kk.html: ✗ He suggested me a good hotel → ✓ He suggested a good hotel to me. ✗ I bought to my sister a gift → ✓ I bought my sister a gift / a gift for my sister. ✗ Say me the truth → ✓ Tell me the truth / Say it to me.

ru.title: Кратко
ru.html:
• Дитранзитивные глаголы: V + IO + DO или V + DO + to/for + IO.
  • to = передача получателю; for = для кого-то.
  • Порядок по конечному весу/фокусу; длинное или новое — в конец.
  • explain/describe/suggest берут только DO + to + IO — IO никогда не первым.

kk.title: Қысқаша
kk.html:
• Дитранзитивті етістіктер V + IO + DO не V + DO + to/for + IO алады.
  • to = алушыға беру; for = біреу үшін.
  • Соңғы салмақ/фокуспен рет; ұзын не жаңа ақпарат соңында.
  • explain/describe/suggest тек DO + to + IO — IO ешқашан алдында емес.

activities.prompt: Sort each verb by the preposition it takes when the recipient follows.
activities.why: Verbs of transfer (give/lend/show) take ‘to’; verbs of doing something on someone's behalf (buy/cook/find) take ‘for’.

items.s: She offered me a lift.
They awarded the prize to a newcomer.
He explained me the instructions.
I suggested her a solution.

activities.why: explain and suggest don't allow IO first: ‘explained the instructions to me’, ‘suggested a solution to her’.

activities.before: She stayed up late to make a costume
activities.why: ‘make’ is done on someone's behalf → for her son.
describe takes DO + to + IO → describe the procedure to the interns.

alts: is given
activities.why: English prefers the person as passive subject → each graduate was given a certificate.
the long indirect object goes last in a to-phrase for good end-weight.

activities.q: Complete: “Can you pass ___?” (the object is a pronoun)
why: ‘pass me it’ is possible but marked in many varieties.
‘it me’ is not standard.
Correct — with a pronoun direct object, ‘pass it to me’ is the natural order.

activities.q: Which sentence is ungrammatical?
why: ‘tell’ allows IO + DO.
Correct — ‘mention’ can't take IO first: ‘mentioned a rumour to me’.
‘send’ allows IO + DO.

activities.prompt: Sort each verb.
activities.why: give/lend allow the person directly after the verb; explain/suggest require ‘to + person’ after the thing.
explain can't take IO first → “explained the history of the castle to us.”

activities.instruction: Rewrite moving the recipient after the direct object:
activities.prompt: My uncle bought me a bicycle.
activities.answer: my uncle bought a bicycle for me
alts: My uncle bought a bicycle for me
my uncle bought a bicycle for me.
activities.why: ‘buy’ takes ‘for’ when the recipient follows the direct object → a bicycle for me.

activities.typeLabel: Extended context: sorting out a gift

steps.npc: Did you get anything for Grandpa's birthday?

options.t: Yes — I bought him a warm scarf.
options.why: buy allows V + IO + DO (bought him a scarf).

options.t: Yes — I bought to him a warm scarf.
options.why: ‘buy’ uses either ‘bought him a scarf’ or ‘bought a scarf for him’ — not ‘to him’.

steps.npc: Did the shop assistant help you choose?

options.t: She did — she suggested a couple of options to me.
options.why: suggest takes DO + to + IO (a couple of options to me).

options.t: She did — she suggested me a couple of options.
options.why: ‘suggest’ can't take IO first; use ‘suggested a couple of options to me’.

activities.why: Use V + IO + DO or V + DO + to/for + IO by end-weight, pick to (transfer) vs for (on behalf of), and remember explain/describe/suggest never take the indirect object first.

learn.title: The core split: which verbs take what
learn.html: Some verbs are followed by an -ing form, others by a to-infinitive, and some by either. This is largely lexical (learned per verb), but patterns help: verbs of liking/avoiding/finishing often take -ing; verbs of wanting/planning/deciding often take to-infinitive.

learn.title: Verb + -ing
learn.html: -ing follows: enjoy, avoid, finish, mind, suggest, imagine, deny, admit, risk, practise, consider, keep (on), give up, can't help, can't stand, feel like: I enjoy cooking. · She admitted taking it. · I couldn't help laughing.

learn.title: Verb + to-infinitive
learn.html: to-infinitive follows: want, hope, decide, plan, offer, refuse, promise, manage, agree, learn, afford, pretend, seem, appear, tend, fail, arrange, deserve: I decided to leave. · He refused to sign. · They managed to escape.

learn.title: Verb + object + to-infinitive
learn.html: Many verbs need an object before the to-infinitive: want, ask, tell, advise, allow, expect, encourage, persuade, force, enable, remind, warn, invite: I want you to stay. · They persuaded him to apply. · She reminded me to call.

learn.title: Verbs taking either, with little change
learn.html: begin, start, continue, bother, intend, can't bear take either form with almost no meaning change: It began to rain / began raining. (Avoid two -ing forms together: It's starting to rain, not ‘starting raining’.) like, love, hate, prefer also take either — a nuance covered in the next unit.

learn.title: Advanced learner errors
learn.html:
✗ I enjoy to cook. → ✓ I enjoy cooking.

✗ She suggested to go out. → ✓ She suggested going out / that we go out.

✗ They want that I stay. → ✓ They want me to stay.

learn.title: In a nutshell
learn.html:
• -ing verbs: enjoy, avoid, finish, mind, suggest, admit, can't help…
  • to-infinitive verbs: want, decide, hope, refuse, manage, agree…
  • Many verbs need object + to-infinitive: tell/ask/advise/persuade someone to do.
  • begin/start/continue take either form with no real change.

ru.title: Ключевое деление: какой глагол что берёт
ru.html: После одних глаголов идёт -ing форма, после других to-infinitive, а после некоторых оба. Это в основном лексика (учится по глаголу), но модели помогают: глаголы нравится/избегать/заканчивать часто берут -ing; хотеть/планировать/решать часто to-infinitive.

kk.title: Негізгі бөліну: қай етістік нені алады
kk.html: Кейбір етістіктен кейін -ing форма, басқасынан to-infinitive, кейбірінен екеуі де келеді. Бұл негізінен лексикалық (әр етістікке үйреніледі), бірақ үлгі көмектеседі: ұнату/қашу/бітіру етістіктері жиі -ing; қалау/жоспарлау/шешу жиі to-infinitive.

ru.title: Verb + -ing
ru.html: -ing следует за: enjoy, avoid, finish, mind, suggest, imagine, deny, admit, risk, practise, consider, keep (on), give up, can't help, can't stand, feel like: I enjoy cooking. She admitted taking it. I couldn't help laughing.

kk.title: Verb + -ing
kk.html: -ing кейін келеді: enjoy, avoid, finish, mind, suggest, imagine, deny, admit, risk, practise, consider, keep (on), give up, can't help, can't stand, feel like: I enjoy cooking. She admitted taking it. I couldn't help laughing.

ru.title: Verb + to-infinitive
ru.html: to-infinitive следует за: want, hope, decide, plan, offer, refuse, promise, manage, agree, learn, afford, pretend, seem, appear, tend, fail, arrange, deserve: I decided to leave. He refused to sign. They managed to escape.

kk.title: Verb + to-infinitive
kk.html: to-infinitive кейін келеді: want, hope, decide, plan, offer, refuse, promise, manage, agree, learn, afford, pretend, seem, appear, tend, fail, arrange, deserve: I decided to leave. He refused to sign. They managed to escape.

ru.title: Verb + object + to-infinitive
ru.html: Многие глаголы требуют объект перед to-infinitive: want, ask, tell, advise, allow, expect, encourage, persuade, force, enable, remind, warn, invite: I want you to stay. They persuaded him to apply. She reminded me to call.

kk.title: Verb + object + to-infinitive
kk.html: Көп етістік to-infinitive алдында объект қажет етеді: want, ask, tell, advise, allow, expect, encourage, persuade, force, enable, remind, warn, invite: I want you to stay. They persuaded him to apply. She reminded me to call.

ru.title: Глаголы, берущие оба, почти без изменения
ru.html: begin, start, continue, bother, intend, can't bear берут оба без изменения смысла: It began to rain / began raining. (Избегай двух -ing подряд: It's starting to rain, не ‘starting raining’.) like, love, hate, prefer тоже берут оба — нюанс в следующем разделе.

kk.title: Екеуін де алатын, мағына аз өзгеретін етістіктер
kk.html: begin, start, continue, bother, intend, can't bear екі форманы да мағына өзгертпей алады: It began to rain / began raining. (Қатар екі -ing-тен қаш: It's starting to rain, ‘starting raining’ емес.) like, love, hate, prefer де екеуін алады — нюансы келесі бөлімде.

ru.title: Ошибки продвинутого уровня
ru.html: ✗ I enjoy to cook → ✓ I enjoy cooking. ✗ She suggested to go out → ✓ She suggested going out / that we go out. ✗ They want that I stay → ✓ They want me to stay.

kk.title: Жоғары деңгей қателері
kk.html: ✗ I enjoy to cook → ✓ I enjoy cooking. ✗ She suggested to go out → ✓ She suggested going out / that we go out. ✗ They want that I stay → ✓ They want me to stay.

ru.title: Кратко
ru.html:
• -ing глаголы: enjoy, avoid, finish, mind, suggest, admit, can't help…
  • to-infinitive глаголы: want, decide, hope, refuse, manage, agree…
  • Многие требуют объект + to-infinitive: tell/ask/advise/persuade someone to do.
  • begin/start/continue берут обе формы без реального изменения.

kk.title: Қысқаша
kk.html:
• -ing етістіктер: enjoy, avoid, finish, mind, suggest, admit, can't help…
  • to-infinitive етістіктер: want, decide, hope, refuse, manage, agree…
  • Көбі объект + to-infinitive қажет етеді: tell/ask/advise/persuade someone to do.
  • begin/start/continue екі форманы да нақты өзгеріссіз алады.

activities.prompt: Sort each verb by the form that follows it.
activities.why: avoid/admit/can't help take -ing; decide/refuse/manage take a to-infinitive.

items.s: They agreed to postpone the trip.
I don't mind waiting.
She suggested to meet at noon.
He wants that we help.

activities.why: suggest takes -ing/that-clause (suggested meeting); want needs object + to-infinitive (wants us to help).
give up + -ing → gave up trying.
advise + object + to-infinitive → advised me to eat.
manage + to-infinitive → managed to board.
persuade + object + to-infinitive.

activities.q: “I can't stand ___ in queues.”
why: ‘can't stand’ doesn't take a to-infinitive.
Correct — can't stand + -ing → waiting.
a bare form can't follow ‘can't stand’.

activities.q: Which sentence is complete and correct?
options: I expect to finish by five.
I expect that finish by five.
why: Correct — expect + to-infinitive (no object here) is fine.
expect doesn't take -ing.
ungrammatical.

activities.typeLabel: Does it need an object before ‘to’?
activities.prompt: Sort each verb.
activities.why: promise/offer take a to-infinitive directly; remind/encourage need an object first (remind someone to do).
suggest takes -ing (or a that-clause) → “suggested holding the meeting online.”

activities.instruction: Rewrite using want + object + to-infinitive:
activities.prompt: I'd like you to be there.
activities.answer: I want you to be there
alts: I want you to be there.
i want you to be there
activities.why: want + object + to-infinitive → want you to be there.

steps.npc: So, how do we get started on this?

options.t: Well, I suggest breaking it into smaller tasks.
options.why: suggest + -ing (suggested breaking).

options.t: Well, I suggest to break it into smaller tasks.
options.why: ‘suggest’ takes -ing or a that-clause, not a to-infinitive.

steps.npc: And who leads each part?

options.t: I'd ask Dana to coordinate; she's good at it.
options.why: ask + object + to-infinitive (ask Dana to coordinate).

options.t: I'd ask that Dana coordinating; she's good at it.
options.why: ‘ask’ takes object + to-infinitive: ‘ask Dana to coordinate’.

activities.why: Learn each verb's complement: -ing (enjoy/avoid/suggest), to-infinitive (want/decide/manage), or object + to-infinitive (tell/ask/advise someone to do).

learn.title: When the form changes the meaning
learn.html: A small set of verbs takes both -ing and to-infinitive, but the meaning shifts. The key pairs are remember, forget, regret, stop, try, go on, mean, need. Getting these right is a hallmark of C1 precision.

learn.title: remember / forget / regret
learn.html: With these, -ing looks back (the action happened first); the to-infinitive looks forward (the action follows the remembering). I remember locking the door (I locked it, and recall it) vs I remembered to lock it (I recalled, then locked). I regret telling you (I told; I'm sorry) vs I regret to tell you (formal bad news, now).

learn.title: stop / go on
learn.html: stop + -ing = cease that activity: He stopped smoking. stop + to-infinitive = pause in order to do something: He stopped to smoke. go on + -ing = continue the same thing; go on + to-infinitive = move to a new thing: She went on talking vs She went on to become a judge.

learn.title: try / mean / need
learn.html: try + -ing = experiment with something as a solution; try + to-infinitive = attempt/make an effort: Try restarting it (as a fix) vs Try to lift it (make the effort). mean + -ing = involve/entail; mean + to-infinitive = intend: It means leaving early vs I meant to call. need + -ing = passive (needs doing); need + to-inf = active.

learn.title: Perception verbs: see/hear + -ing vs bare infinitive
learn.html: see/hear/watch/feel + object + -ing = an action in progress, partly seen: I saw him crossing the road. + bare infinitive = the whole, completed action: I saw him cross the road. A subtle but real aspectual difference.

learn.title: Advanced learner errors
learn.html:
✗ I stopped to smoke to mean I quit → ✓ I stopped smoking.

✗ I regret to tell you the news yesterday. → ✓ I regret telling you (past) / I regret to tell you (now).

✗ Remember calling him tomorrow. → ✓ Remember to call him tomorrow.

learn.title: In a nutshell
learn.html:
• remember/forget/regret: -ing looks back, to-infinitive looks forward.
  • stop + -ing = cease; stop + to-inf = pause in order to. go on + -ing = continue; + to-inf = move on.
  • try + -ing = experiment; try + to-inf = make an effort. mean + -ing = entail; + to-inf = intend.
  • Perception + -ing = action in progress; + bare infinitive = complete action.

ru.title: Когда форма меняет смысл
ru.html: Небольшой набор глаголов берёт оба — -ing и to-infinitive — но смысл сдвигается. Ключевые пары: remember, forget, regret, stop, try, go on, mean, need. Верное употребление — признак точности C1.

kk.title: Форма мағынаны өзгерткенде
kk.html: Кішкене топ етістік екеуін де — -ing және to-infinitive — алады, бірақ мағына ауысады. Негізгі жұптар: remember, forget, regret, stop, try, go on, mean, need. Мұны дұрыс істеу — C1 дәлдігінің белгісі.

ru.title: remember / forget / regret
ru.html: С ними -ing смотрит назад (действие было раньше); to-infinitive смотрит вперёд (действие после припоминания). I remember locking the door (запер, помню) / I remembered to lock it (вспомнил, затем запер). I regret telling you (сказал, сожалею) / I regret to tell you (формальная плохая новость, сейчас).

kk.title: remember / forget / regret
kk.html: Бұлармен -ing артқа қарайды (әрекет бұрын болды); to-infinitive алға қарайды (әрекет естен кейін). I remember locking the door (құлыптадым, есте) / I remembered to lock it (естедім, сосын құлыптадым). I regret telling you (айттым, өкінемін) / I regret to tell you (ресми жаман хабар, қазір).

ru.title: stop / go on
ru.html: stop + -ing = прекратить это действие: He stopped smoking. stop + to-infinitive = остановиться, чтобы что-то сделать: He stopped to smoke. go on + -ing = продолжать то же; go on + to-infinitive = перейти к новому: She went on talking / She went on to become a judge.

kk.title: stop / go on
kk.html: stop + -ing = сол әрекетті тоқтату: He stopped smoking. stop + to-infinitive = бірдеңе істеу үшін кідіру: He stopped to smoke. go on + -ing = сол нәрсені жалғастыру; go on + to-infinitive = жаңа нәрсеге көшу: She went on talking / She went on to become a judge.

ru.title: try / mean / need
ru.html: try + -ing = попробовать как решение; try + to-infinitive = пытаться/приложить усилие: Try restarting it (решение) / Try to lift it (постарайся). mean + -ing = влечь/означать; mean + to-infinitive = намереваться: It means leaving early / I meant to call. need + -ing = пассив (needs doing); need + to-inf = актив.

kk.title: try / mean / need
kk.html: try + -ing = шешім ретінде байқап көру; try + to-infinitive = тырысу/күш салу: Try restarting it (шешім) / Try to lift it (күш сал). mean + -ing = қамту/талап ету; mean + to-infinitive = ниеттену: It means leaving early / I meant to call. need + -ing = пассив (needs doing); need + to-inf = актив.

ru.title: Глаголы восприятия: see/hear + -ing vs bare infinitive
ru.html: see/hear/watch/feel + object + -ing = действие в процессе, частично увиденное: I saw him crossing the road. + bare infinitive = целое, завершённое действие: I saw him cross the road. Тонкое, но реальное аспектуальное различие.

kk.title: Қабылдау етістіктері: see/hear + -ing vs bare infinitive
kk.html: see/hear/watch/feel + object + -ing = орындалып жатқан, ішінара көрінген әрекет: I saw him crossing the road. + bare infinitive = толық, аяқталған әрекет: I saw him cross the road. Нәзік, бірақ нақты аспектілік айырма.

ru.title: Ошибки продвинутого уровня
ru.html: ✗ I stopped to smoke (в значении ‘бросил’) → ✓ I stopped smoking. ✗ I regret to tell you the news yesterday → ✓ I regret telling you (прошлое) / I regret to tell you (сейчас). ✗ Remember calling him tomorrow → ✓ Remember to call him tomorrow.

kk.title: Жоғары деңгей қателері
kk.html: ✗ I stopped to smoke (тастадым мағынасында) → ✓ I stopped smoking. ✗ I regret to tell you the news yesterday → ✓ I regret telling you (өткен) / I regret to tell you (қазір). ✗ Remember calling him tomorrow → ✓ Remember to call him tomorrow.

ru.title: Кратко
ru.html:
• remember/forget/regret: -ing назад, to-infinitive вперёд.
  • stop + -ing = прекратить; stop + to-inf = остановиться чтобы. go on + -ing = продолжать; + to-inf = перейти.
  • try + -ing = попробовать; try + to-inf = постараться. mean + -ing = влечь; + to-inf = намереваться.
  • Восприятие + -ing = в процессе; + bare infinitive = целое действие.

kk.title: Қысқаша
kk.html:
• remember/forget/regret: -ing артқа, to-infinitive алға қарайды.
  • stop + -ing = тоқтату; stop + to-inf = кідіру. go on + -ing = жалғастыру; + to-inf = көшу.
  • try + -ing = байқау; try + to-inf = тырысу. mean + -ing = қамту; + to-inf = ниеттену.
  • Қабылдау + -ing = орындалып жатқан; + bare infinitive = толық әрекет.

activities.prompt: Sort each remember/forget/regret use.
activities.why: -ing refers back to an earlier action; the to-infinitive points forward to what follows the remembering/regretting.

items.s: He stopped to tie his shoelace. (= he paused)
Try restarting the router. (= as a possible fix)
I stopped smoking means I paused to have a cigarette.
She went on talking means she changed to a new topic.

activities.why: ‘stopped smoking’ = quit; ‘went on talking’ = continued the same thing (not a new topic).
pausing in order to do something → stopped to admire.

activities.after: it off and on again. (try / turn)
activities.why: try + -ing = experiment with a possible fix → try turning.

activities.typeLabel: Move on to a new stage
activities.before: She started as an intern and
activities.why: go on + to-infinitive = progress to a new thing → went on to run.
remember + -ing = recalling an earlier completed action.

activities.q: “I meant to email you, but I got distracted.”
options: Emailing involved a lot of work.
why: that's ‘mean + -ing’ (entail).
Correct — mean + to-infinitive = intend.
the sentence says it didn't happen.

activities.q: “I saw the thief ___ the wallet and run off.” (I saw the complete action)
why: ‘taking’ would frame it as in progress, but the context is the whole completed action.
Correct — see + object + bare infinitive = the whole, completed action.
perception verbs don't take a to-infinitive here.

activities.prompt: Match each to its meaning.
activities.why: -ing = cease/entail/experiment; to-infinitive = pause in order to / make an effort / intend.
a future task to carry out → remember + to-infinitive → “remember to post this letter.”

activities.instruction: Rewrite so it means he quit the habit (not that he paused):
activities.prompt: He stopped to check his phone.
alts: He stopped checking his phone
he stopped checking his phone.
activities.why: ceasing the activity → stop + -ing → stopped checking.

activities.scene: Choose the form that carries the intended meaning.

steps.npc: My laptop's crawling — any ideas?

options.t: Try closing a few background apps first.
options.why: try + -ing = experiment with a possible solution.

options.t: Try to close a few background apps first.
options.why: here you're suggesting an experiment (try closing), not urging effort against difficulty.

steps.npc: Still slow. Did you actually update it last week like I said?

options.t: Honestly, I forgot to run the update.
options.why: a task not carried out → forget + to-infinitive.

options.t: Honestly, I forgot running the update.
options.why: ‘forgot running’ would mean you did it but don't recall; you didn't do it, so ‘forgot to run’.

activities.why: Choose -ing to look back/entail/experiment and the to-infinitive to look forward/intend/make an effort; use -ing vs bare infinitive after perception verbs for in-progress vs completed actions.

learn.title: An overview of reporting choices
learn.html: Reporting isn't one rule but a set of choices: which reporting verb (say, tell, admit, claim, warn…), which structure (that-clause, to-infinitive, -ing, wh-clause), whether to backshift the tense, and what to do with pronouns, time and place words.

learn.title: say vs tell (and others)
learn.html: tell needs a person object (She told me that…); say does not (She said that…, ✗ said me). Other patterns: explain/suggest/mention + to + person; ask/advise/remind/warn + person + to-infinitive.

learn.title: Direct → reported: what changes
learn.html: Moving from direct to reported speech typically shifts: tense back (is → was), pronouns (I → she), and time/place (now → then, here → there, tomorrow → the next day). “I'm tired now,” she said → She said she was tired then.

learn.title: Reporting verbs carry meaning
learn.html: The verb chosen interprets the original: claim (doubtful), admit (reluctant confession), insist (forceful), point out (drawing attention), warn, boast, complain. Choosing precisely is a major C1 skill — it summarises intention, not just words.

learn.title: Reporting thoughts & feelings
learn.html: The same structures report mental states: think, believe, feel, know, suppose, wonder, doubt, hope, be afraid: I thought (that) you were joking. · She wondered whether to stay. Negative reporting often shifts to the reporting verb: I don't think he'll come (rather than ‘I think he won't’).

learn.title: Advanced learner errors
learn.html:
✗ She said me that she was busy. → ✓ She told me / said that…

✗ He told that he agreed. → ✓ He said that… / told us that…

✗ I think he won't come (usually) → ✓ I don't think he'll come.

learn.title: In a nutshell
learn.html:
• Reporting = choose the verb + structure, and adjust tense/pronouns/time-place.
  • tell needs a person object; say doesn't.
  • The reporting verb interprets the original (claim/admit/insist/warn…).
  • Report thoughts too; prefer ‘don't think’ over ‘think … not’.

ru.title: Обзор выбора при передаче речи
ru.html: Передача речи — не одно правило, а набор выборов: какой глагол (say, tell, admit, claim, warn…), какая структура (that-clause, to-infinitive, -ing, wh-clause), нужен ли backshift, и что делать с местоимениями, словами времени и места.

kk.title: Баяндау таңдауларына шолу
kk.html: Баяндау — бір ереже емес, таңдаулар жиыны: қай баяндау етістігі (say, tell, admit, claim, warn…), қай құрылым (that-сөйлем, to-infinitive, -ing, wh-сөйлем), шақты backshift ету керек пе, және есімдік, уақыт, орын сөздерімен не істеу.

ru.title: say vs tell (и другие)
ru.html: tell требует объект-лицо (She told me that…); say — нет (She said that…, ✗ said me). Другие модели: explain/suggest/mention + to + лицо; ask/advise/remind/warn + лицо + to-infinitive.

kk.title: say vs tell (және басқалар)
kk.html: tell адам объектісін қажет етеді (She told me that…); say қажет етпейді (She said that…, ✗ said me). Басқа үлгілер: explain/suggest/mention + to + адам; ask/advise/remind/warn + адам + to-infinitive.

ru.title: Прямая → косвенная: что меняется
ru.html: Переход от прямой к косвенной обычно сдвигает: время назад (is → was), местоимения (I → she), время/место (now → then, here → there, tomorrow → the next day). “I'm tired now,” she said → She said she was tired then.

kk.title: Тура → баяндау: не өзгереді
kk.html: Турадан баяндауға көшкенде әдетте ауысады: шақ артқа (is → was), есімдіктер (I → she), уақыт/орын (now → then, here → there, tomorrow → the next day). “I'm tired now,” she said → She said she was tired then.

ru.title: Глаголы передачи несут значение
ru.html: Выбранный глагол интерпретирует оригинал: claim (сомнительно), admit (неохотное признание), insist (настойчиво), point out (привлечь внимание), warn, boast, complain. Точный выбор — важный навык C1: он резюмирует намерение, а не только слова.

kk.title: Баяндау етістіктері мағына тасиды
kk.html: Таңдалған етістік түпнұсқаны түсіндіреді: claim (күмәнді), admit (амалсыз мойындау), insist (қатты), point out (назар аудару), warn, boast, complain. Дәл таңдау — маңызды C1 дағдысы — ол сөзді ғана емес, ниетті қорытады.

ru.title: Передача мыслей и чувств
ru.html: Те же структуры передают ментальные состояния: think, believe, feel, know, suppose, wonder, doubt, hope, be afraid: I thought (that) you were joking. She wondered whether to stay. Отрицание часто сдвигается к глаголу: I don't think he'll come (а не ‘I think he won't’).

kk.title: Ой мен сезімді баяндау
kk.html: Дәл сол құрылымдар ой-күйді баяндайды: think, believe, feel, know, suppose, wonder, doubt, hope, be afraid: I thought (that) you were joking. She wondered whether to stay. Болымсыз баяндау жиі баяндау етістігіне ауысады: I don't think he'll come (‘I think he won't’ емес).

ru.title: Ошибки продвинутого уровня
ru.html: ✗ She said me that she was busy → ✓ She told me / said that… ✗ He told that he agreed → ✓ He said that… / told us that… ✗ I think he won't come (обычно) → ✓ I don't think he'll come.

kk.title: Жоғары деңгей қателері
kk.html: ✗ She said me that she was busy → ✓ She told me / said that… ✗ He told that he agreed → ✓ He said that… / told us that… ✗ I think he won't come (әдетте) → ✓ I don't think he'll come.

ru.title: Кратко
ru.html:
• Передача = выбрать глагол + структуру и подстроить время/местоимения/время-место.
  • tell требует объект-лицо; say — нет.
  • Глагол передачи интерпретирует оригинал (claim/admit/insist/warn…).
  • Передавай и мысли; предпочитай ‘don't think’ вместо ‘think … not’.

kk.title: Қысқаша
kk.html:
• Баяндау = етістік + құрылым таңдап, шақ/есімдік/уақыт-орынды түзету.
  • tell адам объектісін қажет етеді; say етпейді.
  • Баяндау етістігі түпнұсқаны түсіндіреді (claim/admit/insist/warn…).
  • Ойды да баянда; ‘think … not’ орнына ‘don't think’.

activities.prompt: Sort each reporting verb by whether it takes a person object directly.
activities.why: tell/remind/warn take a person object directly; say/explain/suggest don't (they use ‘to + person’).

items.s: She told me she'd be late.
He admitted breaking the vase.
She said me she was tired.
He explained me the reason.

activities.why: say and explain don't take a person object directly: ‘said (that)’, ‘explained … to me’.
present continuous backshifts to past continuous → he said he was leaving.

activities.after: that the meeting had moved. (tell / us)
activities.why: tell requires a person object → told us that…

activities.after: he'll agree to that. (I / not think)
activities.why: English prefers to negate the reporting verb → I don't think he'll agree.
admit + that-clause reports a reluctant confession.

activities.q: Original: “It wasn't me — honestly, I didn't touch it!” The best report is: “He ___ touching it.”
why: admit is the opposite — he's not confessing.
Correct — a firm rejection of the accusation → denied.
suggest doesn't fit a denial.

activities.q: Choose the correct verb: “She ___ that the results were delayed.”
why: ‘told’ needs a person object right after it.
Correct — ‘said that…’ needs no person object.
‘told to us’ is not the right pattern (either ‘told us’ or ‘said to us’).

activities.typeLabel: What attitude does the verb signal?
activities.prompt: Match each reporting verb to the attitude it conveys.
activities.why: claim/allege distance the speaker from the truth; admit signals reluctant confession; insist signals forceful assertion.
‘say’ can't take a person object; use ‘told us’ or ‘said (that)’ → “The guide told us that…”.

activities.instruction: Report in the past, backshifting: She said, →
activities.prompt: “I don't know where my keys are.”
activities.answer: she said she didn't know where her keys were
alts: She said she didn't know where her keys were
she said (that) she didn't know where her keys were
activities.why: backshift present → past and adjust the pronoun → she said she didn't know where her keys were.

steps.npc: What did the landlord actually say on the phone?

options.t: He told me the repair would be done by Friday.
options.why: tell + person object + backshifted that-clause.

options.t: He said me the repair would be done by Friday.
options.why: ‘say’ can't take a person object; use ‘told me’ or ‘said (that)’.

steps.npc: Did he sound sure about it?

options.t: Not really — he claimed the parts had arrived, but I doubt it.
options.why: ‘claim’ signals the speaker's doubt about the truth.

options.t: Not really — he admitted the parts had arrived, but I doubt it.
options.why: ‘admit’ would mean a reluctant confession of truth, contradicting ‘I doubt it’; ‘claim’ fits.

activities.why: Choose say vs tell correctly, adjust tense/pronouns/time-place, and pick a reporting verb that interprets the speaker's attitude.

learn.title: The that-clause as the core report
learn.html: The commonest way to report a statement is reporting verb + (that) + clause: She said (that) she was ready. · They argued (that) the plan was flawed. In informal English that is often dropped; in formal writing it's usually kept.

learn.title: Which verbs take a that-clause
learn.html: A large group: say, tell (sb), admit, claim, argue, believe, complain, explain, mention, point out, reply, report, reveal, suggest, warn, add, announce, insist, promise. Some also allow other patterns; the that-clause is the neutral report of a statement.

learn.title: Backshift — and when to keep the tense
learn.html: After a past reporting verb, tenses usually shift back. But we keep the original tense when the statement is still true or a general fact: She said the Earth revolves around the Sun. · He told me he works in Almaty (still does). Backshift is optional here, not obligatory.

learn.title: Verb patterns beyond that
learn.html: Some verbs pair the that-clause with a person differently: tell sb that (needs object); say / admit / explain that (no object, or to sb); promise/warn (sb) that. Learn whether the person is direct (tell me) or with to (explain to me).

learn.title: Register: formality of the that-clause
learn.html: Keeping that and choosing verbs like argue, maintain, assert, contend, observe, note raises the register — ideal for academic and formal writing: The author contends that… · It should be noted that… Dropping that and using say/tell lowers it toward speech.

learn.title: Advanced learner errors
learn.html:
✗ She said that to be ready. → ✓ She said (that) she was ready.

✗ He suggested me that… → ✓ He suggested (to me) that…

✗ Backshifting a permanent truth: He said water boiled at 100°C is fine, but … boils is often better for a general fact.

learn.title: In a nutshell
learn.html:
• Report statements with reporting verb + (that) + clause.
  • Drop ‘that’ informally; keep it in formal writing.
  • Backshift after a past verb, but keep the tense for still-true facts.
  • Match the person pattern: tell sb that vs explain/suggest (to sb) that.

ru.title: that-clause как основа передачи
ru.html: Самый частый способ передать утверждение — глагол + (that) + клауза: She said (that) she was ready. They argued (that) the plan was flawed. В неформальном английском that часто опускается; в формальном письме обычно сохраняется.

kk.title: that-сөйлем — баяндаудың өзегі
kk.html: Мәлімдемені баяндаудың ең кең тараған жолы — баяндау етістігі + (that) + сөйлем: She said (that) she was ready. They argued (that) the plan was flawed. Бейресми ағылшында that жиі түсіріледі; ресми жазуда әдетте сақталады.

ru.title: Какие глаголы берут that-clause
ru.html: Большая группа: say, tell (sb), admit, claim, argue, believe, complain, explain, mention, point out, reply, report, reveal, suggest, warn, add, announce, insist, promise. Некоторые допускают и другие модели; that-clause — нейтральная передача утверждения.

kk.title: Қай етістіктер that-сөйлем алады
kk.html: Үлкен топ: say, tell (sb), admit, claim, argue, believe, complain, explain, mention, point out, reply, report, reveal, suggest, warn, add, announce, insist, promise. Кейбірі басқа үлгіні де алады; that-сөйлем — мәлімдеменің бейтарап баяны.

ru.title: Backshift — и когда сохранять время
ru.html: После прошедшего глагола времена обычно сдвигаются назад. Но мы сохраняем исходное время, когда утверждение всё ещё истинно или общий факт: She said the Earth revolves around the Sun. He told me he works in Almaty (и сейчас). Здесь backshift необязателен.

kk.title: Backshift — және шақты сақтау
kk.html: Өткен баяндау етістігінен кейін шақтар әдетте артқа ауысады. Бірақ мәлімдеме әлі шын не жалпы факт болса, түпнұсқа шақты сақтаймыз: She said the Earth revolves around the Sun. He told me he works in Almaty (әлі де). Мұнда backshift міндетті емес.

ru.title: Модели глаголов кроме that
ru.html: Некоторые глаголы сочетают that-clause с лицом по-разному: tell sb that (нужен объект); say / admit / explain that (без объекта или to sb); promise/warn (sb) that. Учи, идёт ли лицо напрямую (tell me) или с to (explain to me).

kk.title: that-тен тыс етістік үлгілері
kk.html: Кейбір етістіктер that-сөйлемді адаммен басқаша жұптайды: tell sb that (объект керек); say / admit / explain that (объектсіз, не to sb); promise/warn (sb) that. Адам тікелей (tell me) ме әлде to-мен (explain to me) ме — үйрен.

ru.title: Регистр: формальность that-clause
ru.html: Сохранение that и выбор глаголов argue, maintain, assert, contend, observe, note повышает регистр — идеально для академического и формального письма: The author contends that… It should be noted that… Опущение that и say/tell снижают его к речи.

kk.title: Регистр: that-сөйлемнің ресмилігі
kk.html: that-ты сақтап, argue, maintain, assert, contend, observe, note сияқты етістіктерді таңдау регистрді көтереді — академиялық және ресми жазуға қолайлы: The author contends that… It should be noted that… that-ты түсіріп, say/tell қолдану оны тілге қарай төмендетеді.

ru.title: Ошибки продвинутого уровня
ru.html: ✗ She said that to be ready → ✓ She said (that) she was ready. ✗ He suggested me that… → ✓ He suggested (to me) that… ✗ Backshift постоянной истины: He said water boiled at 100°C допустимо, но … boils часто лучше для общего факта.

kk.title: Жоғары деңгей қателері
kk.html: ✗ She said that to be ready → ✓ She said (that) she was ready. ✗ He suggested me that… → ✓ He suggested (to me) that… ✗ Тұрақты шындықты backshift ету: He said water boiled at 100°C дұрыс, бірақ … boils жалпы факт үшін жиі жақсырақ.

ru.title: Кратко
ru.html:
• Передавай утверждения глаголом + (that) + клауза.
  • Опускай ‘that’ неформально; сохраняй в формальном письме.
  • Backshift после прошедшего глагола, но сохраняй время для всё ещё истинных фактов.
  • Согласуй модель лица: tell sb that vs explain/suggest (to sb) that.

kk.title: Қысқаша
kk.html:
• Мәлімдемені баяндау етістігі + (that) + сөйлеммен баянда.
  • Бейресми ‘that’-ты түсір; ресми жазуда сақта.
  • Өткен етістіктен кейін backshift, бірақ әлі шын фактіге шақты сақта.
  • Адам үлгісін сәйкестендір: tell sb that vs explain/suggest (to sb) that.

activities.prompt: Decide whether the underlined idea should backshift or can stay.

items.t: He said the Earth ORBITS the Sun.
They said the shop HAD closed.
She told me she WORKS in IT (still does).
He said he HAD lost his phone.

activities.why: Backshift for finished or changed situations; keep the present for still-true situations and general facts.

items.s: She argued that the policy was unfair.
He pointed out that we had missed a step.
He suggested me that I apply.
She said me that it was fine.

activities.why: suggest and say don't take a person object directly: ‘suggested (to me) that…’, ‘said (that)…’.

activities.before: “I've finished the report.” → She said she
alts: 'd finished
activities.why: present perfect backshifts to past perfect → she said she had finished.

alts: boiled
activities.why: a general scientific fact → keep the present boils (backshift is optional).

alts: noted
stated
activities.why: a formal verb introducing the that-clause → revealed that…
insist + that-clause, backshifted for a finished action.

activities.q: Reporting a colleague who still lives in Astana: “She told me she ___ in Astana.”
why: possible, but implies she may no longer live there.
Correct — since it's still true, keeping ‘lives’ is natural (backshift optional).
past perfect wrongly signals she moved away earlier.

activities.q: Which sentence best suits a formal academic essay?
options: The writer says climate models aren't perfect.
The author acknowledges that climate models are imperfect.
She goes on about models not being perfect.
why: ‘says’ and the contraction are informal.
Correct — ‘acknowledges that…’ with the retained ‘that’ suits academic register.
colloquial and dismissive in tone.

activities.prompt: Sort each verb by how it takes the listener.
activities.why: tell/remind take a direct person object; explain/admit use ‘to + person’ or no object.
after the past ‘claimed’, the present perfect backshifts → “claimed that she had discovered…”.

activities.instruction: Report in the past: The manager admitted, →
activities.prompt: “We have underestimated the costs.”
activities.answer: the manager admitted that they had underestimated the costs
alts: The manager admitted that they had underestimated the costs
the manager admitted they had underestimated the costs
activities.why: admit + that-clause with present perfect → past perfect and pronoun shift → had underestimated.

steps.npc: So what was the headline from the finance meeting?

options.t: They reported that revenue had fallen for the third quarter.
options.why: report + that-clause, backshifted for a finished period.

options.t: They reported us that revenue had fallen for the third quarter.
options.why: ‘report’ doesn't take a direct person object; use ‘reported (to us) that…’.

steps.npc: Did they mention anything that's still the case?

options.t: Yes — they noted that the core business remains profitable.
options.why: a still-true fact keeps the present ‘remains’.

options.t: Yes — they noted that the core business remained profitable, though it still is.
options.why: for a still-true fact, keeping ‘remains’ avoids the contradiction with ‘it still is’.

activities.why: Report statements with reporting verb + (that) + clause, backshift only for finished/changed situations, and match each verb's person pattern and register.

learn.title: Reporting questions & indirect wh-structures
learn.html: To report questions or embed them, we use a wh-clause (what, where, when, why, how, who, which, whether/if) with statement word order — no inversion, no do: I asked where she lived. · Do you know what time it is?

learn.title: whether / if for yes-no questions
learn.html: Report a yes/no question with whether or if: He asked whether/if I was coming. Prefer whether before to-infinitive (I wondered whether to stay), before or not immediately (whether or not), and in formal writing. Use if mainly in informal reports.

learn.title: Verbs that take a wh-clause
learn.html: Many verbs of asking, knowing and thinking: ask, wonder, know, understand, decide, explain, discover, find out, forget, remember, depend on, be sure, have no idea: It depends on how much it costs. · I can't remember where I parked.

learn.title: wh- + to-infinitive
learn.html: A neat, economical pattern: wh-word + to-infinitive = an implied ‘should’: I don't know what to say. · Tell me how to do it. · She's deciding where to go. (Not with why: ✗ why to go.) This packs a whole reported question into a phrase.

learn.title: No inversion — the key trap
learn.html: The single biggest error is keeping question word order inside the wh-clause: ✗ I don't know where is he. → ✓ … where he is. The embedded clause behaves like a statement, whatever the main clause does.

learn.title: Advanced learner errors
learn.html:
✗ Tell me what did you do. → ✓ Tell me what you did.

✗ I wonder that he wants. → ✓ I wonder what he wants.

✗ I don't know why to leave. → ✓ I don't know whether to leave / why I should leave.

learn.title: In a nutshell
learn.html:
• Report questions with a wh-clause in statement word order (no inversion, no do).
  • Use whether/if for yes-no; prefer whether before to-inf, or not, and in formal writing.
  • wh-word + to-infinitive compresses a reported question (not with why).
  • The embedded clause is a statement — that's the crucial pattern.

ru.title: Передача вопросов и косвенные wh-структуры
ru.html: Чтобы передать или встроить вопросы, используем wh-clause (what, where, when, why, how, who, which, whether/if) с порядком утверждения — без инверсии, без do: I asked where she lived. Do you know what time it is?

kk.title: Сұрақтарды баяндау және жанама wh-құрылымдар
kk.html: Сұрақтарды баяндау не енгізу үшін wh-сөйлемді (what, where, when, why, how, who, which, whether/if) сөйлем сөз ретімен қолданамыз — инверсия жоқ, do жоқ: I asked where she lived. Do you know what time it is?

ru.title: whether / if для да-нет вопросов
ru.html: Да/нет вопрос передавай через whether или if: He asked whether/if I was coming. Предпочитай whether перед to-infinitive (I wondered whether to stay), перед or not напрямую (whether or not) и в формальном письме. if в основном в неформальной передаче.

kk.title: whether / if yes-no сұрақтарға
kk.html: Yes/no сұрақты whether не if-пен баянда: He asked whether/if I was coming. whether-ды to-infinitive алдында (I wondered whether to stay), or not тікелей алдында (whether or not), және ресми жазуда таңда. if-ты негізінен бейресми баянда қолдан.

ru.title: Глаголы с wh-clause
ru.html: Многие глаголы вопроса, знания и мысли: ask, wonder, know, understand, decide, explain, discover, find out, forget, remember, depend on, be sure, have no idea: It depends on how much it costs. I can't remember where I parked.

kk.title: wh-сөйлем алатын етістіктер
kk.html: Сұрау, білу, ойлаудың көп етістігі: ask, wonder, know, understand, decide, explain, discover, find out, forget, remember, depend on, be sure, have no idea: It depends on how much it costs. I can't remember where I parked.

ru.title: wh- + to-infinitive
ru.html: Экономная модель: wh-слово + to-infinitive = подразумеваемое ‘should’: I don't know what to say. Tell me how to do it. She's deciding where to go. (Не с why: ✗ why to go.) Упаковывает целый косвенный вопрос во фразу.

kk.title: wh- + to-infinitive
kk.html: Ықшам үлгі: wh-сөз + to-infinitive = жасырын ‘should’: I don't know what to say. Tell me how to do it. She's deciding where to go. (why-мен емес: ✗ why to go.) Бұл толық баяндалған сұрақты бір тіркеске сыйдырады.

ru.title: Нет инверсии — ключевая ловушка
ru.html: Самая большая ошибка — сохранять вопросительный порядок внутри wh-clause: ✗ I don't know where is he → ✓ … where he is. Встроенная клауза ведёт себя как утверждение, что бы ни делала главная.

kk.title: Инверсия жоқ — басты тұзақ
kk.html: Ең үлкен қате — wh-сөйлем ішінде сұрақ сөз ретін сақтау: ✗ I don't know where is he → ✓ … where he is. Енгізілген сөйлем басты сөйлем не істесе де, мәлімдеме сияқты болады.

ru.title: Ошибки продвинутого уровня
ru.html: ✗ Tell me what did you do → ✓ Tell me what you did. ✗ I wonder that he wants → ✓ I wonder what he wants. ✗ I don't know why to leave → ✓ I don't know whether to leave / why I should leave.

kk.title: Жоғары деңгей қателері
kk.html: ✗ Tell me what did you do → ✓ Tell me what you did. ✗ I wonder that he wants → ✓ I wonder what he wants. ✗ I don't know why to leave → ✓ I don't know whether to leave / why I should leave.

ru.title: Кратко
ru.html:
• Передавай вопросы через wh-clause в порядке утверждения (без инверсии, без do).
  • whether/if для да-нет; whether перед to-inf, or not и в формальном письме.
  • wh-слово + to-infinitive сжимает косвенный вопрос (не с why).
  • Встроенная клауза — утверждение, ключевая модель.

kk.title: Қысқаша
kk.html:
• Сұрақтарды wh-сөйлеммен, сөйлем сөз ретінде баянда (инверсия жоқ, do жоқ).
  • yes-no-ға whether/if; to-inf, or not алдында және ресми жазуда whether.
  • wh-сөз + to-infinitive баяндалған сұрақты ықшамдайды (why-мен емес).
  • Енгізілген сөйлем — мәлімдеме, ең маңызды үлгі.

activities.prompt: Sort each embedded clause as correct or incorrect.
activities.why: Embedded wh-clauses use statement word order — no inversion and no do-support.

items.s: I wonder whether to accept the offer.
Can you tell me what time the train leaves?
Do you know where is the station?
I don't know why to complain.

activities.why: embedded clauses drop inversion (where the station is), and ‘why + to-infinitive’ isn't used.
statement word order in the embedded clause → why he left.

activities.after: the job or keep looking. (whether / accept)
activities.why: whether + to-infinitive expresses a choice → whether to accept.
wh-word + to-infinitive = an implied ‘should’ → how to use.
embedded wh-clause in statement order (what the message meant).

activities.q: Which is preferred: “She was unsure ___ to tell him or not.”
why: ‘if’ isn't used before a to-infinitive.
Correct — before a to-infinitive (and with ‘or not’), use ‘whether’.
‘that’ can't introduce a yes/no choice.

activities.q: Which is the correct embedded question?
options: I asked her where did she buy it.
I asked her where she bought it.
I asked her where bought she it.
why: keeps question inversion/do — wrong in an embedded clause.
Correct — statement order with no ‘did’.
garbled word order.

activities.prompt: Sort each context by the better choice.

items.t: The report examines ___ or not it works.
Let me know ___ you're coming (text to a friend).

activities.why: whether suits to-infinitives, ‘or not’, and formal writing; if is fine in informal, everyday reports.
an embedded question drops ‘does’ and uses statement order → “how this machine works.”

activities.instruction: Report in the past: He asked me, →
activities.prompt: “When did you arrive?”
activities.answer: he asked me when I had arrived
alts: He asked me when I had arrived
he asked me when I arrived
He asked me when I arrived
activities.why: embed with statement order and backshift → when I had arrived (or ‘when I arrived’).

steps.npc: Excuse me — could you tell me where the ticket office is?

options.t: Of course. Do you know which platform you need first?
