options.why: embedded wh-clause in statement order (which platform you need).

options.t: Of course. Do you know which platform do you need first?
options.why: an embedded question can't keep ‘do you’; say ‘which platform you need’.

steps.npc: I'm honestly not sure I'm on the right train.

options.t: Let me check whether this one stops at your station.
options.why: a yes/no embedded question → whether + statement order.

options.t: Let me check does this one stop at your station.
options.why: embed it: ‘whether this one stops…’, not the question form ‘does this one stop’.

activities.why: Report questions with a wh-clause in statement order, use whether/if appropriately, and compress with wh-word + to-infinitive — never keeping inversion inside the clause.

learn.title: Backshift is a choice, not a reflex
learn.html:
After a past reporting verb, tenses often move back one step, but this is optional and meaning-driven, not automatic. Backshift when the situation is finished or no longer relevant; keep the tense when it is still true, habitual, or a general fact.

She said she was tired.then; now unknown → backshift
She said she works here.still true → keep present

learn.title: The backshift ladder
learn.html: present → past; present perfect / past → past perfect; will → would; can → could; may → might; must → had to. “I can help.” → He said he could help. · “I have seen it.” → She said she had seen it.

learn.title: When NOT to backshift
learn.html: Keep the original tense for: present/general truths (He said the train leaves at nine — timetable), still-true states (She said she lives nearby), and when the reporting verb is present (She says she is busy). Past perfect and would/could/might generally don't shift further.

learn.title: Unreal & modal forms resist shifting
learn.html: Second/third conditionals and would/could/should/might/ought to usually stay the same: “If I had time, I'd help.” → She said (that) if she had time, she'd help. must for deduction stays must; must for obligation may become had to.

learn.title: Time & place adjustments follow the tense
learn.html: If you backshift, also convert deictic words: now → then, today → that day, tomorrow → the next day, yesterday → the day before, this → that, here → there, ago → before. Keep them unchanged if the report is made in the same time frame: “I'll call tomorrow.” reported today can stay tomorrow.

learn.title: Advanced learner errors
learn.html:
✗ She said she is tired yesterday. → ✓ She said she was tired.

✗ Over-backshifting a fact: He said the sun rose in the east → often better … rises in the east.

✗ He said he will come (past report) → ✓ … he would come.

learn.title: In a nutshell
learn.html:
• Backshift after a past verb for finished/changed situations; keep the tense for still-true facts.
  • Ladder: present→past, perfect/past→past perfect, will→would, can→could, may→might.
  • Unreal conditionals and would/could/might/should don't shift further.
  • Convert time/place words to match — unless the time frame is unchanged.

ru.title: Backshift — выбор, а не рефлекс
ru.html: После прошедшего глагола времена часто сдвигаются на шаг назад, но это выбор, зависящий от смысла, а не автомат. Сдвигай, когда ситуация завершена или неактуальна; сохраняй время, когда она всё ещё истинна, привычна или общий факт. She said she was tired / She said she works here.

kk.title: Backshift — таңдау, рефлекс емес
kk.html: Өткен баяндау етістігінен кейін шақтар жиі бір қадам артқа жылжиды, бірақ бұл таңдау әрі мағынаға тәуелді, автомат емес. Жағдай аяқталған не өзекті емес болса backshift; әлі шын, әдеттегі, не жалпы факт болса шақты сақта. She said she was tired / She said she works here.

ru.title: Лестница backshift
ru.html: present → past; present perfect / past → past perfect; will → would; can → could; may → might; must → had to. “I can help.” → He said he could help. “I have seen it.” → She said she had seen it.

kk.title: Backshift сатысы
kk.html: present → past; present perfect / past → past perfect; will → would; can → could; may → might; must → had to. “I can help.” → He said he could help. “I have seen it.” → She said she had seen it.

ru.title: Когда НЕ делать backshift
ru.html: Сохраняй исходное время для: настоящих/общих истин (He said the train leaves at nine — расписание), всё ещё истинных состояний (She said she lives nearby) и когда глагол в настоящем (She says she is busy). Past perfect и would/could/might обычно дальше не сдвигаются.

kk.title: Қашан backshift ЕТПЕУ керек
kk.html: Түпнұсқа шақты сақта: осы/жалпы шындық (He said the train leaves at nine — кесте), әлі шын күй (She said she lives nearby), және баяндау етістігі осы шақ болғанда (She says she is busy). Past perfect және would/could/might әдетте одан әрі жылжымайды.

ru.title: Нереальные и модальные формы не сдвигаются
ru.html: Second/third conditionals и would/could/should/might/ought to обычно остаются: “If I had time, I'd help.” → She said if she had time, she'd help. Дедуктивное must — must; must обязательства может стать had to.

kk.title: Шартты және модаль формалар жылжуға қарсы
kk.html: Second/third conditionals және would/could/should/might/ought to әдетте өзгермейді: “If I had time, I'd help.” → She said if she had time, she'd help. Тұжырымдық must — must; міндеттік must — had to болуы мүмкін.

ru.title: Корректировки времени/места следуют за временем
ru.html: Если делаешь backshift, преобразуй и дейктические слова: now → then, today → that day, tomorrow → the next day, yesterday → the day before, this → that, here → there, ago → before. Оставь их, если передача в той же временнóй рамке: “I'll call tomorrow.”, переданное сегодня, может остаться tomorrow.

kk.title: Уақыт/орын түзетулері шақпен жүреді
kk.html: Backshift етсең, deictic сөздерді де түрлендір: now → then, today → that day, tomorrow → the next day, yesterday → the day before, this → that, here → there, ago → before. Баян сол уақыт шеңберінде жасалса, оларды өзгертпе: бүгін баяндалған “I'll call tomorrow.” — tomorrow қалуы мүмкін.

ru.title: Ошибки продвинутого уровня
ru.html: ✗ She said she is tired yesterday → ✓ She said she was tired. ✗ Чрезмерный backshift факта: He said the sun rose in the east → часто лучше … rises in the east. ✗ He said he will come (прошлая передача) → ✓ … he would come.

kk.title: Жоғары деңгей қателері
kk.html: ✗ She said she is tired yesterday → ✓ She said she was tired. ✗ Фактіні артық backshift ету: He said the sun rose in the east → жиі жақсырақ … rises in the east. ✗ He said he will come (өткен баян) → ✓ … he would come.

ru.title: Кратко
ru.html:
• Backshift для завершённых/изменённых ситуаций; сохраняй время для всё ещё истинных фактов.
  • Лестница: present→past, perfect/past→past perfect, will→would, can→could, may→might.
  • Нереальные условные и would/could/might/should дальше не сдвигаются.
  • Преобразуй слова времени/места — если рамка неизменна, оставь как есть.

kk.title: Қысқаша
kk.html:
• Аяқталған/өзгерген жағдайға backshift; әлі шын фактіге шақты сақта.
  • Сатысы: present→past, perfect/past→past perfect, will→would, can→could, may→might.
  • Шартты сөйлемдер мен would/could/might/should одан әрі жылжымайды.
  • Уақыт/орын сөздерін сәйкестендір — уақыт шеңбері өзгермесе, өзгертпе.

activities.prompt: Decide for each reported idea.

items.t: “The meeting starts at nine.” (timetable, still valid)

activities.why: Backshift for finished/changed situations; keep the tense for general truths, timetables and still-true states.

items.s: He said he would call the next day.
She said she had already eaten.
He said he will help tomorrow (past report).
They said they are leaving then.

activities.why: after a past verb, will→would and present→past: ‘he would help’, ‘they were leaving’.

activities.before: “I can't make it.” → He said he
activities.why: can/can't backshifts to could/couldn't → he said he couldn't make it.

alts: travelled
activities.why: a general scientific truth → keep the present travels.

activities.before: “If I were rich, I'd travel.” → She said that if she
alts: was
activities.why: the unreal past of a second conditional doesn't shift further → were.
past perfect + ‘the day before’ (yesterday → the day before).

activities.q: Direct (said last week): “I'll see the doctor tomorrow.” Reported now: “She said she'd see the doctor ___.”
why: ‘tomorrow’ no longer matches, since the report is much later.
Correct — from a past viewpoint, ‘tomorrow’ becomes ‘the next day’.
‘yesterday’ reverses the time direction.

activities.q: Best report of “The Nile is the longest river”: “The guide said the Nile ___ the longest river.”
why: possible, but suggests it might no longer be true.
Correct — a still-true general fact keeps the present ‘is’.
past perfect wrongly implies it changed.

activities.prompt: Sort each by whether it changes when reported after a past verb.
activities.why: will→would and present perfect→past perfect shift; would and might are already back-shifted and stay.
after the past ‘said’, ‘will’ backshifts to ‘would’ → “she said she would phone me…”.

activities.instruction: Report in the past: He said, →
activities.prompt: “I have been waiting here since noon.”
activities.answer: he said he had been waiting there since noon
alts: He said he had been waiting there since noon
he said that he had been waiting there since noon
activities.why: present perfect continuous → past perfect continuous, and ‘here’ → ‘there’ → had been waiting there.

activities.typeLabel: Extended context: passing on what was said
activities.scene: Choose the correctly (or wisely un-)shifted report.

steps.npc: What did the estate agent tell you about the flat?

options.t: He said it's still available and the rent includes bills.
options.why: a currently-true situation can keep the present (‘is still available’).

options.t: He said it was still available, though it definitely still is.
options.why: since it's still true, keeping the present avoids the awkward clash with ‘it still is’.

steps.npc: Did he say anything about viewing times?

options.t: Yes — he said he could show us round the next day.
options.why: can → could, and tomorrow → the next day from a past viewpoint.

options.t: Yes — he said he can show us round tomorrow (reporting days later).
options.why: reported much later, ‘can’ → ‘could’ and ‘tomorrow’ → ‘the next day’.

activities.why: Backshift only when the situation is finished or changed, keep the present for still-true facts, and convert time/place words to match the reporting viewpoint.

learn.title: Matching function to pattern
learn.html: Beyond statements and questions, we report speech acts — offers, promises, orders, suggestions, warnings — with specific patterns. The reporting verb + its structure captures what the speaker was doing, not just saying.

learn.title: Verb + to-infinitive
learn.html: For offers, promises, threats, refusals, agreements about one's own action: offer, promise, agree, refuse, threaten, decide, claim + to-infinitive: He offered to help. · She refused to answer. · They threatened to sue.

learn.title: Verb + object + to-infinitive
learn.html: For getting someone else to act — orders, advice, requests, warnings, encouragement: tell, ask, advise, order, warn, remind, encourage, invite, persuade, urge, forbid + object + to-infinitive: She told him to wait. · They advised us to book early. · He warned them not to touch it.

learn.title: Verb + -ing / that-clause: suggestions
learn.html: For suggestions and admissions: suggest, recommend, propose + -ing or that-clause: He suggested waiting / suggested (that) we wait. Also admit/deny/apologise for + -ing: She apologised for being late. · He denied taking it.

learn.title: Prepositional reporting patterns
learn.html: Some verbs need a preposition + -ing: accuse sb of doing, blame sb for doing, congratulate sb on doing, insist on doing, warn sb against doing, thank sb for doing: They accused him of cheating. · She insisted on paying.

learn.title: Advanced learner errors
learn.html:
✗ He suggested me to wait. → ✓ He suggested (that) I wait / waiting.

✗ She promised me helping. → ✓ She promised to help.

✗ They accused him to steal. → ✓ They accused him of stealing.

learn.title: In a nutshell
learn.html:
• Own action (offer/promise/refuse) → verb + to-infinitive.
  • Get someone to act (tell/advise/order/warn) → verb + object + to-infinitive.
  • Suggestions (suggest/recommend) → -ing or that-clause, not object + to-infinitive.
  • Some verbs need preposition + -ing: accuse of, apologise for, insist on.

ru.title: Согласование функции с моделью
ru.html: Кроме утверждений и вопросов, мы передаём речевые акты — предложения, обещания, приказы, советы, предупреждения — специальными моделями. Глагол + его структура отражает, что говорящий делал, а не только сказал.

kk.title: Функцияны үлгіге сәйкестендіру
kk.html: Мәлімдеме мен сұрақтан бөлек, біз сөйлеу актілерін — ұсыныс, уәде, бұйрық, кеңес, ескерту — нақты үлгілермен баяндаймыз. Баяндау етістігі + құрылымы сөйлеуші не істегенін береді, тек айтқанын емес.

ru.title: Verb + to-infinitive
ru.html: Для предложений, обещаний, угроз, отказов, согласий о своём действии: offer, promise, agree, refuse, threaten, decide, claim + to-infinitive: He offered to help. She refused to answer. They threatened to sue.

kk.title: Verb + to-infinitive
kk.html: Өз әрекеті туралы ұсыныс, уәде, қорқыту, бас тарту, келісімге: offer, promise, agree, refuse, threaten, decide, claim + to-infinitive: He offered to help. She refused to answer. They threatened to sue.

ru.title: Verb + object + to-infinitive
ru.html: Чтобы побудить другого к действию — приказы, советы, просьбы, предупреждения: tell, ask, advise, order, warn, remind, encourage, invite, persuade, urge, forbid + object + to-infinitive: She told him to wait. They advised us to book early. He warned them not to touch it.

kk.title: Verb + object + to-infinitive
kk.html: Басқаны әрекетке итермелеу — бұйрық, кеңес, өтініш, ескерту, жігерлендіру: tell, ask, advise, order, warn, remind, encourage, invite, persuade, urge, forbid + object + to-infinitive: She told him to wait. They advised us to book early. He warned them not to touch it.

ru.title: Verb + -ing / that-clause: предложения
ru.html: Для предложений и признаний: suggest, recommend, propose + -ing или that-clause: He suggested waiting / suggested (that) we wait. Также admit/deny/apologise for + -ing: She apologised for being late. He denied taking it.

kk.title: Verb + -ing / that-сөйлем: ұсыныстар
kk.html: Ұсыныс пен мойындауға: suggest, recommend, propose + -ing не that-сөйлем: He suggested waiting / suggested (that) we wait. Сондай-ақ admit/deny/apologise for + -ing: She apologised for being late. He denied taking it.

ru.title: Предложные модели передачи
ru.html: Некоторым глаголам нужен предлог + -ing: accuse sb of doing, blame sb for doing, congratulate sb on doing, insist on doing, warn sb against doing, thank sb for doing: They accused him of cheating. She insisted on paying.

kk.title: Предлогтық баяндау үлгілері
kk.html: Кейбір етістік предлог + -ing қажет етеді: accuse sb of doing, blame sb for doing, congratulate sb on doing, insist on doing, warn sb against doing, thank sb for doing: They accused him of cheating. She insisted on paying.

ru.title: Ошибки продвинутого уровня
ru.html: ✗ He suggested me to wait → ✓ He suggested (that) I wait / waiting. ✗ She promised me helping → ✓ She promised to help. ✗ They accused him to steal → ✓ They accused him of stealing.

kk.title: Жоғары деңгей қателері
kk.html: ✗ He suggested me to wait → ✓ He suggested (that) I wait / waiting. ✗ She promised me helping → ✓ She promised to help. ✗ They accused him to steal → ✓ They accused him of stealing.

ru.title: Кратко
ru.html:
• Своё действие (offer/promise/refuse) → verb + to-infinitive.
  • Побудить другого (tell/advise/order/warn) → verb + object + to-infinitive.
  • Предложения (suggest/recommend) → -ing или that-clause, не object + to-infinitive.
  • Некоторым нужен предлог + -ing: accuse of, apologise for, insist on.

kk.title: Қысқаша
kk.html:
• Өз әрекеті (offer/promise/refuse) → verb + to-infinitive.
  • Басқаны әрекетке (tell/advise/order/warn) → verb + object + to-infinitive.
  • Ұсыныстар (suggest/recommend) → -ing не that-сөйлем, object + to-infinitive емес.
  • Кейбірі предлог + -ing қажет етеді: accuse of, apologise for, insist on.

activities.prompt: Sort each verb by its structure.
activities.why: offer/refuse take to-infinitive; advise/remind take object + to-infinitive; suggest/recommend take -ing or a that-clause.

items.s: She promised to pay me back.
They warned us not to swim there.
He suggested me to leave early.
She apologised to be late.

activities.why: suggest takes -ing/that-clause (not object + to-inf); apologise takes ‘for + -ing’ (apologised for being late).
refuse + to-infinitive (own action) → refused to say.
advise + object + to-infinitive → advised me to drink.
accuse sb of + -ing → accused him of manipulating.
suggest + that-clause (bare/subjunctive verb).

activities.q: Original: “Why don't we take a break?” Best report: “She ___.”
options: suggested us to take a break
advised taking a break to us
why: suggest can't take object + to-infinitive.
Correct — suggest + -ing (suggested taking a break).
garbled; advise would need an object + to-infinitive.

activities.q: Original: “If you don't pay, we'll take you to court.” Best report:
options: They offered to take him to court.
They threatened to take him to court.
They advised him to take them to court.
why: an offer is a positive proposal, not a threat.
Correct — a conditional harm → threaten + to-infinitive.
reverses who is doing what.

activities.prompt: Match each reporting verb to the preposition it takes before -ing.
activities.why: accuse of, apologise/blame/thank for, insist on / warn against / congratulate on — fixed preposition + -ing.
encourage takes object + to-infinitive → “encouraged the players to train harder.”

activities.instruction: Report using offer + to-infinitive: He said, →
activities.prompt: “I'll give you a lift to the station.”
activities.answer: he offered to give me a lift to the station
alts: He offered to give me a lift to the station
he offered to give me a lift
activities.why: a spontaneous offer about one's own action → offer + to-infinitive → offered to give me a lift.

steps.npc: So how did the argument with the supplier go?

options.t: Badly — they accused us of missing the deadline.
options.why: accuse sb of + -ing.

options.t: Badly — they accused us to miss the deadline.
options.why: ‘accuse’ takes ‘of + -ing’: ‘accused us of missing’.

steps.npc: How did you leave it?

options.t: They insisted on renegotiating the whole contract.
options.why: insist on + -ing.

options.t: They insisted us to renegotiate the whole contract.
options.why: ‘insist’ takes ‘on + -ing’ or a that-clause, not object + to-infinitive.

activities.why: Match the speech act to its pattern: to-infinitive for one's own action, object + to-infinitive to direct others, -ing/that-clause for suggestions, and the right preposition + -ing for accuse/apologise/insist.

learn.title: How modals behave in reported speech
learn.html:
Some modals shift back in reported speech; others can't shift because they have no further past form. Knowing which is which is a precise C1 skill.

will → would; can → could; may → might; must (obligation) → had to; shall → should/wouldthese shift
would, could, might, should, ought to, used to, must (deduction)these stay

learn.title: The shifting modals
learn.html: “I will call.” → He said he would call. · “I can swim.” → She said she could swim. · “It may rain.” → They said it might rain. · “You must go.” → He said I had to go.

learn.title: The unchanging modals
learn.html: would, could, might, should, ought to, had better, used to are already past/hypothetical, so they stay the same: “You should rest.” → She said I should rest. · “I could be wrong.” → He admitted he could be wrong.

learn.title: must: obligation vs deduction
learn.html: must splits by meaning. Obligation must usually becomes had to (external necessity) — though must can be kept for a lasting rule. Deduction must stays must: “She must be exhausted.” → He said she must be exhausted.

learn.title: mustn't, needn't & negatives
learn.html: mustn't (prohibition) usually stays: He said I mustn't tell anyone. needn't may stay or become didn't have to / wouldn't have to. Prohibition and permission meanings are preserved even when the form is unchanged.

learn.title: Advanced learner errors
learn.html:
✗ She said she will help. → ✓ She said she would help.

✗ He said she must have been the one (deduction) — this is fine; don't change deductive must to had to.

✗ They said we could to leave. → ✓ … we could leave.

learn.title: In a nutshell
learn.html:
• Shift: will→would, can→could, may→might, must (obligation)→had to.
  • Stay: would, could, might, should, ought to, used to, must (deduction).
  • must obligation → had to; must deduction → must.
  • mustn't (prohibition) usually stays; keep the meaning even if the form doesn't change.

ru.title: Как модальные ведут себя в косвенной речи
ru.html: Некоторые модальные сдвигаются назад; другие не могут, так как не имеют дальнейшей прошедшей формы. Знать, что есть что, — точный навык C1. will→would, can→could, may→might, must (обязательство)→had to сдвигаются; would, could, might, should, ought to, used to, must (дедукция) остаются.

kk.title: Модальдар баяндауда қалай өзгереді
kk.html: Кейбір модаль баяндауда артқа жылжиды; басқалары одан әрі өткен форма болмағандықтан жылжи алмайды. Қайсысы қайсы екенін білу — дәл C1 дағдысы. will→would, can→could, may→might, must (міндет)→had to жылжиды; would, could, might, should, ought to, used to, must (тұжырым) қалады.

ru.title: Сдвигающиеся модальные
ru.html: “I will call.” → He said he would call. “I can swim.” → She said she could swim. “It may rain.” → They said it might rain. “You must go.” → He said I had to go.

kk.title: Жылжитын модальдар
kk.html: “I will call.” → He said he would call. “I can swim.” → She said she could swim. “It may rain.” → They said it might rain. “You must go.” → He said I had to go.

ru.title: Неизменные модальные
ru.html: would, could, might, should, ought to, had better, used to уже прошедшие/гипотетические, поэтому остаются: “You should rest.” → She said I should rest. “I could be wrong.” → He admitted he could be wrong.

kk.title: Өзгермейтін модальдар
kk.html: would, could, might, should, ought to, had better, used to қазірдің өзінде өткен/болжамды, сондықтан өзгермейді: “You should rest.” → She said I should rest. “I could be wrong.” → He admitted he could be wrong.

ru.title: must: обязательство vs дедукция
ru.html: must делится по значению. Обязательство must обычно становится had to (внешняя необходимость) — но для постоянного правила must может сохраняться. Дедуктивное must остаётся must: “She must be exhausted.” → He said she must be exhausted.

kk.title: must: міндет vs тұжырым
kk.html: must мағынаға қарай бөлінеді. Міндеттік must әдетте had to болады (сыртқы қажеттілік) — бірақ тұрақты ереже үшін must сақталуы мүмкін. Тұжырымдық must must болып қалады: “She must be exhausted.” → He said she must be exhausted.

ru.title: mustn't, needn't и отрицания
ru.html: mustn't (запрет) обычно остаётся: He said I mustn't tell anyone. needn't может остаться или стать didn't have to / wouldn't have to. Значения запрета и разрешения сохраняются, даже если форма не меняется.

kk.title: mustn't, needn't және болымсыздар
kk.html: mustn't (тыйым) әдетте қалады: He said I mustn't tell anyone. needn't қалуы не didn't have to / wouldn't have to болуы мүмкін. Форма өзгермесе де, тыйым мен рұқсат мағынасы сақталады.

ru.title: Ошибки продвинутого уровня
ru.html: ✗ She said she will help → ✓ She said she would help. ✗ He said she must have been the one (дедукция) — это верно; не меняй дедуктивное must на had to. ✗ They said we could to leave → ✓ … we could leave.

kk.title: Жоғары деңгей қателері
kk.html: ✗ She said she will help → ✓ She said she would help. ✗ He said she must have been the one (тұжырым) — бұл дұрыс; тұжырымдық must-ты had to-ға айналдырма. ✗ They said we could to leave → ✓ … we could leave.

ru.title: Кратко
ru.html:
• Сдвиг: will→would, can→could, may→might, must (обязательство)→had to.
  • Остаются: would, could, might, should, ought to, used to, must (дедукция).
  • must обязательства → had to; must дедукции → must.
  • mustn't (запрет) обычно остаётся; сохраняй значение, даже если форма не меняется.

kk.title: Қысқаша
kk.html:
• Жылжиды: will→would, can→could, may→might, must (міндет)→had to.
  • Қалады: would, could, might, should, ought to, used to, must (тұжырым).
  • Міндеттік must → had to; тұжырымдық must → must.
  • mustn't (тыйым) әдетте қалады; форма өзгермесе де мағынаны сақта.

activities.prompt: Sort each modal by whether it changes when reported after a past verb.
activities.why: will/can/may shift (would/could/might); should/might/ought to are already back-shifted and stay.

items.s: He said he would be there by eight.
She said I should see a doctor.
They said we can leave early (past report).
He said she has to had left (deduction).

activities.why: ‘can’ shifts to ‘could’ after a past verb; deductive ‘must’ stays as ‘must’, not ‘has to had’.

activities.before: “I'll sort it out.” → She promised she
activities.why: will → would after a past reporting verb → she promised she would sort it out.

activities.before: “You must renew your visa.” → They told me I
activities.why: obligation ‘must’ shifts to had to in reported speech.

activities.before: “They must be lost.” → He said they
activities.why: deductive ‘must’ doesn't change → he said they must be lost.
‘might’ is already back-shifted, so it stays unchanged.

activities.q: Report the rule as an obligation: “You must wear a badge.” → “The guard said we ___ wear a badge.”
why: ‘must have’ changes it to a past deduction.
Correct — external obligation ‘must’ → ‘had to’.
‘should have’ turns it into a criticism/regret.

activities.q: Report: “I could help if needed.” → “He said he ___ help if needed.”
why: ‘can’ would be the shift of a present ability, but the original is already ‘could’.
Correct — ‘could’ is already back-shifted and stays.
‘must’ changes the meaning entirely.

activities.prompt: Sort each so you know whether to use ‘had to’ or keep ‘must’.
activities.why: Obligation ‘must’ becomes ‘had to’; deductive ‘must’ (a conclusion) stays ‘must’ when reported.
after the past ‘assured’, ‘will’ shifts to ‘would’ → “he assured us that he would finish…”.

activities.instruction: Report in the past: She said, →
activities.prompt: “I can't make it, but I may send someone.”
activities.answer: she said she couldn't make it but might send someone
alts: She said she couldn't make it but might send someone
she said she couldn't make it but she might send someone
activities.why: can't → couldn't and may → might → couldn't … might.

steps.npc: What did reception say about parking?

options.t: They said we had to register the car at the desk.
options.why: obligation ‘must’ → ‘had to’ in the report.

options.t: They said we must to register the car at the desk.
options.why: reported external obligation becomes ‘had to’, and modals never take ‘to’.

steps.npc: Did they think the garage would be full?

options.t: They said it might be, so we should arrive early.
options.why: ‘may’ → ‘might’, and ‘should’ stays unchanged.

options.t: They said it may be, so we must arrive early.
options.why: reported ‘may’ shifts to ‘might’; ‘should’ (advice) is the natural, unchanged form here.

activities.why: Shift will/can/may and obligation-must (→ had to), but keep would/could/might/should/ought to and deductive must unchanged.

learn.title: Reporting beyond verbs
learn.html: We can report not only with verbs but with nouns and adjectives that take a that-clause. This is a hallmark of academic and formal style, letting ideas be packaged as things (a claim, the suggestion) or stances (aware, confident).

learn.title: Noun + that-clause
learn.html: Abstract nouns of saying/thinking take a that-clause: fact, belief, claim, argument, idea, suggestion, view, hope, fear, news, evidence, possibility, assumption: The claim that the drug is safe is disputed. · There is evidence that levels are rising. This nominalises the report.

learn.title: Adjective + that-clause
learn.html: Adjectives of feeling and certainty take a that-clause: aware, confident, sure, certain, glad, afraid, surprised, worried, aware, convinced, doubtful, conscious: I'm aware that it's late. · She was confident that she'd pass.

learn.title: Nominalisation & the subjunctive
learn.html: Nouns like suggestion, demand, recommendation, insistence, proposal, requirement often take a that-clause with should or a bare (subjunctive) verb: the suggestion that he (should) resign · their demand that she be released. This mirrors the mandative pattern of their related verbs.

learn.title: Register & academic packaging
learn.html: Noun-based reporting compresses arguments and lets you reference and evaluate claims: This assumption that growth is limitless is questionable. Adjective reporting expresses stance economically. Both are core to essay writing, where you constantly report and appraise sources.

learn.title: Advanced learner errors
learn.html:
✗ the fact of that… → ✓ the fact that… (no ‘of’ before the clause)

✗ I'm aware of that it's late. → ✓ I'm aware that it's late.

✗ the suggestion to he resigns → ✓ the suggestion that he (should) resign.

learn.title: In a nutshell
learn.html:
• Abstract nouns (claim/idea/evidence) + that-clause nominalise a report.
  • Stance adjectives (aware/confident/afraid) + that-clause express attitude.
  • Mandative nouns (suggestion/demand) + that + (should) bare verb.
  • No ‘of’ before the that-clause: the fact that…, aware that…

ru.title: Передача не только глаголами
ru.html: Мы можем передавать не только глаголами, но и существительными и прилагательными, берущими that-clause. Это черта академического и формального стиля, позволяющая упаковывать идеи как вещи (a claim, the suggestion) или позиции (aware, confident).

kk.title: Етістіктен тыс баяндау
kk.html: Біз тек етістікпен емес, that-сөйлем алатын зат есім мен сын есіммен де баяндай аламыз. Бұл — академиялық және ресми стильдің белгісі, идеяны зат (a claim, the suggestion) не ұстаным (aware, confident) ретінде орауға мүмкіндік береді.

ru.title: Существительное + that-clause
ru.html: Абстрактные существительные говорения/мысли берут that-clause: fact, belief, claim, argument, idea, suggestion, view, hope, fear, news, evidence, possibility, assumption: The claim that the drug is safe is disputed. There is evidence that levels are rising. Это номинализирует передачу.

kk.title: Зат есім + that-сөйлем
kk.html: Айту/ойлаудың абстракт зат есімдері that-сөйлем алады: fact, belief, claim, argument, idea, suggestion, view, hope, fear, news, evidence, possibility, assumption: The claim that the drug is safe is disputed. There is evidence that levels are rising. Бұл баянды номиналдандырады.

ru.title: Прилагательное + that-clause
ru.html: Прилагательные чувства и уверенности берут that-clause: aware, confident, sure, certain, glad, afraid, surprised, worried, convinced, doubtful, conscious: I'm aware that it's late. She was confident that she'd pass.

kk.title: Сын есім + that-сөйлем
kk.html: Сезім мен сенімділік сын есімдері that-сөйлем алады: aware, confident, sure, certain, glad, afraid, surprised, worried, convinced, doubtful, conscious: I'm aware that it's late. She was confident that she'd pass.

ru.title: Номинализация и subjunctive
ru.html: Существительные suggestion, demand, recommendation, insistence, proposal, requirement часто берут that-clause с should или голым (subjunctive) глаголом: the suggestion that he (should) resign · their demand that she be released. Это отражает mandative-модель родственных глаголов.

kk.title: Номиналдандыру және subjunctive
kk.html: suggestion, demand, recommendation, insistence, proposal, requirement сияқты зат есімдер жиі should не жалаң (subjunctive) етістікпен that-сөйлем алады: the suggestion that he (should) resign · their demand that she be released. Бұл олардың етістіктерінің mandative үлгісін қайталайды.

ru.title: Регистр и академическая упаковка
ru.html: Передача на существительных сжимает аргументы и позволяет ссылаться и оценивать утверждения: This assumption that growth is limitless is questionable. Передача прилагательными экономно выражает позицию. Оба центральны для эссе, где постоянно передаёшь и оцениваешь источники.

kk.title: Регистр және академиялық орау
kk.html: Зат есімге негізделген баяндау дәлелдерді ықшамдап, талаптарды сілтеу мен бағалауға мүмкіндік береді: This assumption that growth is limitless is questionable. Сын есім баяндауы ұстанымды үнемді береді. Екеуі де эссе жазудың өзегі, онда сен үнемі дереккөзді баяндап, бағалайсың.

ru.title: Ошибки продвинутого уровня
ru.html: ✗ the fact of that… → ✓ the fact that… (без ‘of’ перед клаузой). ✗ I'm aware of that it's late → ✓ I'm aware that it's late. ✗ the suggestion to he resigns → ✓ the suggestion that he (should) resign.

kk.title: Жоғары деңгей қателері
kk.html: ✗ the fact of that… → ✓ the fact that… (сөйлем алдында ‘of’ жоқ). ✗ I'm aware of that it's late → ✓ I'm aware that it's late. ✗ the suggestion to he resigns → ✓ the suggestion that he (should) resign.

ru.title: Кратко
ru.html:
• Абстрактные существительные (claim/idea/evidence) + that-clause номинализируют передачу.
  • Прилагательные позиции (aware/confident/afraid) + that-clause выражают отношение.
  • Mandative-существительные (suggestion/demand) + that + (should) голый глагол.
  • Без ‘of’ перед that-clause: the fact that…, aware that…

kk.title: Қысқаша
kk.html:
• Абстракт зат есімдер (claim/idea/evidence) + that-сөйлем баянды номиналдандырады.
  • Ұстаным сын есімдері (aware/confident/afraid) + that-сөйлем көзқарасты береді.
  • Mandative зат есімдер (suggestion/demand) + that + (should) жалаң етістік.
  • that-сөйлем алдында ‘of’ жоқ: the fact that…, aware that…

activities.prompt: Sort each reporting word.
activities.why: Abstract nouns (claim/evidence/possibility) and stance adjectives (aware/confident/afraid) both take a that-clause.

items.s: There is evidence that the climate is warming.
She was confident that she would win.
I'm aware of that it's difficult.
The fact of that he lied is clear.

activities.why: no ‘of’ before the that-clause: ‘aware that…’, ‘the fact that…’.
an abstract noun of feeling + that-clause → the fear that…

alts: conscious
activities.why: a stance adjective + that-clause → aware that…

activities.after: that the rule be scrapped. (propose → noun)
alts: suggestion
activities.why: a mandative noun + that + bare verb → the proposal that the rule be scrapped.
noun (assumption) + that-clause, then a main-clause comment.

activities.q: Rewrite ‘Many people believe that the system is broken’ as a noun phrase subject:
options: The belief that the system is broken is widespread.
The belief of that the system is broken is widespread.
The believe that the system is broken is widespread.
why: Correct — ‘the belief that…’ nominalises the report cleanly.
‘of’ can't precede the that-clause.
‘believe’ is the verb; the noun is ‘belief’.

activities.q: Which noun most naturally takes ‘that + (should) + bare verb’?
why: ‘evidence that’ takes an ordinary clause (the evidence that it works).
Correct — ‘demand that…’ is mandative: the demand that he (should) apologise / apologise.
‘news that’ takes an ordinary indicative clause.

activities.prompt: Sort each noun by the clause it takes.
activities.why: fact/belief take an ordinary indicative clause; requirement/insistence take a mandative clause with should/bare verb.
‘surprised’ takes a that-clause with no ‘of’ → “I was surprised that she had resigned.”

activities.instruction: Rewrite so the report is a noun phrase: ‘Some argue that…’ →
activities.prompt: Some argue that the tax is unfair.
activities.answer: the argument that the tax is unfair
alts: The argument that the tax is unfair
the claim that the tax is unfair
activities.why: nominalise with the related noun → the argument that the tax is unfair.

steps.npc: So what's the core of the paper's argument?

options.t: Essentially, the claim that automation destroys more jobs than it creates.
options.why: noun + that-clause nominalises the reported claim.

options.t: Essentially, the claim of that automation destroys more jobs than it creates.
options.why: no ‘of’ before the that-clause: ‘the claim that…’.

steps.npc: And are the authors sure about it?

options.t: They seem confident that the data support them.
options.why: stance adjective + that-clause.

options.t: They seem confident of that the data support them.
options.why: ‘confident’ + that-clause takes no ‘of’: ‘confident that…’.

activities.why: Report with abstract nouns and stance adjectives + that-clause (no ‘of’), and use the mandative that + (should) bare verb after nouns like demand/suggestion.

learn.title: Mandative clauses: two equivalent forms
learn.html: After verbs/adjectives/nouns of importance, demand or recommendation, the that-clause uses either should + bare infinitive or the bare (subjunctive) verb: I insist that he should apologise / that he apologise. The subjunctive is more formal (and standard in American English).

learn.title: Triggers: verbs, adjectives, nouns
learn.html: Verbs: suggest, recommend, propose, insist, demand, request, require, advise, urge, order, ask. Adjectives: (it is) essential, vital, important, crucial, necessary, imperative, advisable, desirable. Nouns: suggestion, demand, requirement, recommendation, insistence.

learn.title: How the subjunctive looks
learn.html: The present subjunctive is just the base form for all persons — no -s, no do, and be stays be: It's vital that she be informed. · We ask that he not leave. · They demand that it happen now. Note the negative is not + base, without do.

learn.title: Register & regional preference
learn.html: American English favours the bare subjunctive (demand that he be present); British English often prefers should (demand that he should be present) or, more informally, an ordinary indicative (demand that he is present — common in speech, avoided in formal writing).

learn.title: Fixed subjunctive expressions
learn.html: Some set phrases keep an old subjunctive: be that as it may, come what may, God save the Queen, far be it from me, suffice it to say, if need be. Recognise these as fossilised forms, not productive grammar.

learn.title: Advanced learner errors
learn.html:
✗ It's essential that he goes. (formal) → ✓ … that he go / should go.

✗ I insist that she doesn't leave. (formal) → ✓ … that she not leave.

✗ They demanded that he was present. → ✓ … that he be present.

learn.title: In a nutshell
learn.html:
• Mandative that-clauses: should + base, or the bare subjunctive (base form for all persons).
  • Triggers: insist/demand/recommend; essential/vital/important; suggestion/requirement.
  • Subjunctive: no -s, no do; be stays be; negative = not + base.
  • AmE prefers the subjunctive; BrE often prefers should.

ru.title: Mandative-клаузы: две равные формы
ru.html: После глаголов/прилагательных/существительных важности, требования или рекомендации that-clause берёт либо should + голый инфинитив, либо голый (subjunctive) глагол: I insist that he should apologise / that he apologise. Subjunctive формальнее (и стандарт в американском).

kk.title: Mandative сөйлемдер: екі тең форма
kk.html: Маңыздылық, талап не ұсыныс етістік/сын есім/зат есімінен кейін that-сөйлем should + жалаң инфинитив не жалаң (subjunctive) етістік алады: I insist that he should apologise / that he apologise. Subjunctive ресмилеу (әрі американдық ағылшында стандарт).

ru.title: Триггеры: глаголы, прилагательные, существительные
ru.html: Глаголы: suggest, recommend, propose, insist, demand, request, require, advise, urge, order, ask. Прилагательные: (it is) essential, vital, important, crucial, necessary, imperative, advisable, desirable. Существительные: suggestion, demand, requirement, recommendation, insistence.

kk.title: Триггерлер: етістік, сын есім, зат есім
kk.html: Етістіктер: suggest, recommend, propose, insist, demand, request, require, advise, urge, order, ask. Сын есімдер: (it is) essential, vital, important, crucial, necessary, imperative, advisable, desirable. Зат есімдер: suggestion, demand, requirement, recommendation, insistence.

ru.title: Как выглядит subjunctive
ru.html: Настоящий subjunctive — просто базовая форма для всех лиц: без -s, без do, be остаётся be: It's vital that she be informed. We ask that he not leave. They demand that it happen now. Отрицание — not + база, без do.

kk.title: Subjunctive қалай көрінеді
kk.html: Осы subjunctive — барлық жаққа негізгі форма: -s жоқ, do жоқ, be be болып қалады: It's vital that she be informed. We ask that he not leave. They demand that it happen now. Болымсызы — not + негізгі форма, do-сыз.

ru.title: Регистр и региональное предпочтение
ru.html: Американский предпочитает голый subjunctive (demand that he be present); британский часто предпочитает should (demand that he should be present) или, неформальнее, обычный indicative (demand that he is present — част в речи, избегается в формальном письме).

kk.title: Регистр және аймақтық қалау
kk.html: Американдық ағылшын жалаң subjunctive-ты қалайды (demand that he be present); британдық жиі should-ты қалайды (demand that he should be present) не, бейресмилеу, кәдімгі indicative (demand that he is present — тілде жиі, ресми жазуда қашады).

ru.title: Устойчивые subjunctive-выражения
ru.html: Некоторые устойчивые фразы хранят старый subjunctive: be that as it may, come what may, God save the Queen, far be it from me, suffice it to say, if need be. Воспринимай их как окаменелые формы, не продуктивную грамматику.

kk.title: Тұрақты subjunctive тіркестері
kk.html: Кейбір тұрақты тіркес ескі subjunctive-ты сақтайды: be that as it may, come what may, God save the Queen, far be it from me, suffice it to say, if need be. Оларды өнімді грамматика емес, қатып қалған форма деп таны.

ru.title: Ошибки продвинутого уровня
ru.html: ✗ It's essential that he goes (формально) → ✓ … that he go / should go. ✗ I insist that she doesn't leave (формально) → ✓ … that she not leave. ✗ They demanded that he was present → ✓ … that he be present.

kk.title: Жоғары деңгей қателері
kk.html: ✗ It's essential that he goes (ресми) → ✓ … that he go / should go. ✗ I insist that she doesn't leave (ресми) → ✓ … that she not leave. ✗ They demanded that he was present → ✓ … that he be present.

ru.title: Кратко
ru.html:
• Mandative that-clauses: should + база, или голый subjunctive (базовая форма для всех лиц).
  • Триггеры: insist/demand/recommend; essential/vital/important; suggestion/requirement.
  • Subjunctive: без -s, без do; be остаётся be; отрицание = not + база.
  • AmE предпочитает subjunctive; BrE часто should.

kk.title: Қысқаша
kk.html:
• Mandative that-сөйлемдер: should + негіз, не жалаң subjunctive (барлық жаққа негізгі форма).
  • Триггерлер: insist/demand/recommend; essential/vital/important; suggestion/requirement.
  • Subjunctive: -s жоқ, do жоқ; be — be; болымсыз = not + негіз.
  • AmE subjunctive-ты; BrE жиі should-ты қалайды.

activities.typeLabel: Does it trigger a mandative clause?
activities.prompt: Sort each word by whether it takes a subjunctive/should that-clause.
activities.why: insist/essential/recommend/requirement trigger the mandative (subjunctive/should); believe/know take an ordinary indicative clause.

items.s: It is vital that every member be present.
They demanded that he resign immediately.
I insist that she doesn't tell anyone.
It's essential that he goes at once. (formal)

activities.why: the formal subjunctive is ‘not tell’ and ‘go’ (base form, no -s, no do): ‘that she not tell’, ‘that he go’.

activities.before: It is imperative that the report
activities.why: the subjunctive keeps ‘be’ unchanged → that the report be submitted.

activities.before: The judge ordered that the witness
activities.why: the negative subjunctive is ‘not + base’, no ‘do’ → that the witness not leave.
the British ‘should’ alternative → that every applicant should submit.
suggest + subjunctive (base ‘apologise’, no -s).

activities.q: Which sentence uses the mandative subjunctive correctly?
options: It's crucial that she is on time.
It's crucial that she be on time.
It's crucial that she being on time.
why: ‘is’ is the indicative, avoided in formal mandative use.
Correct — ‘be’ is the subjunctive base form for all persons.
‘being’ isn't a finite verb.

activities.q: Formal equivalent of ‘I insist that he doesn't smoke here’:
why: Correct — negative subjunctive = ‘not + base’ (not smoke).
the subjunctive drops the -s (not ‘smokes’).
‘doesn't smoking’ is ungrammatical.

activities.prompt: Sort each that-clause context.
activities.why: Mandative triggers (demand/essential) take the subjunctive; know/sure take an ordinary indicative clause.
the mandative subjunctive uses the base form → “recommended that the CEO resign.”

activities.instruction: Rewrite formally using the bare subjunctive:
activities.prompt: It is important that everyone is informed.
activities.answer: it is important that everyone be informed
alts: It is important that everyone be informed
it is important that everyone be informed.
activities.why: the mandative subjunctive keeps ‘be’ → that everyone be informed.

activities.typeLabel: Extended context: drafting a formal policy
activities.scene: Choose the correct formal mandative form.

steps.npc: How should we word the attendance rule for the handbook?

options.t: Write: ‘It is required that each employee attend the induction.’
options.why: required + subjunctive base ‘attend’ (no -s) suits a formal handbook.

options.t: Write: ‘It is required that each employee attends the induction.’
options.why: the formal mandative uses the base ‘attend’, not the indicative ‘attends’.

steps.npc: And the confidentiality clause?

options.t: ‘The company demands that no employee disclose client data.’
options.why: demand + subjunctive base ‘disclose’ (all persons).

options.t: ‘The company demands that no employee discloses client data.’
options.why: the subjunctive drops the -s: ‘that no employee disclose’.

activities.why: After mandative triggers, use should + base or the bare subjunctive (base form for all persons, be stays be, negative = not + base).

learn.title: The basic rule — and the tricky cases
learn.html: A verb agrees with its subject in number. Simple in theory, but C1 tests the hard cases: nouns that look plural but are singular, subjects separated from the verb, and words where meaning overrides form.

learn.title: Singular nouns ending in -s
learn.html: Some nouns look plural but take a singular verb: news, mathematics, physics, economics, politics, athletics, measles, billiards: The news is good. · Physics is hard. Names of countries/organisations ending in -s are also singular: The United States is…

learn.title: Plural-only nouns
learn.html: Others are always plural and take a plural verb: trousers, jeans, scissors, glasses, pyjamas, belongings, surroundings, outskirts, goods, thanks: My trousers are torn. Use a pair of to count them: a pair of scissors is…

learn.title: Don't be fooled by words in between
learn.html: The verb agrees with the head noun, not a nearer noun in a modifying phrase: The box of chocolates is on the table (not ‘are’). One of the students has left (head = one). Watch out for a plural noun sitting between the subject and the verb.

learn.title: there is / there are
learn.html: After there, the verb agrees with the following noun: There is a problem. · There are two options. In casual speech ‘there's two…’ is common, but formal writing keeps the agreement.

learn.title: Advanced learner errors
learn.html:
✗ The news are bad. → ✓ The news is bad.

✗ My jeans is dirty. → ✓ My jeans are dirty.

✗ The list of names are long. → ✓ The list of names is long.

learn.title: In a nutshell
learn.html:
• news/physics/economics + singular verb; countries/organisations in -s = singular.
  • trousers/scissors/belongings = plural; use ‘a pair of’ to count.
  • Agree with the head noun, not a noun inside a modifying phrase.
  • there is/are agrees with the noun that follows.

ru.title: Базовое правило — и трудные случаи
ru.html: Глагол согласуется с подлежащим в числе. В теории просто, но C1 проверяет сложные случаи: существительные, выглядящие как множественные, но единственные; подлежащие, отделённые от глагола; и слова, где смысл побеждает форму.

kk.title: Негізгі ереже — және қиын жағдайлар
kk.html: Етістік бастауышпен санда келіседі. Теорияда қарапайым, бірақ C1 қиын жағдайларды тексереді: көпше көрінетін, бірақ жекеше зат есімдер, етістіктен бөлінген бастауыштар, және мағына форманы жеңетін сөздер.

ru.title: Единственные существительные на -s
ru.html: Некоторые существительные выглядят множественными, но берут единственный глагол: news, mathematics, physics, economics, politics, athletics, measles, billiards: The news is good. Physics is hard. Названия стран/организаций на -s тоже единственные: The United States is…

kk.title: -s-пен аяқталатын жекеше зат есімдер
kk.html: Кейбір зат есім көпше көрінеді, бірақ жекеше етістік алады: news, mathematics, physics, economics, politics, athletics, measles, billiards: The news is good. Physics is hard. -s-пен аяқталатын ел/ұйым аты да жекеше: The United States is…

ru.title: Только множественные существительные
ru.html: Другие всегда множественные и берут множественный глагол: trousers, jeans, scissors, glasses, pyjamas, belongings, surroundings, outskirts, goods, thanks: My trousers are torn. Для счёта — a pair of: a pair of scissors is…

kk.title: Тек көпше зат есімдер
kk.html: Басқалары әрдайым көпше әрі көпше етістік алады: trousers, jeans, scissors, glasses, pyjamas, belongings, surroundings, outskirts, goods, thanks: My trousers are torn. Санау үшін a pair of: a pair of scissors is…

ru.title: Не обманись словами между ними
ru.html: Глагол согласуется с главным существительным, не с ближайшим в определяющей фразе: The box of chocolates is on the table (не ‘are’). One of the students has left (главное = one). Остерегайся множественного существительного между подлежащим и глаголом.

kk.title: Арадағы сөздерге алданба
kk.html: Етістік бас зат есіммен келіседі, анықтауыш тіркестегі жақынырақ зат есіммен емес: The box of chocolates is on the table (‘are’ емес). One of the students has left (бас = one). Бастауыш пен етістік арасындағы көпше зат есімнен сақ бол.

ru.title: there is / there are
ru.html: После there глагол согласуется со следующим существительным: There is a problem. There are two options. В разговоре ‘there's two…’ часто, но формальное письмо сохраняет согласование.

kk.title: there is / there are
kk.html: there-ден кейін етістік келесі зат есіммен келіседі: There is a problem. There are two options. Ауызекі тілде ‘there's two…’ жиі, бірақ ресми жазу келісуді сақтайды.

ru.title: Ошибки продвинутого уровня
ru.html: ✗ The news are bad → ✓ The news is bad. ✗ My jeans is dirty → ✓ My jeans are dirty. ✗ The list of names are long → ✓ The list of names is long.

kk.title: Жоғары деңгей қателері
kk.html: ✗ The news are bad → ✓ The news is bad. ✗ My jeans is dirty → ✓ My jeans are dirty. ✗ The list of names are long → ✓ The list of names is long.

ru.title: Кратко
ru.html:
• news/physics/economics + единств. глагол; страны/организации на -s = единственное.
  • trousers/scissors/belongings = множественное; для счёта ‘a pair of’.
  • Согласуй с главным существительным, не с тем, что в определяющей фразе.
  • there is/are согласуется со следующим существительным.

kk.title: Қысқаша
kk.html:
• news/physics/economics + жекеше етістік; -s-пен ел/ұйым = жекеше.
  • trousers/scissors/belongings = көпше; санау үшін ‘a pair of’.
  • Бас зат есіммен келіс, анықтауыш тіркестегі зат есіммен емес.
  • there is/are кейінгі зат есіммен келіседі.

activities.prompt: Sort each subject by the verb it takes.
activities.why: news/economics/country-names in -s are singular; jeans/scissors/belongings are plural-only.

items.s: The box of tools is heavy.
There are several reasons for this.
Mathematics are my favourite subject.
One of the windows are broken.

activities.why: mathematics is singular (is); the head ‘one’ is singular (one … is broken).

activities.before: The latest news about the merger
activities.why: ‘news’ takes a singular verb → the news is encouraging.
the head is ‘a collection’ (singular), not ‘coins’ → is on display.
‘glasses’ is a plural-only noun → are somewhere.
‘there’ agrees with the plural noun ‘two things’ → are.

activities.typeLabel: Which head noun controls the verb?
activities.q: “The quality of the recordings ___ excellent.”
why: agrees wrongly with the nearer plural ‘recordings’.
Correct — the head noun is ‘the quality’ (singular) → is.
past plural, also wrong for a singular head.

activities.q: “A pair of scissors ___ in the drawer.”
why: agrees with ‘scissors’, but the head is ‘a pair’.
Correct — ‘a pair of scissors’ is grammatically singular → is.
plural verb, wrong here.

activities.prompt: Identify whether each subject is singular or plural.
activities.why: The head noun decides: list/politics are singular; participants/trousers are plural.
here ‘statistics’ means the actual figures (plural/countable) → “the statistics … are misleading.”

activities.instruction: Rewrite with the correct verb form:
activities.prompt: The collection of old photographs are priceless.
activities.answer: the collection of old photographs is priceless
alts: The collection of old photographs is priceless
the collection of old photographs is priceless.
activities.why: the head ‘the collection’ is singular → is priceless.

activities.typeLabel: Extended context: sorting out the office

steps.npc: Where's the paperwork for the audit?

options.t: The stack of files is on your desk.
options.why: the head ‘the stack’ is singular → is.

options.t: The stack of files are on your desk.
options.why: the verb agrees with the head noun ‘stack’ (singular), not ‘files’.

steps.npc: And has the news about the deadline come through?

options.t: Yes — the news is that we've got an extra week.
options.why: ‘news’ takes a singular verb (is).

options.t: Yes — the news are that we've got an extra week.
options.why: ‘news’ is uncountable and singular: ‘the news is…’.

activities.why: Agree with the true head noun, treat news/physics as singular and trousers/scissors as plural, and match there is/are to the following noun.

learn.title: Quantifying expressions & agreement
learn.html: When the subject is a quantity expression, agreement depends on what is being quantified and whether it's seen as one whole or many parts. This is where C1 precision shows.

learn.title: a number of vs the number of
learn.html: a number of + plural noun = many → plural verb: A number of people were late. But the number of… refers to the figure itself → singular: The number of complaints has risen. Same words, opposite agreement.

learn.title: Fractions, percentages & ‘of’ phrases
learn.html: With fractions/percentages/some/half/most/all/none + of, the verb agrees with the noun after ‘of’: Half of the cake is gone. · Half of the guests have arrived. · 30% of the water is lost. · 30% of workers are unhappy.

learn.title: Amounts, distances & times = singular
learn.html: When a plural noun of money, time, distance or weight is treated as a single amount, use a singular verb: Ten pounds is too much. · Five miles is a long way. · Two hours is plenty. The quantity is one unit, not counted items.

learn.title: none / neither: notional agreement
learn.html: none of and neither of traditionally take a singular verb (formal): None of them was ready. In modern usage a plural is common and accepted: None of them were ready. Choose singular for strict formal writing.

learn.title: Advanced learner errors
learn.html:
✗ A number of students was absent. → ✓ A number of students were absent.

✗ Twenty dollars are enough. → ✓ Twenty dollars is enough.

✗ The number of errors have fallen. → ✓ The number of errors has fallen.

learn.title: In a nutshell
learn.html:
• a number of + plural = plural; the number of… = singular.
  • Fractions/percentages/most/some/half + of → agree with the noun after ‘of’.
  • Amounts of money/time/distance as one unit → singular verb.
  • none/neither of: singular (formal) or plural (common, accepted).

ru.title: Количественные выражения и согласование
ru.html: Когда подлежащее — количественное выражение, согласование зависит от того, что измеряется и видится ли это как одно целое или много частей. Здесь и проявляется точность C1.

kk.title: Мөлшер өрнектері және келісім
kk.html: Бастауыш мөлшер өрнегі болғанда, келісім не мөлшерленетініне және оның бір тұтас па әлде көп бөлік пе ретінде көрінетініне тәуелді. Дәл осы жерде C1 дәлдігі көрінеді.

ru.title: a number of vs the number of
ru.html: a number of + множ. сущ. = много → множественный глагол: A number of people were late. Но the number of… относится к самой цифре → единственный: The number of complaints has risen. Те же слова, противоположное согласование.

kk.title: a number of vs the number of
kk.html: a number of + көпше зат есім = көп → көпше етістік: A number of people were late. Бірақ the number of… санның өзіне сілтейді → жекеше: The number of complaints has risen. Бірдей сөздер, қарама-қарсы келісім.

ru.title: Дроби, проценты и ‘of’-фразы
ru.html: С дробями/процентами/some/half/most/all/none + of глагол согласуется с существительным после ‘of’: Half of the cake is gone. Half of the guests have arrived. 30% of the water is lost. 30% of workers are unhappy.

kk.title: Бөлшек, пайыз және ‘of’ тіркестері
kk.html: бөлшек/пайыз/some/half/most/all/none + of-пен етістік ‘of’-тан кейінгі зат есіммен келіседі: Half of the cake is gone. Half of the guests have arrived. 30% of the water is lost. 30% of workers are unhappy.

ru.title: Суммы, расстояния, время = единственное
ru.html: Когда множественное существительное денег, времени, расстояния или веса рассматривается как единая сумма, используй единственный глагол: Ten pounds is too much. Five miles is a long way. Two hours is plenty. Количество — одна единица, а не считаемые предметы.

kk.title: Мөлшер, қашықтық, уақыт = жекеше
kk.html: Ақша, уақыт, қашықтық не салмақтың көпше зат есімі бір мөлшер ретінде қаралса, жекеше етістік: Ten pounds is too much. Five miles is a long way. Two hours is plenty. Мөлшер — бір бірлік, саналатын заттар емес.

ru.title: none / neither: смысловое согласование
ru.html: none of и neither of традиционно берут единственный глагол (формально): None of them was ready. В современном употреблении множественный част и принят: None of them were ready. Для строгого формального письма выбирай единственное.

kk.title: none / neither: мағыналық келісім
kk.html: none of және neither of дәстүрлі түрде жекеше етістік алады (ресми): None of them was ready. Қазіргі қолданыста көпше жиі әрі қабылданады: None of them were ready. Қатаң ресми жазуға жекешені таңда.

ru.title: Ошибки продвинутого уровня
ru.html: ✗ A number of students was absent → ✓ A number of students were absent. ✗ Twenty dollars are enough → ✓ Twenty dollars is enough. ✗ The number of errors have fallen → ✓ The number of errors has fallen.

kk.title: Жоғары деңгей қателері
kk.html: ✗ A number of students was absent → ✓ A number of students were absent. ✗ Twenty dollars are enough → ✓ Twenty dollars is enough. ✗ The number of errors have fallen → ✓ The number of errors has fallen.

ru.title: Кратко
ru.html:
• a number of + множ. = множественное; the number of… = единственное.
  • Дроби/проценты/most/some/half + of → согласуй с сущ. после ‘of’.
  • Суммы денег/времени/расстояния как единица → единственный глагол.
  • none/neither of: единственное (формально) или множественное (часто, принято).

kk.title: Қысқаша
kk.html:
• a number of + көпше = көпше; the number of… = жекеше.
  • Бөлшек/пайыз/most/some/half + of → ‘of’-тан кейінгі зат есіммен келіс.
  • Ақша/уақыт/қашықтық бір бірлік ретінде → жекеше етістік.
  • none/neither of: жекеше (ресми) не көпше (жиі, қабылданған).

activities.prompt: Sort each quantified subject.
activities.why: ‘the number of’ and single amounts are singular; ‘a number of’ and ‘of + plural noun’ take a plural verb.

items.s: Three years is a long time to wait.
A number of issues remain unresolved.
The number of visitors have doubled.
Most of the milk are gone.

activities.why: ‘the number of’ is singular (has); ‘most of the milk’ agrees with uncountable ‘milk’ (is).
‘a number of + plural’ = many → plural verb are.
‘the number of…’ refers to the figure → singular has fallen.
a single sum of money → singular verb is.
‘two thirds of the land’ agrees with uncountable ‘land’ → is.

activities.q: “Around 40% of the population ___ under thirty.”
why: Correct — ‘population’ is treated as a group of people here → are (people under thirty).
possible if population is a single mass, but the ‘under thirty’ reading is collective/plural.
past tense, not signalled.

activities.q: Which sentence is correct?
options: A number of factors is involved.
The number of factors are small.
A number of factors are involved.
why: ‘a number of’ = many → should be ‘are’.
‘the number of’ is singular → should be ‘is’.
Correct — ‘a number of factors’ takes a plural verb.

activities.prompt: For each ‘of’ phrase, decide the agreement.
activities.why: With most/none/fractions, the verb follows the noun after ‘of’: report/water (singular) vs pages/students (plural).
‘the number of…’ is singular → “the number of people … has increased.”

activities.instruction: Rewrite with the correct verb:
activities.prompt: Ten kilometres are too far to walk in this heat.
activities.answer: ten kilometres is too far to walk in this heat
alts: Ten kilometres is too far to walk in this heat
ten kilometres is too far to walk in this heat.
activities.why: a single distance treated as one unit → singular is.

steps.npc: How's the customer feedback looking this quarter?

options.t: Encouraging — the number of complaints has dropped.
options.why: ‘the number of’ is singular → has.

options.t: Encouraging — the number of complaints have dropped.
options.why: ‘the number of…’ takes a singular verb: ‘has dropped’.

steps.npc: And are staff happier with the new system?

options.t: Mostly — a number of them have said it saves time.
options.why: ‘a number of them’ = many → plural have.

options.t: Mostly — a number of them has said it saves time.
options.why: ‘a number of them’ means ‘many’ and takes a plural verb: ‘have said’.

activities.why: Distinguish ‘a number of’ (plural) from ‘the number of’ (singular), agree with the noun after ‘of’, and treat single amounts of money/time/distance as singular.

learn.title: Collective nouns, coordination & distributives
learn.html: The final agreement cases turn on how we view a group, how joined subjects behave, and the rules for each/every/either/neither.

learn.title: Collective nouns: one body or many members
learn.html: team, family, government, staff, committee, audience, class, company can take a singular verb (the group as a unit) or a plural verb (the members individually) — especially in British English: The team is top of the league (as a unit) vs The team are arguing among themselves (as individuals). American English tends to prefer the singular.

learn.title: and, or, nor: joining subjects
learn.html: A and B → plural: Tom and Sam are here. (Unless they form one idea: Fish and chips is popular.) With or/nor/either…or/neither…nor, the verb agrees with the nearer subject: Neither the manager nor the staff were informed. · Either the twins or their mother is coming.

learn.title: each, every, either, neither → singular
learn.html: These are grammatically singular: Each student has a locker. · Every seat was taken. · Neither answer is correct. Even with ‘of + plural’: Each of the rooms has a view. (Careful: ‘each’ after a plural subject doesn't change the verb: They each have a key.)

learn.title: Titles, quoted plurals & ‘what’-clauses
learn.html: A title/name is singular even if plural in form: ‘Great Expectations’ is a novel. A what-clause subject is usually singular: What we need is more time (though a plural complement can pull it plural: What we need are volunteers).

learn.title: Advanced learner errors
learn.html:
✗ Neither of the plans are without risk (strict) → ✓ … is without risk (formal).

✗ Each of the answers are wrong. → ✓ Each of the answers is wrong.

✗ Neither the students nor the teacher were happy. → ✓ … the teacher was happy (nearer subject).

learn.title: In a nutshell
learn.html:
• Collective nouns: singular (unit) or plural (members), esp. in BrE.
  • and → plural; or/nor → agree with the nearer subject.
  • each/every/either/neither → singular verb, even with ‘of + plural’.
  • Titles are singular; what-clauses are usually singular.

ru.title: Собирательные существительные, сочинение, дистрибутивы
ru.html: Последние случаи согласования зависят от того, как мы видим группу, как ведут себя соединённые подлежащие, и правил для each/every/either/neither.

kk.title: Ұжымдық зат есім, жалғау, дистрибутив
kk.html: Соңғы келісім жағдайлары топты қалай көретінімізге, біріктірілген бастауыштардың әрекетіне, және each/every/either/neither ережелеріне байланысты.

ru.title: Собирательные: одно тело или много членов
ru.html: team, family, government, staff, committee, audience, class, company берут единственный глагол (группа как единица) или множественный (члены по отдельности) — особ. в британском: The team is top of the league (единица) / The team are arguing among themselves (индивидуумы). Американский предпочитает единственное.

kk.title: Ұжымдық зат есім: бір дене не көп мүше
kk.html: team, family, government, staff, committee, audience, class, company жекеше (топ бірлік ретінде) не көпше (мүшелер жеке-жеке) етістік алады — әсіресе британдық ағылшында: The team is top of the league (бірлік) / The team are arguing among themselves (жеке). Американдық жекешені қалайды.

ru.title: and, or, nor: соединение подлежащих
ru.html: A and B → множественное: Tom and Sam are here. (Если не одна идея: Fish and chips is popular.) С or/nor/either…or/neither…nor глагол согласуется с ближайшим подлежащим: Neither the manager nor the staff were informed. Either the twins or their mother is coming.

kk.title: and, or, nor: бастауыштарды біріктіру
kk.html: A and B → көпше: Tom and Sam are here. (Егер бір ұғым болмаса: Fish and chips is popular.) or/nor/either…or/neither…nor-мен етістік жақынырақ бастауышпен келіседі: Neither the manager nor the staff were informed. Either the twins or their mother is coming.

ru.title: each, every, either, neither → единственное
ru.html: Они грамматически единственные: Each student has a locker. Every seat was taken. Neither answer is correct. Даже с ‘of + множ.’: Each of the rooms has a view. (Осторожно: ‘each’ после множ. подлежащего не меняет глагол: They each have a key.)

kk.title: each, every, either, neither → жекеше
kk.html: Бұлар грамматикалық жекеше: Each student has a locker. Every seat was taken. Neither answer is correct. ‘of + көпше’-мен де: Each of the rooms has a view. (Абайла: көпше бастауыштан кейінгі ‘each’ етістікті өзгертпейді: They each have a key.)

ru.title: Названия, цитируемые множественные, ‘what’-клаузы
ru.html: Название/имя единственное, даже если форма множественная: ‘Great Expectations’ is a novel. Подлежащее what-clause обычно единственное: What we need is more time (хотя множественный комплемент может сделать его множественным: What we need are volunteers).

kk.title: Атаулар, көпше цитаталар, ‘what’-сөйлемдер
kk.html: Атау/есім формасы көпше болса да жекеше: ‘Great Expectations’ is a novel. what-сөйлем бастауышы әдетте жекеше: What we need is more time (бірақ көпше толықтауыш оны көпшеге тартуы мүмкін: What we need are volunteers).

ru.title: Ошибки продвинутого уровня
ru.html: ✗ Neither of the plans are without risk (строго) → ✓ … is without risk (формально). ✗ Each of the answers are wrong → ✓ Each of the answers is wrong. ✗ Neither the students nor the teacher were happy → ✓ … the teacher was happy (ближайшее подлежащее).

kk.title: Жоғары деңгей қателері
kk.html: ✗ Neither of the plans are without risk (қатаң) → ✓ … is without risk (ресми). ✗ Each of the answers are wrong → ✓ Each of the answers is wrong. ✗ Neither the students nor the teacher were happy → ✓ … the teacher was happy (жақын бастауыш).

ru.title: Кратко
ru.html:
• Собирательные: единственное (единица) или множественное (члены), особ. в BrE.
  • and → множественное; or/nor → согласуй с ближайшим подлежащим.
  • each/every/either/neither → единственный глагол, даже с ‘of + множ.’
  • Названия единственные; what-клаузы обычно единственные.

kk.title: Қысқаша
kk.html:
• Ұжымдық зат есім: жекеше (бірлік) не көпше (мүшелер), әсіресе BrE.
  • and → көпше; or/nor → жақын бастауышпен келіс.
  • each/every/either/neither → жекеше, ‘of + көпше’-мен де.
  • Атаулар жекеше; what-сөйлемдер әдетте жекеше.

activities.prompt: Sort each subject.
activities.why: each/every/neither and titles are singular; ‘and’ and members-as-individuals take a plural verb.

items.s: Each of the rooms has its own bathroom.
Neither the coach nor the players were happy.
Every seats were taken.
Fish and chips are my favourite meal.

activities.why: ‘every’ takes a singular noun + verb (every seat was); ‘fish and chips’ as one dish is singular (is).

activities.before: Neither the director nor the actors
activities.why: with nor, the verb agrees with the nearer subject ‘the actors’ → were.
‘each of…’ is singular → has a presentation.

alts: have
activities.why: acting as a single body → singular has (BrE plural ‘have’ also possible if seen as members).
a what-clause subject with a singular complement → is.

activities.q: Which sentence views the group as individuals (BrE)?
options: The team is unbeaten this season.
The team are wearing their new kit and warming up.
The team consists of eleven players.
why: treats the team as a single unit (is).
Correct — the plural ‘are … their’ views members individually.
‘consists’ treats it as one whole.

activities.q: “Either the twins or their father ___ going to collect you.”
why: agrees with the far subject ‘twins’, but the rule uses the nearer one.
Correct — the nearer subject ‘their father’ is singular → is.
past tense isn't signalled.

activities.prompt: Sort by how the joined subjects agree.

items.t: The dog and the cat are…
Either the files or the disk is…

activities.why: ‘and’ makes a plural subject; or/nor make the verb agree with the nearer noun.
‘every one of…’ is singular → “Every one of the machines needs servicing.”

activities.instruction: Rewrite with the correct verb, keeping it formal:
activities.prompt: Neither of the proposals are acceptable.
activities.answer: neither of the proposals is acceptable
alts: Neither of the proposals is acceptable
neither of the proposals is acceptable.
activities.why: strict/formal ‘neither of…’ takes a singular verb → is acceptable.

steps.npc: How did the panel feel about the shortlist?

options.t: Each of the judges has picked a different winner, unfortunately.
options.why: ‘each of…’ is singular → has.

options.t: Each of the judges have picked a different winner, unfortunately.
options.why: ‘each of the judges’ is grammatically singular: ‘has picked’.

steps.npc: Was anyone available to announce it?

options.t: Neither the host nor the organisers were free, sadly.
options.why: with ‘nor’, the verb agrees with the nearer plural ‘organisers’ → were.

options.t: Neither the host nor the organisers was free, sadly.
options.why: the nearer subject ‘organisers’ is plural, so ‘were free’.

activities.why: Treat collective nouns as unit (singular) or members (plural), make or/nor agree with the nearer subject, and keep each/every/either/neither singular.

learn.title: Building complex noun phrases
learn.html: English packs a lot of meaning into noun phrases by stacking nouns as modifiers before a head noun: a government report, a climate change conference, a customer service department. The last noun is the head; the earlier nouns classify it.

learn.title: Noun + noun: the first noun is usually singular
learn.html: When a noun modifies another, it normally stays singular, even for plural meaning: a five-star hotel, a ten-minute break, a shoe shop (sells shoes), a toothbrush. Exceptions exist (a sports car, a customs officer, a clothes shop).

learn.title: Hyphenated pre-modifiers
learn.html: When a number + noun combination comes before a noun, it's hyphenated and singular: a five-year-old child, a three-hour flight, a 20-page document. After the noun it's separate and plural: The child is five years old. · The flight lasts three hours.

learn.title: Order & stress in compounds
learn.html: In noun + noun compounds, the first element usually carries the main stress: a 'BOOKshop, a 'CREDIT card. Meaning flows right-to-left: a car factory worker = a worker in a factory that makes cars. Getting the order right is essential for clarity.

learn.title: Compound noun vs of-phrase vs possessive
learn.html: Three ways to link nouns, with different feels: noun + noun (classifying: a car door), of-phrase (part/quantity/abstract: the roof of the house), possessive 's (usually people/animals/time: the manager's office, a day's work). C1 skill is choosing the natural one.

learn.title: Advanced learner errors
learn.html:
✗ a five-years-old boy → ✓ a five-year-old boy.

✗ a teachers meeting → ✓ a teachers' meeting / a staff meeting.

✗ the door of the car (usually) → often better the car door.

learn.title: In a nutshell
learn.html:
• Stack nouns as modifiers before a head; the last noun is the head.
  • The modifying noun is usually singular (a shoe shop, a ten-minute break).
  • Number + noun before a noun is hyphenated & singular; after the noun, separate & plural.
  • Choose noun+noun, of-phrase or 's by the relationship (classifying / part / possession).

ru.title: Построение сложных именных групп
ru.html: Английский упаковывает много смысла в именные группы, ставя существительные-определения перед главным: a government report, a climate change conference, a customer service department. Последнее существительное — главное; предыдущие его классифицируют.

kk.title: Күрделі есімді тіркестер құру
kk.html: Ағылшын есімді тіркестерге көп мағынаны зат есімдерді анықтауыш ретінде бас зат есім алдына жинау арқылы сыйдырады: a government report, a climate change conference, a customer service department. Соңғы зат есім — бас; алдыңғылар оны жіктейді.

ru.title: Существительное + существительное: первое обычно единственное
ru.html: Когда существительное определяет другое, оно обычно остаётся единственным, даже при множественном смысле: a five-star hotel, a ten-minute break, a shoe shop (продаёт обувь), a toothbrush. Есть исключения (a sports car, a customs officer, a clothes shop).

kk.title: Зат есім + зат есім: біріншісі әдетте жекеше
kk.html: Бір зат есім екіншісін анықтағанда, әдетте жекеше қалады, көпше мағынада да: a five-star hotel, a ten-minute break, a shoe shop (аяқкиім сатады), a toothbrush. Ерекшеліктер бар (a sports car, a customs officer, a clothes shop).

ru.title: Дефисные пре-модификаторы
ru.html: Когда сочетание число + существительное идёт перед существительным, оно через дефис и единственное: a five-year-old child, a three-hour flight, a 20-page document. После существительного — раздельно и множественно: The child is five years old. The flight lasts three hours.

kk.title: Дефиспен анықтауыштар
kk.html: Сан + зат есім тіркесі зат есім алдында келгенде, дефиспен әрі жекеше: a five-year-old child, a three-hour flight, a 20-page document. Зат есімнен кейін бөлек әрі көпше: The child is five years old. The flight lasts three hours.

ru.title: Порядок и ударение в компаундах
ru.html: В компаундах существительное + существительное первый элемент обычно несёт главное ударение: a 'BOOKshop, a 'CREDIT card. Смысл течёт справа налево: a car factory worker = рабочий на заводе, делающем машины. Верный порядок важен для ясности.

kk.title: Компаунда рет пен екпін
kk.html: Зат есім + зат есім компаундында бірінші элемент әдетте басты екпінді алады: a 'BOOKshop, a 'CREDIT card. Мағына оңнан солға ағады: a car factory worker = көлік жасайтын зауыттағы жұмысшы. Ретті дұрыс қою — анықтық үшін маңызды.

ru.title: Компаунд vs of-фраза vs притяжательный
ru.html: Три способа связать существительные, с разными оттенками: сущ. + сущ. (классификация: a car door), of-фраза (часть/количество/абстракт: the roof of the house), притяжательный 's (обычно люди/животные/время: the manager's office, a day's work). Навык C1 — выбрать естественный.

kk.title: Компаунд vs of-тіркес vs тәуелдік
kk.html: Зат есімдерді байланыстырудың үш жолы, әр реңкпен: зат есім + зат есім (жіктеу: a car door), of-тіркес (бөлік/мөлшер/абстракт: the roof of the house), тәуелдік 's (әдетте адам/жануар/уақыт: the manager's office, a day's work). C1 дағдысы — табиғисын таңдау.

ru.title: Ошибки продвинутого уровня
ru.html: ✗ a five-years-old boy → ✓ a five-year-old boy. ✗ a teachers meeting → ✓ a teachers' meeting / a staff meeting. ✗ the door of the car (обычно) → часто лучше the car door.

kk.title: Жоғары деңгей қателері
kk.html: ✗ a five-years-old boy → ✓ a five-year-old boy. ✗ a teachers meeting → ✓ a teachers' meeting / a staff meeting. ✗ the door of the car (әдетте) → жиі жақсырақ the car door.

ru.title: Кратко
ru.html:
• Ставь существительные-определения перед главным; последнее — главное.
  • Определяющее существительное обычно единственное (a shoe shop, a ten-minute break).
  • Число + существительное перед существительным — через дефис и единственно; после — раздельно и множественно.
  • Выбирай сущ.+сущ., of-фразу или 's по отношению (классификация/часть/принадлежность).

kk.title: Қысқаша
kk.html:
• Зат есімдерді бас алдына анықтауыш ретінде жина; соңғысы — бас.
  • Анықтауыш зат есім әдетте жекеше (a shoe shop, a ten-minute break).
  • Зат есім алдында сан + зат есім дефиспен әрі жекеше; кейін бөлек әрі көпше.
  • Қатынасқа қарай зат есім+зат есім, of-тіркес не 's таңда.

activities.typeLabel: How should these nouns be linked?
activities.prompt: Sort each by the most natural structure.
activities.why: Classifying links use noun+noun; parts/abstracts use of-phrases; people/time use the possessive 's.

items.s: It was a three-hour meeting.
She bought a new toothbrush.
He's a five-years-old boy.
I need a shoes shop.

activities.why: pre-modifiers are singular and hyphenated: ‘five-year-old’, ‘shoe shop’.

activities.after: tour of the old town. (two / hour)
activities.why: number + noun before a noun → hyphenated & singular → two-hour tour.
the modifying noun stays singular → a shoe shop.

activities.after: work, he slept for ten hours. (day)
activities.why: a time expression uses the possessive → a day's work.
stacked modifiers before the head noun ‘programme’, read right-to-left.

activities.q: In ‘a wildlife conservation project’, what is the phrase mainly about?
why: a modifier classifying the project.
also a modifier.
Correct — the last noun ‘project’ is the head; the rest classify it.

activities.q: Complete: “The document was long — it ran to ___.”
why: the hyphenated singular form only works before a noun (a twenty-page document).
Correct — after the verb, use the separate plural ‘twenty pages’.
not hyphenated in this position.

activities.prompt: Sort each form by where it belongs.
