activities.why: Hyphenated singular forms modify a following noun; the plural of-phrase or separate plural follows the noun/verb.
a pre-modifier is hyphenated and singular → “a three-day conference.”

activities.instruction: Rewrite the of-phrase as a natural noun + noun compound:
activities.prompt: the handle of the door
alts: the door handle
a door handle
activities.why: a classifying, part-of relationship is more natural as a compound → the door handle.

activities.scene: Choose the correctly formed noun phrase.

steps.npc: How long is the coach journey to the coast?

options.t: It's a five-hour trip, with one rest stop.
options.why: a hyphenated, singular pre-modifier before ‘trip’.

options.t: It's a five-hours trip, with one rest stop.
options.why: before a noun, the modifier is singular and hyphenated: ‘a five-hour trip’.

steps.npc: And is there somewhere to buy walking gear there?

options.t: Yes — there's a big outdoor equipment shop by the harbour.
options.why: stacked singular modifiers before the head ‘shop’.

options.t: Yes — there's a big outdoors equipments shop by the harbour.
options.why: modifying nouns stay singular (equipment is uncountable): ‘outdoor equipment shop’.

activities.why: Build noun phrases with singular modifying nouns and hyphenated number+noun pre-modifiers, and choose noun+noun, of-phrase or 's to match the relationship.

learn.title: a/an and one — related but different
learn.html: a/an and one both go with singular countable nouns, but one stresses number (as opposed to two/several), while a/an is the neutral indefinite article: I'll have a coffee (just some coffee) vs I'll have one coffee (exactly one, not two).

learn.title: a vs an: sound, not spelling
learn.html: Choose by the first sound, not the letter: a before a consonant sound (a university /juː/, a European, a one-way street /w/); an before a vowel sound (an hour /aʊ/, an MP /em/, an honest man). Silent letters and initialisms trip learners up.

learn.title: When only ‘one’ works
learn.html: Use one (not a/an) when the number matters: contrasts (one day, not the next), emphasis (not one person came), ‘one … the other’ (one twin is tall, the other short), and set phrases (one day, one morning, all in one go). a/an would lose the counting sense.

learn.title: Fixed uses of a/an
learn.html: a/an also means per (twice a week, £5 an hour), appears with numbers/quantities (a hundred, a dozen, a few, a little), in exclamations (What a day!), and before jobs/descriptions (She's an engineer).

learn.title: one as a pronoun & substitute
learn.html: one also substitutes for a countable noun (the red one) and, formally, means ‘people in general’ (One should always be polite). Don't confuse these with the article-like ‘one’ of number.

learn.title: Advanced learner errors
learn.html:
✗ a hour, a honest man → ✓ an hour, an honest man.

✗ an university, an one-way street → ✓ a university, a one-way street.

✗ I have a brother and other sister → ✓ … one brother and one sister (if counting).

learn.title: In a nutshell
learn.html:
• a/an = neutral indefinite; one = stresses the number (vs two/several).
  • a vs an by the first sound (an hour, a university, an MP).
  • Use one for contrasts, emphasis, ‘one … the other’, and set time phrases.
  • a/an = per; also in numbers, exclamations, and job descriptions.

ru.title: a/an и one — связаны, но разные
ru.html: a/an и one оба с единственными исчисляемыми, но one подчёркивает число (в отличие от двух/нескольких), а a/an — нейтральный неопределённый артикль: I'll have a coffee (просто кофе) / I'll have one coffee (ровно один, не два).

kk.title: a/an мен one — байланысты, бірақ бөлек
kk.html: a/an мен one екеуі де жекеше саналатын зат есіммен, бірақ one санды баса көрсетеді (екі/бірнешеге қарсы), ал a/an — бейтарап белгісіздік артиклі: I'll have a coffee (жай кофе) / I'll have one coffee (нақ біреу, екеу емес).

ru.title: a vs an: звук, а не написание
ru.html: Выбирай по первому звуку, не букве: a перед согласным звуком (a university /juː/, a European, a one-way street /w/); an перед гласным звуком (an hour /aʊ/, an MP /em/, an honest man). Немые буквы и аббревиатуры путают.

kk.title: a vs an: емле емес, дыбыс
kk.html: Әріппен емес, бірінші дыбыспен таңда: a дауыссыз дыбыс алдында (a university /juː/, a European, a one-way street /w/); an дауысты дыбыс алдында (an hour /aʊ/, an MP /em/, an honest man). Үнсіз әріптер мен қысқартулар шатастырады.

ru.title: Когда работает только ‘one’
ru.html: Используй one (не a/an), когда важно число: контрасты (one day, not the next), акцент (not one person came), ‘one … the other’ (one twin is tall, the other short), устойчивые фразы (one day, one morning, all in one go). a/an потеряло бы счётный смысл.

kk.title: Тек ‘one’ жарайтын кез
kk.html: Сан маңызды болса one (a/an емес): қарама-қарсылық (one day, not the next), екпін (not one person came), ‘one … the other’ (one twin is tall, the other short), тұрақты тіркестер (one day, one morning, all in one go). a/an санау мәнін жоғалтар еді.

ru.title: Устойчивые употребления a/an
ru.html: a/an также значит per (в) (twice a week, £5 an hour), встречается с числами/количеством (a hundred, a dozen, a few, a little), в восклицаниях (What a day!), и перед профессиями/описаниями (She's an engineer).

kk.title: a/an тұрақты қолданысы
kk.html: a/an per (әр) мағынасын да береді (twice a week, £5 an hour), сан/мөлшермен кездеседі (a hundred, a dozen, a few, a little), лептеде (What a day!), және мамандық/сипаттама алдында (She's an engineer).

ru.title: one — местоимение и заместитель
ru.html: one также заменяет исчисляемое существительное (the red one) и, формально, значит ‘люди вообще’ (One should always be polite). Не путай их с числовым ‘one’.

kk.title: one — есімдік және орынбасар
kk.html: one саналатын зат есімнің орнын да басады (the red one) және, ресми түрде, ‘жалпы адамдар’ дегенді білдіреді (One should always be polite). Бұларды сандық ‘one’-мен шатастырма.

ru.title: Ошибки продвинутого уровня
ru.html: ✗ a hour, a honest man → ✓ an hour, an honest man. ✗ an university, an one-way street → ✓ a university, a one-way street. ✗ I have a brother and other sister → ✓ … one brother and one sister (если счёт).

kk.title: Жоғары деңгей қателері
kk.html: ✗ a hour, a honest man → ✓ an hour, an honest man. ✗ an university, an one-way street → ✓ a university, a one-way street. ✗ I have a brother and other sister → ✓ … one brother and one sister (санаса).

ru.title: Кратко
ru.html:
• a/an = нейтральный неопределённый; one = подчёркивает число (vs два/несколько).
  • a vs an по первому звуку (an hour, a university, an MP).
  • Используй one для контрастов, акцента, ‘one … the other’ и устойчивых фраз.
  • a/an = per; также в числах, восклицаниях, описаниях профессий.

kk.title: Қысқаша
kk.html:
• a/an = бейтарап белгісіздік; one = санды баса көрсетеді (екі/бірнешеге қарсы).
  • a vs an бірінші дыбыспен (an hour, a university, an MP).
  • Қарама-қарсылық, екпін, ‘one … the other’, тұрақты тіркеске one.
  • a/an = per; сан, лепте, мамандықта да.

activities.prompt: Sort each noun by the article its first sound requires.
activities.why: Choose by the first sound: university/European/one-off start with consonant sounds; hour/honest/MP start with vowel sounds.

items.s: She earns twelve euros an hour.
Not one student failed the exam.
It's a honour to be here.
I'll take a one, please.

activities.why: ‘honour’ starts with a vowel sound (an honour); ‘a one’ is wrong — say ‘this one’ or ‘one’.

activities.before: The medicine should be taken three times
activities.why: ‘a’ = per here → three times a day.

activities.after: twin studied law, the other medicine. (a/an/one)
activities.why: ‘one … the other’ needs one, not ‘a’.

activities.after: hour and a half to get there. (a/an)
activities.why: ‘hour’ begins with a vowel sound → an hour.
exclamation with ‘an’ before the vowel sound of ‘extraordinary’.

activities.q: “I've told you ___ time and time again.” Which fits the emphatic counting sense?
why: ‘a time’ doesn't fit this idiom.
Correct — ‘time and (time) again’ / emphatic counting uses ‘one’-type stress; here the natural emphatic choice is ‘one’.
‘the’ makes it definite, wrong here.

activities.q: Which phrase uses the article correctly?
why: ‘unique’ starts with a /juː/ consonant sound → ‘a’.
Correct — a unique (consonant /juː/ sound).
‘united’ also starts with /juː/ → ‘a united team’.

activities.typeLabel: a/an or one — which is needed?
activities.prompt: Sort by whether the number is being stressed.
activities.why: Use a/an for neutral reference; use one where the exact number is emphasised or contrasted.
‘honest’ begins with a vowel sound → “an honest opinion.”

activities.instruction: Rewrite so it emphasises exactly one (not more):
activities.prompt: Could I have a day to think it over?
activities.answer: could I have one day to think it over
alts: Could I have one day to think it over
could I have just one day to think it over
activities.why: to stress the single unit of time → one day.

activities.typeLabel: Extended context: at a café counter

steps.npc: What can I get you?

options.t: Just a flat white, please, and an almond croissant.
options.why: neutral indefinite articles, ‘an’ before the vowel sound of ‘almond’.

options.t: Just a flat white, please, and a almond croissant.
options.why: ‘almond’ starts with a vowel sound → ‘an almond croissant’.

steps.npc: Anything else — two of those muffins?

options.t: No, just one — I'm trying to cut down.
options.why: the contrast with ‘two’ stresses the number → one.

options.t: No, just a — I'm trying to cut down.
options.why: to contrast with ‘two’, use ‘one’, not the article ‘a’ standing alone.

activities.why: Use a/an as the neutral article (choosing by sound) and one when the exact number is stressed or contrasted.

learn.title: The core system: indefinite, definite, zero
learn.html: Three choices carry different meanings. a/an = one of many, new to the listener. the = a specific, identifiable one (both know which). zero (no article) = general/uncountable or plural in a general sense. I saw a dog. The dog was huge. Dogs are loyal.

learn.title: First mention vs later mention
learn.html: Introduce with a/an, then switch to the once it's known: She bought a car. The car is red. Use the from the start when context makes the thing uniquely identifiable: Close the door (this room's door).

learn.title: the for shared/unique reference
learn.html: Use the for things that are unique or known from context: the sun, the moon, the government, the internet, the future; and when a phrase narrows the noun to a specific one: the book on the table, the woman who called.

learn.title: Zero article for general meaning
learn.html: Use no article with plural or uncountable nouns talked about in general: Water is essential. · Children learn fast. · I love music. Adding the would make it specific: the water in this bottle, the children next door.

learn.title: Discourse: articles track shared knowledge
learn.html: Article choice is really about what the listener can identify. the signals ‘you know which’; a/an signals ‘here's a new one’; zero signals ‘in general’. Skilled writers use them to guide the reader smoothly through given vs new information.

learn.title: Advanced learner errors
learn.html:
✗ I like the dogs (in general) → ✓ I like dogs.

✗ She is best student → ✓ She is the best student (superlatives take the).

✗ Sun is a star → ✓ The sun is a star (unique object).

learn.title: In a nutshell
learn.html:
• a/an = new, one of many; the = specific/identifiable; zero = general.
  • First mention a/an → later mention the.
  • the for unique things and nouns narrowed by a phrase or superlative.
  • Zero article for plural/uncountable nouns in a general sense.

ru.title: Базовая система: неопределённый, определённый, ноль
ru.html: Три выбора несут разный смысл. a/an = один из многих, новый для слушателя. the = конкретный, опознаваемый (оба знают, какой). ноль (без артикля) = общий/неисчисляемый или множественный в общем смысле. I saw a dog. The dog was huge. Dogs are loyal.

kk.title: Негізгі жүйе: белгісіз, белгілі, нөл
kk.html: Үш таңдау әртүрлі мағына тасиды. a/an = көптің бірі, тыңдаушыға жаңа. the = нақты, анықталатын (екеуі де қайсы екенін біледі). нөл (артикльсіз) = жалпы/саналмайтын не жалпы мағынадағы көпше. I saw a dog. The dog was huge. Dogs are loyal.

ru.title: Первое vs последующее упоминание
ru.html: Вводи через a/an, затем переходи на the, когда оно известно: She bought a car. The car is red. Используй the сразу, когда контекст делает вещь однозначно опознаваемой: Close the door (дверь этой комнаты).

kk.title: Алғашқы vs кейінгі айту
kk.html: a/an-мен таныстыр, сосын белгілі болғанда the-ге ауыс: She bought a car. The car is red. Контекст затты бірегей анықтайтын болса, басынан the: Close the door (осы бөлменің есігі).

ru.title: the для общей/уникальной ссылки
ru.html: Используй the для уникальных или известных из контекста вещей: the sun, the moon, the government, the internet, the future; и когда фраза сужает существительное до конкретного: the book on the table, the woman who called.

kk.title: the ортақ/бірегей сілтемеге
kk.html: Бірегей не контексттен белгілі нәрсеге the: the sun, the moon, the government, the internet, the future; және тіркес зат есімді нақтыға тарылтқанда: the book on the table, the woman who called.

ru.title: Нулевой артикль для общего значения
ru.html: Используй без артикля с множественными или неисчисляемыми, о которых говорят вообще: Water is essential. Children learn fast. I love music. Добавление the сделало бы конкретным: the water in this bottle, the children next door.

kk.title: Жалпы мағынаға нөл артикль
kk.html: Жалпы айтылатын көпше не саналмайтын зат есіммен артикльсіз: Water is essential. Children learn fast. I love music. the қосу оны нақты етеді: the water in this bottle, the children next door.

ru.title: Дискурс: артикли отслеживают общее знание
ru.html: Выбор артикля на деле о том, что слушатель может опознать. the сигналит ‘ты знаешь какой’; a/an — ‘вот новый’; ноль — ‘вообще’. Умелые авторы используют их, чтобы вести читателя через данную vs новую информацию.

kk.title: Дискурс: артикльдер ортақ білімді бақылайды
kk.html: Артикль таңдауы шын мәнінде тыңдаушы нені анықтай алатыны туралы. the ‘қайсы екенін білесің’; a/an ‘міне жаңасы’; нөл ‘жалпы’ дегенді білдіреді. Шебер жазушылар оларды белгілі vs жаңа ақпарат арқылы оқырманды жетелеу үшін қолданады.

ru.title: Ошибки продвинутого уровня
ru.html: ✗ I like the dogs (вообще) → ✓ I like dogs. ✗ She is best student → ✓ She is the best student (превосходная берёт the). ✗ Sun is a star → ✓ The sun is a star (уникальный объект).

kk.title: Жоғары деңгей қателері
kk.html: ✗ I like the dogs (жалпы) → ✓ I like dogs. ✗ She is best student → ✓ She is the best student (superlative the алады). ✗ Sun is a star → ✓ The sun is a star (бірегей нысан).

ru.title: Кратко
ru.html:
• a/an = новый, один из многих; the = конкретный/опознаваемый; ноль = общий.
  • Первое упоминание a/an → последующее the.
  • the для уникальных вещей и существительных, суженных фразой или превосходной степенью.
  • Нулевой артикль для множественных/неисчисляемых в общем смысле.

kk.title: Қысқаша
kk.html:
• a/an = жаңа, көптің бірі; the = нақты/анықталатын; нөл = жалпы.
  • Алғашқы айту a/an → кейінгі айту the.
  • Бірегей нәрсеге және тіркес/superlative тарылтқан зат есімге the.
  • Жалпы мағынадағы көпше/саналмайтын зат есімге нөл артикль.

activities.prompt: Sort each gap by the article it needs.

items.t: Pass me ___ pen on the desk.

activities.why: a/an for new/one-of-many, the for the specific/identifiable one, zero for general uncountable/plural nouns.

items.s: Water boils at 100°C.
She's the tallest in the class.
I love the nature.
Sun rises in the east.

activities.why: ‘nature’ in general takes no article; the unique ‘sun’ needs ‘the’ (The sun rises…).

activities.before: We adopted a kitten last week.
activities.why: second mention of a known thing → The kitten.

activities.after: smartphones have changed how we communicate. (a/an/the/zero)
alts: Zero
activities.why: a general statement about a whole class → zero article (leave it blank).
the relative clause makes it specific/identifiable → the article.
‘information’ is narrowed by the relative clause → the (though uncountable, it's now specific).

activities.q: “___ are among the most intelligent animals.” (talking about the whole species)
why: ‘the dolphins’ would mean specific ones.
Correct — a general statement about the species → zero article + plural.
‘a dolphin’ singles out one, changing the generalisation.

activities.q: Why does ‘Please shut the window’ use ‘the’?
options: The window is uniquely identifiable from the situation.
why: first mention would usually take ‘a’.
Correct — context makes ‘the window’ (this room's) uniquely identifiable.
it refers to one specific window, not windows generally.

activities.prompt: Sort each article by what it signals.

items.t: The friend I mentioned is here.

activities.why: a/an introduces new referents; the points to ones the listener can already identify.
‘happiness’ in a general sense takes no article → “I think happiness matters more than money.”

activities.instruction: Rewrite so it refers to the class in general (not specific ones):
activities.prompt: The books on that shelf are expensive.
alts: Books are expensive
books can be expensive
activities.why: a general statement about the whole class → zero article + plural → Books are expensive.

activities.typeLabel: Extended context: moving into a flat

steps.npc: How's the new place?

options.t: Good, though there's a strange smell in the kitchen.
options.why: ‘a strange smell’ (new) and ‘the kitchen’ (identifiable in the flat).

options.t: Good, though there's the strange smell in a kitchen.
options.why: a new smell takes ‘a’, and the specific kitchen takes ‘the’: ‘a strange smell in the kitchen’.

steps.npc: Do you like the neighbourhood generally?

options.t: Yes — people here are really friendly.
options.why: ‘people’ in general → zero article.

options.t: Yes — the people here are really friendly, and I love the nature.
options.why: ‘the people here’ is fine, but ‘nature’ in general takes no article (‘I love nature’).

activities.why: Use a/an for new referents, the for identifiable ones, and zero for general plural/uncountable nouns, tracking what the listener can identify.

learn.title: Generic reference & institutions
learn.html: This unit covers talking about things in general and a set of fixed article patterns — institutions, meals, illnesses, and going ‘to’ places — where English behaves in ways that must be learned, not reasoned out.

learn.title: Institutions: purpose vs building
learn.html: With school, university, hospital, church, prison, bed, work, use zero article when you mean the institution/its purpose: go to school (to study), in hospital (as a patient, BrE), go to bed (to sleep). Use the for the building/a visit: I went to the school to fix the boiler.

learn.title: Meals, illnesses & transport
learn.html: Meals take zero article in general: have breakfast, after lunch (but a lovely dinner when described). By + transport is zero: by car, by train, on foot. Illnesses vary: have flu/the flu, have a cold, have toothache.

learn.title: Generic the: species, instruments, inventions
learn.html: the + singular can express a whole class in formal/technical style: The tiger is endangered (the species), play the piano (instruments), The telephone changed society (the invention). Compare the everyday generic plural: Tigers are endangered.

learn.title: the + adjective = a group
learn.html: the + adjective refers to a whole group of people: the rich, the poor, the elderly, the unemployed, the French. These take a plural verb: The rich get richer.

learn.title: Advanced learner errors
learn.html:
✗ She's in the hospital (BrE, as a patient) → ✓ She's in hospital.

✗ I go to work by the car. → ✓ … by car.

✗ I play piano. → ✓ I play the piano.

learn.title: In a nutshell
learn.html:
• Zero article for institutions used for their purpose; the for the building/visit.
  • Meals and by + transport take zero article; illnesses vary.
  • the + singular = a whole species/instrument/invention (formal generic).
  • the + adjective = a group of people, with a plural verb.

ru.title: Родовая ссылка и учреждения
ru.html: Этот раздел охватывает разговор о вещах вообще и набор фиксированных артиклевых моделей — учреждения, приёмы пищи, болезни, поездки ‘to’ куда-либо — где английский ведёт себя так, что это надо выучить, а не вывести.

kk.title: Жалпы сілтеме және мекемелер
kk.html: Бұл бөлім заттар туралы жалпы айтуды және тұрақты артикль үлгілерін — мекемелер, тамақ, аурулар, ‘to’ орындарға бару — қамтиды, мұнда ағылшын логикамен емес, жаттаумен меңгерілетін жолмен әрекет етеді.

ru.title: Учреждения: цель vs здание
ru.html: С school, university, hospital, church, prison, bed, work используй ноль, когда имеешь в виду учреждение/его цель: go to school (учиться), in hospital (как пациент, BrE), go to bed (спать). Используй the для здания/визита: I went to the school to fix the boiler.

kk.title: Мекемелер: мақсат vs ғимарат
kk.html: school, university, hospital, church, prison, bed, work-пен мекемені/оның мақсатын білдірсең нөл артикль: go to school (оқу үшін), in hospital (науқас ретінде, BrE), go to bed (ұйықтау үшін). Ғимарат/бару үшін the: I went to the school to fix the boiler.

ru.title: Приёмы пищи, болезни, транспорт
ru.html: Приёмы пищи в общем берут ноль: have breakfast, after lunch (но a lovely dinner при описании). By + транспорт — ноль: by car, by train, on foot. Болезни варьируются: have flu/the flu, have a cold, have toothache.

kk.title: Тамақ, аурулар, көлік
kk.html: Тамақ жалпы нөл артикль алады: have breakfast, after lunch (бірақ сипатталса a lovely dinner). By + көлік нөл: by car, by train, on foot. Аурулар әртүрлі: have flu/the flu, have a cold, have toothache.

ru.title: Родовое the: вид, инструмент, изобретение
ru.html: the + единственное в формальном/техническом стиле выражает целый класс: The tiger is endangered (вид), play the piano (инструмент), The telephone changed society (изобретение). Сравни обиходное родовое множественное: Tigers are endangered.

kk.title: Жалпы the: түр, аспап, өнертабыс
kk.html: the + жекеше ресми/техникалық стильде бүкіл класты білдіреді: The tiger is endangered (түр), play the piano (аспап), The telephone changed society (өнертабыс). Күнделікті жалпы көпшемен салыстыр: Tigers are endangered.

ru.title: the + прилагательное = группа
ru.html: the + прилагательное обозначает целую группу людей: the rich, the poor, the elderly, the unemployed, the French. Они берут множественный глагол: The rich get richer.

kk.title: the + сын есім = топ
kk.html: the + сын есім бүкіл адамдар тобын білдіреді: the rich, the poor, the elderly, the unemployed, the French. Олар көпше етістік алады: The rich get richer.

ru.title: Ошибки продвинутого уровня
ru.html: ✗ She's in the hospital (BrE, пациент) → ✓ She's in hospital. ✗ I go to work by the car → ✓ … by car. ✗ I play piano → ✓ I play the piano.

kk.title: Жоғары деңгей қателері
kk.html: ✗ She's in the hospital (BrE, науқас) → ✓ She's in hospital. ✗ I go to work by the car → ✓ … by car. ✗ I play piano → ✓ I play the piano.

ru.title: Кратко
ru.html:
• Ноль для учреждений по назначению; the для здания/визита.
  • Приёмы пищи и by + транспорт берут ноль; болезни варьируются.
  • the + единственное = целый вид/инструмент/изобретение (формальное родовое).
  • the + прилагательное = группа людей, с множественным глаголом.

kk.title: Қысқаша
kk.html:
• Мақсаты үшін мекемеге нөл артикль; ғимарат/баруға the.
  • Тамақ пен by + көлік нөл артикль алады; аурулар әртүрлі.
  • the + жекеше = бүкіл түр/аспап/өнертабыс (ресми жалпы).
  • the + сын есім = адамдар тобы, көпше етістікпен.

activities.prompt: Sort each institution use.

items.t: the school has a new roof

activities.why: Zero article for the institution's purpose; ‘the’ for the physical building or a specific reference.

items.s: We usually travel by train.
The poor are often overlooked.
She learned to play piano at six.
I had the breakfast at eight.

activities.why: instruments take ‘the’ (play the piano); meals in general take no article (had breakfast).

activities.after: for two years for fraud. (article + prison)
activities.why: ‘prison’ as an institution (as a prisoner) → zero article → to prison.
musical instruments take ‘the’ → play the cello.

activities.after: unemployed have been hit hardest by the changes. (article)
activities.why: the + adjective = a group of people → The unemployed (plural verb).
‘to work’ (purpose) and ‘by bus’ (transport) both take zero article.

activities.q: Which sentence refers to the building rather than the activity?
options: My son started school in September.
The parents were invited to the school for a meeting.
Children go to school to learn.
why: ‘started school’ = began his education (purpose).
Correct — ‘to the school’ refers to the physical building for a visit.
‘go to school to learn’ is the institution's purpose.

activities.q: In formal writing, which best expresses the whole species?
options: A tiger is a magnificent creature.
The tiger is threatened with extinction.
why: ‘a tiger’ picks a representative example, less formal for the species claim.
Correct — the + singular expresses the whole species in formal/technical style.
a countable singular can't stand with no article here.

activities.prompt: Sort each phrase by whether it takes zero or an article.
activities.why: Institutions-for-purpose and meals take zero; a visit to a building and ‘a cold’ take an article.
as a patient (BrE), the institution takes no article → “in hospital after a fall.”

activities.instruction: Rewrite ‘people who are rich’ as a two-word group phrase:
activities.prompt: People who are rich often pay less tax proportionally.
alts: The rich
the rich often pay less tax proportionally
activities.why: the + adjective refers to the group as a whole → the rich (+ plural verb).

steps.npc: I heard your dad's unwell — is it serious?

options.t: He's in hospital for a few days, just for tests.
options.why: as a patient (BrE), the institution takes zero article.

options.t: He's in the hospital for a few days, just for tests.
options.why: as a patient in British English, use ‘in hospital’ without ‘the’.

steps.npc: How are you getting over to see him?

options.t: By train — it's quicker than driving.
options.why: ‘by + transport’ takes zero article.

options.t: By the train — it's quicker than driving.
options.why: ‘by train’ (means of transport) takes no article.

activities.why: Use zero article for institutions-by-purpose, meals and by + transport; use the for buildings/visits, instruments, generic species, and the + adjective groups.

learn.title: Articles with names & places
learn.html: Proper nouns follow conventions that must be memorised. Most take zero article, but many geographical and institutional names take the. The patterns are systematic enough to learn as groups.

learn.title: Zero article: most single-name places
learn.html: No article with: continents, countries, cities, single mountains, lakes, single islands: Asia, Kazakhstan, Almaty, Mount Everest, Lake Como, Cyprus. Also streets, most parks, single stations/airports: Oxford Street, Hyde Park, Heathrow.

learn.title: the: seas, rivers, ranges, plurals
learn.html: Use the with: seas, oceans, rivers, canals, deserts, mountain ranges, groups of islands, plural country names: the Pacific, the Nile, the Sahara, the Alps, the Maldives, the Netherlands, the USA, the UK. Also the before ‘of’ names: the University of Vienna, the Great Wall of China.

learn.title: Buildings, institutions & ‘the … of’
learn.html: Take the: hotels, cinemas, theatres, museums, most newspapers (the Ritz, the Odeon, the British Museum, the Guardian). Take zero: named universities/colleges without ‘of’ (Oxford University, Harvard), shops/banks named after people (Harrods), and most magazines (Time, Vogue).

learn.title: Learn them as patterns, not one by one
learn.html: The reliable rule: plural names and ‘… of …’ names take the; most single proper names take zero. When in doubt, check whether the name is fundamentally a common noun made specific (the Republic of…, the Sea of…) — those take the.

learn.title: Advanced learner errors
learn.html:
✗ the Kazakhstan, the Mount Everest → ✓ Kazakhstan, Mount Everest.

✗ Netherlands is flat. → ✓ The Netherlands is flat.

✗ University of Vienna (as subject) → ✓ the University of Vienna.

learn.title: In a nutshell
learn.html:
• Zero article: continents, countries, cities, single mountains/lakes/islands, streets.
  • the: seas, rivers, deserts, ranges, island groups, plural countries, ‘… of …’ names.
  • the with hotels/museums/newspapers; zero with named universities/shops/magazines.
  • Rule of thumb: plural or ‘… of …’ names take the; single names take zero.

ru.title: Артикли с названиями и местами
ru.html: Имена собственные следуют конвенциям, которые надо запомнить. Большинство берут ноль, но многие географические и институциональные названия берут the. Модели достаточно системны, чтобы учить группами.

kk.title: Атаулар мен орындармен артикльдер
kk.html: Жалқы есімдер дәстүрлерге бағынады, оларды жаттау керек. Көбі нөл артикль алады, бірақ көп географиялық және институционалдық атау the алады. Үлгілер топ ретінде үйренуге жеткілікті жүйелі.

ru.title: Ноль: большинство однословных названий мест
ru.html: Без артикля: континенты, страны, города, отдельные горы, озёра, отдельные острова: Asia, Kazakhstan, Almaty, Mount Everest, Lake Como, Cyprus. Также улицы, большинство парков, отдельные станции/аэропорты: Oxford Street, Hyde Park, Heathrow.

kk.title: Нөл артикль: көп жалғыз атаулы орын
kk.html: Артикльсіз: құрлықтар, елдер, қалалар, жеке таулар, көлдер, жеке аралдар: Asia, Kazakhstan, Almaty, Mount Everest, Lake Como, Cyprus. Сондай-ақ көшелер, көп саябақ, жеке станса/әуежай: Oxford Street, Hyde Park, Heathrow.

ru.title: the: моря, реки, хребты, множественные
ru.html: the: моря, океаны, реки, каналы, пустыни, горные хребты, группы островов, множественные названия стран: the Pacific, the Nile, the Sahara, the Alps, the Maldives, the Netherlands, the USA, the UK. Также the перед ‘of’-названиями: the University of Vienna, the Great Wall of China.

kk.title: the: теңіз, өзен, жота, көпше
kk.html: the: теңіздер, мұхиттар, өзендер, каналдар, шөлдер, тау жоталары, арал топтары, көпше ел атаулары: the Pacific, the Nile, the Sahara, the Alps, the Maldives, the Netherlands, the USA, the UK. Сондай-ақ ‘of’ атаулары алдында the: the University of Vienna, the Great Wall of China.

ru.title: Здания, учреждения, ‘the … of’
ru.html: the: отели, кинотеатры, театры, музеи, большинство газет (the Ritz, the Odeon, the British Museum, the Guardian). Ноль: университеты без ‘of’ (Oxford University, Harvard), магазины/банки по имени человека (Harrods), большинство журналов (Time, Vogue).

kk.title: Ғимараттар, мекемелер, ‘the … of’
kk.html: the алады: қонақүйлер, кинотеатрлар, театрлар, мұражайлар, көп газет (the Ritz, the Odeon, the British Museum, the Guardian). Нөл алады: ‘of’-сыз атаулы университеттер (Oxford University, Harvard), адам атындағы дүкендер/банктер (Harrods), және көп журнал (Time, Vogue).

ru.title: Учи как модели, не по одному
ru.html: Надёжное правило: множественные и ‘… of …’ названия берут the; большинство однословных имён — ноль. Если сомневаешься, проверь, не является ли название по сути нарицательным, ставшим конкретным (the Republic of…, the Sea of…) — они берут the.

kk.title: Оларды бір-бірлеп емес, үлгі ретінде үйрен
kk.html: Сенімді ереже: көпше атаулар мен ‘… of …’ атаулары the алады; көп жалғыз жалқы есім нөл алады. Күмәнданса, атау негізінен нақтыланған жалпы зат есім ме тексер (the Republic of…, the Sea of…) — олар the алады.

ru.title: Ошибки продвинутого уровня
ru.html: ✗ the Kazakhstan, the Mount Everest → ✓ Kazakhstan, Mount Everest. ✗ Netherlands is flat → ✓ The Netherlands is flat. ✗ University of Vienna (как подлежащее) → ✓ the University of Vienna.

kk.title: Жоғары деңгей қателері
kk.html: ✗ the Kazakhstan, the Mount Everest → ✓ Kazakhstan, Mount Everest. ✗ Netherlands is flat → ✓ The Netherlands is flat. ✗ University of Vienna (бастауыш ретінде) → ✓ the University of Vienna.

ru.title: Кратко
ru.html:
• Ноль: континенты, страны, города, отдельные горы/озёра/острова, улицы.
  • the: моря, реки, пустыни, хребты, группы островов, множественные страны, ‘… of …’.
  • the с отелями/музеями/газетами; ноль с университетами/магазинами/журналами.
  • Правило: множественные или ‘… of …’ берут the; одиночные имена — ноль.

kk.title: Қысқаша
kk.html:
• Нөл: құрлық, ел, қала, жеке тау/көл/арал, көше.
  • the: теңіз, өзен, шөл, жота, арал тобы, көпше ел, ‘… of …’ атаулары.
  • the қонақүй/мұражай/газетпен; нөл атаулы университет/дүкен/журналмен.
  • Ереже: көпше не ‘… of …’ атаулары the; жалғыз атаулар нөл.

activities.prompt: Sort each proper name.
activities.why: Single countries/mountains/lakes take zero; plural countries, mountain ranges and seas take ‘the’.

items.s: The Nile flows through several countries.
We flew into Heathrow at dawn.
I've always wanted to climb the Mount Fuji.
She studies at University of Oxford.

activities.why: single mountains take zero (Mount Fuji); ‘… of …’ institution names take ‘the’ (the University of Oxford).

activities.before: Cairo sits on the banks of
activities.why: rivers take ‘the’ → the Nile.

alts: zero
activities.why: a single-name country takes zero article (leave blank) → Canada.
a ‘… of …’ institution name takes ‘the’ → the Museum of Modern Art.
plural country name ‘the United States’ takes ‘the’; single names take zero.

activities.q: Which place name requires ‘the’?
why: a single mountain → zero article.
Correct — deserts take ‘the’ (the Sahara).
a single island → zero article.

activities.q: Which is correctly written?
why: a named university without ‘of’ takes no article.
Correct — ‘Cambridge University’ takes zero article.
wrong word order and article.

activities.prompt: Sort each name.
activities.why: Newspapers and museums take ‘the’; magazines and shops named after people take zero.
oceans take ‘the’ → “sailed across the Atlantic.”

activities.instruction: Rewrite with the correct article (or none):
activities.prompt: Philippines are made up of thousands of islands.
activities.answer: the Philippines are made up of thousands of islands
alts: The Philippines are made up of thousands of islands
the Philippines is made up of thousands of islands
activities.why: a plural/group-of-islands country name takes ‘the’ → The Philippines.

activities.scene: Choose the correct article with place names.

steps.npc: So where does the tour actually take us?

options.t: We start in Istanbul, then cross the Bosphorus into Asia.
options.why: a city takes zero; a strait/waterway takes ‘the’ (the Bosphorus).

options.t: We start in the Istanbul, then cross Bosphorus into Asia.
options.why: cities take no article (Istanbul), and the strait takes ‘the’ (the Bosphorus).

steps.npc: And the mountain leg — is that tough?

options.t: It is — we trek part of the Caucasus before reaching Mount Kazbek.
options.why: a mountain range takes ‘the’ (the Caucasus); a single peak takes zero (Mount Kazbek).

options.t: It is — we trek part of Caucasus before reaching the Mount Kazbek.
options.why: the range takes ‘the’ (the Caucasus); the single peak takes zero (Mount Kazbek).

activities.why: Learn place-name patterns: zero for single countries/cities/mountains, ‘the’ for seas/rivers/ranges/plural names and ‘… of …’ institutions.

learn.title: Assertive vs non-assertive
learn.html: The classic split: some in affirmative sentences, any in negatives and questions: I have some money. · I don't have any. · Do you have any? But this is a starting point, not the whole story — meaning often overrides the rule.

learn.title: some in questions: offers & requests
learn.html: Use some (not any) in questions that are offers, requests, or expect ‘yes’: Would you like some tea? · Could I have some water? · Didn't you buy some bread? Here you assume/hope the thing exists, so ‘some’ feels positive.

learn.title: any = ‘it doesn't matter which’
learn.html: In affirmative sentences, any means ‘whichever / no matter which’: Take any seat. · Any child could do it. · Call me at any time. This is a completely different, positive meaning of any — very common at C1.

learn.title: any in ‘hidden negatives’
learn.html: any appears after words with a negative or conditional flavour: if, without, hardly, never, rarely, refuse, doubt, prevent: If you have any questions… · She left without any explanation. · Hardly anyone came.

learn.title: Compounds: something / anyone, etc.
learn.html: The same logic governs some-/any- + thing/one/body/where: I saw something. · I didn't see anything. · Anyone can join (= everyone). Would you like something to eat? (offer).

learn.title: Advanced learner errors
learn.html:
✗ Would you like any coffee? (offer) → ✓ … some coffee?

✗ You can choose some colour you like. → ✓ … any colour you like.

✗ I don't have some money. → ✓ … any money.

learn.title: In a nutshell
learn.html:
• some = affirmative; any = negatives/questions — as a default.
  • some in offers/requests and questions expecting ‘yes’.
  • any in affirmatives = ‘whichever / no matter which’.
  • any after if/without/hardly/never and other hidden negatives.

ru.title: Ассертивное vs неассертивное
ru.html: Классическое деление: some в утвердительных, any в отрицаниях и вопросах: I have some money. I don't have any. Do you have any? Но это отправная точка, не вся картина; смысл часто побеждает правило.

kk.title: Ассертив vs ассертив емес
kk.html: Классикалық бөліну: some болымды сөйлемде, any болымсыз пен сұрақта: I have some money. I don't have any. Do you have any? Бірақ бұл — бастапқы нүкте, толық емес; мағына жиі ережені жеңеді.

ru.title: some в вопросах: предложения и просьбы
ru.html: В вопросах-предложениях, просьбах или ожидающих ‘да’ используй some (не any): Would you like some tea? Could I have some water? Didn't you buy some bread? Здесь предполагаешь/надеешься, что вещь существует, поэтому ‘some’ звучит позитивно.

kk.title: Сұрақтағы some: ұсыныс пен өтініш
kk.html: Ұсыныс, өтініш, не ‘иә’ күтетін сұрақтарда some (any емес): Would you like some tea? Could I have some water? Didn't you buy some bread? Мұнда заттың бар екенін болжайсың/үміттенесің, сондықтан ‘some’ оң сезіледі.

ru.title: any = ‘неважно какой’
ru.html: В утвердительных предложениях any значит ‘любой / неважно какой’: Take any seat. Any child could do it. Call me at any time. Это совсем другое, позитивное значение any — очень частое на C1.

kk.title: any = ‘қайсы екені маңызды емес’
kk.html: Болымды сөйлемде any ‘қайсы болса да’ дегенді білдіреді: Take any seat. Any child could do it. Call me at any time. Бұл — any-дің мүлдем басқа, оң мағынасы — C1-де өте жиі.

ru.title: any в ‘скрытых отрицаниях’
ru.html: any появляется после слов с отрицательным или условным оттенком: if, without, hardly, never, rarely, refuse, doubt, prevent: If you have any questions… She left without any explanation. Hardly anyone came.

kk.title: ‘Жасырын болымсыздағы’ any
kk.html: any болымсыз не шартты реңкі бар сөздерден кейін кездеседі: if, without, hardly, never, rarely, refuse, doubt, prevent: If you have any questions… She left without any explanation. Hardly anyone came.

ru.title: Составные: something / anyone и т.д.
ru.html: Та же логика управляет some-/any- + thing/one/body/where: I saw something. I didn't see anything. Anyone can join (= все). Would you like something to eat? (предложение).

kk.title: Құрамалар: something / anyone, т.б.
kk.html: Сол логика some-/any- + thing/one/body/where-ды басқарады: I saw something. I didn't see anything. Anyone can join (= бәрі). Would you like something to eat? (ұсыныс).

ru.title: Ошибки продвинутого уровня
ru.html: ✗ Would you like any coffee? (предложение) → ✓ … some coffee? ✗ You can choose some colour you like → ✓ … any colour you like. ✗ I don't have some money → ✓ … any money.

kk.title: Жоғары деңгей қателері
kk.html: ✗ Would you like any coffee? (ұсыныс) → ✓ … some coffee? ✗ You can choose some colour you like → ✓ … any colour you like. ✗ I don't have some money → ✓ … any money.

ru.title: Кратко
ru.html:
• some = утвердительное; any = отрицания/вопросы — по умолчанию.
  • some в предложениях/просьбах и вопросах, ожидающих ‘да’.
  • any в утвердительных = ‘любой / неважно какой’.
  • any после if/without/hardly/never и других скрытых отрицаний.

kk.title: Қысқаша
kk.html:
• some = болымды; any = болымсыз/сұрақ — әдепкі бойынша.
  • Ұсыныс/өтініш пен ‘иә’ күтетін сұрақта some.
  • Болымдыдағы any = ‘қайсы болса да’.
  • if/without/hardly/never және басқа жасырын болымсыздан кейін any.

activities.prompt: Sort each context by the natural choice.
activities.why: some for affirmatives/offers/requests; any for negatives, real questions, and ‘whichever’ meanings.

items.s: Would you like some cake?
Any student who's interested can apply.
I don't need some help.
He left without some warning.

activities.why: negatives and ‘without’ take any: ‘don't need any help’, ‘without any warning’.

activities.before: You look thirsty — would you like
activities.why: an offer expecting ‘yes’ → some juice.

activities.before: This ticket lets you travel on
activities.why: affirmative ‘no matter which’ → any train.

activities.after: any food left by the time we arrived. (hardly)
activities.why: ‘hardly any’ = almost none → hardly any.
‘any time’ (whichever) and ‘anything’ after ‘if’ (conditional).

activities.q: Why is ‘some’ natural in ‘Could you lend me some money?’
options: It's a request, expecting a positive response.
why: it isn't negative.
Correct — requests and offers use ‘some’, assuming a positive answer.
uncountability doesn't decide some vs any here.

activities.q: In which sentence does ‘any’ mean ‘it doesn't matter which’?
options: You can pick any dessert on the menu.
why: negative use (= none).
Correct — affirmative ‘any’ = whichever you like.
non-assertive use in a question.

activities.prompt: Sort each ‘any’ by its meaning.

items.t: Any expert will tell you the same.
Come round any evening you like.

activities.why: ‘any’ is non-assertive in negatives/questions, but means ‘whichever’ in affirmative statements.
‘without’ is a hidden negative → “without any help.”

activities.instruction: Rewrite as a polite offer using ‘some’:
activities.prompt: Do you want any biscuits with your tea?
activities.answer: would you like some biscuits with your tea
alts: Would you like some biscuits with your tea?
would you like some biscuits with your tea?
activities.why: an offer expecting ‘yes’ → ‘Would you like some biscuits…?’

steps.npc: You've hardly eaten — can I get you anything?

options.t: Actually, could I have some more of that salad?
options.why: a request expecting ‘yes’ → some.

options.t: Actually, could I have any more of that salad?
options.why: a request expecting a positive answer uses ‘some’, not ‘any’.

steps.npc: There are three desserts — which would you like?

options.t: Honestly, any of them would be lovely.
options.why: affirmative ‘any’ = whichever, no preference.

options.t: Honestly, some of them would be lovely.
options.why: to say ‘it doesn't matter which’, use ‘any of them’, not ‘some of them’.

activities.why: Default to some (affirmatives/offers/requests) and any (negatives/questions), but use any for ‘whichever’ and after hidden negatives like if/without/hardly.

learn.title: Three ways to say ‘zero’
learn.html: no + noun, not any + noun, and none (of) all express zero quantity, but they differ in grammar and emphasis: There's no milk. · There isn't any milk. · None of the milk is left.

learn.title: no vs not any
learn.html: no goes directly before a noun and can't follow another negative; not any needs a negative verb: I have no idea = I don't have any idea. no is often more emphatic and is required at the start of a sentence: No student passed (not ‘Not any student’).

learn.title: none & none of
learn.html: none stands alone as a pronoun (no noun after): “How many are left?” “None.” none of + determiner/pronoun + noun: None of the students / None of them came. Don't use ‘no of’: ✗ no of them.

learn.title: Agreement with none (of)
learn.html: none of + uncountable → singular verb: None of the water was clean. none of + plural → singular (formal) or plural (common): None of them was/were ready. no + plural noun usually takes a plural verb: No answers were correct.

learn.title: Double negatives & register
learn.html: Avoid the double negative: ✗ I don't have no money. → ✓ I have no money / I don't have any money. no + noun tends to sound more formal/emphatic; not any is more neutral and conversational.

learn.title: Advanced learner errors
learn.html:
✗ I don't have no time. → ✓ I have no time.

✗ No of the guests came. → ✓ None of the guests came.

✗ There isn't no bread. → ✓ There isn't any bread / There's no bread.

learn.title: In a nutshell
learn.html:
• no + noun = not any + noun (no needs no extra negative verb).
  • Use no (not ‘not any’) at the start of a sentence; no is more emphatic.
  • none stands alone; none of + determiner + noun (never ‘no of’).
  • none of + uncountable = singular; + plural = singular (formal) / plural (common). Avoid double negatives.

ru.title: Три способа сказать ‘ноль’
ru.html: no + сущ., not any + сущ. и none (of) — все выражают нулевое количество, но различаются грамматикой и акцентом: There's no milk. There isn't any milk. None of the milk is left.

kk.title: ‘Нөлді’ айтудың үш жолы
kk.html: no + зат есім, not any + зат есім, және none (of) бәрі нөл мөлшерді білдіреді, бірақ грамматика мен екпінде ерекшеленеді: There's no milk. There isn't any milk. None of the milk is left.

ru.title: no vs not any
ru.html: no идёт прямо перед существительным и не может следовать за другим отрицанием; not any требует отрицательного глагола: I have no idea = I don't have any idea. no часто эмфатичнее и обязателен в начале предложения: No student passed (не ‘Not any student’).

kk.title: no vs not any
kk.html: no зат есім алдында тікелей келеді әрі басқа болымсыздан кейін тұра алмайды; not any болымсыз етістік қажет етеді: I have no idea = I don't have any idea. no жиі екпіндірек және сөйлем басында міндетті: No student passed (‘Not any student’ емес).

ru.title: none и none of
ru.html: none стоит отдельно как местоимение (без существительного после): “How many are left?” “None.” none of + определитель/местоимение + сущ.: None of the students / None of them came. Не используй ‘no of’: ✗ no of them.

kk.title: none және none of
kk.html: none есімдік ретінде жеке тұрады (кейін зат есім жоқ): “How many are left?” “None.” none of + анықтауыш/есімдік + зат есім: None of the students / None of them came. ‘no of’ қолданба: ✗ no of them.

ru.title: Согласование с none (of)
ru.html: none of + неисчисляемое → единственный глагол: None of the water was clean. none of + множ. → единственный (формально) или множественный (часто): None of them was/were ready. no + множ. сущ. обычно берёт множественный глагол: No answers were correct.

kk.title: none (of)-пен келісім
kk.html: none of + саналмайтын → жекеше етістік: None of the water was clean. none of + көпше → жекеше (ресми) не көпше (жиі): None of them was/were ready. no + көпше зат есім әдетте көпше етістік алады: No answers were correct.

ru.title: Двойное отрицание и регистр
ru.html: Избегай двойного отрицания: ✗ I don't have no money → ✓ I have no money / I don't have any money. no + сущ. звучит формальнее/эмфатичнее; not any нейтральнее и разговорнее.

kk.title: Қос болымсыз және регистр
kk.html: Қос болымсыздан қаш: ✗ I don't have no money → ✓ I have no money / I don't have any money. no + зат есім ресми/екпіндірек естіледі; not any бейтараптау әрі әңгімелік.

ru.title: Ошибки продвинутого уровня
ru.html: ✗ I don't have no time → ✓ I have no time. ✗ No of the guests came → ✓ None of the guests came. ✗ There isn't no bread → ✓ There isn't any bread / There's no bread.

kk.title: Жоғары деңгей қателері
kk.html: ✗ I don't have no time → ✓ I have no time. ✗ No of the guests came → ✓ None of the guests came. ✗ There isn't no bread → ✓ There isn't any bread / There's no bread.

ru.title: Кратко
ru.html:
• no + сущ. = not any + сущ. (no не требует доп. отрицательного глагола).
  • В начале предложения — no (не ‘not any’); no эмфатичнее.
  • none стоит отдельно; none of + определитель + сущ. (никогда ‘no of’).
  • none of + неисчисляемое = единственное; + множ. = единственное (формально)/множ. (часто). Избегай двойного отрицания.

kk.title: Қысқаша
kk.html:
• no + зат есім = not any + зат есім (no қосымша болымсыз етістік қажет етпейді).
  • Сөйлем басында no (‘not any’ емес); no екпіндірек.
  • none жеке тұрады; none of + анықтауыш + зат есім (ешқашан ‘no of’ емес).
  • none of + саналмайтын = жекеше; + көпше = жекеше (ресми)/көпше (жиі). Қос болымсыздан қаш.

activities.prompt: Sort each gap by the correct form.
activities.why: ‘no + noun’ needs no extra negative; ‘not any’ needs a negative verb; ‘none (of)’ stands alone or before ‘of’.

items.s: None of the information was useful.
No buses run after midnight.
I don't have no idea.
No of the guests stayed late.

activities.why: avoid double negatives (‘have no idea’) and use ‘none of’, never ‘no of’.

activities.after: applicant met all the criteria. (no/not any)
activities.why: at the start of a sentence, use No (not ‘Not any’) → No applicant.
‘none of + uncountable (equipment)’ → singular has.
standing alone as a pronoun → none.
‘none of + plural’ with the common plural verb ‘were’.

activities.q: Which is the more emphatic, formal way to say ‘We haven't got any evidence’?
options: We don't have not any evidence.
why: Correct — ‘have no evidence’ is the emphatic, well-formed version.
double marking of negation is ungrammatical.
a double negative, non-standard.

activities.q: In formal writing: “None of the samples ___ contaminated.”
why: Correct — strict/formal ‘none of…’ takes a singular verb.
common in speech, but the formal choice is singular.
present tense doesn't match the reporting context.

activities.prompt: Sort each phrase as correct or incorrect.

items.t: not any money (after a negative verb)

activities.why: ‘no of’ is never correct; use ‘none of’, ‘no + noun’, or ‘not any + noun’ with a negative verb.
avoid the double negative → “couldn't find any exit” (or ‘found no exit’).

activities.instruction: Rewrite using ‘no + noun’ (more emphatic):
activities.prompt: There isn't any excuse for this delay.
activities.answer: there's no excuse for this delay
alts: There's no excuse for this delay
there is no excuse for this delay
activities.why: ‘not any’ → the emphatic ‘no + noun’ → there's no excuse.

steps.npc: How much of the printer paper have we got?

options.t: None of it is left, I'm afraid — we're completely out.
options.why: ‘none of it’ + singular verb for the uncountable ‘paper’.

options.t: No of it is left, I'm afraid — we're completely out.
options.why: ‘no of’ is never correct; use ‘none of it’.

steps.npc: And were there any complaints about the shortage?

options.t: Surprisingly, there were no complaints at all.
options.why: ‘no + plural noun’ with a plural verb, emphatic.

options.t: Surprisingly, there weren't no complaints at all.
options.why: a double negative; say ‘there were no complaints’ or ‘there weren't any complaints’.

activities.why: Use no + noun (emphatic, sentence-initial), not any + noun (with a negative verb), and none (of) as a pronoun — never ‘no of’, and never a double negative.

learn.title: Big quantities: countable vs uncountable
learn.html: The starting split: many (+ of) with countable plural nouns, much (+ of) with uncountable nouns, and a lot of / lots of with both: many books, much time, a lot of books/time.

learn.title: Register: much/many vs a lot of
learn.html: much and many are most natural in negatives and questions: I don't have much time. · Were there many people? In affirmative statements, especially in speech, a lot of / lots of is preferred: I have a lot of work (rather than ‘much work’, which sounds formal/stiff).

learn.title: much/many in formal & emphatic use
learn.html: much/many do appear in affirmatives when formal or after so, too, as, how: There is much to discuss. · Many believe this. · too much salt, so many problems, how much? ‘a great/good deal of’ and ‘a large number of’ are formal equivalents.

learn.title: of: partitive vs not
learn.html: Use of before a determiner or pronoun (a specific subset): many of the students, much of the day, a lot of them. Drop of before a bare noun: many students, much time. lots keeps ‘of’ before a noun (lots of) but stands alone otherwise (“How many?” “Lots.”).

learn.title: much as an adverb; too much / very much
learn.html: much also modifies verbs, comparatives and past participles: I don't much like it. · much better · much appreciated · thank you very much. Note ‘very much’ (not ‘very many’) for degree.

learn.title: Advanced learner errors
learn.html:
✗ I have much friends. → ✓ I have many/a lot of friends.

✗ There were much people. → ✓ … many people.

✗ much of time → ✓ much of the time / a lot of time.

learn.title: In a nutshell
learn.html:
• many + countable plural; much + uncountable; a lot of/lots of + both.
  • Prefer much/many in negatives/questions; a lot of in affirmative speech.
  • much/many in affirmatives = formal or after so/too/as/how.
  • Add ‘of’ before a determiner/pronoun; ‘much’ also works as an adverb.

ru.title: Большие количества: исчисляемые vs неисчисляемые
ru.html: Начальное деление: many (+ of) с исчисляемыми множественными, much (+ of) с неисчисляемыми, a lot of / lots of с обоими: many books, much time, a lot of books/time.

kk.title: Үлкен мөлшер: саналатын vs саналмайтын
kk.html: Бастапқы бөліну: many (+ of) саналатын көпше зат есіммен, much (+ of) саналмайтын зат есіммен, a lot of / lots of екеуімен де: many books, much time, a lot of books/time.

ru.title: Регистр: much/many vs a lot of
ru.html: much и many самые естественные в отрицаниях и вопросах: I don't have much time. Were there many people? В утвердительных, особ. в речи, предпочтительнее a lot of / lots of: I have a lot of work (‘much work’ звучит формально/натянуто).

kk.title: Регистр: much/many vs a lot of
kk.html: much пен many болымсыз пен сұрақта ең табиғи: I don't have much time. Were there many people? Болымды мәлімдемеде, әсіресе тілде, a lot of / lots of артық: I have a lot of work (‘much work’ ресми/қатаң естіледі).

ru.title: much/many в формальном и эмфатическом употреблении
ru.html: much/many появляются в утвердительных, когда формально или после so, too, as, how: There is much to discuss. Many believe this. too much salt, so many problems, how much? ‘a great/good deal of’ и ‘a large number of’ — формальные эквиваленты.

kk.title: much/many ресми және екпінді қолданыс
kk.html: much/many болымдыда ресми болғанда не so, too, as, how-дан кейін кездеседі: There is much to discuss. Many believe this. too much salt, so many problems, how much? ‘a great/good deal of’ мен ‘a large number of’ — ресми баламалар.

ru.title: of: партитив или нет
ru.html: Используй of перед определителем или местоимением (конкретное подмножество): many of the students, much of the day, a lot of them. Опускай of перед голым существительным: many students, much time. lots сохраняет ‘of’ перед существительным (lots of), иначе стоит отдельно (“How many?” “Lots.”).

kk.title: of: партитив vs жоқ
kk.html: Анықтауыш не есімдік алдында (нақты ішкі жиын) of қолдан: many of the students, much of the day, a lot of them. Жалаң зат есім алдында of-ты түсір: many students, much time. lots зат есім алдында ‘of’-ты сақтайды (lots of), әйтпесе жеке тұрады (“How many?” “Lots.”).

ru.title: much как наречие; too much / very much
ru.html: much также модифицирует глаголы, сравнительные и причастия: I don't much like it. much better · much appreciated · thank you very much. Для степени — ‘very much’ (не ‘very many’).

kk.title: much үстеу ретінде; too much / very much
kk.html: much етістік, салыстырмалы, есімшені де анықтайды: I don't much like it. much better · much appreciated · thank you very much. Дәреже үшін ‘very much’ (‘very many’ емес).

ru.title: Ошибки продвинутого уровня
ru.html: ✗ I have much friends → ✓ I have many/a lot of friends. ✗ There were much people → ✓ … many people. ✗ much of time → ✓ much of the time / a lot of time.

kk.title: Жоғары деңгей қателері
kk.html: ✗ I have much friends → ✓ I have many/a lot of friends. ✗ There were much people → ✓ … many people. ✗ much of time → ✓ much of the time / a lot of time.

ru.title: Кратко
ru.html:
• many + исчисляемое множ.; much + неисчисляемое; a lot of/lots of + оба.
  • Предпочитай much/many в отрицаниях/вопросах; a lot of в утвердительной речи.
  • much/many в утвердительных = формально или после so/too/as/how.
  • Добавляй ‘of’ перед определителем/местоимением; ‘much’ работает и как наречие.

kk.title: Қысқаша
kk.html:
• many + саналатын көпше; much + саналмайтын; a lot of/lots of + екеуі.
  • Болымсыз/сұрақта much/many; болымды тілде a lot of.
  • Болымдыдағы much/many = ресми не so/too/as/how-дан кейін.
  • Анықтауыш/есімдік алдында ‘of’ қос; ‘much’ үстеу де.

activities.typeLabel: much, many or a lot of?
activities.prompt: Sort each noun by the quantifier it takes.
activities.why: much goes with uncountables, many with countable plurals, and a lot of with either.

items.s: I don't have much time today.
There's a lot of traffic this morning.
I have much friends here.
Were there much people at the talk?

activities.why: ‘friends’ and ‘people’ are countable, so use many/a lot of, not much.
a negative + uncountable ‘evidence’ → much.

activities.after: of the students had already left. (much/many)
activities.why: a specific subset of a countable plural → Many of the students.

alts: lots of
activities.why: an affirmative statement with a countable plural → a lot of deadlines.
formal affirmative ‘much’ + infinitive.

activities.q: In casual conversation, which is most natural?
options: I've got a lot of homework tonight.
why: ‘much homework’ in an affirmative sounds stiff/formal in speech.
Correct — ‘a lot of’ is natural in affirmative speech.
‘homework’ is uncountable, so ‘many’ is wrong anyway.

activities.q: Complete: “There's ___ salt in this soup.”
why: ‘many’ is for countables, but ‘salt’ is uncountable.
Correct — ‘too much’ + uncountable ‘salt’.
‘a lot’ needs ‘of’ before a noun.

activities.prompt: Sort each phrase by whether ‘of’ is required.
activities.why: Use ‘of’ before a determiner/pronoun (many of the guests) but not before a bare noun (many guests).
‘money’ is uncountable → “doesn't earn much money.”

activities.typeLabel: Rewrite in a more formal register
activities.instruction: Rewrite using ‘much’ instead of ‘a lot of’:
activities.prompt: There is a lot of interest in the proposal.
activities.answer: there is much interest in the proposal
alts: There is much interest in the proposal
there is much interest in the proposal.
activities.why: a formal affirmative with an uncountable → much interest.

activities.scene: Choose much, many or a lot of.

steps.npc: How's the new job? Busy?

options.t: Really busy — there's a lot of responsibility.
options.why: affirmative speech + uncountable → a lot of.

options.t: Really busy — there's much responsibility.
options.why: in affirmative speech, ‘a lot of responsibility’ is far more natural than ‘much’.

steps.npc: Do you get much support from the team?

options.t: Honestly, not many of them have time to help.
options.why: ‘many of them’ (countable subset) fits the negative context.

options.t: Honestly, not much of them have time to help.
options.why: ‘them’ is countable, so use ‘many of them’, not ‘much of them’.

activities.why: Use much (uncountable) and many (countable) especially in negatives/questions, a lot of in affirmative speech, and add ‘of’ before a determiner or pronoun.

learn.title: Totality & distribution
learn.html: all, whole, every, each all cover a complete set, but differ in viewpoint: all/whole see the totality; every sees every member as part of a group; each sees members one by one.

learn.title: all vs whole
learn.html: all + (the) + noun; the whole + noun. With singular countables, prefer the whole: the whole day / all day (both fine) but the whole book (not ‘all the book’ usually). all works with uncountables and plurals: all the water, all the students. whole needs an article/possessive: my whole life.

learn.title: every vs each
learn.html: Both take a singular noun and verb. every stresses the group as a whole and works for large numbers, generalisations, and with almost/nearly: every student, almost every day. each stresses individuals and is used for two and with of: each of the two teams, each player.

learn.title: all of / each of + determiner
learn.html: Before a pronoun or determiner, use of: all of them, all of the cake, each of the rooms. Before a bare noun, drop it: all students. every never takes ‘of’ directly — use every one of: every one of them.

learn.title: Position of ‘all’ & ‘each’
learn.html: all and each can float to mid-position (after the subject or auxiliary): They all agreed. · We have each been given a copy. · The tickets are each £20. This flexibility is a neat C1 feature.

learn.title: Advanced learner errors
learn.html:
✗ Every of the students → ✓ Each of the students / Every one of the students.

✗ all the my friends → ✓ all of my friends / all my friends.

✗ Every rooms were booked. → ✓ Every room was booked.

learn.title: In a nutshell
learn.html:
• all/whole = totality; every = all members as a group; each = one by one.
  • the whole + singular noun; all + uncountable/plural; whole needs an article/possessive.
  • every/each + singular noun & verb; each for two and with ‘of’; every one of (not ‘every of’).
  • all/each can float to mid-position after the subject/auxiliary.

ru.title: Целостность и распределение
ru.html: all, whole, every, each все охватывают полное множество, но различаются точкой зрения: all/whole видят целое; every видит каждого члена как часть группы; each видит членов по одному.

kk.title: Тұтастық пен бөліну
kk.html: all, whole, every, each бәрі толық жиынды қамтиды, бірақ көзқараста ерекшеленеді: all/whole тұтастықты көреді; every әр мүшені топ бөлігі ретінде көреді; each мүшелерді бір-бірлеп көреді.

ru.title: all vs whole
ru.html: all + (the) + сущ.; the whole + сущ. С единственными исчисляемыми предпочитай the whole: the whole day / all day (оба) но the whole book (обычно не ‘all the book’). all с неисчисляемыми и множественными: all the water, all the students. whole требует артикль/притяжательное: my whole life.

kk.title: all vs whole
kk.html: all + (the) + зат есім; the whole + зат есім. Жекеше саналатынмен the whole-ды таңда: the whole day / all day (екеуі де) бірақ the whole book (әдетте ‘all the book’ емес). all саналмайтын мен көпшемен: all the water, all the students. whole артикль/тәуелдік қажет етеді: my whole life.

ru.title: every vs each
ru.html: Оба берут единственное сущ. и глагол. every подчёркивает группу целиком и работает для больших чисел, обобщений, с almost/nearly: every student, almost every day. each подчёркивает индивидуумов и используется для двух и с of: each of the two teams, each player.

kk.title: every vs each
kk.html: Екеуі де жекеше зат есім мен етістік алады. every топты тұтас баса көрсетеді әрі үлкен сан, жалпылау, almost/nearly-мен: every student, almost every day. each жеке дараларды баса көрсетеді әрі екеуге және of-пен: each of the two teams, each player.

ru.title: all of / each of + определитель
ru.html: Перед местоимением или определителем используй of: all of them, all of the cake, each of the rooms. Перед голым существительным опускай: all students. every никогда не берёт ‘of’ напрямую — используй every one of: every one of them.

kk.title: all of / each of + анықтауыш
kk.html: Есімдік не анықтауыш алдында of қолдан: all of them, all of the cake, each of the rooms. Жалаң зат есім алдында түсір: all students. every ешқашан тікелей ‘of’ алмайды — every one of қолдан: every one of them.

ru.title: Позиция ‘all’ и ‘each’
ru.html: all и each могут смещаться в среднюю позицию (после подлежащего или вспомогательного): They all agreed. We have each been given a copy. The tickets are each £20. Эта гибкость — приятная черта C1.

kk.title: ‘all’ мен ‘each’ орны
kk.html: all мен each орта позицияға (бастауыш не көмекшіден кейін) жылжи алады: They all agreed. We have each been given a copy. The tickets are each £20. Бұл икемділік — әдемі C1 ерекшелігі.

ru.title: Ошибки продвинутого уровня
ru.html: ✗ Every of the students → ✓ Each of the students / Every one of the students. ✗ all the my friends → ✓ all of my friends / all my friends. ✗ Every rooms were booked → ✓ Every room was booked.

kk.title: Жоғары деңгей қателері
kk.html: ✗ Every of the students → ✓ Each of the students / Every one of the students. ✗ all the my friends → ✓ all of my friends / all my friends. ✗ Every rooms were booked → ✓ Every room was booked.

ru.title: Кратко
ru.html:
• all/whole = целое; every = все члены как группа; each = по одному.
  • the whole + единственное сущ.; all + неисчисляемое/множ.; whole требует артикль/притяжательное.
  • every/each + единственное сущ. и глагол; each для двух и с ‘of’; every one of (не ‘every of’).
  • all/each могут смещаться в среднюю позицию после подлежащего/вспомогательного.

kk.title: Қысқаша
kk.html:
• all/whole = тұтастық; every = барлық мүше топ ретінде; each = бір-бірлеп.
  • the whole + жекеше зат есім; all + саналмайтын/көпше; whole артикль/тәуелдік қажет етеді.
  • every/each + жекеше зат есім мен етістік; each екеуге және ‘of’-пен; every one of (‘every of’ емес).
  • all/each бастауыш/көмекшіден кейін орта позицияға жылжи алады.

activities.prompt: Sort each quantifier by what follows it.
activities.why: every/each/whole/every one of take a singular noun and verb; all/all of + plural or uncountable nouns.

items.s: Each of the candidates was interviewed.
She spent the whole weekend revising.
Every of the rooms has a balcony.
All the my books are in boxes.

activities.why: ‘every’ can't take ‘of’ (use ‘each of / every one of’); ‘all my books’ or ‘all of my books’ (not ‘all the my’).

activities.after: of the finalists received a medal. (each/every)
activities.why: ‘of’ requires ‘each’ (not ‘every’) → Each of the finalists.
a single countable period → the whole week (‘all week’ also possible without ‘the’).

activities.after: seat in the theatre was taken. (every/all)
activities.why: every + singular noun + singular verb → Every seat was taken.
‘all’ floats to mid-position after the auxiliary.

activities.q: “There were only two options, and ___ had drawbacks.”
why: ‘every’ isn't normally used for just two.
Correct — ‘each’ is preferred for two, viewing them individually.
‘all’ would need a plural verb and lose the one-by-one sense.

activities.q: Which is the natural phrasing?
options: I read all the book last night.
I read the whole book last night.
I read whole book last night.
why: ‘all the book’ is unusual with a singular countable.
Correct — ‘the whole book’ is the natural form.
‘whole’ needs an article (‘the whole book’).

activities.prompt: Sort each by whether ‘of’ is used before ‘them’.
activities.why: all of / each of / every one of take ‘of’; ‘every’ alone can't (‘every of them’ is wrong).
‘every’ takes a singular noun (and verb) → “Every student … has passed.”

activities.instruction: Rewrite using ‘every one of’ instead of ‘all’:
activities.prompt: All of the machines needs checking.
activities.answer: every one of the machines needs checking
alts: Every one of the machines needs checking
every one of the machines needs checking.
activities.why: ‘every one of’ + singular verb → every one of the machines needs checking.

activities.scene: Choose all, whole, every or each.

steps.npc: Have you looked through the competition entries?

options.t: Yes — I read each of them carefully.
options.why: ‘each of them’ views the entries individually, one by one.

options.t: Yes — I read every of them carefully.
options.why: ‘every’ can't take ‘of’; use ‘each of them’ or ‘every one of them’.

steps.npc: Did any stand out over the others?

options.t: A few — but honestly, the whole batch was strong.
options.why: ‘the whole batch’ (singular collective) expresses totality.

options.t: A few — but honestly, all the batch were strong.
options.why: with a singular collective, ‘the whole batch was’ is more natural than ‘all the batch were’.

activities.why: Use all/whole for totality, every for the group and each for individuals, keeping every/each with singular nouns and ‘every one of’ (never ‘every of’).

learn.title: Small quantities: countable vs uncountable
learn.html: few/fewer go with countable plural nouns; little/less go with uncountable nouns: few mistakes, fewer cars; little time, less money. Matching the noun type is the foundation.

learn.title: few vs a few; little vs a little
learn.html: The article changes the tone. a few / a little = a small but positive amount (‘some’): I have a few friends here (some). few / little (no article) = almost none, a negative slant: I have few friends here (hardly any). This contrast is a favourite C1 test point.

learn.title: fewer vs less: the prescriptive rule
learn.html: Traditionally, fewer for countables (fewer people, fewer items) and less for uncountables (less traffic, less sugar). In practice ‘less’ with plurals is common in speech (‘less people’) but avoided in careful/formal writing. Note ‘less’ is standard with measurements: less than 10 miles, less than £50.

learn.title: fewer/less + of; quite a few
learn.html: Use of before a determiner/pronoun: few of the guests, little of the report, fewer of them. quite a few and a good few actually mean quite a lot (not few!): Quite a few people came.

learn.title: Register & softening
learn.html: few/little (negative) can sound formal or emphatic; in speech people often prefer not many/not much or hardly any: There were few objections (formal) vs There weren't many objections (neutral). a little also softens: a little tired, a little more time.

learn.title: Advanced learner errors
learn.html:
✗ less mistakes (formal) → ✓ fewer mistakes.

✗ I have a little friends. → ✓ I have a few friends.

✗ few of water → ✓ little water / a little water.

learn.title: In a nutshell
learn.html:
• few/fewer + countable plural; little/less + uncountable.
  • a few/a little = some (positive); few/little = hardly any (negative).
  • fewer for countables, less for uncountables (and measurements) in careful writing.
  • Add ‘of’ before a determiner; ‘quite a few’ means quite a lot.

ru.title: Малые количества: исчисляемые vs неисчисляемые
ru.html: few/fewer с исчисляемыми множественными; little/less с неисчисляемыми: few mistakes, fewer cars; little time, less money. Согласование типа существительного — основа.

kk.title: Аз мөлшер: саналатын vs саналмайтын
kk.html: few/fewer саналатын көпше зат есіммен; little/less саналмайтын зат есіммен: few mistakes, fewer cars; little time, less money. Зат есім түрін сәйкестендіру — негіз.

ru.title: few vs a few; little vs a little
ru.html: Артикль меняет тон. a few / a little = малое, но положительное количество (‘some’): I have a few friends here (несколько). few / little (без артикля) = почти нет, негативный оттенок: I have few friends here (едва ли). Этот контраст — любимая точка теста C1.

kk.title: few vs a few; little vs a little
kk.html: Артикль реңкті өзгертеді. a few / a little = аз, бірақ оң мөлшер (‘some’): I have a few friends here (біраз). few / little (артикльсіз) = дерлік жоқ, теріс реңк: I have few friends here (әрең). Бұл қарама-қарсылық — сүйікті C1 тест нүктесі.

ru.title: fewer vs less: прескриптивное правило
ru.html: Традиционно: fewer для исчисляемых (fewer people, fewer items), less для неисчисляемых (less traffic, less sugar). На практике ‘less’ с множественными част в речи (‘less people’), но избегается в тщательном/формальном письме. С измерениями ‘less’ стандартно: less than 10 miles, less than £50.

kk.title: fewer vs less: прескриптив ереже
kk.html: Дәстүрлі: саналатынға fewer (fewer people, fewer items), саналмайтынға less (less traffic, less sugar). Іс жүзінде көпшемен ‘less’ тілде жиі (‘less people’), бірақ мұқият/ресми жазуда қашады. Өлшеммен ‘less’ стандарт: less than 10 miles, less than £50.

ru.title: fewer/less + of; quite a few
ru.html: Перед определителем/местоимением — of: few of the guests, little of the report, fewer of them. quite a few и a good few на деле значат довольно много (не few!): Quite a few people came.

kk.title: fewer/less + of; quite a few
kk.html: Анықтауыш/есімдік алдында of: few of the guests, little of the report, fewer of them. quite a few және a good few шын мәнінде едәуір көп дегенді білдіреді (few емес!): Quite a few people came.

ru.title: Регистр и смягчение
ru.html: few/little (негатив) звучат формально или эмфатично; в речи предпочитают not many/not much или hardly any: There were few objections (формально) / There weren't many objections (нейтрально). a little также смягчает: a little tired, a little more time.

kk.title: Регистр және жұмсарту
kk.html: few/little (теріс) ресми не екпінді естіледі; тілде not many/not much не hardly any-ды қалайды: There were few objections (ресми) / There weren't many objections (бейтарап). a little жұмсартады да: a little tired, a little more time.

ru.title: Ошибки продвинутого уровня
ru.html: ✗ less mistakes (формально) → ✓ fewer mistakes. ✗ I have a little friends → ✓ I have a few friends. ✗ few of water → ✓ little water / a little water.

kk.title: Жоғары деңгей қателері
kk.html: ✗ less mistakes (ресми) → ✓ fewer mistakes. ✗ I have a little friends → ✓ I have a few friends. ✗ few of water → ✓ little water / a little water.

ru.title: Кратко
ru.html:
• few/fewer + исчисляемое множ.; little/less + неисчисляемое.
  • a few/a little = несколько (позитив); few/little = почти нет (негатив).
  • В тщательном письме fewer для исчисляемых, less для неисчисляемых (и измерений).
  • Добавляй ‘of’ перед определителем; ‘quite a few’ значит довольно много.

kk.title: Қысқаша
kk.html:
• few/fewer + саналатын көпше; little/less + саналмайтын.
  • a few/a little = біраз (оң); few/little = дерлік жоқ (теріс).
  • Мұқият жазуда саналатынға fewer, саналмайтынға (және өлшемге) less.
  • Анықтауыш алдында ‘of’ қос; ‘quite a few’ = едәуір көп.

activities.prompt: Sort each noun by the quantifier it takes.
activities.why: few/fewer go with countable plurals; little/less go with uncountable nouns.

items.s: I've got a little money, so let's get a coffee.
Few people understood the lecture, sadly.
There are less mistakes in this draft. (formal writing)
She has a few interest in politics.

activities.why: careful writing uses ‘fewer mistakes’; ‘interest’ is uncountable, so ‘a little interest’, not ‘a few’.
a small but positive amount → a little time.

activities.after: people showed any interest in the scheme. (few/a few)
activities.why: ‘hardly any’, negative slant → few (no article).

activities.after: cyclists on the roads in winter. (fewer/less)
activities.why: ‘cyclists’ is countable → fewer (careful writing).
‘quite a few’ means ‘quite a lot’; ‘of them’ before the pronoun.

activities.q: Which sentence suggests you're pleased to have some?
options: I have few contacts in the industry.
I have a few contacts in the industry.
I have little contact with the industry.
why: ‘few’ (no article) = hardly any, a negative slant.
Correct — ‘a few’ = some, a positive small amount.
‘little contact’ = hardly any, negative.

activities.q: In formal writing: “The new policy resulted in ___ complaints.”
why: ‘less’ with a countable plural is avoided in formal writing.
Correct — ‘complaints’ is countable → fewer.
‘lesser’ means ‘smaller in importance’, not quantity.

activities.prompt: Sort each by its meaning.
activities.why: With the article (a few/a little) = some; without it (few/little) = hardly any.
‘items’ is countable → “fewer than ten items” (the classic supermarket-sign error).

activities.instruction: Rewrite using ‘few’ (no article) to stress ‘hardly any’:
activities.prompt: A few of the students actually did the reading.
activities.answer: few of the students actually did the reading
alts: Few of the students actually did the reading
few of the students did the reading
activities.why: dropping the article gives the negative ‘hardly any’ sense → few of the students.

activities.scene: Choose the correct quantifier and slant.

steps.npc: How was the turnout at the workshop?

options.t: Disappointing — few people bothered to come.
options.why: ‘few’ (no article) conveys the negative ‘hardly any’.

options.t: Disappointing — a few people bothered to come.
options.why: ‘a few’ sounds positive (‘some came’), clashing with ‘disappointing’; use ‘few’.

steps.npc: At least the feedback was good?

options.t: Yes — there were fewer complaints than last time, thankfully.
options.why: ‘complaints’ is countable → fewer.

options.t: Yes — there were less complaints than last time, thankfully.
options.why: in careful speech/writing, countable ‘complaints’ takes ‘fewer’.

activities.why: Match few/fewer to countables and little/less to uncountables, and use the article (a few/a little) for ‘some’ vs no article (few/little) for ‘hardly any’.

learn.title: Defining vs non-defining clauses
learn.html: A defining (restrictive) relative clause is essential — it tells us which one: The man who called was rude. A non-defining clause adds extra, non-essential information, set off by commas: My boss, who is from Cork, is retiring. The comma changes the meaning.

learn.title: who / which / that
learn.html: who for people, which for things, that for either — but only in defining clauses: the book that/which I read; the person who/that helped. that is not used in non-defining clauses: ✗ My car, that is red, … → ✓ My car, which is red, …

learn.title: Omitting the relative pronoun
learn.html: In defining clauses, you can drop the relative pronoun when it's the object: the film (that) I saw; the person (who/whom) I met. You can't omit it when it's the subject: the man who called (not ‘the man called’). Never omit in non-defining clauses.

learn.title: which referring to a whole clause
learn.html: In non-defining clauses, which can refer back to the whole preceding idea: He passed his exams, which delighted his parents. (‘which’ = the whole fact). This is only possible with which, never what: ✗ … what delighted his parents.

learn.title: Register & punctuation
learn.html: Getting commas right is a written-register skill: no commas = defining (restricts the noun); commas = non-defining (adds an aside). Non-defining clauses are more common in formal writing; in speech, we often split into two sentences instead.

learn.title: Advanced learner errors
learn.html:
✗ My sister, that lives in Rome, … → ✓ … who lives in Rome, …

✗ The man who I saw him left. → ✓ The man (who) I saw left (no resumptive ‘him’).

✗ He was late, what annoyed me. → ✓ … , which annoyed me.

learn.title: In a nutshell
learn.html:
• Defining = essential, no commas; non-defining = extra info, with commas.
  • who (people), which (things), that (either) — but not ‘that’ in non-defining clauses.
  • Omit the pronoun only when it's the object of a defining clause.
