  • which (not what) can refer to a whole preceding clause; no resumptive pronoun.

ru.title: Определительные vs не-определительные клаузы
ru.html: Определительная (ограничительная) относительная клауза существенна — говорит который: The man who called was rude. Не-определительная добавляет дополнительную, несущественную информацию, выделяется запятыми: My boss, who is from Cork, is retiring. Запятая меняет смысл.

kk.title: Айқындаушы vs айқындамайтын сөйлемдер
kk.html: Айқындаушы (шектеуші) қатыстық сөйлем маңызды — ол қайсы екенін айтады: The man who called was rude. Айқындамайтын сөйлем қосымша, маңызды емес ақпарат қосады, үтірмен бөлінеді: My boss, who is from Cork, is retiring. Үтір мағынаны өзгертеді.

ru.title: who / which / that
ru.html: who для людей, which для вещей, that для обоих — но только в определительных: the book that/which I read; the person who/that helped. that не используется в не-определительных: ✗ My car, that is red, … → ✓ My car, which is red, …

kk.title: who / which / that
kk.html: Адамға who, затқа which, екеуіне that — бірақ тек айқындаушы сөйлемде: the book that/which I read; the person who/that helped. that айқындамайтын сөйлемде қолданылмайды: ✗ My car, that is red, … → ✓ My car, which is red, …

ru.title: Опущение относительного местоимения
ru.html: В определительных клаузах можно опустить местоимение, когда оно дополнение: the film (that) I saw; the person (who/whom) I met. Нельзя опустить, когда оно подлежащее: the man who called (не ‘the man called’). В не-определительных никогда не опускай.

kk.title: Қатыстық есімдікті түсіру
kk.html: Айқындаушы сөйлемде қатыстық есімдік объект болғанда оны түсіруге болады: the film (that) I saw; the person (who/whom) I met. Ол бастауыш болғанда түсіруге болмайды: the man who called (‘the man called’ емес). Айқындамайтын сөйлемде ешқашан түсірме.

ru.title: which, ссылающееся на всю клаузу
ru.html: В не-определительных which может отсылать к целой предыдущей идее: He passed his exams, which delighted his parents. (‘which’ = весь факт). Возможно только с which, никогда с what: ✗ … what delighted his parents.

kk.title: which бүкіл сөйлемге сілтегенде
kk.html: Айқындамайтын сөйлемде which алдыңғы бүкіл ойға сілтей алады: He passed his exams, which delighted his parents. (‘which’ = бүкіл факт). Бұл тек which-пен мүмкін, ешқашан what-пен емес: ✗ … what delighted his parents.

ru.title: Регистр и пунктуация
ru.html: Верная запятая — навык письменного регистра: без запятых = определительная (ограничивает существительное); с запятыми = не-определительная (добавляет отступление). Не-определительные чаще в формальном письме; в речи мы часто делим на два предложения.

kk.title: Регистр және тыныс белгілері
kk.html: Үтірді дұрыс қою — жазба-регистр дағдысы: үтірсіз = айқындаушы (зат есімді шектейді); үтірмен = айқындамайтын (қосымша ой қосады). Айқындамайтын сөйлемдер ресми жазуда жиірек; тілде оларды екі сөйлемге бөлеміз.

ru.title: Ошибки продвинутого уровня
ru.html: ✗ My sister, that lives in Rome, … → ✓ … who lives in Rome, … ✗ The man who I saw him left → ✓ The man (who) I saw left (без ‘him’). ✗ He was late, what annoyed me → ✓ … , which annoyed me.

kk.title: Жоғары деңгей қателері
kk.html: ✗ My sister, that lives in Rome, … → ✓ … who lives in Rome, … ✗ The man who I saw him left → ✓ The man (who) I saw left (‘him’ қайталанбайды). ✗ He was late, what annoyed me → ✓ … , which annoyed me.

ru.title: Кратко
ru.html:
• Определительная = существенна, без запятых; не-определительная = доп. информация, с запятыми.
  • who (люди), which (вещи), that (оба) — но не ‘that’ в не-определительных.
  • Опускай местоимение только когда оно дополнение определительной клаузы.
  • which (не what) может отсылать к целой предыдущей клаузе; без резумптивного местоимения.

kk.title: Қысқаша
kk.html:
• Айқындаушы = маңызды, үтірсіз; айқындамайтын = қосымша ақпарат, үтірмен.
  • who (адам), which (зат), that (екеуі) — бірақ айқындамайтында ‘that’ емес.
  • Есімдікті тек айқындаушы сөйлемнің объектісі болғанда түсір.
  • which (what емес) бүкіл алдыңғы сөйлемге сілтей алады; қайталама есімдік жоқ.

activities.prompt: Sort each relative clause.

items.t: The woman who lives next door is a vet.
My mother, who is a vet, retires soon.
The book that won the prize sold out.
‘War and Peace’, which is very long, is a classic.
The keys I lost were expensive.
Almaty, which sits below mountains, is beautiful.

activities.why: Defining clauses identify which one (no commas); non-defining clauses add extra information (commas).

items.s: The report that you sent was very clear.
My uncle, who lives abroad, is visiting.
My car, that is quite old, still runs well.
He apologised, what surprised everyone.

activities.why: ‘that’ isn't used in non-defining clauses (use ‘which’), and a clause-referring relative is ‘which’, not ‘what’.

activities.after: attracts millions of visitors, is in St Petersburg. (relative word)
activities.why: a non-defining clause about a thing → which (never ‘that’).
referring to the whole preceding fact → which.

alts: that
activities.why: the relative is the subject, so it can't be omitted → who.
defining clause with the object pronoun omitted (the email (that) I sent).

activities.q: In which sentence can the relative pronoun be omitted?
options: The man who phoned was angry.
The film that we watched was dull.
The dog which bit him ran off.
why: ‘who’ is the subject — can't be dropped.
Correct — ‘that’ is the object of the defining clause, so it can be omitted.
‘which’ is the subject — can't be dropped.

activities.q: Which means you have only one brother?
options: My brother who lives in Paris is a chef.
My brother, who lives in Paris, is a chef.
My brother that lives in Paris is a chef.
why: no commas implies you have more than one brother (specifying which).
Correct — commas make it non-defining: you have one brother, added info about Paris.
no commas again implies more than one brother.

activities.prompt: Sort by what can fill the gap in a DEFINING clause.
activities.why: In defining clauses, who/that refer to people and which/that refer to things.
no resumptive pronoun is needed → “The hotel where we stayed was near the beach.”

activities.instruction: Combine into one sentence with a non-defining clause:
activities.prompt: My neighbour is a pilot. He often flies to Tokyo.
activities.answer: my neighbour, who is a pilot, often flies to Tokyo
alts: My neighbour, who is a pilot, often flies to Tokyo
my neighbour, who is a pilot, often flies to Tokyo.
activities.why: add non-essential info with commas and ‘who’ → my neighbour, who is a pilot, …

steps.npc: Which of your colleagues should I speak to about the budget?

options.t: Talk to Aida, who handles all the finances.
options.why: a non-defining clause adds info about a specific, named person → commas + who.

options.t: Talk to Aida, that handles all the finances.
options.why: non-defining clauses don't use ‘that’; use ‘who’.

steps.npc: And is the figure I was given reliable?

options.t: The figure they quoted was out of date, which caused the confusion.
options.why: ‘which’ refers to the whole preceding fact.

options.t: The figure they quoted was out of date, what caused the confusion.
options.why: use ‘which’, not ‘what’, to refer back to a whole clause.

activities.why: Distinguish defining (no commas) from non-defining (commas) clauses, choose who/which/that correctly, omit only object pronouns in defining clauses, and use ‘which’ for whole-clause reference.

learn.title: Beyond who/which/that
learn.html: Relative clauses also use whose (possession), and the relative adverbs where (place), when (time), why (reason), plus formal connectors like whereby, whereupon, whereas. These link information more economically than a full ‘preposition + which’.

learn.title: whose = of whom/of which
learn.html: whose shows possession, for both people and (formally) things: the author whose book won; a company whose profits doubled. It replaces the clumsy of whom/of which: the man the son of whom = the man whose son.

learn.title: where / when / why
learn.html: These relative adverbs avoid a preposition: the town where I grew up (= in which), the day when we met (= on which), the reason why he left (= for which). After ‘reason’, ‘why’ or ‘that’ or nothing is possible: the reason (why/that) I called.

learn.title: Formal connectors: whereby, whereupon
learn.html: whereby = ‘by which / by means of which’ (a method/system): a scheme whereby costs are shared. whereupon = ‘and then, immediately after which’: He resigned, whereupon chaos ensued. These are markedly formal/literary.

learn.title: Register & precision
learn.html: These words compress meaning elegantly and belong to careful writing. In speech, people often use simpler alternatives: the place where → the place we stayed, whereby → a way of / a system that lets you…. Recognising the formal forms is a strong C1 marker.

learn.title: Advanced learner errors
learn.html:
✗ the man who's car was stolen → ✓ the man whose car was stolen.

✗ the place where I visited → ✓ the place (that/which) I visited (visit takes an object, not ‘where’).

✗ the day where we met → ✓ the day when we met.

learn.title: In a nutshell
learn.html:
• whose = possession (people & things), replacing of whom/of which.
  • where/when/why are relative adverbs = preposition + which.
  • whereby = by which (a method); whereupon = and then — both formal.
  • Use ‘where/when’ only for adverbial place/time, not when the noun is the object.

ru.title: Кроме who/which/that
ru.html: Относительные клаузы также используют whose (принадлежность) и относительные наречия where (место), when (время), why (причина), плюс формальные коннекторы whereby, whereupon, whereas. Они связывают информацию экономнее, чем полное ‘preposition + which’.

kk.title: who/which/that-тан тыс
kk.html: Қатыстық сөйлемдер whose (иелену), және қатыстық үстеулер where (орын), when (уақыт), why (себеп), плюс ресми жалғаулар whereby, whereupon, whereas қолданады. Бұлар толық ‘preposition + which’-тен үнемдірек байланыстырады.

ru.title: whose = of whom/of which
ru.html: whose показывает принадлежность, для людей и (формально) вещей: the author whose book won; a company whose profits doubled. Заменяет неуклюжее of whom/of which: the man the son of whom = the man whose son.

kk.title: whose = of whom/of which
kk.html: whose иеленуді көрсетеді, адам мен (ресми) зат үшін де: the author whose book won; a company whose profits doubled. Ол ебедейсіз of whom/of which-ты алмастырады: the man the son of whom = the man whose son.

ru.title: where / when / why
ru.html: Эти относительные наречия избегают предлога: the town where I grew up (= in which), the day when we met (= on which), the reason why he left (= for which). После ‘reason’ — ‘why’ или ‘that’ или ничего: the reason (why/that) I called.

kk.title: where / when / why
kk.html: Бұл қатыстық үстеулер предлогты болдырмайды: the town where I grew up (= in which), the day when we met (= on which), the reason why he left (= for which). ‘reason’-нан кейін ‘why’ не ‘that’ не ештеңе: the reason (why/that) I called.

ru.title: Формальные коннекторы: whereby, whereupon
ru.html: whereby = ‘by which / посредством чего’ (метод/система): a scheme whereby costs are shared. whereupon = ‘и затем, сразу после чего’: He resigned, whereupon chaos ensued. Это заметно формально/литературно.

kk.title: Ресми жалғаулар: whereby, whereupon
kk.html: whereby = ‘by which / арқылы’ (әдіс/жүйе): a scheme whereby costs are shared. whereupon = ‘содан кейін, дереу кейін’: He resigned, whereupon chaos ensued. Бұлар айқын ресми/әдеби.

ru.title: Регистр и точность
ru.html: Эти слова изящно сжимают смысл и принадлежат тщательному письму. В речи используют проще: the place where → the place we stayed, whereby → a way of / a system that lets you… Узнавание формальных форм — сильный маркер C1.

kk.title: Регистр және дәлдік
kk.html: Бұл сөздер мағынаны талғампаз ықшамдайды әрі мұқият жазуға жатады. Тілде қарапайым баламаларды қолданады: the place where → the place we stayed, whereby → a way of / a system that lets you… Ресми формаларды тану — күшті C1 белгісі.

ru.title: Ошибки продвинутого уровня
ru.html: ✗ the man who's car was stolen → ✓ the man whose car was stolen. ✗ the place where I visited → ✓ the place (that/which) I visited (visit берёт дополнение, не ‘where’). ✗ the day where we met → ✓ the day when we met.

kk.title: Жоғары деңгей қателері
kk.html: ✗ the man who's car was stolen → ✓ the man whose car was stolen. ✗ the place where I visited → ✓ the place (that/which) I visited (visit объект алады, ‘where’ емес). ✗ the day where we met → ✓ the day when we met.

ru.title: Кратко
ru.html:
• whose = принадлежность (люди и вещи), заменяет of whom/of which.
  • where/when/why — относительные наречия = preposition + which.
  • whereby = by which (метод); whereupon = и затем — оба формальны.
  • Используй ‘where/when’ только для наречного места/времени, не когда сущ. — дополнение.

kk.title: Қысқаша
kk.html:
• whose = иелену (адам мен зат), of whom/of which-ты алмастырады.
  • where/when/why — қатыстық үстеулер = preposition + which.
  • whereby = by which (әдіс); whereupon = содан кейін — екеуі ресми.
  • ‘where/when’-ды тек үстеулік орын/уақытқа қолдан, зат есім объект болғанда емес.

activities.typeLabel: Which relative word fits the meaning?
activities.prompt: Match each gap to the relative word type.
activities.why: whose = possession; where/when = place/time adverbs; whereby/whereupon are formal ‘by which / and then’.

items.s: The town where I was born has changed a lot.
She's an artist whose work sells for millions.
That's the restaurant where I recommended.
I'll never forget the day where we met.

activities.why: ‘recommend’ takes an object (the restaurant that I recommended); time needs ‘when’, not ‘where’.
possession of the discovery → whose discovery.
place (= in which) → where.
a method/system ‘by which’ → whereby.
time reference → ‘when’ (= during which).

activities.q: Choose the correct clause: “This is the house ___ my father built.”
why: ‘build’ takes an object (the house), so ‘where’ is wrong here.
Correct — the house is the object of ‘built’ → (that) my father built.
‘when’ is for time, not this object relation.

activities.q: Which sentence correctly uses ‘whose’ for a thing?
options: a theory whose implications are vast
a theory which implications are vast
a theory who's implications are vast
why: Correct — ‘whose’ can show possession for things too.
‘which’ can't show possession like this.
‘who's’ = ‘who is’, not possessive.

activities.prompt: Match each adverb to its full equivalent.
activities.why: where = in/at which; when = on/at which; why = for which — the adverbs replace preposition + which.
possession needs ‘whose’, not ‘who's’ (= who is) → “the author whose latest book won.”

activities.instruction: Combine using ‘whose’:
activities.prompt: I met a woman. Her paintings hang in the National Gallery.
activities.answer: I met a woman whose paintings hang in the National Gallery
alts: I met a woman whose paintings hang in the National Gallery.
a woman whose paintings hang in the National Gallery
activities.why: possession of the paintings → a woman whose paintings…

steps.npc: Can you summarise how the new loyalty programme works?

options.t: It's a system whereby customers earn points on every purchase.
options.why: a method/system ‘by which’ → whereby (formal).

options.t: It's a system where customers earn points on every purchase.
options.why: for a method/means, the precise word is ‘whereby’, not the place adverb ‘where’.

steps.npc: And who came up with it?

options.t: A colleague whose background is in data analytics designed it.
options.why: possession of the background → whose.

options.t: A colleague who's background is in data analytics designed it.
options.why: ‘who's’ means ‘who is’; possession needs ‘whose’.

activities.why: Use whose for possession, where/when/why as relative adverbs (= preposition + which), and reserve whereby/whereupon for formal ‘by which / and then’.

learn.title: Two positions for the preposition
learn.html: When a relative clause contains a verb/adjective that needs a preposition, the preposition can go to the front (before whom/which) or stay stranded at the end: the person to whom I spoke (formal) vs the person who I spoke to (everyday).

learn.title: Fronted: formal, with whom/which
learn.html: The fronted preposition requires whom (people) or which (things) — never who or that: the colleague with whom I work; the tool with which it was made. This is the style of formal and academic writing.

learn.title: Stranded: informal, pronoun often dropped
learn.html: The stranded preposition allows who/that or no pronoun: the person (who/that) I spoke to; the house (which/that) I live in. This is the norm in speech and informal writing. Don't front the preposition and strand it too.

learn.title: Quantifiers: some of whom, most of which
learn.html: In non-defining clauses, a quantifier + of + whom/which is common and formal: the guests, many of whom were strangers; proposals, none of which passed; findings, the details of which follow. This adds precise information about a subset.

learn.title: Register: matching the preposition to the verb
learn.html: The preposition is fixed by the verb/adjective in the clause: rely on → the person on whom I rely; interested in → the subject in which I'm interested; deal with → the issue with which we're dealing. Choosing the right preposition is essential; the position is a register choice.

learn.title: Advanced learner errors
learn.html:
✗ the person who I spoke to him → ✓ the person (who) I spoke to (no resumptive ‘him’).

✗ the man to who I wrote → ✓ the man to whom I wrote.

✗ the topic about that we argued → ✓ the topic about which we argued / … (that) we argued about.

learn.title: In a nutshell
learn.html:
• Preposition fronted (formal, with whom/which) or stranded at the end (informal).
  • Fronted needs whom/which — never who/that; stranded allows who/that or no pronoun.
  • Quantifier + of whom/which (some of whom, most of which) in non-defining clauses.
  • The verb fixes the preposition; the position is a register choice; no resumptive pronoun.

ru.title: Две позиции предлога
ru.html: Когда в относительной клаузе есть глагол/прилагательное, требующее предлог, предлог может идти вперёд (перед whom/which) или оставаться в конце: the person to whom I spoke (формально) / the person who I spoke to (обиходно).

kk.title: Предлогтың екі орны
kk.html: Қатыстық сөйлемде предлог қажет ететін етістік/сын есім болса, предлог алға (whom/which алдында) шыға алады не соңында қалады: the person to whom I spoke (ресми) / the person who I spoke to (күнделікті).

ru.title: Вынесенный вперёд: формально, с whom/which
ru.html: Вынесенный предлог требует whom (люди) или which (вещи) — никогда who или that: the colleague with whom I work; the tool with which it was made. Это стиль формального и академического письма.

kk.title: Алға шыққан: ресми, whom/which-пен
kk.html: Алға шыққан предлог whom (адам) не which (зат) қажет етеді — ешқашан who не that емес: the colleague with whom I work; the tool with which it was made. Бұл — ресми және академиялық жазу стилі.

ru.title: Стрэндинг: неформально, местоимение часто опущено
ru.html: Оставленный в конце предлог допускает who/that или без местоимения: the person (who/that) I spoke to; the house (which/that) I live in. Это норма речи и неформального письма. Не выноси предлог вперёд и не оставляй его в конце одновременно.

kk.title: Соңында қалған: бейресми, есімдік жиі түсіріледі
kk.html: Соңында қалған предлог who/that не есімдіксіз-ге рұқсат етеді: the person (who/that) I spoke to; the house (which/that) I live in. Бұл — тіл мен бейресми жазудың нормасы. Предлогты алға шығарып, сосын соңында да қалдырма.

ru.title: Квантификаторы: some of whom, most of which
ru.html: В не-определительных клаузах квантификатор + of + whom/which част и формален: the guests, many of whom were strangers; proposals, none of which passed; findings, the details of which follow. Добавляет точную информацию о подмножестве.

kk.title: Мөлшерлеуіш: some of whom, most of which
kk.html: Айқындамайтын сөйлемде мөлшерлеуіш + of + whom/which жиі әрі ресми: the guests, many of whom were strangers; proposals, none of which passed; findings, the details of which follow. Бұл ішкі жиын туралы дәл ақпарат қосады.

ru.title: Регистр: согласование предлога с глаголом
ru.html: Предлог задан глаголом/прилагательным в клаузе: rely on → the person on whom I rely; interested in → the subject in which I'm interested; deal with → the issue with which we're dealing. Выбор верного предлога важен; позиция — вопрос регистра.

kk.title: Регистр: предлогты етістікке сәйкестендіру
kk.html: Предлог сөйлемдегі етістік/сын есіммен бекітіледі: rely on → the person on whom I rely; interested in → the subject in which I'm interested; deal with → the issue with which we're dealing. Дұрыс предлогты таңдау маңызды; орны — регистр таңдауы.

ru.title: Ошибки продвинутого уровня
ru.html: ✗ the person who I spoke to him → ✓ the person (who) I spoke to (без ‘him’). ✗ the man to who I wrote → ✓ the man to whom I wrote. ✗ the topic about that we argued → ✓ the topic about which we argued / … (that) we argued about.

kk.title: Жоғары деңгей қателері
kk.html: ✗ the person who I spoke to him → ✓ the person (who) I spoke to (‘him’ қайталанбайды). ✗ the man to who I wrote → ✓ the man to whom I wrote. ✗ the topic about that we argued → ✓ the topic about which we argued / … (that) we argued about.

ru.title: Кратко
ru.html:
• Предлог вперёд (формально, с whom/which) или в конце (неформально).
  • Вынесенный требует whom/which — не who/that; в конце допускает who/that или без местоимения.
  • Квантификатор + of whom/which (some of whom, most of which) в не-определительных.
  • Глагол задаёт предлог; позиция — регистр; без резумптивного местоимения.

kk.title: Қысқаша
kk.html:
• Предлог алға (ресми, whom/which-пен) не соңында (бейресми).
  • Алға — whom/which қажет; соңында who/that не есімдіксіз.
  • Айқындамайтында мөлшерлеуіш + of whom/which (some of whom, most of which).
  • Етістік предлогты бекітеді; орны — регистр; қайталама есімдік жоқ.

activities.prompt: Sort each relative clause by register.

items.t: the client to whom we reported
the tools with which they work
the issue on which we disagree

activities.why: A fronted preposition + whom/which is formal; a stranded preposition (pronoun often dropped) is informal.

items.s: The report, the accuracy of which is disputed, was withdrawn.
the woman to whom I owe everything
the man to who I gave the keys
the friend who I stayed with her

activities.why: a fronted preposition needs ‘whom’ (not ‘who’), and there's no resumptive pronoun (‘with her’).
fronted preposition + person → to whom.
a quantifier referring to a subset of things → none of which.
‘rely on’ + person, fronted → on whom I rely.
stranded preposition with the object pronoun omitted (the chair (that) … about).

activities.q: Formal version of ‘the people who I work with’:
options: the people with who I work
the people with whom I work
the people whom I work with them
why: a fronted preposition needs ‘whom’, not ‘who’.
Correct — fronted preposition + whom.
adds a resumptive ‘them’, which is wrong.

activities.q: Which is correctly formed?
options: the students, many of who passed
the students, many of whom passed
the students, many of them who passed
why: after ‘of’, use ‘whom’, not ‘who’.
Correct — ‘many of whom’ is the standard formal structure.
‘of them who’ is redundant and wrong.

activities.typeLabel: Which preposition does the verb take?
activities.prompt: Match each verb to the preposition needed in the relative clause.
activities.why: The verb fixes the preposition: depend/insist on, believe in, cope with.
the preposition is already fronted, so don't repeat it or add a pronoun → “The charity for which we raised money closed.”

activities.instruction: Rewrite with a fronted preposition and ‘whom/which’:
activities.prompt: That's the professor I did my research under.
activities.answer: that's the professor under whom I did my research
alts: That's the professor under whom I did my research
that's the professor under whom I did my research.
activities.why: front the preposition + whom → the professor under whom I did my research.

activities.typeLabel: Extended context: a formal reference letter

steps.npc: How should I describe my former supervisor in the letter?

options.t: Try: ‘Dr Lee, under whom I trained for three years, …’
options.why: fronted preposition + whom suits a formal letter.

options.t: Try: ‘Dr Lee, who I trained under her for three years, …’
options.why: drop the resumptive ‘her’; formal style prefers ‘under whom I trained’.

steps.npc: And the projects I worked on?

options.t: ‘…several projects, two of which won awards.’
options.why: quantifier + of which for a subset, in a non-defining clause.

options.t: ‘…several projects, two of them which won awards.’
options.why: use ‘two of which’, not the redundant ‘two of them which’.

activities.why: Front the preposition with whom/which for formal register or strand it (dropping the pronoun) for informal, match the preposition to the verb, and use quantifier + of whom/which — never a resumptive pronoun.

learn.title: Apposition: naming again in a phrase
learn.html: Apposition places a second noun phrase next to the first to rename or explain it, without a relative pronoun or verb: Almaty, Kazakhstan's largest city, sits below mountains. · My friend Dana is a doctor. It's a compact alternative to a non-defining clause (‘…, which is…’).

learn.title: Non-restrictive vs restrictive apposition
learn.html: With commas (non-restrictive), the phrase is extra: Chekhov, a Russian writer, … Without commas (restrictive), it identifies: the poet Keats; my brother Tom (specifying which brother). The comma choice mirrors defining vs non-defining clauses.

learn.title: Reduced from a relative clause
learn.html: Apposition often reduces a non-defining clause: Ann, who is a lawyer, → Ann, a lawyer,. Dropping ‘who is / which is’ tightens the sentence — a key move for concise, formal writing.

learn.title: Titles, roles & explanatory phrases
learn.html: Common patterns: role/description (Dr Kim, head of surgery), definitions (photosynthesis, the process by which plants make food), and examples (several cities — Astana, Almaty and Shymkent — …). These pack information efficiently before or after the head noun.

learn.title: Register & punctuation choices
learn.html: Non-restrictive appositives are set off by commas, dashes or brackets, in rising order of separation: …, a former minister, … / … — a former minister — … / … (a former minister) …. Dashes feel more emphatic; brackets more of an aside. This is a written-style skill.

learn.title: Advanced learner errors
learn.html:
✗ My friend, Dana, is a doctor (if you have several friends) → ✓ no commas: My friend Dana… (identifying).

✗ Chekhov who a writer… → ✓ Chekhov, a writer, … (drop ‘who is’).

✗ Missing the closing comma: Ann, a lawyer works here → ✓ Ann, a lawyer, works here.

learn.title: In a nutshell
learn.html:
• Apposition renames a noun with a second noun phrase, no relative pronoun/verb.
  • Commas = extra info (non-restrictive); no commas = identifying (restrictive).
  • It reduces non-defining clauses (…, who is a lawyer → …, a lawyer).
  • Use commas, dashes or brackets; always close a non-restrictive appositive.

ru.title: Аппозиция: повторное называние фразой
ru.html: Аппозиция ставит вторую именную группу рядом с первой, чтобы переименовать или объяснить, без относительного местоимения и глагола: Almaty, Kazakhstan's largest city, sits below mountains. My friend Dana is a doctor. Компактная альтернатива не-определительной клаузе (‘…, which is…’).

kk.title: Аппозиция: тіркеспен қайта атау
kk.html: Аппозиция екінші есімді тіркесті біріншісінің қасына қойып, оны қайта атайды не түсіндіреді, қатыстық есімдіксіз әрі етістіксіз: Almaty, Kazakhstan's largest city, sits below mountains. My friend Dana is a doctor. Бұл — айқындамайтын сөйлемнің (‘…, which is…’) ықшам баламасы.

ru.title: Нерестриктивная vs рестриктивная аппозиция
ru.html: С запятыми (нерестриктивная) фраза добавочная: Chekhov, a Russian writer, … Без запятых (рестриктивная) она идентифицирует: the poet Keats; my brother Tom (уточняя, какой брат). Выбор запятой отражает определительные vs не-определительные клаузы.

kk.title: Шектемейтін vs шектеуші аппозиция
kk.html: Үтірмен (шектемейтін) тіркес қосымша: Chekhov, a Russian writer, … Үтірсіз (шектеуші) ол айқындайды: the poet Keats; my brother Tom (қай ағаны нақтылап). Үтір таңдауы айқындаушы vs айқындамайтын сөйлемдерді қайталайды.

ru.title: Сокращение из относительной клаузы
ru.html: Аппозиция часто сокращает не-определительную клаузу: Ann, who is a lawyer, → Ann, a lawyer,. Опущение ‘who is / which is’ уплотняет предложение — ключевой приём краткого, формального письма.

kk.title: Қатыстық сөйлемнен қысқарту
kk.html: Аппозиция жиі айқындамайтын сөйлемді қысқартады: Ann, who is a lawyer, → Ann, a lawyer,. ‘who is / which is’-ты түсіру сөйлемді ықшамдайды — қысқа, ресми жазудың басты тәсілі.

ru.title: Титулы, роли, поясняющие фразы
ru.html: Частые модели: роль/описание (Dr Kim, head of surgery), определения (photosynthesis, the process by which plants make food), примеры (several cities — Astana, Almaty and Shymkent — …). Они эффективно упаковывают информацию до или после главного существительного.

kk.title: Атаулар, рөлдер, түсіндірме тіркестер
kk.html: Жиі үлгілер: рөл/сипаттама (Dr Kim, head of surgery), анықтамалар (photosynthesis, the process by which plants make food), мысалдар (several cities — Astana, Almaty and Shymkent — …). Бұлар бас зат есім алдында не кейін ақпаратты тиімді жинайды.

ru.title: Регистр и выбор пунктуации
ru.html: Нерестриктивные аппозитивы выделяются запятыми, тире или скобками, по возрастанию обособления: …, a former minister, … / … — a former minister — … / … (a former minister) … Тире эмфатичнее; скобки — больше отступление. Это навык письменного стиля.

kk.title: Регистр және тыныс белгісі таңдауы
kk.html: Шектемейтін аппозитивтер үтір, тире не жақшамен бөлінеді, бөліну дәрежесі өсе береді: …, a former minister, … / … — a former minister — … / … (a former minister) … Тире екпіндірек; жақша көбірек шегініс. Бұл — жазба-стиль дағдысы.

ru.title: Ошибки продвинутого уровня
ru.html: ✗ My friend, Dana, is a doctor (если друзей несколько) → ✓ без запятых: My friend Dana… (идентификация). ✗ Chekhov who a writer… → ✓ Chekhov, a writer, … (опусти ‘who is’). ✗ Нет закрывающей запятой: Ann, a lawyer works here → ✓ Ann, a lawyer, works here.

kk.title: Жоғары деңгей қателері
kk.html: ✗ My friend, Dana, is a doctor (бірнеше досың болса) → ✓ үтірсіз: My friend Dana… (айқындаушы). ✗ Chekhov who a writer… → ✓ Chekhov, a writer, … (‘who is’-ты түсір). ✗ Жабатын үтір жоқ: Ann, a lawyer works here → ✓ Ann, a lawyer, works here.

ru.title: Кратко
ru.html:
• Аппозиция переименовывает существительное второй фразой, без местоимения/глагола.
  • Запятые = доп. информация (нерестриктивная); без запятых = идентификация (рестриктивная).
  • Сокращает не-определительные клаузы (…, who is a lawyer → …, a lawyer).
  • Используй запятые, тире или скобки; всегда закрывай нерестриктивный аппозитив.

kk.title: Қысқаша
kk.html:
• Аппозиция зат есімді екінші тіркеспен қайта атайды, қатыстық есімдік/етістіксіз.
  • Үтір = қосымша ақпарат (шектемейтін); үтірсіз = айқындаушы (шектеуші).
  • Ол айқындамайтын сөйлемдерді қысқартады (…, who is a lawyer → …, a lawyer).
  • Үтір, тире не жақша қолдан; шектемейтін аппозитивті әрдайым жап.

activities.prompt: Sort each appositive.

items.t: my colleague, an expert in tax, …

activities.why: Non-restrictive appositives (commas) add extra info; restrictive ones (no commas) identify which one.

items.s: Darwin, the naturalist, changed biology.
My friend Ali speaks five languages.
Darwin the naturalist, changed biology.
My friend, Ali speaks five languages.

activities.why: a non-restrictive appositive needs a comma on both sides; an identifying one needs none.

activities.after: , will chair the meeting. (who is our finance director → appositive)
alts: a finance director
activities.why: drop ‘who is’ to make an appositive → Maria, our finance director, …

activities.after: , erodes savings. (a sustained rise in prices)
activities.why: an explanatory noun phrase renaming ‘inflation’ → , a sustained rise in prices,
a restrictive appositive identifies which poet, with no commas → the poet Rumi.
apposition renaming Tokyo (commas would be added in writing).

activities.q: You have three sisters. Which correctly identifies one?
options: My sister, Lena, is a pilot.
My sister Lena is a pilot.
My sister Lena, is a pilot.
why: commas imply you have only one sister (extra info).
Correct — no commas identifies which sister (restrictive).
the stray single comma is ungrammatical.

activities.q: Which is the tightest way to add the information?
options: Orwell, who was a writer who wrote ‘1984’, criticised totalitarianism.
Orwell, the author of ‘1984’, criticised totalitarianism.
Orwell criticised totalitarianism and he wrote ‘1984’ too.
why: wordy and repetitive.
Correct — an appositive tightly packs the information.
loosely tacked on with ‘and’.

activities.prompt: Sort each by the effect of the punctuation.

items.t: Sara, a keen runner, joined us.
The result — a total disaster — shocked everyone.
Berlin, the German capital, is vibrant.
He said one word — sorry — and left.

activities.why: Commas give a neutral aside; dashes give a more emphatic, dramatic break.
a non-restrictive appositive needs a closing comma → “Nabokov, the author of ‘Lolita’, was also…”.

activities.instruction: Rewrite the relative clause as an appositive phrase:
activities.prompt: Dr Adilova, who is a leading cardiologist, will speak first.
activities.answer: Dr Adilova, a leading cardiologist, will speak first
alts: Dr Adilova, a leading cardiologist, will speak first.
Dr Adilova, a leading cardiologist,
activities.why: drop ‘who is’ → Dr Adilova, a leading cardiologist, …

steps.npc: How should I introduce our guest in the programme?

options.t: Write: ‘Professor Idris, a specialist in renewable energy, will open the session.’
options.why: a non-restrictive appositive with commas on both sides.

options.t: Write: ‘Professor Idris a specialist in renewable energy will open the session.’
options.why: a non-restrictive appositive must be set off with commas.

steps.npc: And there are two Dr Khans — how do I show which?

options.t: Use no commas: ‘Dr Khan the surgeon will present the case.’
options.why: a restrictive appositive (no commas) identifies which Dr Khan.

options.t: Use commas: ‘Dr Khan, the surgeon, will present the case.’
options.why: with two Dr Khans you need the identifying (restrictive) form without commas.

activities.why: Use apposition to rename a noun concisely, with commas for extra info (non-restrictive) and none for identifying (restrictive), reducing non-defining clauses where possible.

learn.title: Postmodifying with prepositional phrases
learn.html: A prepositional phrase after a noun adds information, often replacing a relative clause: the book on the table (= that is on the table), a man of great skill, the road to the coast, concern about costs. This ‘postmodifier’ is compact and very frequent.

learn.title: Reduced relative clauses
learn.html: Many prepositional postmodifiers are reduced relatives (drop ‘which/who is’): the woman (who is) in the blue coat; the items (which are) on sale. Keeping only the phrase makes writing leaner without losing meaning.

learn.title: of-phrases: many relationships
learn.html: of expresses possession, part, quality, quantity and content: the roof of the house, a piece of advice, a woman of courage, a cup of tea, the idea of freedom. The ‘s possessive and noun + noun compounds often compete with it.

learn.title: Stacking & avoiding ambiguity
learn.html: Multiple postmodifiers can stack, but watch for ambiguous attachment: the girl in the car with the dog — who has the dog? Reorder or repunctuate for clarity: the girl with the dog, in the car. Skilled writers control which noun each phrase modifies.

learn.title: Register & noun + preposition collocations
learn.html: Nouns take fixed prepositions: a solution to, an increase in, a reason for, an effect on, an interest in, the cause of. Choosing the right one is a C1 accuracy point; these collocations are learned per noun, not by rule.

learn.title: Advanced learner errors
learn.html:
✗ a solution of the problem → ✓ a solution to the problem.

✗ an increase of unemployment → ✓ an increase in unemployment.

✗ the reason of the delay → ✓ the reason for the delay.

learn.title: In a nutshell
learn.html:
• Prepositional phrases postmodify nouns, often reducing a relative clause.
  • of expresses possession/part/quality/quantity/content.
  • Watch stacked phrases for ambiguous attachment; reorder for clarity.
  • Learn noun + preposition collocations (solution to, increase in, reason for).

ru.title: Постмодификация предложной фразой
ru.html: Предложная фраза после существительного добавляет информацию, часто заменяя относительную клаузу: the book on the table (= that is on the table), a man of great skill, the road to the coast, concern about costs. Этот ‘постмодификатор’ компактен и очень част.

kk.title: Предлогтық тіркеспен постмодификация
kk.html: Зат есімнен кейінгі предлогтық тіркес ақпарат қосады, жиі қатыстық сөйлемді алмастырады: the book on the table (= that is on the table), a man of great skill, the road to the coast, concern about costs. Бұл ‘постмодификатор’ ықшам әрі өте жиі.

ru.title: Сокращённые относительные клаузы
ru.html: Многие предложные постмодификаторы — сокращённые относительные (опусти ‘which/who is’): the woman (who is) in the blue coat; the items (which are) on sale. Оставляя только фразу, делаешь письмо лаконичнее без потери смысла.

kk.title: Қысқартылған қатыстық сөйлемдер
kk.html: Көп предлогтық постмодификатор — қысқартылған қатыстық сөйлем (‘which/who is’-ты түсір): the woman (who is) in the blue coat; the items (which are) on sale. Тек тіркесті қалдыру мағынаны жоғалтпай жазуды ықшамдайды.

ru.title: of-фразы: много отношений
ru.html: of выражает принадлежность, часть, качество, количество и содержание: the roof of the house, a piece of advice, a woman of courage, a cup of tea, the idea of freedom. ‘s притяжательный и сущ. + сущ. компаунды часто конкурируют с ним.

kk.title: of-тіркестер: көп қатынас
kk.html: of иелену, бөлік, сапа, мөлшер, мазмұнды білдіреді: the roof of the house, a piece of advice, a woman of courage, a cup of tea, the idea of freedom. ‘s тәуелдік пен зат есім + зат есім компаундтары онымен жиі бәсекелеседі.

ru.title: Наслоение и избегание двусмысленности
ru.html: Несколько постмодификаторов могут наслаиваться, но следи за двусмысленным присоединением: the girl in the car with the dog — у кого собака? Переставь или перепунктуируй ради ясности: the girl with the dog, in the car. Умелые авторы контролируют, какое существительное модифицирует каждая фраза.

kk.title: Жинау және екіұштылықтан қашу
kk.html: Бірнеше постмодификатор жиналуы мүмкін, бірақ екіұшты байланысқа абай бол: the girl in the car with the dog — итті кім ұстап тұр? Анықтық үшін ретте не тыныс белгісін өзгерт: the girl with the dog, in the car. Шебер жазушылар әр тіркес қай зат есімді анықтайтынын бақылайды.

ru.title: Регистр и сочетаемость сущ. + предлог
ru.html: Существительные берут фиксированные предлоги: a solution to, an increase in, a reason for, an effect on, an interest in, the cause of. Выбор верного — точка точности C1; эти сочетания учатся по существительному, не по правилу.

kk.title: Регистр және зат есім + предлог тіркесімі
kk.html: Зат есімдер тұрақты предлог алады: a solution to, an increase in, a reason for, an effect on, an interest in, the cause of. Дұрысын таңдау — C1 дәлдік тұсы; бұл тіркесімдер ережемен емес, әр зат есімге үйреніледі.

ru.title: Ошибки продвинутого уровня
ru.html: ✗ a solution of the problem → ✓ a solution to the problem. ✗ an increase of unemployment → ✓ an increase in unemployment. ✗ the reason of the delay → ✓ the reason for the delay.

kk.title: Жоғары деңгей қателері
kk.html: ✗ a solution of the problem → ✓ a solution to the problem. ✗ an increase of unemployment → ✓ an increase in unemployment. ✗ the reason of the delay → ✓ the reason for the delay.

ru.title: Кратко
ru.html:
• Предложные фразы постмодифицируют существительные, часто сокращая относительную клаузу.
  • of выражает принадлежность/часть/качество/количество/содержание.
  • Следи за двусмысленным присоединением наслоённых фраз; переставляй ради ясности.
  • Учи сочетания сущ. + предлог (solution to, increase in, reason for).

kk.title: Қысқаша
kk.html:
• Предлогтық тіркестер зат есімді постмодификациялайды, жиі қатыстық сөйлемді қысқартады.
  • of иелену/бөлік/сапа/мөлшер/мазмұнды білдіреді.
  • Жиналған тіркестерде екіұшты байланысқа абай бол; анықтық үшін ретте.
  • Зат есім + предлог тіркесімдерін үйрен (solution to, increase in, reason for).

activities.typeLabel: Which preposition does the noun take?
activities.prompt: Match each noun to its preposition.
activities.why: solution/answer to; rise/interest in; reason/need for — fixed noun + preposition collocations.

items.s: There's been a sharp increase in demand.
They found a solution to the dispute.
The cause for the fire is unknown.
He has no interest of politics.

activities.why: the correct collocations are ‘the cause of’ and ‘interest in’.
a prepositional postmodifier (= who were on) → the passengers on the flight.
‘effect of X on Y’ → the effect of caffeine.
‘of + quality’ describes the noun → a leader of great integrity.
stacked prepositional postmodifiers (at the end / of the street) locating the house.

activities.q: ‘the report on the shelf about the merger’ is ambiguous. Which rewrite is clearest if the report is ABOUT the merger?
options: the report about the merger, on the shelf
the report on the shelf, about the merger
the merger report on the shelf about it
why: Correct — putting ‘about the merger’ next to ‘report’ makes the topic clear.
still risks attaching ‘about the merger’ to ‘shelf’.
clumsy and still unclear.

activities.q: Which is the most natural way to express ‘the door belonging to the car’?
options: the car's door / the car door
why: grammatical but less natural for a part of an object.
Correct — for a part of a thing, ‘the car door’ (compound) is the natural choice.
‘from’ wrongly implies removal/origin.

activities.prompt: Sort each ‘of’ phrase by its relationship.
activities.why: ‘of’ can express a part/possession (leg of, mayor of) or a quality/quantity (of honour, of water).
‘solution’ takes ‘to’ → “a solution to the housing shortage.”

activities.instruction: Rewrite the relative clause as a prepositional postmodifier:
activities.prompt: The people who were at the meeting all agreed.
activities.answer: the people at the meeting all agreed
alts: The people at the meeting all agreed
the people at the meeting all agreed.
activities.why: drop ‘who were’ → the people at the meeting all agreed.

steps.npc: What's the main finding of the study?

options.t: A clear rise in stress levels among remote workers.
options.why: ‘a rise in’ is the correct collocation.

options.t: A clear rise of stress levels among remote workers.
options.why: the collocation is ‘a rise in’, not ‘a rise of’.

steps.npc: Does it propose anything?

options.t: Yes — a practical solution to the problem of burnout.
options.why: ‘solution to’ and ‘the problem of’ are both correct collocations.

options.t: Yes — a practical solution of the problem for burnout.
options.why: it's ‘a solution to the problem of burnout’, not ‘solution of … for’.

activities.why: Postmodify nouns with prepositional phrases (often reduced relatives), control stacking to avoid ambiguity, and use the correct noun + preposition collocation.

learn.title: Participle clauses: compressing two ideas
learn.html: A participle clause uses an -ing or -ed form to attach a second action/reason to the main clause, without a conjunction: Feeling tired, she went to bed. · Shocked by the news, he sat down. It compresses ‘Because/When/As…’ into a phrase.

learn.title: -ing (active) vs -ed (passive)
learn.html: -ing clauses have an active meaning (the subject does it): Opening the door, she saw… -ed clauses have a passive meaning (the subject receives it): Built in 1900, the house… (= which was built). Choose by whether the subject acts or is acted upon.

learn.title: Adverbial meanings: time, reason, result
learn.html: Participle clauses express: time (Arriving home, I made tea = when I arrived), reason (Not knowing the way, we got lost = because we didn't), and result (The bomb exploded, killing ten people = and killed).

learn.title: Same subject — the golden rule
learn.html: The participle's implied subject must be the same as the main clause's subject. Walking home, I saw a fox (I walked, I saw ✓). A dangling participle mismatches them: ✗ Walking home, the fox appeared (the fox wasn't walking!). This is a top C1 accuracy point.

learn.title: Register: concise, formal, literary
learn.html: Participle clauses tighten writing and are common in narrative and formal prose. Adding a conjunction clarifies the meaning: When writing, keep it clear. · Once finished, submit it. Overusing them, or dangling them, marks weaker writing.

learn.title: Advanced learner errors
learn.html:
✗ Being tired, the film sent me to sleep. (dangling) → ✓ Being tired, I fell asleep during the film.

✗ Written in haste, I made mistakes (the letter was written, not I) → ✓ Written in haste, the letter had errors.

✗ Arrived home, … → ✓ Arriving home, … (active → -ing).

learn.title: In a nutshell
learn.html:
• Participle clauses attach a second idea with -ing (active) or -ed (passive).
  • They express time, reason or result, replacing when/because/and.
  • The implied subject must match the main clause's subject (avoid dangling).
  • Concise and formal; add a conjunction (when/once) to clarify meaning.

ru.title: Причастные обороты: сжатие двух идей
ru.html: Причастный оборот использует -ing или -ed форму, чтобы присоединить второе действие/причину к главной клаузе без союза: Feeling tired, she went to bed. Shocked by the news, he sat down. Сжимает ‘Because/When/As…’ во фразу.

kk.title: Есімшелі сөйлемдер: екі ойды ықшамдау
kk.html: Есімшелі сөйлем -ing не -ed форманы қолданып, екінші әрекет/себепті басты сөйлемге жалғаулықсыз тіркейді: Feeling tired, she went to bed. Shocked by the news, he sat down. Ол ‘Because/When/As…’-ты тіркеске ықшамдайды.

ru.title: -ing (актив) vs -ed (пассив)
ru.html: -ing обороты имеют активное значение (подлежащее делает): Opening the door, she saw… -ed обороты — пассивное (подлежащее получает): Built in 1900, the house… (= which was built). Выбирай по тому, действует подлежащее или на него действуют.

kk.title: -ing (актив) vs -ed (пассив)
kk.html: -ing сөйлемдер актив мағыналы (бастауыш істейді): Opening the door, she saw… -ed сөйлемдер пассив мағыналы (бастауыш қабылдайды): Built in 1900, the house… (= which was built). Бастауыш істей ме әлде оған әсер ете ме — соған қарай таңда.

ru.title: Наречные значения: время, причина, результат
ru.html: Причастные обороты выражают: время (Arriving home, I made tea = когда пришёл), причину (Not knowing the way, we got lost = так как не знали), и результат (The bomb exploded, killing ten people = и убил).

kk.title: Үстеулік мағыналар: уақыт, себеп, нәтиже
kk.html: Есімшелі сөйлемдер: уақыт (Arriving home, I made tea = келгенде), себеп (Not knowing the way, we got lost = білмегендіктен), және нәтиже (The bomb exploded, killing ten people = және өлтірді) білдіреді.

ru.title: Одинаковое подлежащее — золотое правило
ru.html: Подразумеваемое подлежащее причастия должно совпадать с подлежащим главной клаузы. Walking home, I saw a fox (я шёл, я увидел ✓). Висячее причастие их рассогласовывает: ✗ Walking home, the fox appeared (лиса не шла!). Это главная точка точности C1.

kk.title: Бірдей бастауыш — алтын ереже
kk.html: Есімшенің жасырын бастауышы басты сөйлемнің бастауышымен бірдей болуы керек. Walking home, I saw a fox (мен жүрдім, мен көрдім ✓). Ілініп қалған есімше оларды сәйкессіздендіреді: ✗ Walking home, the fox appeared (түлкі жүрген жоқ!). Бұл — басты C1 дәлдік тұсы.

ru.title: Регистр: лаконичный, формальный, литературный
ru.html: Причастные обороты уплотняют письмо и часты в повествовании и формальной прозе. Добавление союза уточняет смысл: When writing, keep it clear. Once finished, submit it. Злоупотребление ими или висячие причастия — признак слабого письма.

kk.title: Регистр: ықшам, ресми, әдеби
kk.html: Есімшелі сөйлемдер жазуды ықшамдайды әрі әңгіме мен ресми прозада жиі. Жалғаулық қосу мағынаны нақтылайды: When writing, keep it clear. Once finished, submit it. Оларды артық қолдану не ілініп қалдыру әлсіз жазуды көрсетеді.

ru.title: Ошибки продвинутого уровня
ru.html: ✗ Being tired, the film sent me to sleep (висячее) → ✓ Being tired, I fell asleep during the film. ✗ Written in haste, I made mistakes (письмо написано, не я) → ✓ Written in haste, the letter had errors. ✗ Arrived home, … → ✓ Arriving home, … (актив → -ing).

kk.title: Жоғары деңгей қателері
kk.html: ✗ Being tired, the film sent me to sleep (ілінген) → ✓ Being tired, I fell asleep during the film. ✗ Written in haste, I made mistakes (хат жазылды, мен емес) → ✓ Written in haste, the letter had errors. ✗ Arrived home, … → ✓ Arriving home, … (актив → -ing).

ru.title: Кратко
ru.html:
• Причастные обороты присоединяют вторую идею через -ing (актив) или -ed (пассив).
  • Выражают время, причину или результат, заменяя when/because/and.
  • Подразумеваемое подлежащее должно совпадать с подлежащим главной клаузы (избегай висячих).
  • Лаконичны и формальны; добавляй союз (when/once) для ясности.

kk.title: Қысқаша
kk.html:
• Есімшелі сөйлемдер екінші ойды -ing (актив) не -ed (пассив)-пен тіркейді.
  • Олар уақыт, себеп не нәтиже білдіреді, when/because/and-ты алмастырады.
  • Жасырын бастауыш басты сөйлемнің бастауышымен сәйкес болуы керек (ілінуден қаш).
  • Ықшам әрі ресми; мағынаны нақтылауға жалғаулық (when/once) қос.

activities.prompt: Sort each participle clause by voice.

items.t: Exhausted by the climb, they rested.
Written in Latin, the text is hard.
Not knowing what to say, I left.
Damaged in transit, the goods were returned.

activities.why: -ing clauses are active (the subject acts); -ed clauses are passive (the subject is acted upon).

items.s: Feeling nervous, I forgot my lines.
Built on a hill, the castle dominates the town.
Running for the bus, my keys fell out.
Left alone, the noise frightened the child.

activities.why: the participle's subject must match the main clause: keys weren't running; the noise wasn't left alone (the child was).

activities.after: what to do, she asked for help. (not know)
activities.why: a reason clause, active → Not knowing what to do.
passive meaning (= having been built) → Built in 1889.
a result following the main event → causing widespread damage.
time/action participle clause with the same subject (we turned, we saw).

activities.q: Complete correctly: “Having finished the exam, ___.”
why: the results didn't finish the exam.
Correct — ‘the students’ finished the exam and left, so the subject matches.
‘it’ didn't finish the exam.

activities.q: “Being a holiday, the shops were shut.” The participle clause expresses:
why: it's not primarily a time reference.
Correct — ‘Being a holiday’ = because it was a holiday (reason).
the result is the shops being shut, but the clause itself gives the reason.

activities.prompt: Classify each participle clause.

items.t: The dam burst, flooding the valley.
Finishing early, we went for a walk.

activities.why: ‘Seeing/Finishing’ = when; ‘Being ill’ = because; ‘flooding’ = and so flooded (result).
dangling participle — the dog wasn't waiting → “Waiting at the bus stop, I was approached by a dog.”

activities.instruction: Combine using an -ing participle clause:
activities.prompt: She didn't want to wake anyone. She crept upstairs quietly.
activities.answer: not wanting to wake anyone, she crept upstairs quietly
alts: Not wanting to wake anyone, she crept upstairs quietly
not wanting to wake anyone, she crept upstairs
activities.why: a reason clause, active, same subject → Not wanting to wake anyone, she crept upstairs.

steps.npc: So how did you end up locked out?

options.t: Rushing to leave, I left the keys inside.
options.why: active -ing clause, same subject (I rushed, I left).

options.t: Rushing to leave, the keys ended up inside.
options.why: dangling — the keys weren't rushing; keep ‘I’ as the subject.

steps.npc: Couldn't the landlord help?

options.t: Called at midnight, he wasn't best pleased.
options.why: passive -ed clause (he was called), subject matches.

options.t: Calling at midnight, he wasn't best pleased.
options.why: if he was the one called, use the passive ‘Called at midnight’, not active ‘Calling’.

activities.why: Use -ing (active) and -ed (passive) participle clauses for time/reason/result, always keeping the implied subject the same as the main clause's.

learn.title: Perfect, passive & conjunction-led participles
learn.html: Building on the basics, this unit covers perfect participles (Having done…), being + -ed passives, participles after conjunctions/prepositions (when, after, before, on, without, by), and ‘with + noun + participle’ absolutes.

learn.title: Perfect participle: Having + past participle
learn.html: Having + pp shows the action was completed before the main clause — clarifying sequence: Having finished lunch, we set off. · Having been warned, they were careful. Use it (not the plain -ing) when the ‘before’ relationship matters.

learn.title: Participles after conjunctions & prepositions
learn.html: Add a conjunction/preposition for precision: After leaving school… · Before deciding… · On arriving (= as soon as)… · Without saying a word… · By working hard… · When heated, metal expands. These fix the exact adverbial meaning.

learn.title: ‘with’ + noun + participle (absolute)
learn.html: An absolute clause lets the participle have its own subject, often with with: With the door locked, we felt safe. · With prices rising, people are cutting back. · She sat, her eyes closed. This adds a simultaneous circumstance.

learn.title: Register & discourse economy
learn.html: These structures are hallmarks of sophisticated, formal writing — they layer background, cause and timing without extra clauses. They must still obey the same-subject rule (except absolutes, which supply their own subject). Precision and economy are the payoff.

learn.title: Advanced learner errors
learn.html:
✗ Finishing the report, I sent it (if clearly before) → better Having finished the report, I sent it.

✗ With the sun was setting, … → ✓ With the sun setting, … (no finite verb).

✗ After to arrive… → ✓ After arriving… (prepositions take -ing).

learn.title: In a nutshell
learn.html:
• Having + pp = completed before the main action (clarifies sequence).
  • Use conjunctions/prepositions (after/before/on/without/by/when) + -ing/-ed for precision.
  • ‘with + noun + participle’ is an absolute clause with its own subject.
  • Keep the same subject (except absolutes); prize precision and economy.

ru.title: Перфектные, пассивные и союзные причастия
ru.html: Опираясь на основы, этот раздел охватывает перфектные причастия (Having done…), being + -ed пассивы, причастия после союзов/предлогов (when, after, before, on, without, by), и ‘with + сущ. + причастие’ абсолюты.

kk.title: Perfect, пассив және жалғаулықты есімшелер
kk.html: Негіздерге сүйене, бұл бөлім perfect есімшелерді (Having done…), being + -ed пассивтерді, жалғаулық/предлогтан кейінгі есімшелерді (when, after, before, on, without, by), және ‘with + зат есім + есімше’ абсолюттерін қамтиды.

ru.title: Перфектное причастие: Having + past participle
ru.html: Having + pp показывает, что действие завершено до главной клаузы — уточняя последовательность: Having finished lunch, we set off. Having been warned, they were careful. Используй его (не простое -ing), когда важно отношение ‘до’.

kk.title: Perfect есімше: Having + past participle
kk.html: Having + pp әрекеттің басты сөйлемнен бұрын аяқталғанын көрсетеді — тізбекті нақтылап: Having finished lunch, we set off. Having been warned, they were careful. ‘Бұрын’ қатынасы маңызды болғанда оны (жай -ing емес) қолдан.

ru.title: Причастия после союзов и предлогов
ru.html: Для точности добавляй союз/предлог: After leaving school… Before deciding… On arriving (= как только)… Without saying a word… By working hard… When heated, metal expands. Они фиксируют точное наречное значение.

kk.title: Жалғаулық пен предлогтан кейінгі есімшелер
kk.html: Дәлдік үшін жалғаулық/предлог қос: After leaving school… Before deciding… On arriving (= сол сәтте)… Without saying a word… By working hard… When heated, metal expands. Бұлар нақты үстеулік мағынаны бекітеді.

ru.title: ‘with’ + сущ. + причастие (абсолют)
ru.html: Абсолютный оборот даёт причастию своё подлежащее, часто с with: With the door locked, we felt safe. With prices rising, people are cutting back. She sat, her eyes closed. Добавляет одновременное обстоятельство.

kk.title: ‘with’ + зат есім + есімше (абсолют)
kk.html: Абсолют сөйлем есімшеге өз бастауышын береді, жиі with-пен: With the door locked, we felt safe. With prices rising, people are cutting back. She sat, her eyes closed. Бұл қатар жүретін жағдайды қосады.

ru.title: Регистр и экономия дискурса
ru.html: Эти конструкции — черта изощрённого, формального письма — они наслаивают фон, причину и время без лишних клауз. Они всё же должны подчиняться правилу одинакового подлежащего (кроме абсолютов, дающих своё подлежащее). Точность и экономия — награда.

kk.title: Регистр және дискурс үнемі
kk.html: Бұл құрылымдар күрделі, ресми жазудың белгісі — олар фон, себеп, уақытты артық сөйлемсіз қабаттайды. Олар әлі де бірдей-бастауыш ережесіне бағынуы керек (өз бастауышын беретін абсолюттерден басқа). Дәлдік пен үнем — олжасы.

ru.title: Ошибки продвинутого уровня
ru.html: ✗ Finishing the report, I sent it (если явно до) → лучше Having finished the report, I sent it. ✗ With the sun was setting, … → ✓ With the sun setting, … (без финитного глагола). ✗ After to arrive… → ✓ After arriving… (предлоги берут -ing).

kk.title: Жоғары деңгей қателері
kk.html: ✗ Finishing the report, I sent it (анық бұрын болса) → жақсырақ Having finished the report, I sent it. ✗ With the sun was setting, … → ✓ With the sun setting, … (finite етістік жоқ). ✗ After to arrive… → ✓ After arriving… (предлогтар -ing алады).

ru.title: Кратко
ru.html:
• Having + pp = завершено до главного действия (уточняет последовательность).
  • Используй союзы/предлоги (after/before/on/without/by/when) + -ing/-ed для точности.
  • ‘with + сущ. + причастие’ — абсолютный оборот со своим подлежащим.
  • Сохраняй одинаковое подлежащее (кроме абсолютов); цени точность и экономию.

kk.title: Қысқаша
kk.html:
• Having + pp = басты әрекеттен бұрын аяқталған (тізбекті нақтылайды).
  • Дәлдік үшін жалғаулық/предлог (after/before/on/without/by/when) + -ing/-ed қолдан.
  • ‘with + зат есім + есімше’ — өз бастауышы бар абсолют сөйлем.
  • Бірдей бастауышты сақта (абсолюттерден басқа); дәлдік пен үнемді бағала.

activities.prompt: Match each clause to its type.

items.t: With the kids asleep, they watched a film.
On hearing the news, I called you.
With time running out, we hurried.

activities.why: Having + pp = perfect; after/on + -ing = conjunction-led; with + noun + participle = absolute.

items.s: Having finished the course, she applied for jobs.
With the lights off, the room felt eerie.
After to finish, we left.
With the rain was falling, we stayed in.

activities.why: prepositions take -ing (After finishing); an absolute uses a participle, not a finite verb (With the rain falling).

activities.after: all the evidence, the jury reached a verdict. (having / consider)
activities.why: an action completed before the main clause → Having considered.

activities.after: checking the figures twice, she signed off the report. (after)
activities.why: a preposition + -ing clause for sequence → After checking.

activities.after: the deadline approaching, the team worked late. (with)
activities.why: an absolute clause with its own subject → With the deadline approaching.
perfect passive participle (Having been + pp) for a completed prior event.

activities.q: To stress that one action clearly finished before the next, choose: “___ the keys, he drove off.”
why: ‘Finding’ suggests simultaneity, not clear sequence.
Correct — ‘Having found’ marks the action as completed before driving off.
‘Found’ (passive) wrongly implies he was found.

activities.q: Which is a correctly formed absolute clause?
options: With her homework was done, she relaxed.
With her homework done, she relaxed.
With her homework doing, she relaxed.
why: an absolute has no finite verb (‘was’).
Correct — ‘with + noun + past participle’ (homework done).
‘doing’ (active) doesn't fit; the homework is done, not doing.

activities.prompt: Match each meaning to the linker.
activities.why: On + -ing = as soon as; By + -ing = method; Without + -ing = negative accompaniment.
an absolute clause has no finite verb → “With the engine overheating, we pulled over.”

activities.instruction: Combine, showing the first action finished first, using ‘Having…’:
activities.prompt: He read the contract carefully. Then he signed it.
activities.answer: having read the contract carefully, he signed it
alts: Having read the contract carefully, he signed it
having read the contract carefully, he signed it.
activities.why: a completed prior action → Having read the contract carefully, he signed it.

activities.scene: Choose the correct advanced participle structure.

steps.npc: Why did the committee take so long to approve it?

options.t: Having weighed up every objection, they wanted to be sure.
options.why: perfect participle shows they finished weighing before deciding.

options.t: Weighing up every objection, they wanted to be sure, which they'd already done.
options.why: since the weighing was completed first, ‘Having weighed up’ is clearer than the simultaneous ‘Weighing up’.

steps.npc: What finally pushed them to decide?

options.t: With the deadline looming, they had to commit.
options.why: an absolute clause supplies a simultaneous pressure (its own subject).

options.t: With the deadline was looming, they had to commit.
options.why: an absolute clause takes no finite verb: ‘With the deadline looming’.

activities.why: Use Having + pp for completed prior actions, conjunctions/prepositions + -ing/-ed for precise adverbial meaning, and ‘with + noun + participle’ absolutes for simultaneous circumstances.

learn.title: Three jobs of reflexive pronouns
learn.html: Reflexive pronouns (myself, yourself, himself, herself, itself, ourselves, yourselves, themselves) do three jobs: reflexive (subject = object), emphatic (stressing who), and reciprocal-like in fixed phrases. Choosing correctly is a subtle C1 point.

learn.title: Reflexive: the action turns back
learn.html: Use a reflexive when the subject and object are the same: She hurt herself. · They enjoyed themselves. · I taught myself to code. Without it, the meaning changes: She hurt her = another woman.

learn.title: Emphatic: stressing the person
learn.html: The emphatic reflexive stresses that a particular person did it, often meaning ‘personally / without help’: I'll do it myself. · The manager herself apologised. · The house itself is fine; it's the area that's the problem. It can go after the noun or at the end.

learn.title: When NOT to use a reflexive
learn.html: Some verbs that are reflexive in other languages aren't in English: ✗ I feel myself tired → ✓ I feel tired. ✗ concentrate yourself → ✓ concentrate. Also, after prepositions of place, use an object pronoun: She closed the door behind her (not ‘herself’).

learn.title: by myself; each other vs themselves
learn.html: by + myself = alone/unaided: I live by myself. Don't confuse reflexives with reciprocals: They blamed themselves (each blamed him/herself) vs They blamed each other (mutually).

learn.title: Advanced learner errors
learn.html:
✗ I feel myself fine. → ✓ I feel fine.

✗ He and myself agreed. → ✓ He and I agreed.

✗ They looked at themselves (meaning mutually) → ✓ They looked at each other.

learn.title: In a nutshell
learn.html:
• Reflexive when subject = object (hurt herself, enjoyed themselves).
  • Emphatic reflexive stresses the person (I'll do it myself; the CEO herself).
  • Some verbs aren't reflexive in English (feel, concentrate, relax).
  • by myself = alone; each other = mutually, not themselves.

ru.title: Три функции возвратных местоимений
ru.html: Возвратные местоимения (myself, yourself, himself, herself, itself, ourselves, yourselves, themselves) делают три работы: возвратную (подлежащее = дополнение), эмфатическую (подчёркивая кто), и в устойчивых фразах похожую на взаимную. Верный выбор — тонкая точка C1.

kk.title: Өздік есімдіктердің үш қызметі
kk.html: Өздік есімдіктер (myself, yourself, himself, herself, itself, ourselves, yourselves, themselves) үш жұмыс істейді: өздік (бастауыш = объект), екпінді (кімді баса көрсету), және тұрақты тіркестерде өзаралыққа ұқсас. Дұрыс таңдау — нәзік C1 тұсы.

ru.title: Возвратное: действие возвращается
ru.html: Используй возвратное, когда подлежащее и дополнение совпадают: She hurt herself. They enjoyed themselves. I taught myself to code. Без него смысл меняется: She hurt her = другая женщина.

kk.title: Өздік: әрекет өзіне қайтады
kk.html: Бастауыш пен объект бірдей болғанда өздікті қолдан: She hurt herself. They enjoyed themselves. I taught myself to code. Онсыз мағына өзгереді: She hurt her = басқа әйел.

ru.title: Эмфатическое: подчёркивая человека
ru.html: Эмфатическое возвратное подчёркивает, что именно этот человек сделал, часто ‘лично / без помощи’: I'll do it myself. The manager herself apologised. The house itself is fine; it's the area that's the problem. Может стоять после существительного или в конце.

kk.title: Екпінді: адамды баса көрсету
kk.html: Екпінді өздік белгілі бір адамның істегенін баса көрсетеді, жиі ‘жеке өзі / көмексіз’ мағынасында: I'll do it myself. The manager herself apologised. The house itself is fine; it's the area that's the problem. Ол зат есімнен кейін не соңында тұра алады.

ru.title: Когда НЕ использовать возвратное
ru.html: Некоторые глаголы, возвратные в других языках, в английском не возвратны: ✗ I feel myself tired → ✓ I feel tired. ✗ concentrate yourself → ✓ concentrate. Также после предлогов места используй объектное местоимение: She closed the door behind her (не ‘herself’).

kk.title: Өздікті ҚАШАН қолданбау керек
kk.html: Басқа тілде өздік болатын кейбір етістік ағылшында өздік емес: ✗ I feel myself tired → ✓ I feel tired. ✗ concentrate yourself → ✓ concentrate. Сондай-ақ орын предлогтарынан кейін объект есімдігін қолдан: She closed the door behind her (‘herself’ емес).

ru.title: by myself; each other vs themselves
ru.html: by + myself = один/без помощи: I live by myself. Не путай возвратные с взаимными: They blamed themselves (каждый себя) / They blamed each other (друг друга).

kk.title: by myself; each other vs themselves
kk.html: by + myself = жалғыз/көмексіз: I live by myself. Өздіктерді өзаралықпен шатастырма: They blamed themselves (әрқайсысы өзін) / They blamed each other (өзара).

ru.title: Ошибки продвинутого уровня
ru.html: ✗ I feel myself fine → ✓ I feel fine. ✗ He and myself agreed → ✓ He and I agreed. ✗ They looked at themselves (взаимно) → ✓ They looked at each other.

kk.title: Жоғары деңгей қателері
kk.html: ✗ I feel myself fine → ✓ I feel fine. ✗ He and myself agreed → ✓ He and I agreed. ✗ They looked at themselves (өзара мағынасында) → ✓ They looked at each other.

ru.title: Кратко
ru.html:
• Возвратное, когда подлежащее = дополнение (hurt herself, enjoyed themselves).
  • Эмфатическое возвратное подчёркивает человека (I'll do it myself; the CEO herself).
  • Некоторые глаголы в английском не возвратны (feel, concentrate, relax).
  • by myself = один; each other = друг друга, не themselves.

kk.title: Қысқаша
kk.html:
• Бастауыш = объект болғанда өздік (hurt herself, enjoyed themselves).
  • Екпінді өздік адамды баса көрсетеді (I'll do it myself; the CEO herself).
  • Кейбір етістік ағылшында өздік емес (feel, concentrate, relax).
  • by myself = жалғыз; each other = өзара, themselves емес.

activities.prompt: Sort each use of the reflexive pronoun.

items.t: I spoke to the director herself.
They blamed themselves for the loss.

activities.why: Reflexive when the action turns back on the subject; emphatic when it stresses a particular person/thing.

items.s: We enjoyed ourselves at the party.
He built the shed himself.
Please relax yourself and sit down.
She looked behind herself nervously.

activities.why: ‘relax’ isn't reflexive in English; after ‘behind’ (place) use ‘her’, not ‘herself’.

activities.before: Be careful with that knife — you'll cut
activities.why: subject and object are the same → yourself.

activities.before: Don't ask the assistant — the manager
activities.why: emphasising that the manager personally did it → the manager herself.
‘by + reflexive’ = alone/unaided → by herself.
‘by themselves’ = without help; emphatic-reflexive sense.

activities.q: Which sentence is correct English?
options: I feel myself much better today.
I feel me much better today.
why: ‘feel’ isn't reflexive in English.
Correct — ‘I feel better’ needs no object pronoun.
‘me’ can't be the object of ‘feel’ here.

activities.q: “After the argument, the two friends refused to speak to ___.” (mutually)
why: ‘themselves’ would mean each refused to speak to him/herself.
Correct — a mutual action → each other.
‘them’ would refer to other people.

activities.prompt: Sort each gap by what's needed.
activities.why: After prepositions of place/accompaniment use an object pronoun; use a reflexive when the action returns to the subject.
as a subject, use ‘I’, not the reflexive → “My brother and I organised…”.

activities.instruction: Rewrite to stress that the president personally did it, using a reflexive:
activities.prompt: The president signed the treaty.
activities.answer: the president signed the treaty herself
alts: The president herself signed the treaty
the president himself signed the treaty
The president signed the treaty himself
activities.why: an emphatic reflexive stresses the person → the president herself / signed it herself.

activities.typeLabel: Extended context: after a group project

steps.npc: Did you get help with the presentation?

options.t: No, I put it together myself, actually.
options.why: emphatic reflexive = personally/without help.

options.t: No, I put it together me, actually.
options.why: to stress ‘without help’, use the reflexive ‘myself’, not ‘me’.

steps.npc: And did the team get on well?

options.t: Mostly — though two of them stopped speaking to each other.
options.why: a mutual action → each other.

options.t: Mostly — though two of them stopped speaking to themselves.
options.why: for a mutual falling-out, use ‘each other’, not ‘themselves’.

activities.why: Use reflexives when the action turns back on the subject or to add emphasis, avoid them with verbs like feel/relax, and use each other for mutual actions.

learn.title: one/ones: avoiding repetition
learn.html: one (singular) and ones (plural) substitute for a countable noun already mentioned, to avoid repeating it: “Which cup?” “The blue one.” · I prefer the small ones. They stand for the noun, not the whole noun phrase's reference.

learn.title: When one/ones is needed
learn.html: Use one/ones after an adjective (the red one), after this/that/these/those when an adjective or phrase follows (that one over there), and after which (Which ones?). It keeps the noun class present without repetition.

learn.title: When one/ones is NOT used
learn.html: Don't use one/ones with uncountable nouns (✗ I prefer the white one for rice → ✓ the white rice / the white kind), and it's usually omitted after possessives, superlatives and ‘own’ when the meaning is clear: mine, the best, my own (not ‘my own one’ in most cases).

learn.title: Dropping one after this/that alone
learn.html: After this/that/these/those used alone (as pronouns), drop one/ones: I'll take this (not ‘this one’ when nothing follows) — though ‘this one’ is fine for contrast. After colours and some adjectives, ‘one’ is optional: the dark (one).

learn.title: one of + plural; ones vs them
learn.html: one of + the/my + plural noun: She's one of my best friends. Don't confuse substitute ones with the object pronoun them: I like the red ones (substituting a noun) vs I like them (referring to specific known items).

learn.title: Advanced learner errors
learn.html:
✗ I prefer the blue. (colour + missing noun) → ✓ I prefer the blue one.

✗ She gave me an advice one → ✓ … some advice (uncountable, no ‘one’).

✗ my own one (usually) → ✓ my own.

learn.title: In a nutshell
learn.html:
• one/ones substitute for a countable noun to avoid repetition.
  • Used after adjectives, which, and this/that + modifier.
  • Not with uncountables; often dropped after possessives/superlatives/own.
  • one of + plural noun; don't confuse ‘ones’ (substitute) with ‘them’ (reference).

ru.title: one/ones: избегание повтора
ru.html: one (ед.) и ones (мн.) заменяют уже упомянутое исчисляемое существительное, чтобы не повторять его: “Which cup?” “The blue one.” I prefer the small ones. Они стоят за существительное, а не за всю референцию фразы.

kk.title: one/ones: қайталаудан қашу
kk.html: one (жекеше) мен ones (көпше) бұрын айтылған саналатын зат есімнің орнын басады, оны қайталамау үшін: “Which cup?” “The blue one.” I prefer the small ones. Олар зат есімнің орнына тұрады, бүкіл тіркестің сілтемесі емес.

ru.title: Когда нужно one/ones
ru.html: Используй one/ones после прилагательного (the red one), после this/that/these/those, когда следует прилагательное/фраза (that one over there), и после which (Which ones?). Сохраняет класс существительного без повтора.

kk.title: one/ones қашан керек
kk.html: Сын есімнен кейін (the red one), this/that/these/those-тан кейін сын есім/тіркес болса (that one over there), және which-тен кейін (Which ones?) one/ones қолдан. Ол зат есім класын қайталаусыз сақтайды.

ru.title: Когда one/ones НЕ используется
ru.html: Не используй one/ones с неисчисляемыми (✗ I prefer the white one про рис → ✓ the white rice / the white kind), и обычно опускается после притяжательных, превосходной степени и ‘own’, когда смысл ясен: mine, the best, my own (в большинстве случаев не ‘my own one’).

kk.title: one/ones қашан қолданылмайды
kk.html: Саналмайтын зат есіммен one/ones қолданба (✗ I prefer the white one күріш үшін → ✓ the white rice / the white kind), және мағына анық болса тәуелдік, superlative, ‘own’-нан кейін әдетте түсіріледі: mine, the best, my own (көп жағдайда ‘my own one’ емес).

ru.title: Опущение one после this/that отдельно
ru.html: После this/that/these/those, используемых отдельно (как местоимения), опускай one/ones: I'll take this (не ‘this one’, когда ничего не следует) — хотя ‘this one’ уместно для контраста. После цветов и некоторых прилагательных ‘one’ необязательно: the dark (one).

kk.title: this/that жалғыз болғанда one-ды түсіру
kk.html: this/that/these/those жалғыз (есімдік ретінде) қолданылса, one/ones-ты түсір: I'll take this (ештеңе ермегенде ‘this one’ емес) — бірақ қарама-қарсылыққа ‘this one’ жарайды. Түс пен кейбір сын есімнен кейін ‘one’ міндетті емес: the dark (one).

ru.title: one of + множ.; ones vs them
ru.html: one of + the/my + множ. сущ.: She's one of my best friends. Не путай заместитель ones с объектным them: I like the red ones (замена существительного) / I like them (ссылка на конкретные известные предметы).

kk.title: one of + көпше; ones vs them
kk.html: one of + the/my + көпше зат есім: She's one of my best friends. Орынбасар ones-ты объект есімдігі them-мен шатастырма: I like the red ones (зат есімді алмастыру) / I like them (белгілі заттарға сілтеу).

ru.title: Ошибки продвинутого уровня
ru.html: ✗ I prefer the blue (цвет + нет сущ.) → ✓ I prefer the blue one. ✗ She gave me an advice one → ✓ … some advice (неисчисляемое, без ‘one’). ✗ my own one (обычно) → ✓ my own.

kk.title: Жоғары деңгей қателері
kk.html: ✗ I prefer the blue (түс + зат есім жоқ) → ✓ I prefer the blue one. ✗ She gave me an advice one → ✓ … some advice (саналмайтын, ‘one’ жоқ). ✗ my own one (әдетте) → ✓ my own.

ru.title: Кратко
ru.html:
• one/ones заменяют исчисляемое существительное во избежание повтора.
  • Используются после прилагательного, which, this/that + модификатор.
  • Не с неисчисляемыми; часто опускаются после притяжательных/превосходной/own.
  • one of + множ. сущ.; не путай ‘ones’ (заместитель) с ‘them’ (ссылка).

kk.title: Қысқаша
kk.html:
• one/ones саналатын зат есімді қайталаудан қашу үшін алмастырады.
  • Сын есім, which, this/that + анықтауыштан кейін қолданылады.
  • Саналмайтынмен емес; тәуелдік/superlative/own-нан кейін жиі түсіріледі.
  • one of + көпше зат есім; ‘ones’ (орынбасар) мен ‘them’ (сілтеме) шатастырма.

activities.prompt: Sort each context.
activities.why: Use one/ones after adjectives and which for countables; not with uncountables or (usually) after ‘own’.

items.s: I'll take the smaller one.
These shoes are nicer than those ones.
Could I have a coffee one, please?
I need some advices ones.

activities.why: ‘coffee’ and ‘advice’ are uncountable, so ‘one/ones’ can't substitute for them.

activities.before: I don't like these plates — do you have any smaller
activities.why: substituting a countable plural after an adjective → ones.
choosing between countable options → Which one.
‘one of + superlative + plural noun’ → one of the best.
‘one’ substitutes a countable noun after a describing word.

activities.q: Which is the most natural?
options: Is this your bag or my one?
Is this your bag or mine?
Is this your bag or my one bag?
why: ‘my one’ is unnatural after a possessive.
Correct — after a possessive, use the possessive pronoun ‘mine’.
redundant repetition of ‘bag’.

activities.q: Pointing at a specific pair already in your hand: “I'll buy ___.”
why: ‘the ones’ needs a defining phrase after it.
possible, but for a specific known pair in hand…
Correct — ‘them’ refers to the specific, known items.

activities.prompt: Sort each phrase by the correct form.
activities.why: Singular countable → one; plural countable → ones; ‘this’ alone or an uncountable → no substitute.
a countable noun is being substituted after an adjective → “bought a new one.”

activities.instruction: Rewrite using one/ones to avoid repeating the noun:
activities.prompt: I don't want the cheap sunglasses; I want the expensive sunglasses.
activities.answer: I don't want the cheap sunglasses; I want the expensive ones
alts: I don't want the cheap ones; I want the expensive ones
I want the expensive ones
activities.why: substitute the repeated plural noun → the expensive ones.

activities.typeLabel: Extended context: shopping for a gift
activities.scene: Choose one, ones, or the right pronoun.

steps.npc: There are loads of notebooks — which do you think she'd like?

options.t: Probably the leather ones; they look nicer.
options.why: substituting a countable plural after an adjective → ones.

options.t: Probably the leather; they look nicer.
