options.why: after the adjective ‘leather’, you need ‘ones’ to stand for ‘notebooks’.

steps.npc: And a pen to go with it?

options.t: Yes — that blue one on the top shelf.
options.why: ‘that … one’ substitutes the singular countable ‘pen’.

options.t: Yes — that blue on the top shelf.
options.why: after ‘that blue’, add ‘one’ to stand for ‘pen’.

activities.why: Use one/ones to substitute countable nouns after adjectives/which/this-that, avoid them with uncountables, and prefer possessive pronouns over ‘my one’.

learn.title: Economy: standing in for a whole clause
learn.html: so and not can replace a whole that-clause after certain verbs, avoiding repetition: “Is it going to rain?” “I think so” (= I think it will) / “I hope not” (= I hope it won't).

learn.title: Verbs that take so/not
learn.html: Common verbs: think, believe, suppose, expect, imagine, guess, hope, be afraid, presume, reckon: “Are we late?” “I suppose so. · I'm afraid so. · I expect not.” The choice of so or not tracks the positive/negative answer.

learn.title: Two ways to make it negative
learn.html: Some verbs negate with not after them (I hope not, I'm afraid not, I guess not); others negate the verb itself (I don't think so, I don't suppose so, I don't believe so). hope and be afraid only take ‘not’; think/believe/suppose prefer ‘don't … so’.

learn.title: so with say, tell & do
learn.html: so also works with say/tell (“He's resigned.” “So I hear / So she said”) and after if (“Is it true?” “If so, we should act”; “If not, never mind”). ‘I told you so’ is a fixed phrase.

learn.title: Register & fronted so
learn.html: so can front for emphasis/agreement: “It's freezing.” “So it is!” (surprised agreement) · “I'm tired.” “So am I” (= me too). These economical replies are very natural in speech and a strong C1 marker of fluency.

learn.title: Advanced learner errors
learn.html:
✗ I think it. (answering yes) → ✓ I think so.

✗ I hope don't. → ✓ I hope not.

✗ I don't think it will is fine, but the neat form is I don't think so.

learn.title: In a nutshell
learn.html:
• so/not replace a whole that-clause after think/hope/expect/be afraid, etc.
  • hope/be afraid + not; think/believe/suppose prefer don't … so.
  • so also appears with say/tell and after if (if so / if not).
  • Fronted ‘So am I / So it is’ gives economical agreement.

ru.title: Экономия: замена целой клаузы
ru.html: so и not могут заменить целую that-clause после некоторых глаголов, избегая повтора: “Is it going to rain?” “I think so” (= it will) / “I hope not” (= it won't).

kk.title: Үнем: бүкіл сөйлемнің орнына тұру
kk.html: so мен not кейбір етістіктен кейін бүкіл that-сөйлемді алмастыра алады, қайталаудан қашып: “Is it going to rain?” “I think so” (= it will) / “I hope not” (= it won't).

ru.title: Глаголы с so/not
ru.html: Частые глаголы: think, believe, suppose, expect, imagine, guess, hope, be afraid, presume, reckon: “Are we late?” “I suppose so. I'm afraid so. I expect not.” Выбор so или not отслеживает положительный/отрицательный ответ.

kk.title: so/not алатын етістіктер
kk.html: Жиі етістіктер: think, believe, suppose, expect, imagine, guess, hope, be afraid, presume, reckon: “Are we late?” “I suppose so. I'm afraid so. I expect not.” so не not таңдауы оң/теріс жауапты қадағалайды.

ru.title: Два способа отрицания
ru.html: Некоторые глаголы отрицаются через not после них (I hope not, I'm afraid not, I guess not); другие отрицают сам глагол (I don't think so, I don't suppose so, I don't believe so). hope и be afraid берут только ‘not’; think/believe/suppose предпочитают ‘don't … so’.

kk.title: Теріс етудің екі жолы
kk.html: Кейбір етістік өзінен кейін not-пен терістейді (I hope not, I'm afraid not, I guess not); басқалары етістіктің өзін терістейді (I don't think so, I don't suppose so, I don't believe so). hope мен be afraid тек ‘not’ алады; think/believe/suppose ‘don't … so’-ды қалайды.

ru.title: so с say, tell и do
ru.html: so также работает с say/tell (“He's resigned.” “So I hear / So she said”) и после if (“Is it true?” “If so, we should act”; “If not, never mind”). ‘I told you so’ — устойчивая фраза.

kk.title: say, tell мен do-мен so
kk.html: so say/tell-мен де істейді (“He's resigned.” “So I hear / So she said”) және if-тен кейін (“Is it true?” “If so, we should act”; “If not, never mind”). ‘I told you so’ — тұрақты тіркес.

ru.title: Регистр и вынесенное so
ru.html: so может выноситься для акцента/согласия: “It's freezing.” “So it is!” (удивлённое согласие) · “I'm tired.” “So am I” (= я тоже). Эти экономные ответы очень естественны в речи и сильный маркер беглости C1.

kk.title: Регистр және алға шыққан so
kk.html: so екпін/келісім үшін алға шыға алады: “It's freezing.” “So it is!” (таңданған келісім) · “I'm tired.” “So am I” (= мен де). Бұл үнемді жауаптар тілде өте табиғи әрі күшті C1 еркіндік белгісі.

ru.title: Ошибки продвинутого уровня
ru.html: ✗ I think it (отвечая ‘да’) → ✓ I think so. ✗ I hope don't → ✓ I hope not. ✗ I don't think it will допустимо, но лаконичная форма — I don't think so.

kk.title: Жоғары деңгей қателері
kk.html: ✗ I think it (иә деп жауап беру) → ✓ I think so. ✗ I hope don't → ✓ I hope not. ✗ I don't think it will дұрыс, бірақ ықшам форма — I don't think so.

ru.title: Кратко
ru.html:
• so/not заменяют целую that-clause после think/hope/expect/be afraid и т.д.
  • hope/be afraid + not; think/believe/suppose предпочитают don't … so.
  • so также с say/tell и после if (if so / if not).
  • Вынесенное ‘So am I / So it is’ даёт экономное согласие.

kk.title: Қысқаша
kk.html:
• so/not think/hope/expect/be afraid т.б. кейін бүкіл that-сөйлемді алмастырады.
  • hope/be afraid + not; think/believe/suppose ‘don't … so’-ды қалайды.
  • so say/tell-мен және if-тен кейін де (if so / if not).
  • Алға шыққан ‘So am I / So it is’ үнемді келісім береді.

activities.typeLabel: so or not for this answer?
activities.prompt: Sort each reply by whether it needs ‘so’ or ‘not’.

items.t: “Will they win?” “I hope ___.” (you want them to)
“Will it work?” “I hope ___.” (you fear it won't)

activities.why: ‘so’ carries a positive answer; ‘not’ (or don't…so) carries a negative one.

items.s: “Is it true?” “I believe so.”
“Will it rain?” “I hope not.”
“Are they open?” “I hope don't.”
“Is he ready?” “I think it.”

activities.why: negate ‘hope’ with ‘not’ (I hope not), and use ‘so’, not ‘it’, to stand for the clause (I think so).

activities.before: “Do you reckon we'll make it?” “Yes, I
activities.why: a positive answer replacing the clause → I think so.
‘be afraid’ negates with ‘not’ → I'm afraid not.
‘think’ prefers negating the verb → I don't think so.
‘if not’ substitutes a negative condition clause.

activities.q: Choose the natural negative reply: “Do you think it'll be ready?”
why: ‘I think not’ is very formal/rare with ‘think’.
Correct — ‘think’ normally negates as ‘I don't think so’.
ungrammatical word order.

activities.q: “I'm exhausted.” Which reply means ‘me too’?
why: Correct — ‘So am I’ = me too (agreement with inversion).
‘So I am’ means ‘yes, I really am’ (confirming about oneself).
not idiomatic.

activities.prompt: Sort each verb by how it's normally made negative.
activities.why: hope/be afraid take ‘not’; think/believe normally negate the verb (don't … so).
‘hope’ negates with ‘not’ → “I hope not.”

activities.instruction: Rewrite the reply using ‘so’ or ‘not’ to avoid repeating the clause:
activities.prompt: “Is the train delayed?” “I expect that the train is delayed.”
alts: I expect so.
Yes, I expect so
activities.why: replace the whole clause with ‘so’ → I expect so.

steps.npc: Do you think the event will still go ahead in this weather?

options.t: I doubt it, honestly — but I hope so.
options.why: ‘hope so’ carries the positive wish, replacing the clause.

options.t: I doubt it, honestly — but I hope it.
options.why: use ‘so’ to stand for the clause, not ‘it’: ‘I hope so’.

steps.npc: If it's cancelled, will they refund us?

options.t: I'm afraid not — the tickets are non-refundable.
options.why: ‘be afraid’ negates with ‘not’ for the negative answer.

options.t: I'm afraid don't — the tickets are non-refundable.
options.why: ‘be afraid’ takes ‘not’, not ‘don't’: ‘I'm afraid not’.

activities.why: Use so/not to replace a whole clause after think/hope/expect/be afraid, negating with ‘not’ (hope/afraid) or ‘don't…so’ (think/believe), plus if so/if not and fronted ‘So am I’.

learn.title: do so & such: two economy devices
learn.html: do so substitutes for a verb phrase already mentioned (avoiding repetition), and such substitutes for or refers to a previously described thing/idea. Both are hallmarks of formal, cohesive writing.

learn.title: do so = the whole verb phrase
learn.html: do so replaces a full verb + complement from before: She asked me to sign, and I did so (= signed). It's more formal than plain ‘did it’ and refers to a deliberate, specific action. Tense varies on ‘do’: does so, did so, doing so, will do so.

learn.title: do so vs do it/that
learn.html: do so is for the same, definite action just described, in formal register. do it/that is more informal and can point to a broader or new action: “Can you fix it?” “I'll do it.” In careful writing, do so signals precise reference back.

learn.title: such: referring back & forward
learn.html: such means ‘of that kind / like that’: I've never seen such chaos. · Such behaviour is unacceptable (behaviour of that type). such + a/an + noun in the singular: such a mess. It can also introduce examples: animals such as lions.

learn.title: such … that; no such
learn.html: such (a) … that expresses result: It was such a good film that I watched it twice. · Such was the demand that they printed more. no such = ‘not any of that kind’: There's no such thing as a free lunch.

learn.title: Advanced learner errors
learn.html:
✗ I did so it. → ✓ I did so (no extra object).

✗ such a nice weather → ✓ such nice weather (uncountable, no ‘a’).

✗ so a big problem → ✓ such a big problem / so big a problem.

learn.title: In a nutshell
learn.html:
• do so replaces a whole verb phrase (formal, definite, same action).
  • do it/that is more informal and can be broader/new.
  • such = ‘of that kind’; such a/an + singular noun; such as = for example.
  • such (a) … that expresses result; no such thing = none of that kind.

ru.title: do so и such: два средства экономии
ru.html: do so заменяет уже упомянутую глагольную группу (избегая повтора), а such заменяет или отсылает к ранее описанной вещи/идее. Оба — черты формального, связного письма.

kk.title: do so мен such: екі үнем құралы
kk.html: do so бұрын айтылған етістік тіркесін алмастырады (қайталаудан қашып), ал such бұрын сипатталған зат/идеяны алмастырады не оған сілтейді. Екеуі де ресми, байланысты жазудың белгісі.

ru.title: do so = вся глагольная группа
ru.html: do so заменяет полный глагол + дополнение из прежнего: She asked me to sign, and I did so (= подписал). Формальнее простого ‘did it’ и отсылает к намеренному, конкретному действию. Время меняется на ‘do’: does so, did so, doing so, will do so.

kk.title: do so = бүкіл етістік тіркесі
kk.html: do so алдыңғы толық етістік + толықтауышты алмастырады: She asked me to sign, and I did so (= қол қойдым). Ол жай ‘did it’-тен ресмилеу әрі әдейі, нақты әрекетке сілтейді. Шақ ‘do’-да өзгереді: does so, did so, doing so, will do so.

ru.title: do so vs do it/that
ru.html: do so — для того же, определённого действия, только что описанного, в формальном регистре. do it/that — неформальнее и может указывать на более широкое или новое действие: “Can you fix it?” “I'll do it.” В тщательном письме do so сигналит точную обратную ссылку.

kk.title: do so vs do it/that
kk.html: do so — жаңа сипатталған сол, нақты әрекет үшін, ресми регистрде. do it/that — бейресмилеу әрі кеңірек не жаңа әрекетке нұсқай алады: “Can you fix it?” “I'll do it.” Мұқият жазуда do so дәл кері сілтемені білдіреді.

ru.title: such: ссылка назад и вперёд
ru.html: such значит ‘такого рода / как то’: I've never seen such chaos. Such behaviour is unacceptable (поведение того типа). such + a/an + сущ. в единственном: such a mess. Может вводить примеры: animals such as lions.

kk.title: such: кері және алға сілтеу
kk.html: such ‘сондай / сол сияқты’ дегенді білдіреді: I've never seen such chaos. Such behaviour is unacceptable (сол типтегі мінез). such + a/an + зат есім жекешеде: such a mess. Ол мысал да енгізе алады: animals such as lions.

ru.title: such … that; no such
ru.html: such (a) … that выражает результат: It was such a good film that I watched it twice. Such was the demand that they printed more. no such = ‘ни одного такого рода’: There's no such thing as a free lunch.

kk.title: such … that; no such
kk.html: such (a) … that нәтижені білдіреді: It was such a good film that I watched it twice. Such was the demand that they printed more. no such = ‘сол типтен ешбірі’: There's no such thing as a free lunch.

ru.title: Ошибки продвинутого уровня
ru.html: ✗ I did so it → ✓ I did so (без доп. объекта). ✗ such a nice weather → ✓ such nice weather (неисчисляемое, без ‘a’). ✗ so a big problem → ✓ such a big problem / so big a problem.

kk.title: Жоғары деңгей қателері
kk.html: ✗ I did so it → ✓ I did so (қосымша объект жоқ). ✗ such a nice weather → ✓ such nice weather (саналмайтын, ‘a’ жоқ). ✗ so a big problem → ✓ such a big problem / so big a problem.

ru.title: Кратко
ru.html:
• do so заменяет всю глагольную группу (формально, определённо, то же действие).
  • do it/that неформальнее и может быть шире/новым.
  • such = ‘такого рода’; such a/an + сущ. в ед.; such as = например.
  • such (a) … that выражает результат; no such thing = ничего такого рода.

kk.title: Қысқаша
kk.html:
• do so бүкіл етістік тіркесін алмастырады (ресми, нақты, сол әрекет).
  • do it/that бейресмилеу әрі кеңірек/жаңа болуы мүмкін.
  • such = ‘сол типтегі’; such a/an + жекеше зат есім; such as = мысалы.
  • such (a) … that нәтиже білдіреді; no such thing = сол типтен ешбірі.

activities.typeLabel: do so or such — which device?
activities.prompt: Sort each by what it substitutes/refers to.

items.t: They were told to leave and did so.
Please sign here and, having done so, …

activities.why: ‘do so’ replaces a verb phrase; ‘such’ refers to a thing or idea ‘of that kind’.

items.s: It was such a shock that she sat down.
They asked us to wait, and we did so.
He gave me such a good advice.
I did so the task quickly.

activities.why: ‘advice’ is uncountable (such good advice); ‘do so’ takes no extra object (I did so).

activities.before: Applicants must register online; those failing to
activities.why: replacing ‘register online’ formally → failing to do so.
‘generosity’ is uncountable → such generosity (no ‘a’).

activities.after: a boring lecture that half the room left. (such)
activities.why: ‘such a … that’ expresses result → such a boring lecture that…
‘no such thing as’ = none of that kind.

activities.q: In a formal report: “Members were asked to submit feedback, and most ___ within a week.”
why: ‘did it’ is more informal for a formal report.
Correct — ‘did so’ formally replaces ‘submitted feedback’.
‘made it’ has a different meaning (arrived/succeeded).

activities.q: Which is correctly formed?
options: It was so a difficult decision.
It was such a difficult decision.
It was such difficult a decision.
why: ‘so’ needs the order ‘so difficult a decision’.
Correct — ‘such a + adjective + noun’.
wrong word order for ‘such’.

activities.typeLabel: such, such a, or such as?
activities.prompt: Sort each gap by the correct form.
activities.why: such + uncountable/plural; such a + singular countable; such as introduces examples.
‘do so’ already stands for the action, so no object follows → “…and we did so.”

activities.instruction: Rewrite the second clause using ‘do so’:
activities.prompt: The committee agreed to review the policy, and they reviewed the policy promptly.
activities.answer: the committee agreed to review the policy and did so promptly
alts: The committee agreed to review the policy and did so promptly
and did so promptly
activities.why: replace the repeated verb phrase → and did so promptly.

steps.npc: Did the company respond after you complained?

options.t: They promised to refund me and, to be fair, did so within days.
options.why: ‘did so’ formally replaces ‘refund me’.

options.t: They promised to refund me and, to be fair, did so it within days.
options.why: ‘do so’ takes no object; drop ‘it’.

steps.npc: Were you surprised by how quick it was?

options.t: Honestly, yes — such efficiency is rare these days.
options.why: ‘such + uncountable’ refers to efficiency of that kind.

options.t: Honestly, yes — such an efficiency is rare these days.
options.why: ‘efficiency’ is uncountable, so ‘such efficiency’ without ‘an’.

activities.why: Use ‘do so’ to replace a verb phrase in formal writing (no added object) and ‘such (a/as)’ to refer to things of a described kind, including result (such … that) and examples (such as).

learn.title: Ellipsis after auxiliaries
learn.html: To avoid repeating a verb phrase, English leaves out everything after the auxiliary/modal, keeping just the auxiliary: She can't swim, but I can (swim). · He hasn't finished, but I have (finished). This auxiliary ellipsis is everywhere in natural English.

learn.title: Keep (or supply) the right auxiliary
learn.html: The remaining auxiliary must match the tense/meaning: “Who broke it?” “I did.” · “Are you coming?” “I might.” If the full verb was in the present/past simple with no auxiliary, use a form of do: She sings better than I do.

learn.title: so / neither / nor + inversion
learn.html: For agreement, use so + auxiliary + subject (positive) or neither/nor + auxiliary + subject (negative), with inversion: “I love jazz.” “So do I.” · “I can't drive.” “Neither can I.” The auxiliary matches the first clause.

learn.title: Additions & short answers
learn.html: The same ellipsis powers short answers (Yes, I do / No, she isn't), question tags (You're coming, aren't you?), and additions (I was tired, and so was he / he was too; I didn't know, and neither did she).

learn.title: Register & emphasis
learn.html: Keeping the auxiliary can also add emphasis or contrast: I do like it (emphatic) · She said she'd help, and she did. This ellipsis is essential to fluent, non-repetitive English at C1.

learn.title: Advanced learner errors
learn.html:
✗ “I'm tired.” “So I am.” (agreement) → ✓ “So am I.”

✗ She likes it more than I like. → ✓ … than I do.

✗ “I can swim.” “Also I.” → ✓ “So can I / Me too.”

learn.title: In a nutshell
learn.html:
• Leave out the verb phrase after an auxiliary/modal (she can't, but I can).
  • Supply a form of ‘do’ if there was no auxiliary (…than I do).
  • so/neither/nor + auxiliary + subject (inversion) for agreement.
  • Powers short answers, tags and additions; keep the correct auxiliary.

ru.title: Эллипсис после вспомогательных
ru.html: Чтобы не повторять глагольную группу, английский опускает всё после вспомогательного/модального, оставляя лишь вспомогательный: She can't swim, but I can (swim). He hasn't finished, but I have (finished). Этот вспомогательный эллипсис повсюду в естественном английском.

kk.title: Көмекшіден кейін эллипсис
kk.html: Етістік тіркесін қайталамау үшін ағылшын көмекші/модальдан кейінгінің бәрін түсіріп, тек көмекшіні қалдырады: She can't swim, but I can (swim). He hasn't finished, but I have (finished). Бұл көмекші эллипсисі табиғи ағылшында жиі.

ru.title: Сохрани (или подставь) верный вспомогательный
ru.html: Оставшийся вспомогательный должен соответствовать времени/смыслу: “Who broke it?” “I did.” “Are you coming?” “I might.” Если полный глагол был в present/past simple без вспомогательного, используй форму do: She sings better than I do.

kk.title: Дұрыс көмекшіні сақта (не бер)
kk.html: Қалған көмекші шақ/мағынаға сай болуы керек: “Who broke it?” “I did.” “Are you coming?” “I might.” Толық етістік present/past simple-да көмекшісіз болса, do формасын қолдан: She sings better than I do.

ru.title: so / neither / nor + инверсия
ru.html: Для согласия используй so + вспом. + подлежащее (положит.) или neither/nor + вспом. + подлежащее (отриц.), с инверсией: “I love jazz.” “So do I.” “I can't drive.” “Neither can I.” Вспомогательный соответствует первой клаузе.

kk.title: so / neither / nor + инверсия
kk.html: Келісім үшін so + көмекші + бастауыш (оң) не neither/nor + көмекші + бастауыш (теріс), инверсиямен: “I love jazz.” “So do I.” “I can't drive.” “Neither can I.” Көмекші бірінші сөйлемге сай.

ru.title: Добавления и краткие ответы
ru.html: Тот же эллипсис питает краткие ответы (Yes, I do / No, she isn't), вопросительные хвосты (You're coming, aren't you?), и добавления (I was tired, and so was he / he was too; I didn't know, and neither did she).

kk.title: Қосымшалар мен қысқа жауаптар
kk.html: Сол эллипсис қысқа жауаптарды (Yes, I do / No, she isn't), сұрақ тэгтерін (You're coming, aren't you?), және қосымшаларды (I was tired, and so was he / he was too; I didn't know, and neither did she) қуаттайды.

ru.title: Регистр и акцент
ru.html: Сохранение вспомогательного может добавить акцент или контраст: I do like it (эмфатично) · She said she'd help, and she did. Этот эллипсис важен для беглого, неповторяющегося английского на C1.

kk.title: Регистр және екпін
kk.html: Көмекшіні сақтау екпін не қарама-қарсылық та қоса алады: I do like it (екпінді) · She said she'd help, and she did. Бұл эллипсис C1-де еркін, қайталанбайтын ағылшынға маңызды.

ru.title: Ошибки продвинутого уровня
ru.html: ✗ “I'm tired.” “So I am.” (согласие) → ✓ “So am I.” ✗ She likes it more than I like → ✓ … than I do. ✗ “I can swim.” “Also I.” → ✓ “So can I / Me too.”

kk.title: Жоғары деңгей қателері
kk.html: ✗ “I'm tired.” “So I am.” (келісім) → ✓ “So am I.” ✗ She likes it more than I like → ✓ … than I do. ✗ “I can swim.” “Also I.” → ✓ “So can I / Me too.”

ru.title: Кратко
ru.html:
• Опускай глагольную группу после вспомогательного/модального (she can't, but I can).
  • Подставь форму ‘do’, если вспомогательного не было (…than I do).
  • so/neither/nor + вспом. + подлежащее (инверсия) для согласия.
  • Питает краткие ответы, хвосты и добавления; сохраняй верный вспомогательный.

kk.title: Қысқаша
kk.html:
• Көмекші/модальдан кейін етістік тіркесін түсір (she can't, but I can).
  • Көмекші болмаса, ‘do’ формасын бер (…than I do).
  • Келісімге so/neither/nor + көмекші + бастауыш (инверсия).
  • Қысқа жауап, тэг, қосымшаларды қуаттайды; дұрыс көмекшіні сақта.

activities.prompt: Sort each agreement reply.
activities.why: Positive agreement → so + aux + subject; negative agreement → neither/nor + aux + subject.

items.s: She hasn't replied, but I have.
He runs faster than I do.
“I'm hungry.” “So I am.”
They didn't like it, and neither didn't we.

activities.why: agreement is ‘So am I’ (inversion); ‘neither’ is already negative, so not ‘neither didn't’.

activities.before: My sister earns more than I
activities.why: the full verb had no auxiliary, so use do → than I do.

alts: Nor have I
activities.why: negative agreement with inversion → Neither have I.
keep the auxiliary to contrast → but she did.
so + auxiliary (does) + subject, matching the present simple.

activities.q: “I finished early.” Agreement: “___.”
why: Correct — past simple → ‘So did I’ (do-support in the past).
‘do’ is present, but the original is past.
you can't front the main verb like that.

activities.q: Choose the emphatic reply to ‘You don't really like it, do you?’
why: Correct — ‘I do like it’ uses emphatic ‘do’.
‘I like’ is incomplete.
‘am like’ is ungrammatical.

activities.prompt: Match the full form to the auxiliary kept in ellipsis.
activities.why: Present/past simple → do-support; perfect → have; be/modals keep be or the modal.
‘neither’ is already negative, so the auxiliary is positive → “neither has she.”

activities.instruction: Rewrite avoiding repetition, using an auxiliary:
activities.prompt: He wanted to stay, and I wanted to stay too.
activities.answer: he wanted to stay, and so did I
alts: He wanted to stay, and so did I
he wanted to stay and so did I
and I did too
activities.why: positive agreement with inversion → and so did I (or ‘and I did too’).

steps.npc: I've completely given up coffee, you know.

options.t: Really? So have I, actually — last month.
options.why: perfect tense agreement → So have I.

options.t: Really? So do I, actually — last month.
options.why: the original is present perfect (‘have given up’), so agreement is ‘So have I’.

steps.npc: But I still can't resist chocolate.

options.t: Ha — neither can I, honestly.
options.why: negative agreement with a modal → Neither can I.

options.t: Ha — so can't I, honestly.
options.why: negative agreement uses ‘Neither can I’, not ‘so can't I’.

activities.why: Leave out the verb phrase after an auxiliary, supplying ‘do’ where needed, and use so/neither/nor + auxiliary + subject with inversion for agreement.

learn.title: Reduced infinitives: keeping just ‘to’
learn.html: To avoid repeating a verb, English often keeps only to and drops the rest: “Are you coming?” “I'd love to.” (= to come) · You can leave if you want to. This infinitival ellipsis is very common and natural.

learn.title: After want, would like, hope, try, etc.
learn.html: Keep to after verbs that take a to-infinitive: want, would like/love, hope, plan, mean, try, need, forget, be able, ought: I didn't call, but I meant to. · She might not pass, but she's certainly able to.

learn.title: When to drop ‘to’ as well
learn.html: After some verbs, to is also dropped, especially modals and often after want in short replies: “Will you help?” “I might.” (not ‘might to’). With would like/love, keep to: “Come with us?” “I'd love to.”

learn.title: be going to / used to / have to
learn.html: These keep their final word in ellipsis: “Will you tell her?” “I'm going to.” · “Do you play now?” “I used to.” · “Must you go?” “I have to.” The stranded to stands for the whole action.

learn.title: Register & natural fluency
learn.html: This ellipsis makes speech concise and idiomatic. Repeating the full verb sounds heavy: “Did you lock up?” “I forgot to” (not ‘I forgot to lock up’ unless emphasising). Mastering reduced infinitives is a clear C1 fluency marker.

learn.title: Advanced learner errors
learn.html:
✗ “Do you want to come?” “Yes, I want.” → ✓ “Yes, I'd love to / I want to.”

✗ I used to smoke, but I don't used to now → ✓ … but I don't (any more).

✗ “Can you help?” “I might to.” → ✓ “I might.”

learn.title: In a nutshell
learn.html:
• Keep just ‘to’ to avoid repeating a to-infinitive (I'd love to).
  • Verbs like want/hope/mean/try/be able keep ‘to’.
  • Modals drop ‘to’ (I might, not ‘might to’).
  • be going to / used to / have to keep their final ‘to’ in ellipsis.

ru.title: Редуцированный инфинитив: только ‘to’
ru.html: Чтобы не повторять глагол, английский часто оставляет только to и опускает остальное: “Are you coming?” “I'd love to.” (= to come) · You can leave if you want to. Этот инфинитивный эллипсис очень част и естествен.

kk.title: Қысқартылған инфинитив: тек ‘to’-ны сақтау
kk.html: Етістікті қайталамау үшін ағылшын жиі тек to-ны қалдырып, қалғанын түсіреді: “Are you coming?” “I'd love to.” (= to come) · You can leave if you want to. Бұл инфинитив эллипсисі өте жиі әрі табиғи.

ru.title: После want, would like, hope, try и т.д.
ru.html: Сохраняй to после глаголов с to-infinitive: want, would like/love, hope, plan, mean, try, need, forget, be able, ought: I didn't call, but I meant to. She might not pass, but she's certainly able to.

kk.title: want, would like, hope, try т.б. кейін
kk.html: To-infinitive алатын етістіктерден кейін to-ны сақта: want, would like/love, hope, plan, mean, try, need, forget, be able, ought: I didn't call, but I meant to. She might not pass, but she's certainly able to.

ru.title: Когда опускать и ‘to’
ru.html: После некоторых глаголов to тоже опускается, особ. после модальных и часто после want в кратких ответах: “Will you help?” “I might.” (не ‘might to’). С would like/love сохраняй to: “Come with us?” “I'd love to.”

kk.title: ‘to’-ны да түсіретін кез
kk.html: Кейбір етістіктен кейін to да түсіріледі, әсіресе модальдардан және қысқа жауапта жиі want-тан кейін: “Will you help?” “I might.” (‘might to’ емес). would like/love-пен to-ны сақта: “Come with us?” “I'd love to.”

ru.title: be going to / used to / have to
ru.html: Они сохраняют своё последнее слово в эллипсисе: “Will you tell her?” “I'm going to.” · “Do you play now?” “I used to.” · “Must you go?” “I have to.” Оставленное to стоит за всё действие.

kk.title: be going to / used to / have to
kk.html: Бұлар эллипсисте соңғы сөзін сақтайды: “Will you tell her?” “I'm going to.” · “Do you play now?” “I used to.” · “Must you go?” “I have to.” Оқшауланған to бүкіл әрекеттің орнына тұрады.

ru.title: Регистр и естественная беглость
ru.html: Этот эллипсис делает речь лаконичной и идиоматичной. Повтор полного глагола звучит тяжело: “Did you lock up?” “I forgot to” (не ‘I forgot to lock up’, если не акцент). Владение редуцированными инфинитивами — явный маркер беглости C1.

kk.title: Регистр және табиғи еркіндік
kk.html: Бұл эллипсис сөйлеуді ықшам әрі идиомалық етеді. Толық етістікті қайталау ауыр естіледі: “Did you lock up?” “I forgot to” (‘I forgot to lock up’ емес, баса көрсетпесе). Қысқартылған инфинитивтерді меңгеру — анық C1 еркіндік белгісі.

ru.title: Ошибки продвинутого уровня
ru.html: ✗ “Do you want to come?” “Yes, I want.” → ✓ “Yes, I'd love to / I want to.” ✗ I used to smoke, but I don't used to now → ✓ … but I don't (any more). ✗ “Can you help?” “I might to.” → ✓ “I might.”

kk.title: Жоғары деңгей қателері
kk.html: ✗ “Do you want to come?” “Yes, I want.” → ✓ “Yes, I'd love to / I want to.” ✗ I used to smoke, but I don't used to now → ✓ … but I don't (any more). ✗ “Can you help?” “I might to.” → ✓ “I might.”

ru.title: Кратко
ru.html:
• Оставляй только ‘to’, чтобы не повторять to-infinitive (I'd love to).
  • Глаголы want/hope/mean/try/be able сохраняют ‘to’.
  • Модальные опускают ‘to’ (I might, не ‘might to’).
  • be going to / used to / have to сохраняют последнее ‘to’ в эллипсисе.

kk.title: Қысқаша
kk.html:
• To-infinitive-ты қайталамау үшін тек ‘to’-ны сақта (I'd love to).
  • want/hope/mean/try/be able сияқты етістіктер ‘to’-ны сақтайды.
  • Модальдар ‘to’-ны түсіреді (I might, ‘might to’ емес).
  • be going to / used to / have to эллипсисте соңғы ‘to’-ны сақтайды.

activities.prompt: Sort each reply by whether it ends in ‘to’ or not.

dropping ‘to’ is wrong after ‘ought’.
repeating ‘ought’ is not the reply.

why: ‘I'd love’ needs the stranded ‘to’.
Correct — ‘I'd love to’ keeps just ‘to’.
‘to it’ is wrong.

activities.prompt: Sort each short reply.
activities.why: ‘like/used to’ keep ‘to’; modals (will/should) drop it.
avoid repeating the verb phrase → “No, but I meant to.”

activities.instruction: Rewrite the reply, keeping just ‘to’:
activities.prompt: “Will you visit them?” “I hope to visit them.”

Yes, I hope to

steps.npc: Are you going to enter the competition this year?

options.t: I'd like to, if I can find the time.
options.why: ‘would like’ keeps the stranded ‘to’.

options.t: I'd like, if I can find the time.

steps.npc: You used to compete a lot, didn't you?

options.t: I used to, yeah — before work took over.
options.why: ‘used to’ keeps its final ‘to’ in ellipsis.

options.t: I used, yeah — before work took over.
options.why: ‘used to’ must keep ‘to’: ‘I used to’.

activities.why: Reduce a repeated to-infinitive to just ‘to’ after verbs like want/hope/mean and after be going to/used to/have to, but drop ‘to’ after modals.

learn.title: Attributive vs predicative
learn.html: Most adjectives go in two positions: attributive (before the noun: a tall man) and predicative (after a linking verb: He is tall). But some adjectives work only in one position — a key C1 detail.

learn.title: Attributive-only adjectives
learn.html: Some go only before a noun: the main reason, the only way, a mere child, the former president, an utter disaster, the chief concern. ✗ The reason is main. These often classify or intensify.

learn.title: Predicative-only adjectives
learn.html: Others go only after a linking verb — many begin with a-: asleep, awake, alive, alone, afraid, aware, ashamed, glad, content. ✗ an asleep baby → ✓ a sleeping baby / the baby is asleep.

learn.title: Adjectives that change meaning by position
learn.html: A few shift meaning: the present members (current) vs the members present (here now); a responsible person (reliable) vs the person responsible (who did it); my old friend (long-standing) vs a friend who is old (aged).

learn.title: Order of multiple adjectives
learn.html: When several adjectives pile up before a noun, they follow a rough order: opinion → size → age → shape → colour → origin → material → purpose: a lovely little old round brown Italian wooden table. Commas separate adjectives of the same type only.

learn.title: Advanced learner errors
learn.html:
✗ an alive fish → ✓ a live fish / the fish is alive.

✗ a wooden lovely old table → ✓ a lovely old wooden table.

✗ The only reason isn't only. → ✓ (‘only’ is attributive-only.)

learn.title: In a nutshell
learn.html:
• Most adjectives are both attributive (before noun) and predicative (after be).
  • Attributive-only: main, only, mere, former, chief, utter.
  • Predicative-only (often a-): asleep, awake, alive, afraid, aware.
  • Some change meaning by position; multiple adjectives follow opinion→size→age→colour→origin→material.

ru.title: Атрибутивная vs предикативная
ru.html: Большинство прилагательных стоят в двух позициях: атрибутивной (перед сущ.: a tall man) и предикативной (после связочного глагола: He is tall). Но некоторые работают только в одной — ключевая деталь C1.

kk.title: Атрибутивті vs предикативті
kk.html: Көп сын есім екі позицияда тұрады: атрибутивті (зат есім алдында: a tall man) және предикативті (байланыстырушы етістіктен кейін: He is tall). Бірақ кейбір сын есім тек бір позицияда — маңызды C1 тұсы.

ru.title: Только атрибутивные прилагательные
ru.html: Некоторые только перед существительным: the main reason, the only way, a mere child, the former president, an utter disaster, the chief concern. ✗ The reason is main. Часто классифицируют или усиливают.

kk.title: Тек атрибутивті сын есімдер
kk.html: Кейбірі тек зат есім алдында: the main reason, the only way, a mere child, the former president, an utter disaster, the chief concern. ✗ The reason is main. Олар жиі жіктейді не күшейтеді.

ru.title: Только предикативные прилагательные
ru.html: Другие только после связочного глагола — многие на a-: asleep, awake, alive, alone, afraid, aware, ashamed, glad, content. ✗ an asleep baby → ✓ a sleeping baby / the baby is asleep.

kk.title: Тек предикативті сын есімдер
kk.html: Басқалары тек байланыстырушы етістіктен кейін — көбі a--мен: asleep, awake, alive, alone, afraid, aware, ashamed, glad, content. ✗ an asleep baby → ✓ a sleeping baby / the baby is asleep.

ru.title: Прилагательные, меняющие значение по позиции
ru.html: Некоторые меняют смысл: the present members (нынешние) / the members present (присутствующие сейчас); a responsible person (надёжный) / the person responsible (виновный); my old friend (давний) / a friend who is old (пожилой).

kk.title: Позицияға қарай мағынасы өзгеретін сын есімдер
kk.html: Бірнешеуі мағынасын ауыстырады: the present members (қазіргі) / the members present (қазір мұнда); a responsible person (сенімді) / the person responsible (істеген); my old friend (ежелгі) / a friend who is old (қарт).

ru.title: Порядок нескольких прилагательных
ru.html: Когда перед существительным несколько прилагательных, порядок примерно: мнение → размер → возраст → форма → цвет → происхождение → материал → назначение: a lovely little old round brown Italian wooden table. Запятые разделяют только прилагательные одного типа.

kk.title: Бірнеше сын есім реті
kk.html: Зат есім алдында бірнеше сын есім жиналса, шамамен реті: пікір → өлшем → жас → пішін → түс → тек → материал → мақсат: a lovely little old round brown Italian wooden table. Үтір тек бір типтегі сын есімдерді бөледі.

ru.title: Ошибки продвинутого уровня
ru.html: ✗ an alive fish → ✓ a live fish / the fish is alive. ✗ a wooden lovely old table → ✓ a lovely old wooden table. ✗ The only reason isn't only. → (‘only’ только атрибутивное.)

kk.title: Жоғары деңгей қателері
kk.html: ✗ an alive fish → ✓ a live fish / the fish is alive. ✗ a wooden lovely old table → ✓ a lovely old wooden table. ✗ The only reason isn't only. → (‘only’ тек атрибутивті.)

ru.title: Кратко
ru.html:
• Большинство прилагательных и атрибутивны, и предикативны.
  • Только атрибутивные: main, only, mere, former, chief, utter.
  • Только предикативные (часто a-): asleep, awake, alive, afraid, aware.
  • Некоторые меняют значение по позиции; несколько прилагательных: мнение→размер→возраст→цвет→происхождение→материал.

kk.title: Қысқаша
kk.html:
• Көп сын есім атрибутивті де (зат есім алдында), предикативті де (be-ден кейін).
  • Тек атрибутивті: main, only, mere, former, chief, utter.
  • Тек предикативті (жиі a-): asleep, awake, alive, afraid, aware.
  • Кейбірі позицияға қарай мағынасын өзгертеді; бірнеше сын есім пікір→өлшем→жас→түс→тек→материал ретінде.

activities.prompt: Sort each adjective by where it can go.
activities.why: main/former/utter go only before a noun; asleep/afraid/aware go only after a linking verb.

items.s: The baby is asleep.
It was an utter disaster.
They caught an alive fish.
That is the reason main.

activities.why: ‘alive’ is predicative-only (use ‘live fish’); ‘main’ is attributive-only (the main reason).

activities.before: Don't make a noise — the children are
activities.why: after ‘are’, the predicative form → asleep.
‘chief’ is attributive-only, before the noun → the chief concern.

alts: beautiful, red silk
activities.why: opinion → colour → material → a beautiful red silk scarf.
opinion (charming) → size (small) → material (wooden) → noun.

activities.q: ‘The people present applauded.’ Here ‘present’ means:
why: ‘present’ before the noun (the present members) means current.
Correct — after the noun, ‘the people present’ = those there at the time.
that's the noun ‘present’, not this use.

activities.q: Choose the natural adjective order:
why: origin can't come first.
Correct — opinion (lovely) → age (old) → origin (Japanese).
opinion should precede age.

activities.prompt: Sort each adjective by its slot in the order.
activities.why: Opinion (gorgeous) comes first; size/age (tiny/ancient) next; colour/material (velvet) later.
‘awake’ is predicative-only → use ‘the baby, who was awake,’ or a different attributive form.

activities.instruction: Put the adjectives in natural order:
activities.prompt: a (leather / black / old) jacket
alts: an old black leather jacket
old black leather jacket
activities.why: age → colour → material → an old black leather jacket.

steps.npc: What did you pick up at the antique market?

options.t: A beautiful little Persian rug — I couldn't resist.
options.why: opinion → size → origin, all attributive.

options.t: A Persian little beautiful rug — I couldn't resist.
options.why: the order should be opinion → size → origin: ‘beautiful little Persian rug’.

steps.npc: Was the stallholder helpful?

options.t: Very — and clearly the person responsible for restoring them.
options.why: ‘responsible’ after the noun = who does/did the restoring.

options.t: Very — and clearly the responsible person for restoring them.
options.why: to mean ‘the one who restores them’, use ‘the person responsible’, after the noun.

activities.why: Place adjectives correctly: some are attributive-only (main/utter), some predicative-only (asleep/afraid), some change meaning by position, and multiple adjectives follow the standard order.

learn.title: Two classes of adjective
learn.html: Gradable adjectives describe qualities that can be more or less (cold, big, tired, interesting); non-gradable adjectives describe absolute or extreme qualities that can't (freezing, huge, exhausted, fascinating; dead, perfect, impossible). The class decides which intensifiers fit.

learn.title: Intensifiers for gradable adjectives
learn.html: Gradable adjectives take very, quite, rather, fairly, a bit, extremely, so, too, really: very tired, quite cold, extremely useful. They also form comparatives/superlatives: colder, more interesting.

learn.title: Intensifiers for non-gradable adjectives
learn.html: Non-gradable (extreme/absolute) adjectives take absolutely, completely, totally, utterly, simply, really: absolutely exhausted, completely impossible, utterly ridiculous. ✗ very exhausted (traditionally) → ✓ absolutely exhausted.

learn.title: ‘really’ and ‘quite’ cross over
learn.html: really works with both (really tired / really exhausted). quite shifts meaning: with gradable = ‘fairly’ (quite good = fairly good); with non-gradable = ‘completely’ (quite impossible = totally impossible). This is a subtle C1 nuance.

learn.title: Register & collocation
learn.html: Certain intensifiers collocate strongly: absolutely delighted, utterly devastated, totally different, bitterly disappointed, highly unlikely. Using the expected pairing sounds native; a wrong one (very perfect) sounds off.

learn.title: Advanced learner errors
learn.html:
✗ very freezing → ✓ absolutely freezing.

✗ absolutely tired → ✓ very tired / absolutely exhausted.

✗ more perfect, very unique → ✓ perfect / unique (already absolute).

learn.title: In a nutshell
learn.html:
• Gradable = more/less (cold, tired); non-gradable = absolute/extreme (freezing, exhausted).
  • Gradable + very/quite/extremely; non-gradable + absolutely/completely/utterly.
  • really works with both; quite = ‘fairly’ (gradable) or ‘completely’ (non-gradable).
  • Match the collocating intensifier; don't grade absolute adjectives (very unique ✗).

ru.title: Два класса прилагательных
ru.html: Градуируемые описывают качества, которых может быть больше или меньше (cold, big, tired, interesting); неградуируемые описывают абсолютные или крайние качества, которых нельзя (freezing, huge, exhausted, fascinating; dead, perfect, impossible). Класс решает, какие усилители подходят.

kk.title: Сын есімнің екі класы
kk.html: Дәрежеленетін сын есім көп не аз болатын сапаны сипаттайды (cold, big, tired, interesting); дәрежеленбейтін сын есім бола алмайтын абсолютті не шектік сапаны сипаттайды (freezing, huge, exhausted, fascinating; dead, perfect, impossible). Класс қай күшейткіш жарайтынын шешеді.

ru.title: Усилители для градуируемых
ru.html: Градуируемые берут very, quite, rather, fairly, a bit, extremely, so, too, really: very tired, quite cold, extremely useful. Образуют и сравнительную/превосходную: colder, more interesting.

kk.title: Дәрежеленетінге күшейткіштер
kk.html: Дәрежеленетін сын есім very, quite, rather, fairly, a bit, extremely, so, too, really алады: very tired, quite cold, extremely useful. Олар салыстырмалы/асырмалы да жасайды: colder, more interesting.

ru.title: Усилители для неградуируемых
ru.html: Неградуируемые (крайние/абсолютные) берут absolutely, completely, totally, utterly, simply, really: absolutely exhausted, completely impossible, utterly ridiculous. ✗ very exhausted (традиционно) → ✓ absolutely exhausted.

kk.title: Дәрежеленбейтінге күшейткіштер
kk.html: Дәрежеленбейтін (шектік/абсолютті) сын есім absolutely, completely, totally, utterly, simply, really алады: absolutely exhausted, completely impossible, utterly ridiculous. ✗ very exhausted (дәстүрлі) → ✓ absolutely exhausted.

ru.title: ‘really’ и ‘quite’ пересекаются
ru.html: really работает с обоими (really tired / really exhausted). quite меняет смысл: с градуируемым = ‘довольно’ (quite good = fairly good); с неградуируемым = ‘полностью’ (quite impossible = totally impossible). Тонкий нюанс C1.

kk.title: ‘really’ мен ‘quite’ қиылысады
kk.html: really екеуімен де істейді (really tired / really exhausted). quite мағынасын ауыстырады: дәрежеленетінмен = ‘айтарлықтай’ (quite good = fairly good); дәрежеленбейтінмен = ‘толықтай’ (quite impossible = totally impossible). Бұл — нәзік C1 нюансы.

ru.title: Регистр и сочетаемость
ru.html: Некоторые усилители сильно сочетаются: absolutely delighted, utterly devastated, totally different, bitterly disappointed, highly unlikely. Ожидаемая пара звучит как у носителя; неверная (very perfect) звучит странно.

kk.title: Регистр және тіркесім
kk.html: Кейбір күшейткіш қатты тіркеседі: absolutely delighted, utterly devastated, totally different, bitterly disappointed, highly unlikely. Күтілетін жұпты қолдану ана тіліндей естіледі; қате (very perfect) оғаш естіледі.

ru.title: Ошибки продвинутого уровня
ru.html: ✗ very freezing → ✓ absolutely freezing. ✗ absolutely tired → ✓ very tired / absolutely exhausted. ✗ more perfect, very unique → ✓ perfect / unique (уже абсолютные).

kk.title: Жоғары деңгей қателері
kk.html: ✗ very freezing → ✓ absolutely freezing. ✗ absolutely tired → ✓ very tired / absolutely exhausted. ✗ more perfect, very unique → ✓ perfect / unique (қазірдің өзінде абсолютті).

ru.title: Кратко
ru.html:
• Градуируемые = больше/меньше (cold, tired); неградуируемые = абсолютные/крайние (freezing, exhausted).
  • Градуируемые + very/quite/extremely; неградуируемые + absolutely/completely/utterly.
  • really с обоими; quite = ‘довольно’ (градуир.) или ‘полностью’ (неградуир.).
  • Согласуй сочетающийся усилитель; не градуируй абсолютные (very unique ✗).

kk.title: Қысқаша
kk.html:
• Дәрежеленетін = көп/аз (cold, tired); дәрежеленбейтін = абсолютті/шектік (freezing, exhausted).
  • Дәрежеленетін + very/quite/extremely; дәрежеленбейтін + absolutely/completely/utterly.
  • really екеуімен; quite = ‘fairly’ (дәрежеленетін) не ‘completely’ (дәрежеленбейтін).
  • Тіркесетін күшейткішті сәйкестендір; абсолютті сын есімді дәрежелеме (very unique ✗).

activities.prompt: Sort each adjective.
activities.why: Gradable adjectives allow degree (cold/tired/good); non-gradable ones are already extreme or absolute (freezing/exhausted/perfect).

items.s: I'm absolutely exhausted.
It's very interesting.
It's very freezing outside.
She was absolutely tired.

activities.why: non-gradable ‘freezing’ takes ‘absolutely’; gradable ‘tired’ takes ‘very’ (or use ‘absolutely exhausted’).

alts: completely
utterly
activities.why: ‘exhausted’ is non-gradable → absolutely exhausted.

alts: really
quite
extremely
activities.why: ‘interesting’ is gradable → very interesting.
‘delighted’ (non-gradable) collocates with → absolutely delighted.
‘impossible’ (non-gradable) + completely.

activities.q: ‘The task is quite impossible.’ Here ‘quite’ means:
why: with gradable adjectives ‘quite’ = fairly, but ‘impossible’ is non-gradable.
Correct — with a non-gradable adjective, ‘quite’ = completely.
not the meaning here.

activities.q: Which is correct? ‘The results were ___ different from what we expected.’
why: ‘different’ here is treated as absolute (totally different is the collocation).
Correct — ‘totally different’ is the natural non-gradable pairing.
nonsensical combination.

activities.prompt: Sort each adjective by the intensifier it takes.
activities.why: Gradable (useful/difficult) take very/extremely; non-gradable (ridiculous/impossible) take absolutely/completely.
‘stunning’ is non-gradable → “absolutely stunning.”

activities.instruction: Rewrite so the intensifier matches the adjective:
activities.prompt: I was very devastated by the news.
activities.answer: I was utterly devastated by the news
alts: I was utterly devastated by the news
I was absolutely devastated by the news
I was completely devastated by the news
activities.why: ‘devastated’ is non-gradable → utterly/absolutely devastated.

activities.scene: Choose the intensifier that matches the adjective.

steps.npc: Did you hear they cancelled the whole festival?

options.t: Yes — I'm absolutely gutted, to be honest.
options.why: ‘gutted’ is non-gradable → absolutely.

options.t: Yes — I'm very gutted, to be honest.
options.why: ‘gutted’ is non-gradable, so pair it with ‘absolutely’, not ‘very’.

steps.npc: Was the reason they gave convincing?

options.t: Not really — it was quite weak, actually.
options.why: ‘weak’ is gradable; ‘quite’ = fairly here.

options.t: Not really — it was absolutely weak, actually.
options.why: ‘weak’ is gradable, so use ‘quite/very weak’, not the non-gradable ‘absolutely’.

activities.why: Match intensifiers to the adjective class: very/quite/extremely for gradable, absolutely/completely/utterly for non-gradable, noting really (both) and quite's shift in meaning.

learn.title: Classifying vs qualitative adjectives
learn.html: Some non-gradable adjectives are classifying — they put a noun in a category rather than describing degree: nuclear energy, digital camera, financial crisis, Italian food, wooden chair. You can't say ‘very nuclear’ — it's a yes/no class, not a scale.

learn.title: Emphasising adjectives
learn.html: Emphasising adjectives intensify the noun rather than describe it: a complete stranger, an utter fool, sheer nonsense, the very idea, a total mess, pure luck. They're non-gradable and usually attributive.

learn.title: Some adjectives are both
learn.html: Certain adjectives are gradable in one sense, classifying in another: a criminal offence (classifying) vs a criminal waste of money (= shocking, gradable-ish); a human being (class) vs a very human reaction (typical of people). Context decides.

learn.title: Don't grade classifying adjectives
learn.html: Classifying adjectives resist very/more/comparatives: ✗ a very financial problem, a more digital camera. To grade the idea, rephrase: a serious financial problem, a more advanced digital camera. The grading shifts to a different, gradable word.

learn.title: Register & precision
learn.html: Recognising these classes prevents odd combinations and sharpens meaning. Emphasising adjectives (complete/utter/sheer) are strong and best used sparingly for effect; classifying ones are neutral and factual. Precise use marks advanced control.

learn.title: Advanced learner errors
learn.html:
✗ a very medical issue → ✓ a serious medical issue.

✗ more nuclear power (as a quality) → ✓ more nuclear power (quantity) but not ‘more nuclear’ as a degree.

✗ an utter good meal → ✓ an utter disaster / a really good meal.

learn.title: In a nutshell
learn.html:
• Classifying adjectives put a noun in a category (nuclear, digital, financial) — not gradable.
  • Emphasising adjectives intensify the noun (complete, utter, sheer) — non-gradable, attributive.
  • Some adjectives are gradable in one sense, classifying in another.
  • To grade a classifying idea, use a separate gradable word (serious/advanced).

ru.title: Классифицирующие vs качественные прилагательные
ru.html: Некоторые неградуируемые прилагательные — классифицирующие: они помещают существительное в категорию, а не описывают степень: nuclear energy, digital camera, financial crisis, Italian food, wooden chair. Нельзя сказать ‘very nuclear’ — это класс да/нет, не шкала.

kk.title: Жіктеуші vs сапалық сын есімдер
kk.html: Кейбір дәрежеленбейтін сын есім жіктеуші — олар зат есімді дәрежемен емес, категорияға қояды: nuclear energy, digital camera, financial crisis, Italian food, wooden chair. ‘very nuclear’ деуге болмайды — бұл иә/жоқ класы, шкала емес.

ru.title: Усиливающие прилагательные
ru.html: Усиливающие прилагательные усиливают существительное, а не описывают: a complete stranger, an utter fool, sheer nonsense, the very idea, a total mess, pure luck. Неградуируемы и обычно атрибутивны.

kk.title: Күшейтуші сын есімдер
kk.html: Күшейтуші сын есімдер зат есімді сипаттаудан гөрі күшейтеді: a complete stranger, an utter fool, sheer nonsense, the very idea, a total mess, pure luck. Олар дәрежеленбейтін әрі әдетте атрибутивті.

ru.title: Некоторые прилагательные — оба
ru.html: Некоторые прилагательные градуируемы в одном смысле, классифицирующие в другом: a criminal offence (класс) / a criminal waste of money (= возмутительный, градуируемо); a human being (класс) / a very human reaction (свойственный людям). Решает контекст.

kk.title: Кейбір сын есім екеуі де
kk.html: Кейбір сын есім бір мағынада дәрежеленетін, басқасында жіктеуші: a criminal offence (жіктеуші) / a criminal waste of money (= масқара, дәрежеленетіндей); a human being (класс) / a very human reaction (адамға тән). Контекст шешеді.

ru.title: Не градуируй классифицирующие
ru.html: Классифицирующие сопротивляются very/more/сравнительной: ✗ a very financial problem, a more digital camera. Чтобы градуировать идею, перефразируй: a serious financial problem, a more advanced digital camera. Градация переходит на другое, градуируемое слово.

kk.title: Жіктеуші сын есімді дәрежелеме
kk.html: Жіктеуші сын есімдер very/more/салыстырмалыға қарсы: ✗ a very financial problem, a more digital camera. Идеяны дәрежелеу үшін қайта айт: a serious financial problem, a more advanced digital camera. Дәреже басқа, дәрежеленетін сөзге ауысады.

ru.title: Регистр и точность
ru.html: Узнавание этих классов предотвращает странные сочетания и уточняет смысл. Усиливающие (complete/utter/sheer) сильны и лучше их использовать редко, для эффекта; классифицирующие нейтральны и фактичны. Точное употребление — знак продвинутого контроля.

kk.title: Регистр және дәлдік
kk.html: Бұл кластарды тану оғаш тіркестерден сақтап, мағынаны өткірлейді. Күшейтуші (complete/utter/sheer) күшті әрі әсер үшін сирек қолданылады; жіктеуші бейтарап әрі нақты. Дәл қолдану жоғары бақылауды көрсетеді.

ru.title: Ошибки продвинутого уровня
ru.html: ✗ a very medical issue → ✓ a serious medical issue. ✗ more nuclear power (как качество) → ✓ more nuclear power (количество), но не ‘more nuclear’ как степень. ✗ an utter good meal → ✓ an utter disaster / a really good meal.

kk.title: Жоғары деңгей қателері
kk.html: ✗ a very medical issue → ✓ a serious medical issue. ✗ more nuclear power (сапа ретінде) → ✓ more nuclear power (мөлшер) бірақ дәреже ретінде ‘more nuclear’ емес. ✗ an utter good meal → ✓ an utter disaster / a really good meal.

ru.title: Кратко
ru.html:
• Классифицирующие помещают существительное в категорию (nuclear, digital, financial) — не градуируемы.
  • Усиливающие усиливают существительное (complete, utter, sheer) — неградуируемы, атрибутивны.
  • Некоторые прилагательные градуируемы в одном смысле, классифицирующие в другом.
  • Чтобы градуировать классифицирующую идею, используй отдельное градуируемое слово (serious/advanced).

kk.title: Қысқаша
kk.html:
• Жіктеуші сын есімдер зат есімді категорияға қояды (nuclear, digital, financial) — дәрежеленбейді.
  • Күшейтуші сын есімдер зат есімді күшейтеді (complete, utter, sheer) — дәрежеленбейтін, атрибутивті.
  • Кейбір сын есім бір мағынада дәрежеленетін, басқасында жіктеуші.
  • Жіктеуші идеяны дәрежелеуге бөлек дәрежеленетін сөз қолдан (serious/advanced).

activities.prompt: Sort each adjective by its type.
activities.why: Classifying adjectives name a category (digital/nuclear/financial); emphasising ones intensify the noun (complete/utter/sheer).

items.s: It was pure coincidence.
They're building a solar farm.
It was a very financial decision.
He's a more digital photographer.

activities.why: classifying adjectives (financial/digital) can't be graded with very/more.

alts: utter
complete
pure
activities.why: an emphasising adjective intensifying the noun → sheer nonsense.

activities.typeLabel: Grade the idea, not the class word
alts: major
severe
activities.why: ‘financial’ can't be graded, so grade with a separate word → a serious financial problem.
a classifying adjective naming the category → a digital exhibition.
emphasising ‘complete’ intensifies ‘stranger’ (attributive).

activities.q: In ‘a criminal waste of talent’, ‘criminal’ is used to mean:
why: that's the classifying sense (a criminal offence).
Correct — here ‘criminal’ = shockingly bad, an emphasising use.
the opposite of the meaning.

activities.q: Which corrects ‘a very nuclear threat’?
why: ‘nuclear’ still can't be graded with ‘more’.
Correct — grade with a separate gradable word: ‘a serious nuclear threat’.
‘nuclearly’ isn't a word.

activities.prompt: Sort each adjective by whether it can take ‘very/more’.
activities.why: expensive/beautiful are gradable; economic/chemical classify and can't be graded.
‘industrial’ is classifying and can't take ‘more’ → “a new/revised industrial strategy.”

activities.instruction: Rewrite so the degree is expressed by a gradable word:
activities.prompt: It was a very medical emergency.
activities.answer: it was a serious medical emergency
alts: It was a serious medical emergency
it was a genuine medical emergency
it was a real medical emergency
activities.why: ‘medical’ can't be graded, so grade with ‘serious/genuine’ → a serious medical emergency.

steps.npc: How would you sum up the board's decision?

options.t: Honestly? It was sheer folly.
options.why: an emphasising adjective intensifying the noun.

options.t: Honestly? It was a very folly.
options.why: use an emphasising adjective like ‘sheer/utter folly’, not ‘very folly’.

steps.npc: And the impact on the budget?

options.t: It's created a severe financial shortfall.
options.why: grade the idea with ‘severe’; ‘financial’ stays a classifier.

options.t: It's created a very financial shortfall.
options.why: ‘financial’ can't be graded; grade with a separate word: ‘a severe financial shortfall’.

activities.why: Recognise classifying adjectives (category, ungradable) and emphasising ones (intensify the noun), and grade an idea with a separate gradable word rather than the classifier.

learn.title: -ing vs -ed adjectives
learn.html: -ing adjectives describe the cause or the thing that produces a feeling; -ed adjectives describe the person's feeling: The film was boring (it causes boredom) vs I was bored (I felt it). Mixing them up is one of the most common learner errors.

learn.title: The reliable test
learn.html: Ask: does the noun cause the feeling (→ -ing) or experience it (→ -ed)? an exciting match (causes excitement) / the excited fans (feel it). Common pairs: interesting/interested, tiring/tired, confusing/confused, surprising/surprised, worrying/worried.

learn.title: Compound adjectives: hyphenated combos
learn.html: Compound adjectives join words with a hyphen before a noun: a well-known writer, a five-star hotel, a time-consuming task, a world-famous chef, a last-minute change. Common patterns: adverb/noun/adjective + past participle, or number + noun.

learn.title: Patterns of compound adjectives
learn.html: Frequent formulas: adj/noun + -ed (blue-eyed, left-handed, old-fashioned); adv/noun + -ing (hard-working, English-speaking, good-looking); number + noun (ten-year-old, four-door); noun + noun as adjective. Hyphenate before the noun, not usually after.

learn.title: Register & hyphenation
learn.html: Compound adjectives are common in descriptive and journalistic writing, packing detail economically: a state-of-the-art facility. Note the hyphen disappears when the words follow the noun: a well-known artist → the artist is well known.

learn.title: Advanced learner errors
learn.html:
✗ I was very boring in the lecture. → ✓ I was bored.

✗ a five-years-old boy → ✓ a five-year-old boy.

✗ a well known-writer → ✓ a well-known writer.

learn.title: In a nutshell
learn.html:
• -ing = causes the feeling; -ed = experiences the feeling.
  • Test: does the noun cause or feel the emotion?
  • Compound adjectives are hyphenated before a noun (well-known, five-star).
  • Number + noun compounds use the singular (a ten-year-old); hyphen drops after the noun.

ru.title: -ing vs -ed прилагательные
ru.html: -ing прилагательные описывают причину или то, что вызывает чувство; -ed — чувство человека: The film was boring (вызывает скуку) / I was bored (испытывал). Их путаница — одна из самых частых ошибок.

kk.title: -ing vs -ed сын есімдер
kk.html: -ing сын есімдер сезімнің себебін не оны тудыратын нәрсені сипаттайды; -ed сын есімдер адамның сезімін сипаттайды: The film was boring (жалықтырады) / I was bored (сездім). Оларды шатастыру — ең жиі қателердің бірі.

ru.title: Надёжный тест
ru.html: Спроси: существительное вызывает чувство (→ -ing) или испытывает его (→ -ed)? an exciting match (вызывает восторг) / the excited fans (испытывают). Частые пары: interesting/interested, tiring/tired, confusing/confused, surprising/surprised, worrying/worried.

kk.title: Сенімді тест
kk.html: Сұра: зат есім сезімді тудыра ма (→ -ing) әлде оны сезіне ме (→ -ed)? an exciting match (қуаныш тудырады) / the excited fans (сезінеді). Жиі жұптар: interesting/interested, tiring/tired, confusing/confused, surprising/surprised, worrying/worried.

ru.title: Сложные прилагательные: дефисные сочетания
ru.html: Сложные прилагательные соединяют слова дефисом перед существительным: a well-known writer, a five-star hotel, a time-consuming task, a world-famous chef, a last-minute change. Частые модели: наречие/сущ./прил. + past participle, или число + сущ.

kk.title: Күрделі сын есімдер: дефисті тіркестер
kk.html: Күрделі сын есімдер зат есім алдында сөздерді дефиспен қосады: a well-known writer, a five-star hotel, a time-consuming task, a world-famous chef, a last-minute change. Жиі үлгі: үстеу/зат есім/сын есім + past participle, не сан + зат есім.

ru.title: Модели сложных прилагательных
ru.html: Частые формулы: прил./сущ. + -ed (blue-eyed, left-handed, old-fashioned); наречие/сущ. + -ing (hard-working, English-speaking, good-looking); число + сущ. (ten-year-old, four-door); сущ. + сущ. как прилагательное. Дефис перед существительным, обычно не после.

kk.title: Күрделі сын есім үлгілері
kk.html: Жиі формулалар: сын есім/зат есім + -ed (blue-eyed, left-handed, old-fashioned); үстеу/зат есім + -ing (hard-working, English-speaking, good-looking); сан + зат есім (ten-year-old, four-door); зат есім + зат есім сын есім ретінде. Зат есім алдында дефистеле, кейін әдетте жоқ.

ru.title: Регистр и дефисы
ru.html: Сложные прилагательные часты в описательном и журналистском письме, экономно упаковывая детали: a state-of-the-art facility. Дефис исчезает, когда слова следуют за существительным: a well-known artist → the artist is well known.

kk.title: Регистр және дефистеу
kk.html: Күрделі сын есімдер сипаттаушы және журналистік жазуда жиі, деталды үнемді жинайды: a state-of-the-art facility. Сөздер зат есімнен кейін тұрса, дефис жоғалады: a well-known artist → the artist is well known.

ru.title: Ошибки продвинутого уровня
ru.html: ✗ I was very boring in the lecture → ✓ I was bored. ✗ a five-years-old boy → ✓ a five-year-old boy. ✗ a well known-writer → ✓ a well-known writer.

kk.title: Жоғары деңгей қателері
kk.html: ✗ I was very boring in the lecture → ✓ I was bored. ✗ a five-years-old boy → ✓ a five-year-old boy. ✗ a well known-writer → ✓ a well-known writer.

ru.title: Кратко
ru.html:
• -ing = вызывает чувство; -ed = испытывает чувство.
  • Тест: существительное вызывает или испытывает эмоцию?
  • Сложные прилагательные с дефисом перед существительным (well-known, five-star).
  • Число + сущ. в единственном (a ten-year-old); дефис исчезает после существительного.

kk.title: Қысқаша
kk.html:
• -ing = сезімді тудырады; -ed = сезімді сезінеді.
  • Тест: зат есім эмоцияны тудыра ма әлде сезіне ме?
  • Күрделі сын есімдер зат есім алдында дефистеледі (well-known, five-star).
  • Сан + зат есім жекешеде (a ten-year-old); зат есімнен кейін дефис түседі.

activities.prompt: Sort each adjective by what it describes.
activities.why: -ing describes the cause of a feeling; -ed describes the person who experiences it.

items.s: The lecture was fascinating.
She's a hard-working colleague.
I'm very boring today, I need a nap.
He's a five-years-old boy.

activities.why: you feel ‘bored’ (not ‘boring’), and the compound is ‘five-year-old’ (singular, hyphenated).

activities.before: By the end of the meeting, everyone felt
activities.why: describing the people's feeling → exhausted.
describing what causes the feeling → surprising.
number + noun compound before a noun → five-star.
two hyphenated compound adjectives before the noun.

activities.q: “I don't understand this at all — it's so ___.”
why: ‘confused’ describes the person, but here we describe the thing.
Correct — the thing causes confusion → confusing.
a bare verb doesn't fit.

activities.q: Complete: ‘The building is ___.’ (state-of-the-art)
why: hyphens are used before a noun (a state-of-the-art building).
Correct — after the verb, it's usually written without hyphens.
the article ‘a’ doesn't fit here.

activities.prompt: Sort each compound adjective by its structure.
activities.why: blue-eyed/old-fashioned = + -ed; hard-working = + -ing; three-bedroom = number + noun.
the children feel the emotion → “really excited about the trip.”

activities.instruction: Rewrite using a hyphenated compound adjective:
activities.prompt: a task that consumes a lot of time
alts: a time-consuming task
time-consuming task
activities.why: noun + -ing compound → a time-consuming task.

activities.scene: Choose the correct -ing/-ed or compound form.

steps.npc: How was the hike up the volcano?

options.t: Absolutely exhausting — but the view was worth it.
options.why: the hike causes the feeling → -ing (exhausting).

options.t: Absolutely exhausted — but the view was worth it.
options.why: to describe the hike (the cause), use ‘exhausting’; ‘exhausted’ describes you.

steps.npc: And the lodge you stayed in?

options.t: Lovely — a proper family-run place, very welcoming.
options.why: ‘family-run’ is a correctly hyphenated compound adjective.

options.t: Lovely — a proper family run place, very welcoming.
options.why: the compound adjective before the noun needs a hyphen: ‘family-run’.

activities.why: Use -ing for the cause and -ed for the feeling, and form compound adjectives with hyphens before a noun (singular number + noun).

learn.title: How adjectives take complements
learn.html: Many adjectives are followed by a complement that completes their meaning: a to-infinitive, a preposition + -ing, a that-clause, or a wh-clause. Which pattern fits is learned per adjective — a key C1 accuracy area.

learn.title: Adjective + to-infinitive
learn.html: Common with adjectives of feeling, ease/difficulty, likelihood: happy to help, glad to see you, easy to use, hard to believe, likely to rain, ready to go, keen to learn. Some allow a ‘for + object’: It's important for us to agree.

learn.title: Adjective + preposition + -ing
learn.html: Many adjectives take a fixed preposition + -ing: good at cooking, interested in learning, tired of waiting, afraid of failing, capable of doing, keen on running, worried about being late. The preposition is fixed per adjective.

learn.title: Adjective + that-clause / wh-clause
learn.html: Adjectives of certainty, emotion, importance take a that-clause: sure that…, glad that…, aware that…, essential that… (subjunctive). Some take a wh-clause: not sure whether/what/how…, unclear why…

learn.title: Meaning differences: to-inf vs -ing
learn.html: A few adjectives shift with the complement: I'm sorry to tell you (about a future/present act) vs I'm sorry for telling you (about a past act). afraid to do (reluctant) vs afraid of doing (fearing a consequence). Choose to match the meaning.

learn.title: Advanced learner errors
learn.html:
✗ good in cooking → ✓ good at cooking.

✗ interested to learn languages (general) → ✓ interested in learning languages.

✗ I'm keen learn. → ✓ I'm keen to learn / keen on learning.

learn.title: In a nutshell
learn.html:
• Adjective + to-infinitive: happy/easy/likely/ready to do.
  • Adjective + preposition + -ing: good at, interested in, afraid of.
  • Adjective + that-clause (certainty/emotion) or wh-clause (uncertainty).
  • to-inf vs -ing can shift meaning (sorry to tell / sorry for telling).

ru.title: Как прилагательные берут дополнения
ru.html: Многие прилагательные берут дополнение, завершающее их смысл: to-infinitive, предлог + -ing, that-clause, или wh-clause. Какая модель подходит, учится по прилагательному — ключевая точка точности C1.

kk.title: Сын есімдер толықтауышты қалай алады
kk.html: Көп сын есім мағынасын аяқтайтын толықтауыш алады: to-infinitive, предлог + -ing, that-сөйлем, не wh-сөйлем. Қай үлгі жарайтыны әр сын есімге үйреніледі — маңызды C1 дәлдік аймағы.

ru.title: Прилагательное + to-infinitive
ru.html: Часто с прилагательными чувства, лёгкости/трудности, вероятности: happy to help, glad to see you, easy to use, hard to believe, likely to rain, ready to go, keen to learn. Некоторые допускают ‘for + объект’: It's important for us to agree.

kk.title: Сын есім + to-infinitive
kk.html: Сезім, жеңілдік/қиындық, ықтималдық сын есімдерімен жиі: happy to help, glad to see you, easy to use, hard to believe, likely to rain, ready to go, keen to learn. Кейбірі ‘for + объект’ алады: It's important for us to agree.

ru.title: Прилагательное + предлог + -ing
ru.html: Многие прилагательные берут фиксированный предлог + -ing: good at cooking, interested in learning, tired of waiting, afraid of failing, capable of doing, keen on running, worried about being late. Предлог фиксирован для каждого.

kk.title: Сын есім + предлог + -ing
kk.html: Көп сын есім тұрақты предлог + -ing алады: good at cooking, interested in learning, tired of waiting, afraid of failing, capable of doing, keen on running, worried about being late. Предлог әр сын есімге бекітілген.

ru.title: Прилагательное + that-clause / wh-clause
ru.html: Прилагательные уверенности, эмоции, важности берут that-clause: sure that…, glad that…, aware that…, essential that… (subjunctive). Некоторые берут wh-clause: not sure whether/what/how…, unclear why…

kk.title: Сын есім + that-сөйлем / wh-сөйлем
kk.html: Сенімділік, эмоция, маңыздылық сын есімдері that-сөйлем алады: sure that…, glad that…, aware that…, essential that… (subjunctive). Кейбірі wh-сөйлем алады: not sure whether/what/how…, unclear why…

ru.title: Различия значения: to-inf vs -ing
ru.html: Несколько прилагательных меняются с дополнением: I'm sorry to tell you (о будущем/настоящем действии) / I'm sorry for telling you (о прошлом). afraid to do (нежелание) / afraid of doing (страх последствия). Выбирай по смыслу.

kk.title: Мағына айырмасы: to-inf vs -ing
kk.html: Бірнеше сын есім толықтауышпен ауысады: I'm sorry to tell you (болашақ/қазіргі әрекет туралы) / I'm sorry for telling you (өткен әрекет туралы). afraid to do (қаламау) / afraid of doing (салдардан қорқу). Мағынаға қарай таңда.

ru.title: Ошибки продвинутого уровня
ru.html: ✗ good in cooking → ✓ good at cooking. ✗ interested to learn languages (в целом) → ✓ interested in learning languages. ✗ I'm keen learn → ✓ I'm keen to learn / keen on learning.

kk.title: Жоғары деңгей қателері
kk.html: ✗ good in cooking → ✓ good at cooking. ✗ interested to learn languages (жалпы) → ✓ interested in learning languages. ✗ I'm keen learn → ✓ I'm keen to learn / keen on learning.

ru.title: Кратко
ru.html:
• Прилагательное + to-infinitive: happy/easy/likely/ready to do.
  • Прилагательное + предлог + -ing: good at, interested in, afraid of.
  • Прилагательное + that-clause (уверенность/эмоция) или wh-clause (неопределённость).
  • to-inf vs -ing может менять смысл (sorry to tell / sorry for telling).

kk.title: Қысқаша
kk.html:
• Сын есім + to-infinitive: happy/easy/likely/ready to do.
  • Сын есім + предлог + -ing: good at, interested in, afraid of.
  • Сын есім + that-сөйлем (сенімділік/эмоция) не wh-сөйлем (белгісіздік).
  • to-inf vs -ing мағынаны ауыстыруы мүмкін (sorry to tell / sorry for telling).

activities.typeLabel: Which complement does the adjective take?
activities.prompt: Sort each adjective by its usual complement.
activities.why: likely/ready + to-inf; good at/tired of + -ing; aware/sure + that-clause.

items.s: She's keen to start the course.
I'm not very good at drawing.
He's interested to learn Arabic.
We're afraid of to fail.

activities.why: ‘interested in learning’ (preposition + -ing) and ‘afraid of failing’ (not ‘of to fail’).
‘tired of + -ing’ → tired of waiting.

activities.before: This new software is really easy
activities.why: ‘easy + to-infinitive’ → easy to use.
‘delighted + that-clause’ → delighted that…
‘capable of + -ing’.

activities.q: “I'm sorry ___ that your application was unsuccessful.” (delivering the news now)
why: Correct — ‘sorry to tell you’ for a present/immediate act of telling.
‘sorry for telling’ apologises for a past act.
‘of telling’ isn't the pattern for ‘sorry’.

activities.q: Complete: “She's brilliant ___ solving problems.”
why: ‘good/brilliant’ takes ‘at’, not ‘in’.
Correct — ‘good/brilliant at + -ing’.
‘of’ doesn't collocate here.

activities.typeLabel: Which preposition does the adjective take?
activities.prompt: Match each adjective to its fixed preposition.
activities.why: skilled/hopeless at; interested in; capable of — fixed adjective + preposition collocations.
‘interested’ takes ‘in’ before a noun → “interested in photography.”

activities.instruction: Rewrite using ‘worried about + -ing’:
activities.prompt: She worries that she might miss the deadline.
activities.answer: she's worried about missing the deadline
alts: She's worried about missing the deadline
she is worried about missing the deadline
activities.why: ‘worried about + -ing’ → worried about missing the deadline.

steps.npc: Why are you drawn to this role?

options.t: I'm keen to take on more responsibility, honestly.
options.why: ‘keen + to-infinitive’.

options.t: I'm keen taking on more responsibility, honestly.
options.why: ‘keen’ takes ‘to + infinitive’ (or ‘keen on + -ing’), not a bare -ing.

steps.npc: Are you comfortable managing a team?

options.t: Yes — I'm confident about leading people.
options.why: ‘confident about + -ing’ is a correct pattern.

options.t: Yes — I'm confident to leading people.
options.why: ‘confident’ takes ‘about + -ing’ or ‘that…’, not ‘to leading’.

activities.why: Learn each adjective's complement: to-infinitive (ready/easy), preposition + -ing (good at/afraid of), or that/wh-clause (sure/unclear), and mind meaning shifts.

learn.title: Adjective or adverb — modifying what?
learn.html: An adjective describes a noun; an adverb describes a verb, adjective or another adverb: a quick reply (adj) vs reply quickly (adv). After a linking verb, use an adjective (it describes the subject): She seems happy, not ‘happily’.

learn.title: Confusable pairs
learn.html: good (adj) vs well (adv): a good singer / sings well. But well is also an adjective meaning ‘healthy’ (I feel well). hard = adj and adverb (work hard); hardly = ‘almost not’. late vs lately (= recently); near vs nearly.

learn.title: Flat adverbs (no -ly)
learn.html: Some adverbs have the same form as the adjective (‘flat adverbs’): drive fast, work hard, arrive early, aim high, fly direct, get up late. ✗ drive fastly. A few have both forms with different meanings (hard/hardly, late/lately).

learn.title: Linking verbs vs action verbs
learn.html: The tricky cases involve verbs that can be linking or action: She looked happy (linking, adjective) vs She looked carefully (action, adverb). The soup tastes good (linking) vs He tasted the soup carefully (action).

learn.title: Register & ‘good/well’ in speech
learn.html: In informal speech, ‘I'm good’ (feeling fine) is common, but ‘I did well’ (adverb) is standard for performance. Confusing good/well or adding -ly to flat adverbs (‘fastly’) marks a learner. Precise adjective/adverb use is a clear accuracy signal.

learn.title: Advanced learner errors
learn.html:
✗ She sings very good. → ✓ She sings very well.

✗ Drive slowly and safe. → ✓ … safely.

✗ I've been working hardly. → ✓ … working hard (‘hardly’ = almost not).

learn.title: In a nutshell
learn.html:
• Adjectives modify nouns; adverbs modify verbs/adjectives/adverbs.
  • Use an adjective after a linking verb (seem/look/taste + adjective).
  • Flat adverbs (fast, hard, early) have no -ly; hardly/lately differ in meaning.
  • good (adj) vs well (adv, also ‘healthy’); watch look/taste as linking vs action verbs.

ru.title: Прилагательное или наречие — что модифицирует?
ru.html: Прилагательное описывает существительное; наречие — глагол, прилагательное или другое наречие: a quick reply (прил.) / reply quickly (нар.). После связочного глагола — прилагательное (описывает подлежащее): She seems happy, не ‘happily’.

kk.title: Сын есім бе әлде үстеу — нені анықтайды?
kk.html: Сын есім зат есімді сипаттайды; үстеу етістік, сын есім не басқа үстеуді сипаттайды: a quick reply (сын есім) / reply quickly (үстеу). Байланыстырушы етістіктен кейін сын есім қолдан (бастауышты сипаттайды): She seems happy, ‘happily’ емес.

ru.title: Путающие пары
ru.html: good (прил.) vs well (нар.): a good singer / sings well. Но well ещё и прилагательное ‘здоровый’ (I feel well). hard = прил. и наречие (work hard); hardly = ‘почти не’. late vs lately (= недавно); near vs nearly.

kk.title: Шатастыратын жұптар
kk.html: good (сын есім) vs well (үстеу): a good singer / sings well. Бірақ well ‘сау’ мағынасында сын есім де (I feel well). hard = сын есім әрі үстеу (work hard); hardly = ‘дерлік емес’. late vs lately (= жақында); near vs nearly.

ru.title: Плоские наречия (без -ly)
ru.html: Некоторые наречия имеют ту же форму, что и прилагательное (‘плоские наречия’): drive fast, work hard, arrive early, aim high, fly direct, get up late. ✗ drive fastly. У некоторых есть обе формы с разным значением (hard/hardly, late/lately).

kk.title: Жалаң үстеулер (-ly жоқ)
kk.html: Кейбір үстеудің сын есіммен бірдей формасы бар (‘жалаң үстеулер’): drive fast, work hard, arrive early, aim high, fly direct, get up late. ✗ drive fastly. Бірнешеуінің әртүрлі мағынамен екі формасы бар (hard/hardly, late/lately).

ru.title: Связочные vs глаголы действия
ru.html: Трудные случаи — глаголы, которые могут быть связочными или действия: She looked happy (связочный, прил.) / She looked carefully (действие, наречие). The soup tastes good (связочный) / He tasted the soup carefully (действие).

kk.title: Байланыстырушы vs әрекет етістіктері
kk.html: Қиын жағдайлар байланыстырушы не әрекет бола алатын етістіктерде: She looked happy (байланыстырушы, сын есім) / She looked carefully (әрекет, үстеу). The soup tastes good (байланыстырушы) / He tasted the soup carefully (әрекет).

ru.title: Регистр и ‘good/well’ в речи
ru.html: В неформальной речи ‘I'm good’ (чувствую себя хорошо) распространено, но для результата стандарт ‘I did well’ (наречие). Путаница good/well или добавление -ly к плоским наречиям (‘fastly’) выдаёт учащегося. Точное употребление — явный сигнал точности.

kk.title: Регистр және тілдегі ‘good/well’
kk.html: Бейресми тілде ‘I'm good’ (жақсы сезінемін) жиі, бірақ орындауға ‘I did well’ (үстеу) стандарт. good/well-ды шатастыру не жалаң үстеуге -ly қосу (‘fastly’) үйренушіні көрсетеді. Дәл сын есім/үстеу қолданысы — анық дәлдік белгісі.

ru.title: Ошибки продвинутого уровня
ru.html: ✗ She sings very good → ✓ She sings very well. ✗ Drive slowly and safe → ✓ … safely. ✗ I've been working hardly → ✓ … working hard (‘hardly’ = почти не).

kk.title: Жоғары деңгей қателері
kk.html: ✗ She sings very good → ✓ She sings very well. ✗ Drive slowly and safe → ✓ … safely. ✗ I've been working hardly → ✓ … working hard (‘hardly’ = дерлік емес).

ru.title: Кратко
ru.html:
• Прилагательные модифицируют существительные; наречия — глаголы/прилагательные/наречия.
  • После связочного глагола — прилагательное (seem/look/taste + прил.).
  • Плоские наречия (fast, hard, early) без -ly; hardly/lately отличаются по смыслу.
  • good (прил.) vs well (нар., также ‘здоровый’); следи за look/taste как связочными vs действия.

kk.title: Қысқаша
kk.html:
• Сын есім зат есімді; үстеу етістік/сын есім/үстеуді анықтайды.
