activities.prompt: We were late because the traffic was heavy.
activities.answer: we were late because of the heavy traffic
alts: We were late because of the heavy traffic
we were late because of heavy traffic
activities.why: convert the clause to a noun phrase → because of the heavy traffic.

steps.npc: Why did you pull out of the deal in the end?

options.t: Mainly because the terms kept changing.
options.why: ‘because’ + clause gives the key reason.

options.t: Mainly because of the terms kept changing.
options.why: ‘because of’ needs a noun; with a clause use ‘because the terms kept changing’.

steps.npc: Was the timing a factor too?

options.t: Yes — with the market so unstable, it felt too risky.
options.why: ‘with + noun + adjective’ states the attendant circumstance.

options.t: Yes — due to the market was so unstable, it felt too risky.
options.why: ‘due to’ needs a noun; use ‘with the market so unstable’ or ‘because the market was unstable’.

activities.why: Use because for the key reason (clause), as/since for known reasons, because of/due to/owing to + noun, and with + noun for an attendant circumstance.

learn.title: Purpose vs result — a key distinction
learn.html: Purpose = the intended aim (why you do something); result = the actual outcome (what happens). English uses different structures for each, and confusing them is a common C1 slip.

learn.title: Expressing purpose
learn.html: to / in order to / so as to + infinitive (same subject): I left early to catch / in order to catch / so as to catch the train. Negatives: in order not to / so as not to (✗ ‘to not’). For a different subject, use so that / in order that + clause: I spoke slowly so that everyone could follow.

learn.title: Expressing result: so … that / such … that
learn.html: so + adjective/adverb + that; such + (a) noun phrase + that: It was so cold that the lake froze. · It was such a cold day that the lake froze. Also so much/many … that: There was so much noise that we left.

learn.title: for + noun/-ing; result connectors
learn.html: Purpose can also be for + noun/-ing (the function): a knife for cutting bread. Result connectors between sentences: so, therefore, consequently, as a result, thus: It rained; therefore, the match was postponed.

learn.title: Register & precision
learn.html: in order to / so as to and in order that are more formal than plain to / so that. therefore/consequently are formal result markers; so is neutral. Choosing purpose vs result structures accurately signals control of cause-effect logic.

learn.title: Advanced learner errors
learn.html:
✗ I study hard for pass the exam. → ✓ … to/in order to pass.

✗ so as to not fail → ✓ so as not to fail.

✗ It was so a good film… → ✓ It was such a good film that…

learn.title: In a nutshell
learn.html:
• Purpose (same subject): to / in order to / so as to + infinitive; negative = in order/so as not to.
  • Purpose (different subject): so that / in order that + clause.
  • Result: so + adj/adv + that; such (a) + noun + that; so much/many … that.
  • Purpose also for + noun/-ing; result connectors: therefore, consequently, as a result.

ru.title: Цель vs результат — ключевое различие
ru.html: Цель = задуманное намерение (зачем делаешь); результат = фактический итог (что происходит). Английский использует разные структуры для каждого, и их путаница — частая ошибка C1.

kk.title: Мақсат vs нәтиже — басты айырма
kk.html: Мақсат = көзделген мұрат (неге істейсің); нәтиже = нақты қорытынды (не болады). Ағылшын әрқайсысына басқа құрылым қолданады, оларды шатастыру — жиі C1 қателігі.

ru.title: Выражение цели
ru.html: to / in order to / so as to + инфинитив (то же подлежащее): I left early to catch / in order to catch / so as to catch the train. Отрицание: in order not to / so as not to (✗ ‘to not’). Для другого подлежащего — so that / in order that + клауза: I spoke slowly so that everyone could follow.

kk.title: Мақсатты білдіру
kk.html: to / in order to / so as to + инфинитив (бірдей бастауыш): I left early to catch / in order to catch / so as to catch the train. Болымсыз: in order not to / so as not to (✗ ‘to not’). Басқа бастауышқа so that / in order that + сөйлем: I spoke slowly so that everyone could follow.

ru.title: Выражение результата: so … that / such … that
ru.html: so + прил./наречие + that; such + (a) именная группа + that: It was so cold that the lake froze. It was such a cold day that the lake froze. Также so much/many … that: There was so much noise that we left.

kk.title: Нәтижені білдіру: so … that / such … that
kk.html: so + сын есім/үстеу + that; such + (a) есімді тіркес + that: It was so cold that the lake froze. It was such a cold day that the lake froze. Сондай-ақ so much/many … that: There was so much noise that we left.

ru.title: for + сущ./-ing; коннекторы результата
ru.html: Цель также может быть for + сущ./-ing (функция): a knife for cutting bread. Коннекторы результата между предложениями: so, therefore, consequently, as a result, thus: It rained; therefore, the match was postponed.

kk.title: for + зат есім/-ing; нәтиже жалғаулары
kk.html: Мақсат for + зат есім/-ing да болады (функция): a knife for cutting bread. Сөйлемдер арасындағы нәтиже жалғаулары: so, therefore, consequently, as a result, thus: It rained; therefore, the match was postponed.

ru.title: Регистр и точность
ru.html: in order to / so as to и in order that формальнее простых to / so that. therefore/consequently — формальные маркеры результата; so нейтрально. Точный выбор структур цели vs результата показывает контроль логики причины-следствия.

kk.title: Регистр және дәлдік
kk.html: in order to / so as to және in order that жай to / so that-тан ресмилеу. therefore/consequently ресми нәтиже белгілері; so бейтарап. Мақсат vs нәтиже құрылымдарын дәл таңдау себеп-салдар логикасын бақылауды көрсетеді.

ru.title: Ошибки продвинутого уровня
ru.html: ✗ I study hard for pass the exam → ✓ … to/in order to pass. ✗ so as to not fail → ✓ so as not to fail. ✗ It was so a good film… → ✓ It was such a good film that…

kk.title: Жоғары деңгей қателері
kk.html: ✗ I study hard for pass the exam → ✓ … to/in order to pass. ✗ so as to not fail → ✓ so as not to fail. ✗ It was so a good film… → ✓ It was such a good film that…

ru.title: Кратко
ru.html:
• Цель (то же подлежащее): to / in order to / so as to + инфинитив; отриц. = in order/so as not to.
  • Цель (другое подлежащее): so that / in order that + клауза.
  • Результат: so + прил./наречие + that; such (a) + сущ. + that; so much/many … that.
  • Цель также for + сущ./-ing; результат: therefore, consequently, as a result.

kk.title: Қысқаша
kk.html:
• Мақсат (бірдей бастауыш): to / in order to / so as to + инфинитив; болымсыз = in order/so as not to.
  • Мақсат (басқа бастауыш): so that / in order that + сөйлем.
  • Нәтиже: so + сын есім/үстеу + that; such (a) + зат есім + that; so much/many … that.
  • Мақсат for + зат есім/-ing та; нәтиже: therefore, consequently, as a result.

activities.prompt: Sort each structure.

items.t: so tired that I fell asleep
such a mess that we cleaned up
so much rain that it flooded

activities.why: Purpose expresses the intended aim; result expresses the actual outcome (so/such … that).

items.s: She whispered so as not to wake the baby.
It was such a long queue that we gave up.
I saved up for buy a car.
He trained hard so as to not lose.

activities.why: purpose uses ‘to/in order to buy’ (not ‘for buy’), and the negative is ‘so as not to lose’.

activities.after: more people could afford it. (so that)
activities.why: a different subject (people) needs a clause → so that more people could afford it.

activities.after: a moving speech that many people cried. (so/such)
activities.why: ‘such + a + adjective + noun + that’ → such a moving speech that…

alts: in order not to
activities.why: negative purpose → so as not to mix them up.
‘so + adjective + that’ expresses result.

activities.q: Complete: “There were ___ many applicants that we extended the deadline.”
why: Correct — ‘so many + noun + that’.
‘such’ needs ‘such a lot of applicants’ or a noun phrase.
‘such a’ can't precede a plural count noun directly.

activities.q: Which continues ‘The bridge was unsafe; ___ it was closed.’ (formal result)
why: ‘so that’ introduces purpose, not a result between sentences.
Correct — ‘therefore’ is a formal result connector.
‘in order to’ marks purpose.

activities.prompt: Sort each purpose structure by which it needs.
buckets: same subject (to/in order to/so as to + inf)

items.t: I called so that she could check.
so that the team can rest

activities.why: to/in order to/so as to need the same subject; so that + clause allows a different subject.
purpose with a different subject needs ‘so that everyone could hear’ (not ‘for everyone to hear’ in this position) → “so that everyone could hear him.”

activities.instruction: Combine using ‘so … that’:
activities.prompt: The instructions were very confusing. Nobody could follow them.
activities.answer: the instructions were so confusing that nobody could follow them
alts: The instructions were so confusing that nobody could follow them
the instructions were so confusing that nobody could follow them.
activities.why: ‘so + adjective + that’ expresses the result → so confusing that nobody could follow them.

steps.npc: Why are you starting the project so early?

options.t: In order to leave enough time for testing.
options.why: purpose with the same subject → in order to + infinitive.

options.t: For leave enough time for testing.
options.why: purpose takes ‘to/in order to leave’, not ‘for leave’.

steps.npc: Is the timeline realistic?

options.t: It's so tight that we've had to drop some features.
options.why: ‘so + adjective + that’ expresses the result.

options.t: It's so tight so that we've had to drop some features.
options.why: for a result, use ‘so tight that’; ‘so that’ would introduce a purpose.

activities.why: Express purpose with to/in order to/so as to (+ so that for a different subject) and result with so/such … that, keeping the negative as so as/in order not to.

learn.title: Concession vs contrast
learn.html: Concession = ‘despite this, that’ (although, even though, though); contrast = ‘this, but that is different’ (while, whereas). Both link two ideas that clash, but concession admits an obstacle while contrast sets two things side by side.

learn.title: although / though / even though
learn.html: All introduce a concessive clause: Although/Though it was late, we carried on. even though is more emphatic (a stronger contrast); though can also go at the end (informal): It was late. We carried on, though. Don't add ‘but’ in the main clause.

learn.title: while / whereas (direct contrast)
learn.html: while and whereas set two facts in direct contrast, usually of equal weight: Whereas the north is industrial, the south is agricultural. · She likes coffee, while he prefers tea. whereas is more formal; both often front the clause.

learn.title: despite / in spite of + noun/-ing
learn.html: despite / in spite of take a noun or -ing, not a clause: Despite the rain / the fact that it rained, we went. · In spite of being tired, she carried on. To use a clause, add the fact that. Contrast connectors between sentences: however, nevertheless, on the other hand, still, yet.

learn.title: even if vs even though
learn.html: even though = ‘despite the fact that’ (real): Even though he apologised, she was angry (he did apologise). even if = ‘whether or not’ (hypothetical): Even if he apologises, she'll be angry (he may not). A frequently confused pair.

learn.title: Advanced learner errors
learn.html:
✗ Although it was late, but we continued. → ✓ (drop ‘but’).

✗ Despite it was raining → ✓ Despite the rain / Despite the fact that it was raining.

✗ Even though he comes tomorrow, I'll be out (hypothetical) → ✓ Even if he comes…

learn.title: In a nutshell
learn.html:
• although/though/even though + clause (concession); no ‘but’ in the main clause.
  • while/whereas set two facts in direct contrast (whereas is formal).
  • despite/in spite of + noun/-ing/the fact that; however/nevertheless link sentences.
  • even though = real fact; even if = hypothetical (whether or not).

ru.title: Уступка vs контраст
ru.html: Уступка = ‘несмотря на это’ (although, even though, though); контраст = ‘это, но то иначе’ (while, whereas). Оба связывают две противоречащие идеи, но уступка признаёт препятствие, а контраст ставит две вещи рядом.

kk.title: Мойындау vs қарама-қарсылық
kk.html: Мойындау = ‘осыған қарамастан’ (although, even though, though); қарама-қарсылық = ‘бұл, бірақ ол басқа’ (while, whereas). Екеуі де қайшы екі ойды байланыстырады, бірақ мойындау кедергіні мойындайды, қарама-қарсылық екеуін қатар қояды.

ru.title: although / though / even though
ru.html: Все вводят уступительную клаузу: Although/Though it was late, we carried on. even though эмфатичнее (сильнее контраст); though может идти и в конец (неформально): It was late. We carried on, though. Не добавляй ‘but’ в главную клаузу.

kk.title: although / though / even though
kk.html: Бәрі мойындау сөйлемін енгізеді: Although/Though it was late, we carried on. even though екпіндірек (күштірек қарама-қарсылық); though соңында да (бейресми): It was late. We carried on, though. Басты сөйлемге ‘but’ қоспа.

ru.title: while / whereas (прямой контраст)
ru.html: while и whereas ставят два факта в прямой контраст, обычно равного веса: Whereas the north is industrial, the south is agricultural. She likes coffee, while he prefers tea. whereas формальнее; оба часто выносят клаузу вперёд.

kk.title: while / whereas (тікелей қарама-қарсылық)
kk.html: while мен whereas екі фактіні тікелей қарама-қарсы қояды, әдетте тең салмақта: Whereas the north is industrial, the south is agricultural. She likes coffee, while he prefers tea. whereas ресмилеу; екеуі де жиі сөйлемді алға шығарады.

ru.title: despite / in spite of + сущ./-ing
ru.html: despite / in spite of берут существительное или -ing, не клаузу: Despite the rain / the fact that it rained, we went. In spite of being tired, she carried on. Для клаузы добавь the fact that. Коннекторы контраста между предложениями: however, nevertheless, on the other hand, still, yet.

kk.title: despite / in spite of + зат есім/-ing
kk.html: despite / in spite of сөйлем емес, зат есім не -ing алады: Despite the rain / the fact that it rained, we went. In spite of being tired, she carried on. Сөйлем қолдану үшін the fact that қос. Сөйлемдер арасындағы қарама-қарсылық: however, nevertheless, on the other hand, still, yet.

ru.title: even if vs even though
ru.html: even though = ‘несмотря на то, что’ (реально): Even though he apologised, she was angry (он извинился). even if = ‘даже если’ (гипотетически): Even if he apologises, she'll be angry (может и не извиниться). Часто путаемая пара.

kk.title: even if vs even though
kk.html: even though = ‘факт болса да’ (нақты): Even though he apologised, she was angry (кешірім сұрады). even if = ‘болса да болмаса да’ (болжамды): Even if he apologises, she'll be angry (сұрамауы мүмкін). Жиі шатастырылатын жұп.

ru.title: Ошибки продвинутого уровня
ru.html: ✗ Although it was late, but we continued → ✓ (убери ‘but’). ✗ Despite it was raining → ✓ Despite the rain / Despite the fact that it was raining. ✗ Even though he comes tomorrow, I'll be out (гипотетически) → ✓ Even if he comes…

kk.title: Жоғары деңгей қателері
kk.html: ✗ Although it was late, but we continued → ✓ (‘but’-ты түсір). ✗ Despite it was raining → ✓ Despite the rain / Despite the fact that it was raining. ✗ Even though he comes tomorrow, I'll be out (болжамды) → ✓ Even if he comes…

ru.title: Кратко
ru.html:
• although/though/even though + клауза (уступка); без ‘but’ в главной клаузе.
  • while/whereas ставят два факта в прямой контраст (whereas формально).
  • despite/in spite of + сущ./-ing/the fact that; however/nevertheless связывают предложения.
  • even though = реальный факт; even if = гипотетически (даже если).

kk.title: Қысқаша
kk.html:
• although/though/even though + сөйлем (мойындау); басты сөйлемде ‘but’ жоқ.
  • while/whereas екі фактіні тікелей қарама-қарсы қояды (whereas ресми).
  • despite/in spite of + зат есім/-ing/the fact that; however/nevertheless сөйлемдерді байланыстырады.
  • even though = нақты факт; even if = болжамды (болса да болмаса да).

activities.prompt: Sort each contrast word.
activities.why: although/even though/whereas take a clause; despite/in spite of take a noun or -ing (add ‘the fact that’ for a clause).

items.s: Despite the delay, we caught the connection.
Whereas he is cautious, she is bold.
Although it was hard, but we finished.
Despite it was cold, we swam.

activities.why: don't add ‘but’ after an ‘although’ clause; ‘despite’ needs a noun (Despite the cold).

activities.after: she had little experience, she got the job. (although/despite)
alts: Even though
Though
activities.why: a concessive clause → Although she had little experience.

activities.after: feeling unwell, he gave the presentation. (despite)
alts: In spite of
activities.why: ‘despite + -ing’ → Despite feeling unwell.

alts: while
activities.why: two facts set in direct contrast → whereas village life is calm.
‘even if’ = whether or not (hypothetical condition).

activities.q: “___ you beg me, I won't change my mind.” (you might or might not beg)
why: ‘even though’ presents it as a real fact (you did beg).
Correct — ‘even if’ marks a hypothetical (whether or not you beg).
‘despite’ needs a noun/-ing.

activities.typeLabel: Turn a clause into a despite-phrase
activities.q: Rewrite ‘Although he was exhausted, he kept going’ with ‘despite’:
options: Despite he was exhausted, he kept going.
Despite being exhausted, he kept going.
why: ‘despite’ can't take a clause directly.
Correct — ‘Despite being exhausted’ (-ing form).
‘despite exhausted’ is missing a noun/-ing.

activities.prompt: Sort each linker by its function.
activities.why: even though/in spite of concede an obstacle; whereas/while set two facts side by side.

activities.correct: the terrible weather / the fact that
activities.why: ‘in spite of’ needs a noun/-ing → “In spite of the terrible weather” (or ‘the fact that the weather was terrible’).

activities.instruction: Rewrite using ‘despite’ + a noun phrase:
activities.prompt: Although the price was high, the tickets sold out.
activities.answer: despite the high price, the tickets sold out
alts: Despite the high price, the tickets sold out
despite the high price the tickets sold out
activities.why: convert the clause to a noun phrase → Despite the high price, the tickets sold out.

steps.npc: You still went ahead with the launch?

options.t: Yes — despite the risks, we felt the timing was right.
options.why: ‘despite + noun’ concedes the risks.

options.t: Yes — despite the risks were high, we felt the timing was right.
options.why: ‘despite’ takes a noun/-ing; use ‘despite the risks’ or ‘despite the fact that the risks were high’.

steps.npc: How does it compare with the last launch?

options.t: This one was smooth, whereas the last one was chaotic.
options.why: ‘whereas’ sets two facts in direct contrast.

options.t: This one was smooth, whereas the last one was chaotic, but it was different.
options.why: ‘whereas’ already carries the contrast; the tacked-on ‘but it was different’ is redundant.

activities.why: Use although/even though + clause (no ‘but’), while/whereas for direct contrast, despite/in spite of + noun/-ing, and distinguish even though (real) from even if (hypothetical).

learn.title: The main conditional patterns
learn.html: Four core types: zero (general truths: If you heat ice, it melts), first (real future: If it rains, we'll stay in), second (unreal present/future: If I had time, I'd help), third (unreal past: If I'd known, I'd have come).

learn.title: The tense frames
learn.html:
zeroif + present, present
firstif + present, will/can/may + inf
secondif + past, would/could/might + inf
thirdif + past perfect, would have + pp

learn.title: No ‘will’ / ‘would’ in the if-clause
learn.html: The classic rule: keep will/would out of the if-clause: ✗ If it will rain… → ✓ If it rains… Exception: will for willingness/politeness (If you'll just wait a moment…) and would in polite requests.

learn.title: First vs second: how likely?
learn.html: The choice signals probability. First = a real, likely possibility: If I get the job, I'll celebrate. Second = unlikely, imaginary or hypothetical: If I won the lottery, I'd travel. Same situation, different stance on likelihood.

learn.title: Variations: could/might, imperatives
learn.html: Modals vary the meaning: If you're free, you could join us (possibility), … it might help (tentative). The result can be an imperative: If you see her, tell her to call. unless = ‘if not’ (covered later).

learn.title: Advanced learner errors
learn.html:
✗ If I will see him, I'll tell him. → ✓ If I see him…

✗ If I would have time, I'd help. → ✓ If I had time…

✗ If it rains, we stay in (for a specific future) → ✓ … we'll stay in.

learn.title: In a nutshell
learn.html:
• Zero: present/present; first: present/will; second: past/would; third: past perfect/would have.
  • Keep will/would out of the if-clause (except willingness/politeness).
  • First = likely; second = unlikely/imaginary.
  • Vary with could/might; the result can be an imperative.

ru.title: Основные условные типы
ru.html: Четыре основных типа: нулевой (общие истины: If you heat ice, it melts), первый (реальное будущее: If it rains, we'll stay in), второй (нереальное настоящее/будущее: If I had time, I'd help), третий (нереальное прошлое: If I'd known, I'd have come).

kk.title: Негізгі шартты үлгілер
kk.html: Төрт негізгі түр: нөлдік (жалпы шындық: If you heat ice, it melts), бірінші (нақты болашақ: If it rains, we'll stay in), екінші (шындыққа жанаспайтын осы/болашақ: If I had time, I'd help), үшінші (шындыққа жанаспайтын өткен: If I'd known, I'd have come).

ru.title: Временны́е рамки
ru.html:
нулевойif + present, present
первыйif + present, will/can/may + inf
второйif + past, would/could/might + inf
третийif + past perfect, would have + pp

kk.title: Шақ шеңберлері
kk.html:
нөлдікif + осы, осы
біріншіif + осы, will/can/may + инф
екіншіif + өткен, would/could/might + инф
үшіншіif + past perfect, would have + pp

ru.title: Без ‘will’ / ‘would’ в if-клаузе
ru.html: Классическое правило: держи will/would вне if-клаузы: ✗ If it will rain… → ✓ If it rains… Исключение: will для готовности/вежливости (If you'll just wait a moment…) и would в вежливых просьбах.

kk.title: if-сөйлемде ‘will’ / ‘would’ жоқ
kk.html: Классикалық ереже: will/would-ды if-сөйлемнен тыс ұста: ✗ If it will rain… → ✓ If it rains… Ерекшелік: will келісім/сыпайылыққа (If you'll just wait a moment…) және would сыпайы өтініште.

ru.title: Первый vs второй: насколько вероятно?
ru.html: Выбор сигнализирует о вероятности. Первый = реальная, вероятная возможность: If I get the job, I'll celebrate. Второй = маловероятное, воображаемое или гипотетическое: If I won the lottery, I'd travel. Одна ситуация, разная позиция о вероятности.

kk.title: Бірінші vs екінші: қаншалық ықтимал?
kk.html: Таңдау ықтималдықты білдіреді. Бірінші = нақты, ықтимал мүмкіндік: If I get the job, I'll celebrate. Екінші = екіталай, қиялдағы не болжамды: If I won the lottery, I'd travel. Бірдей жағдай, ықтималдыққа әртүрлі көзқарас.

ru.title: Варианты: could/might, императивы
ru.html: Модальные варьируют смысл: If you're free, you could join us (возможность), … it might help (осторожно). Результат может быть императивом: If you see her, tell her to call. unless = ‘если не’ (позже).

kk.title: Нұсқалар: could/might, бұйрық
kk.html: Модальдар мағынаны түрлендіреді: If you're free, you could join us (мүмкіндік), … it might help (сақ). Нәтиже бұйрық та болуы мүмкін: If you see her, tell her to call. unless = ‘if not’ (кейін).

ru.title: Ошибки продвинутого уровня
ru.html: ✗ If I will see him, I'll tell him → ✓ If I see him… ✗ If I would have time, I'd help → ✓ If I had time… ✗ If it rains, we stay in (конкретное будущее) → ✓ … we'll stay in.

kk.title: Жоғары деңгей қателері
kk.html: ✗ If I will see him, I'll tell him → ✓ If I see him… ✗ If I would have time, I'd help → ✓ If I had time… ✗ If it rains, we stay in (нақты болашақ) → ✓ … we'll stay in.

ru.title: Кратко
ru.html:
• Нулевой: present/present; первый: present/will; второй: past/would; третий: past perfect/would have.
  • Держи will/would вне if-клаузы (кроме готовности/вежливости).
  • Первый = вероятно; второй = маловероятно/воображаемо.
  • Варьируй через could/might; результат может быть императивом.

kk.title: Қысқаша
kk.html:
• Нөлдік: осы/осы; бірінші: осы/will; екінші: өткен/would; үшінші: past perfect/would have.
  • will/would-ды if-сөйлемнен тыс ұста (келісім/сыпайылықтан басқа).
  • Бірінші = ықтимал; екінші = екіталай/қиялдағы.
  • could/might-пен түрлендір; нәтиже бұйрық болуы мүмкін.

activities.prompt: Sort each sentence.

items.t: If you press this, it beeps.
If it snows, we'll build a snowman.
If I were taller, I'd play basketball.
If she'd studied, she'd have passed.
If I have time, I'll call.
If I won, I'd share it.

activities.why: Zero/first conditionals describe real situations; second/third describe unreal/hypothetical ones.

items.s: If you heat water to 100°C, it boils.
If I had more money, I'd travel more.
If it will rain, we'll cancel.
If I would know, I'd tell you.

activities.why: keep will/would out of the if-clause: ‘If it rains’, ‘If I knew’.

activities.after: fine tomorrow, we'll have a picnic. (be)
alts: is
activities.why: first conditional: if + present → the weather 's fine.

alts: was
activities.why: second conditional uses the past (subjunctive ‘were’) → If I were you.

activities.before: If you don't water plants, they
activities.why: a general truth → zero conditional, present → they die.
first conditional with an imperative result clause.

activities.q: You think it's quite possible you'll be free. Which fits?
options: If I'm free, I'll join you.
If I were free, I'd join you.
If I will be free, I'll join you.
why: Correct — a real, likely possibility → first conditional.
‘were free’ suggests it's unlikely.
‘will’ can't go in the if-clause.

activities.q: Which correctly uses ‘will’ in an if-clause (politeness/willingness)?
options: If it will rain tomorrow, stay in.
If you'll wait here, I'll fetch the manager.
If I will have time, I'll help.
why: ‘will’ can't mark a future condition here.
Correct — ‘If you'll wait’ = if you're willing to wait (politeness).
‘will’ can't mark the condition here.

activities.prompt: Sort each if-clause by the conditional it belongs to.
activities.why: if + present = first; if + past = second; if + past perfect = third.
no ‘will’ in the if-clause → “If the train is late…”.

activities.instruction: Rewrite to show it's unlikely/imaginary:
activities.prompt: If I have a million dollars, I will buy an island.
activities.answer: if I had a million dollars, I would buy an island
alts: If I had a million dollars, I would buy an island
if I had a million dollars, I'd buy an island
activities.why: unreal present → if + past, would + infinitive → If I had …, I would buy…

steps.npc: Do you think you'll come to the barbecue?

options.t: If the weather holds, I'll definitely be there.
options.why: a real, likely condition → first conditional.

options.t: If the weather would hold, I'll definitely be there.
options.why: keep ‘would’ out of the if-clause: ‘If the weather holds’.

steps.npc: And if it pours down?

options.t: If I were you, I'd move it indoors just in case.
options.why: hypothetical advice → second conditional with ‘were’.

options.t: If I would be you, I'd move it indoors just in case.
options.why: the if-clause takes the past ‘were’, not ‘would be’.

activities.why: Match the conditional to reality (zero/first real, second/third unreal), keep will/would out of the if-clause, and choose first vs second by likelihood.

learn.title: Beyond the four basic types
learn.html: C1 conditionals include mixed conditionals, inversion without ‘if’, and set phrases like if it were not for / if it hadn't been for. These express subtle time and hypothetical relationships.

learn.title: Mixed conditionals
learn.html: Mix time frames when the condition and result belong to different times: past condition → present result (If I'd taken that job, I'd be richer now); present condition → past result (If I weren't so busy, I'd have come yesterday).

learn.title: Inversion instead of ‘if’ (formal)
learn.html: Drop if and invert the auxiliary in formal English: Were I you, … (= If I were) · Had I known, … (= If I had known) · Should you need help, … (= If you should need). This raises the register and is common in writing.

learn.title: if it were not for / if it hadn't been for
learn.html: These mean ‘without X’: If it weren't for your help, I'd be lost (present); If it hadn't been for the rain, we'd have won (past). The inverted forms: Were it not for… / Had it not been for…

learn.title: Other conditional links
learn.html: provided/providing (that), as long as, on condition that, supposing, in case add nuance: You can borrow it as long as you return it. · In case it rains, take an umbrella (= as a precaution, not ‘if’). otherwise = ‘if not’ (result): Hurry, otherwise we'll be late.

learn.title: Advanced learner errors
learn.html:
✗ If I would have known, I'd be there now. → ✓ If I had known, I'd be there now (mixed).

✗ Had I would known… → ✓ Had I known…

✗ Take a coat if it rains (precaution) → ✓ Take a coat in case it rains.

learn.title: In a nutshell
learn.html:
• Mix conditionals when condition and result are in different times.
  • Inversion replaces ‘if’ formally: Were I…, Had I known…, Should you….
  • if it were not for / if it hadn't been for = ‘without X’ (present/past).
  • provided/as long as/on condition that add conditions; in case = precaution; otherwise = if not.

ru.title: За пределами четырёх базовых типов
ru.html: Условные C1 включают смешанные условные, инверсию без ‘if’, и устойчивые фразы вроде if it were not for / if it hadn't been for. Они выражают тонкие временны́е и гипотетические отношения.

kk.title: Төрт негізгі түрден әрі
kk.html: C1 шарттылары аралас шартты, ‘if’-сіз инверсия, және if it were not for / if it hadn't been for сияқты тұрақты тіркестерді қамтиды. Олар нәзік уақыт пен болжамды қатынастарды білдіреді.

ru.title: Смешанные условные
ru.html: Смешивай временны́е рамки, когда условие и результат в разном времени: прошлое условие → настоящий результат (If I'd taken that job, I'd be richer now); настоящее условие → прошлый результат (If I weren't so busy, I'd have come yesterday).

kk.title: Аралас шартты сөйлемдер
kk.html: Шарт пен нәтиже әртүрлі уақытта болса, шеңберлерді араластыр: өткен шарт → осы нәтиже (If I'd taken that job, I'd be richer now); осы шарт → өткен нәтиже (If I weren't so busy, I'd have come yesterday).

ru.title: Инверсия вместо ‘if’ (формально)
ru.html: В формальном английском опусти if и инвертируй вспомогательный: Were I you, … (= If I were) · Had I known, … (= If I had known) · Should you need help, … (= If you should need). Повышает регистр и часто в письме.

kk.title: ‘if’ орнына инверсия (ресми)
kk.html: Ресми ағылшында if-ты түсіріп, көмекшіні инверсиялап: Were I you, … (= If I were) · Had I known, … (= If I had known) · Should you need help, … (= If you should need). Бұл регистрді көтереді әрі жазуда жиі.

ru.title: if it were not for / if it hadn't been for
ru.html: Означают ‘без X’: If it weren't for your help, I'd be lost (настоящее); If it hadn't been for the rain, we'd have won (прошлое). Инвертированные формы: Were it not for… / Had it not been for…

kk.title: if it were not for / if it hadn't been for
kk.html: Бұлар ‘X-сіз’ дегенді білдіреді: If it weren't for your help, I'd be lost (осы); If it hadn't been for the rain, we'd have won (өткен). Инверсиялы формалар: Were it not for… / Had it not been for…

ru.title: Другие условные связи
ru.html: provided/providing (that), as long as, on condition that, supposing, in case добавляют нюанс: You can borrow it as long as you return it. In case it rains, take an umbrella (= на всякий случай, не ‘if’). otherwise = ‘если не’ (результат): Hurry, otherwise we'll be late.

kk.title: Басқа шартты байланыстар
kk.html: provided/providing (that), as long as, on condition that, supposing, in case нюанс қосады: You can borrow it as long as you return it. In case it rains, take an umbrella (= сақтық үшін, ‘if’ емес). otherwise = ‘if not’ (нәтиже): Hurry, otherwise we'll be late.

ru.title: Ошибки продвинутого уровня
ru.html: ✗ If I would have known, I'd be there now → ✓ If I had known, I'd be there now (смешанное). ✗ Had I would known… → ✓ Had I known… ✗ Take a coat if it rains (предосторожность) → ✓ Take a coat in case it rains.

kk.title: Жоғары деңгей қателері
kk.html: ✗ If I would have known, I'd be there now → ✓ If I had known, I'd be there now (аралас). ✗ Had I would known… → ✓ Had I known… ✗ Take a coat if it rains (сақтық) → ✓ Take a coat in case it rains.

ru.title: Кратко
ru.html:
• Смешивай условные, когда условие и результат в разном времени.
  • Инверсия заменяет ‘if’ формально: Were I…, Had I known…, Should you….
  • if it were not for / if it hadn't been for = ‘без X’ (настоящее/прошлое).
  • provided/as long as/on condition that добавляют условия; in case = предосторожность; otherwise = если не.

kk.title: Қысқаша
kk.html:
• Шарт пен нәтиже әртүрлі уақытта болса, шарттыларды араластыр.
  • Инверсия ‘if’-ты ресми алмастырады: Were I…, Had I known…, Should you….
  • if it were not for / if it hadn't been for = ‘X-сіз’ (осы/өткен).
  • provided/as long as/on condition that шарт қосады; in case = сақтық; otherwise = if not.

activities.prompt: Match each to its type.

items.t: If I'd saved more, I'd be comfortable now.
Had I realised, I'd have said something.
If it weren't for you, I'd give up.
If I weren't so tired, I'd have joined you.
Were I in charge, things would change.
Had it not been for the delay, we'd have made it.

activities.why: Mixed conditionals cross time frames; inversion drops ‘if’; if it were/hadn't been for means ‘without X’.

items.s: Had I known, I would have called.
If it weren't for the traffic, we'd be there by now.
If I would have left earlier, I'd be there now.
Should you would need help, ask me.

activities.why: use ‘If I had left’ (not ‘would have’ in the if-clause), and ‘Should you need’ (no ‘would’ after inversion).

activities.after: that course, I'd have a better job now. (take)
alts: had taken
activities.why: past condition affecting the present → if + past perfect → I 'd taken …, I'd have a better job now.

activities.after: the risks, I'd never have invested. (I / know)
activities.why: formal inversion of ‘If I had known’ → Had I known the risks.

activities.after: your quick thinking, it could have been a disaster. (if / not / be / for)
alts: If it hadn't been for
activities.why: ‘without X’ in the past → Had it not been for / If it hadn't been for your quick thinking.
‘as long as’ sets a condition.

activities.q: “Take some cash ___ the card machine is down.” (as a precaution)
why: ‘if’ would make it conditional on the machine being down.
Correct — ‘in case’ = as a precaution against a possibility.
‘unless’ = if not, wrong meaning here.

activities.q: “If she weren't afraid of flying, she ___ us in Tokyo last month.”
why: that's a pure present result, but ‘last month’ is past.
Correct — present condition + past result → would have joined.
a plain past doesn't express the hypothetical.

activities.prompt: Sort each linker by its meaning.
activities.why: provided that/as long as add conditions; in case = precaution; otherwise = if not.
inversion of ‘If I had known’ is ‘Had I known’, with no ‘would have’ → “Had I known earlier…”.

activities.instruction: Rewrite without ‘if’, using inversion:
activities.prompt: If you should have any questions, contact us.
activities.answer: should you have any questions, contact us
alts: Should you have any questions, contact us
should you have any questions, contact us.
activities.why: invert the auxiliary → Should you have any questions, contact us.

activities.typeLabel: Extended context: reflecting on a decision

steps.npc: Do you ever regret turning down that offer?

options.t: A bit — if I'd accepted it, I'd probably be abroad now.
options.why: mixed conditional: past condition → present result.

options.t: A bit — if I'd accepted it, I'd probably have been abroad now.
options.why: for a present result, use ‘I'd be abroad now’, not the past ‘I'd have been’.

steps.npc: Well, you've done well anyway.

options.t: Thanks — though had it not been for my mentor, I'd have struggled.
options.why: ‘had it not been for’ = without X, past result.

options.t: Thanks — though if it wouldn't be for my mentor, I'd have struggled.
options.why: use ‘had it not been for’ or ‘if it hadn't been for’, not ‘if it wouldn't be for’.

activities.why: Use mixed conditionals across time frames, inversion for formal ‘if’-less conditionals, and if it were/hadn't been for to mean ‘without X’.

learn.title: Tentative & subjunctive conditions
learn.html: Beyond standard second conditionals, English has ways to make a hypothesis even more tentative or remote: the were-subjunctive, were to + infinitive, and softeners like supposing, imagine, what if.

learn.title: were for all persons (subjunctive)
learn.html: In hypothetical conditions, formal English uses were for all persons, not ‘was’: If I were rich… · If he were here… · Were she to call… ‘If I was’ is common informally, but ‘If I were you’ is fixed advice.

learn.title: were to + infinitive (remote possibility)
learn.html: if + subject + were to + infinitive makes a future possibility sound more remote/hypothetical: If the sun were to rise in the west… · If we were to lose the contract, we'd cut costs. Often for unlikely or dramatic hypotheticals; the inverted form drops ‘if’: Were he to resign…

learn.title: supposing / imagine / what if
learn.html: Supposing / Suppose / Imagine / What if introduce hypotheses, often as questions or invitations to imagine: Supposing you lost your job, what would you do? · What if we tried a different approach? They take second-conditional-style tenses.

learn.title: Register & softening advice
learn.html: If I were you, I'd… is the standard formula for giving advice tactfully. were to and inversion belong to formal/literary register. These devices let you distance a suggestion from reality, sounding more polite or cautious.

learn.title: Advanced learner errors
learn.html:
✗ If I was you, I'd resign (advice) → ✓ If I were you…

✗ If he would win… → ✓ If he were to win… / If he won…

✗ Supposing you would lose it… → ✓ Supposing you lost it…

learn.title: In a nutshell
learn.html:
• Use ‘were’ for all persons in hypothetical conditions (If I were you).
  • were to + infinitive makes a future hypothesis more remote/dramatic.
  • supposing/imagine/what if introduce hypotheses (second-conditional tenses).
  • Inversion: Were he to…, Were I…; all raise formality and soften.

ru.title: Осторожные и subjunctive условия
ru.html: Кроме стандартного второго условного, в английском есть способы сделать гипотезу ещё более осторожной или отдалённой: were-subjunctive, were to + инфинитив, и смягчители вроде supposing, imagine, what if.

kk.title: Сақ және subjunctive шарттар
kk.html: Стандартты екінші шарттыдан бөлек, ағылшында гипотезаны одан да сақ не алыс ететін жолдар бар: were-subjunctive, were to + инфинитив, және supposing, imagine, what if сияқты жұмсартқыштар.

ru.title: were для всех лиц (subjunctive)
ru.html: В гипотетических условиях формальный английский использует were для всех лиц, не ‘was’: If I were rich… If he were here… Were she to call… ‘If I was’ частотно неформально, но ‘If I were you’ — устойчивый совет.

kk.title: Барлық жаққа were (subjunctive)
kk.html: Гипотетикалық шарттарда ресми ағылшын барлық жаққа were қолданады, ‘was’ емес: If I were rich… If he were here… Were she to call… ‘If I was’ бейресми жиі, бірақ ‘If I were you’ — тұрақты кеңес.

ru.title: were to + инфинитив (отдалённая возможность)
ru.html: if + подлежащее + were to + инфинитив делает будущую возможность более отдалённой/гипотетической: If the sun were to rise in the west… If we were to lose the contract, we'd cut costs. Часто для маловероятных или драматичных гипотез; инвертированная форма опускает ‘if’: Were he to resign…

kk.title: were to + инфинитив (алыс мүмкіндік)
kk.html: if + бастауыш + were to + инфинитив болашақ мүмкіндікті алысырақ/гипотетикалық етеді: If the sun were to rise in the west… If we were to lose the contract, we'd cut costs. Жиі екіталай не драмалық гипотезаға; инверсиялы форма ‘if’-ты түсіреді: Were he to resign…

ru.title: supposing / imagine / what if
ru.html: Supposing / Suppose / Imagine / What if вводят гипотезы, часто как вопросы или приглашения вообразить: Supposing you lost your job, what would you do? What if we tried a different approach? Берут времена в стиле второго условного.

kk.title: supposing / imagine / what if
kk.html: Supposing / Suppose / Imagine / What if гипотезаны енгізеді, жиі сұрақ не елестету шақыруы ретінде: Supposing you lost your job, what would you do? What if we tried a different approach? Олар екінші-шартты стиліндегі шақтарды алады.

ru.title: Регистр и смягчение совета
ru.html: If I were you, I'd… — стандартная формула для тактичного совета. were to и инверсия принадлежат формальному/литературному регистру. Эти средства дистанцируют предложение от реальности, звуча вежливее или осторожнее.

kk.title: Регистр және кеңесті жұмсарту
kk.html: If I were you, I'd… — кеңесті әдеппен беру стандартты формуласы. were to мен инверсия ресми/әдеби регистрге жатады. Бұл құралдар ұсынысты шындықтан алшақтатып, сыпайы не сақ естіледі.

ru.title: Ошибки продвинутого уровня
ru.html: ✗ If I was you, I'd resign (совет) → ✓ If I were you… ✗ If he would win… → ✓ If he were to win… / If he won… ✗ Supposing you would lose it… → ✓ Supposing you lost it…

kk.title: Жоғары деңгей қателері
kk.html: ✗ If I was you, I'd resign (кеңес) → ✓ If I were you… ✗ If he would win… → ✓ If he were to win… / If he won… ✗ Supposing you would lose it… → ✓ Supposing you lost it…

ru.title: Кратко
ru.html:
• Используй ‘were’ для всех лиц в гипотетических условиях (If I were you).
  • were to + инфинитив делает будущую гипотезу отдалённее/драматичнее.
  • supposing/imagine/what if вводят гипотезы (времена второго условного).
  • Инверсия: Were he to…, Were I…; всё повышает формальность и смягчает.

kk.title: Қысқаша
kk.html:
• Гипотетикалық шарттарда барлық жаққа ‘were’ (If I were you).
  • were to + инфинитив болашақ гипотезаны алысырақ/драмалық етеді.
  • supposing/imagine/what if гипотезаны енгізеді (екінші-шартты шақтары).
  • Инверсия: Were he to…, Were I…; бәрі ресмилікті көтереді әрі жұмсартады.

activities.prompt: Match each to its structure.

items.t: If I were you, I'd wait.
If prices were to double, we'd struggle.
What if we changed the design?

activities.why: were-subjunctive for hypotheses/advice; were to + inf for remote futures; supposing/what if to invite imagining.

items.s: If I were you, I'd apologise.
Were she to accept, we'd be delighted.
If he would be here, he'd help.
Supposing you would win, what then?

activities.why: use ‘If he were here’ and ‘Supposing you won’, not ‘would’ in these hypothetical clauses.

activities.after: in your position, I'd take the risk. (be)
activities.why: hypothetical advice uses ‘were’ for all persons → If I were in your position.

activities.after: raise taxes, there'd be protests. (be to / raise)
activities.why: a remote future hypothesis → were to raise taxes.

activities.after: the lottery, how would your life change? (win)
activities.why: ‘supposing’ takes second-conditional tenses → Supposing you won.
inversion of ‘If I were to start again’ (formal remote hypothesis).

activities.q: For tactful advice, complete: “If I ___ you, I'd think it over.”
why: ‘was’ is informal and not used in this fixed advice phrase.
Correct — ‘If I were you’ is the standard subjunctive advice form.
present tense doesn't fit the hypothetical.

activities.q: Which version sounds the most remote/hypothetical about the future?
options: If we lose the client, we'll cut back.
If we lost the client, we'd cut back.
If we were to lose the client, we'd cut back.
why: a real, likely first conditional.
a standard unreal hypothesis.
Correct — ‘were to lose’ frames it as the most remote/dramatic possibility.

activities.prompt: Sort each conditional opener.
activities.why: Inversion drops ‘if’ and fronts ‘were’, raising the formality.
a remote future hypothesis takes ‘were to’, not ‘would’, in the if-clause → “If the company were to relocate…”.

activities.instruction: Rewrite formally without ‘if’, using inversion:
activities.prompt: If she were to resign, who would replace her?
activities.answer: were she to resign, who would replace her
alts: Were she to resign, who would replace her?
were she to resign, who would replace her?
activities.why: invert ‘were’ and drop ‘if’ → Were she to resign, who would replace her?

steps.npc: I'm tempted to quit and start my own thing.

options.t: If I were you, I'd line up some clients first.
options.why: ‘If I were you’ is the tactful advice formula.

options.t: If I was you, I'd line up some clients first.
options.why: the fixed advice phrase uses the subjunctive ‘were’: ‘If I were you’.

steps.npc: And if the business doesn't take off?

options.t: Well, were it to fail, you could always go back to consulting.
options.why: ‘were it to fail’ (inversion) frames a remote hypothesis formally.

options.t: Well, would it fail, you could always go back to consulting.
options.why: use ‘were it to fail’ (or ‘if it failed’), not ‘would it fail’.

activities.why: Use ‘were’ for all persons in hypotheses, ‘were to + infinitive’ (and its inversion) for remote futures, and supposing/what if to invite imagining.

learn.title: Negative conditions & reporting alternatives
learn.html: This unit covers if … not vs unless, the choice of if vs whether in reported questions, and related links like as long as, provided, in case, only if. These fine-tune conditional and reporting meaning.

learn.title: unless = ‘if … not’ (with limits)
learn.html: unless usually means ‘except if / if … not’: I won't go unless you come (= if you don't come). But unless is not always interchangeable: it states the only condition that would change things, and is avoided for unreal/emotional conditions: ✗ I'd be upset unless you called → ✓ … if you didn't call.

learn.title: if vs whether
learn.html: In reported yes/no questions, if and whether often both work: I asked if/whether she was coming. But use whether (not if): before a to-infinitive (whether to go), directly before or not (whether or not), after a preposition (discuss of… → the question of whether), and as a subject (Whether it works remains to be seen).

learn.title: as long as, provided, only if
learn.html: as long as / provided (that) / on condition that = ‘if and only if’: You can go as long as you're back by ten. only if emphasises the sole condition and can trigger inversion when fronted: Only if you apologise will I forgive you.

learn.title: in case vs if; whether … or
learn.html: in case = ‘as a precaution’, not a condition: Bring cash in case the card fails. whether … or (not) covers both possibilities: Whether you like it or not, it's happening.

learn.title: Advanced learner errors
learn.html:
✗ I'd be sad unless you came. → ✓ … if you didn't come.

✗ I'm not sure if to accept. → ✓ … whether to accept.

✗ Take an umbrella if it rains (precaution) → ✓ … in case it rains.

learn.title: In a nutshell
learn.html:
• unless = ‘except if / if … not’, but not for unreal/emotional conditions.
  • Use whether (not if) before to-infinitive, or not, prepositions, and as subject.
  • as long as/provided/only if state sole conditions; only if fronted → inversion.
  • in case = precaution; whether … or (not) covers both options.

ru.title: Отрицательные условия и альтернативы передачи
ru.html: Этот раздел охватывает if … not vs unless, выбор if vs whether в косвенных вопросах, и связи вроде as long as, provided, in case, only if. Они уточняют условное и передающее значение.

kk.title: Болымсыз шарттар & баяндау баламалары
kk.html: Бұл бөлім if … not vs unless-ты, баяндалған сұрақтарда if vs whether таңдауын, және as long as, provided, in case, only if сияқты байланыстарды қамтиды. Олар шартты мен баяндау мағынасын нақтылайды.

ru.title: unless = ‘if … not’ (с ограничениями)
ru.html: unless обычно значит ‘кроме если / если … не’: I won't go unless you come (= if you don't come). Но unless не всегда взаимозаменяемо: оно называет единственное условие, которое изменило бы дело, и избегается для нереальных/эмоциональных условий: ✗ I'd be upset unless you called → ✓ … if you didn't call.

kk.title: unless = ‘if … not’ (шектеулермен)
kk.html: unless әдетте ‘except if / if … not’ дегенді білдіреді: I won't go unless you come (= if you don't come). Бірақ unless әрдайым алмаспайды: ол жағдайды өзгертетін жалғыз шартты айтады, әрі шындыққа жанаспайтын/эмоциялық шартқа қолданылмайды: ✗ I'd be upset unless you called → ✓ … if you didn't call.

ru.title: if vs whether
ru.html: В косвенных да/нет вопросах if и whether часто оба: I asked if/whether she was coming. Но используй whether (не if): перед to-инфинитивом (whether to go), прямо перед or not (whether or not), после предлога (the question of whether), и как подлежащее (Whether it works remains to be seen).

kk.title: if vs whether
kk.html: Баяндалған yes/no сұрақтарда if пен whether жиі екеуі де: I asked if/whether she was coming. Бірақ whether қолдан (if емес): to-инфинитив алдында (whether to go), or not тікелей алдында (whether or not), предлогтан кейін (the question of whether), және бастауыш ретінде (Whether it works remains to be seen).

ru.title: as long as, provided, only if
ru.html: as long as / provided (that) / on condition that = ‘если и только если’: You can go as long as you're back by ten. only if подчёркивает единственное условие и может вызвать инверсию при выносе вперёд: Only if you apologise will I forgive you.

kk.title: as long as, provided, only if
kk.html: as long as / provided (that) / on condition that = ‘тек сонда ғана’: You can go as long as you're back by ten. only if жалғыз шартты баса көрсетеді әрі алға шыққанда инверсия тудыруы мүмкін: Only if you apologise will I forgive you.

ru.title: in case vs if; whether … or
ru.html: in case = ‘на всякий случай’, не условие: Bring cash in case the card fails. whether … or (not) охватывает обе возможности: Whether you like it or not, it's happening.

kk.title: in case vs if; whether … or
kk.html: in case = ‘сақтық үшін’, шарт емес: Bring cash in case the card fails. whether … or (not) екі мүмкіндікті де қамтиды: Whether you like it or not, it's happening.

ru.title: Ошибки продвинутого уровня
ru.html: ✗ I'd be sad unless you came → ✓ … if you didn't come. ✗ I'm not sure if to accept → ✓ … whether to accept. ✗ Take an umbrella if it rains (предосторожность) → ✓ … in case it rains.

kk.title: Жоғары деңгей қателері
kk.html: ✗ I'd be sad unless you came → ✓ … if you didn't come. ✗ I'm not sure if to accept → ✓ … whether to accept. ✗ Take an umbrella if it rains (сақтық) → ✓ … in case it rains.

ru.title: Кратко
ru.html:
• unless = ‘кроме если / если … не’, но не для нереальных/эмоциональных условий.
  • whether (не if) перед to-инфинитивом, or not, предлогами и как подлежащее.
  • as long as/provided/only if называют единственные условия; вынесенное only if → инверсия.
  • in case = предосторожность; whether … or (not) охватывает обе опции.

kk.title: Қысқаша
kk.html:
• unless = ‘except if / if … not’, бірақ шындыққа жанаспайтын/эмоциялық шартқа емес.
  • whether (if емес) to-инфинитив, or not, предлог алдында және бастауыш ретінде.
  • as long as/provided/only if жалғыз шартты айтады; алға шыққан only if → инверсия.
  • in case = сақтық; whether … or (not) екі нұсқаны қамтиды.

activities.prompt: Sort each context by which is required (or preferred).
activities.why: Use ‘whether’ before to-infinitives, ‘or not’, and prepositions; ‘if’ is fine in ordinary reported questions.

items.s: Unless you hurry, you'll miss it.
I'm not sure whether to stay or go.
I'd be disappointed unless you came.
She asked if or not I agreed.

activities.why: emotional/unreal conditions use ‘if … not’ (if you didn't come); and ‘whether or not’, not ‘if or not’.
‘the only condition’ → unless you have a ticket (= if you don't).
before a to-infinitive, use whether.
a precaution, not a condition → in case the power cuts out.
‘whether … or …’ covers both possibilities.

activities.q: Which is correct for this emotional condition? “I'd be really hurt ___.”
why: ‘unless’ is avoided for unreal/emotional conditions.
Correct — use ‘if … not’ for this emotional hypothetical.
‘would’ can't go in the if-clause anyway.

activities.q: Complete: “Only if the board approves ___ the project go ahead.”
why: no inversion, but fronted ‘only if’ requires it.
Correct — fronting ‘only if’ triggers inversion: ‘will the project go ahead’.
wrong modal and no inversion.

activities.prompt: Sort each linker.
activities.why: provided that/as long as set conditions; in case is a precaution; whether … or not covers both options.
before ‘or not’, use ‘whether’ → “Let me know whether or not you can make it.”

activities.instruction: Rewrite using ‘unless’ (where it's valid):
activities.prompt: If you don't back up your files, you'll lose them.
activities.answer: unless you back up your files, you'll lose them
alts: Unless you back up your files, you'll lose them
unless you back up your files you'll lose them
activities.why: a real ‘if … not’ condition → Unless you back up your files.

steps.npc: Can I extend the deadline?

options.t: Yes, as long as you let me know by Friday.
options.why: ‘as long as’ states the sole condition.

options.t: Yes, in case you let me know by Friday.
options.why: this is a condition, not a precaution; use ‘as long as’, not ‘in case’.

steps.npc: And I still haven't decided which option to pick.

options.t: Take your time deciding whether to go with A or B.
options.why: ‘whether’ before a to-infinitive and before ‘or’.

options.t: Take your time deciding if to go with A or B.
options.why: before a to-infinitive, use ‘whether to go’, not ‘if to go’.

activities.why: Use unless for real ‘if … not’ (not emotional ones), whether before to-infinitive/or not/prepositions, in case for precautions, and as long as/only if to state sole conditions.

learn.title: Conjunctions vs conjuncts
learn.html: Two kinds of linker. Conjunctions (and, but, so, because, although) join clauses within one sentence. Conjuncts/connectors (however, therefore, moreover, nevertheless) link separate sentences and are adverbs — they need a full stop or semicolon before them, not just a comma.

learn.title: Punctuation: the key difference
learn.html: It rained, but we went out (conjunction, comma) vs It rained. However, we went out / It rained; however, we went out (connector, needs a stronger break). ✗ It rained, however we went out (comma splice).

learn.title: Connectors by function
learn.html: Addition: moreover, furthermore, in addition, also, besides. Contrast: however, nevertheless, on the other hand, still. Result: therefore, consequently, as a result, thus. Reformulation: in other words, that is. Example: for instance, for example. Sequence: firstly, then, finally.

learn.title: Position & flexibility
learn.html: Connectors usually go at the front (Nevertheless, …) but can go mid or end: The plan, however, was flawed. · It didn't work, though. Commas set them off. This flexibility helps control emphasis and flow.

learn.title: Register & cohesion
learn.html: Connectors are the backbone of cohesive, formal writing — they signpost the logical relationship between ideas. Overusing them (a connector every sentence) feels mechanical; using them precisely at key turns makes an argument easy to follow. This is a top C1 writing skill.

learn.title: Advanced learner errors
learn.html:
✗ Although it was late, however we continued. → ✓ pick one linker.

✗ I was tired, therefore I went to bed (comma splice) → ✓ … tired; therefore, I went to bed.

✗ Despite of the rain → ✓ Despite the rain (no ‘of’).

learn.title: In a nutshell
learn.html:
• Conjunctions join clauses in one sentence; connectors link separate sentences (need a full stop/semicolon).
  • Don't join two clauses with just a comma + a connector (comma splice).
  • Group connectors by function: addition, contrast, result, example, sequence.
  • Vary position; use connectors precisely for cohesion, not on every sentence.

ru.title: Союзы vs соединители
ru.html: Два вида связок. Союзы (and, but, so, because, although) соединяют клаузы внутри одного предложения. Соединители (however, therefore, moreover, nevertheless) связывают отдельные предложения и являются наречиями — перед ними нужна точка или точка с запятой, не просто запятая.

kk.title: Жалғаулықтар vs жалғағыштар
kk.html: Екі түрлі байланыстырғыш. Жалғаулықтар (and, but, so, because, although) бір сөйлем ішінде сөйлемдерді қосады. Жалғағыштар (however, therefore, moreover, nevertheless) бөлек сөйлемдерді байланыстырады әрі үстеу — олардың алдында нүкте не нүктелі үтір керек, жай үтір емес.

ru.title: Пунктуация: ключевое различие
ru.html: It rained, but we went out (союз, запятая) / It rained. However, we went out / It rained; however, we went out (соединитель, нужен более сильный разрыв). ✗ It rained, however we went out (запятой сплайс).

kk.title: Тыныс белгісі: басты айырма
kk.html: It rained, but we went out (жалғаулық, үтір) / It rained. However, we went out / It rained; however, we went out (жалғағыш, күштірек үзіліс керек). ✗ It rained, however we went out (үтір сплайс).

ru.title: Соединители по функции
ru.html: Добавление: moreover, furthermore, in addition, also, besides. Контраст: however, nevertheless, on the other hand, still. Результат: therefore, consequently, as a result, thus. Переформулировка: in other words, that is. Пример: for instance, for example. Последовательность: firstly, then, finally.

kk.title: Жалғағыштар қызметі бойынша
kk.html: Қосу: moreover, furthermore, in addition, also, besides. Қарама-қарсылық: however, nevertheless, on the other hand, still. Нәтиже: therefore, consequently, as a result, thus. Қайта тұжырымдау: in other words, that is. Мысал: for instance, for example. Тізбек: firstly, then, finally.

ru.title: Позиция и гибкость
ru.html: Соединители обычно идут вперёд (Nevertheless, …), но могут в середину или конец: The plan, however, was flawed. It didn't work, though. Запятые их выделяют. Эта гибкость помогает управлять акцентом и потоком.

kk.title: Позиция мен икемділік
kk.html: Жалғағыштар әдетте алдында (Nevertheless, …) бірақ ортада не соңында да: The plan, however, was flawed. It didn't work, though. Үтір оларды бөледі. Бұл икемділік екпін мен ағынды бақылауға көмектеседі.

ru.title: Регистр и связность
ru.html: Соединители — основа связного, формального письма: они обозначают логическое отношение между идеями. Их избыток (в каждом предложении) кажется механическим; точное использование на ключевых поворотах делает аргумент легко отслеживаемым. Это ведущий навык письма C1.

kk.title: Регистр және байланыстырушылық
kk.html: Жалғағыштар — байланысты, ресми жазудың тірегі: олар идеялар арасындағы логикалық қатынасты көрсетеді. Оларды артық қолдану (әр сөйлемде) механикалық сезіледі; басты бұрылыстарда дәл қолдану дәлелді оңай ұстауға мүмкіндік береді. Бұл — басты C1 жазу дағдысы.

ru.title: Ошибки продвинутого уровня
ru.html: ✗ Although it was late, however we continued → ✓ выбери одну связку. ✗ I was tired, therefore I went to bed (запятой сплайс) → ✓ … tired; therefore, I went to bed. ✗ Despite of the rain → ✓ Despite the rain (без ‘of’).

kk.title: Жоғары деңгей қателері
kk.html: ✗ Although it was late, however we continued → ✓ бір байланыстырғышты таңда. ✗ I was tired, therefore I went to bed (үтір сплайс) → ✓ … tired; therefore, I went to bed. ✗ Despite of the rain → ✓ Despite the rain (‘of’ жоқ).

ru.title: Кратко
ru.html:
• Союзы соединяют клаузы в одном предложении; соединители связывают отдельные (нужна точка/точка с запятой).
  • Не соединяй две клаузы просто запятой + соединителем (запятой сплайс).
  • Группируй соединители по функции: добавление, контраст, результат, пример, последовательность.
  • Варьируй позицию; используй точно для связности, не в каждом предложении.

kk.title: Қысқаша
kk.html:
• Жалғаулықтар бір сөйлемде сөйлемдерді қосады; жалғағыштар бөлек сөйлемдерді (нүкте/нүктелі үтір керек).
  • Екі сөйлемді жай үтір + жалғағышпен қоспа (үтір сплайс).
  • Жалғағыштарды қызметі бойынша топта: қосу, қарама-қарсылық, нәтиже, мысал, тізбек.
  • Позицияны түрлендір; байланыс үшін дәл қолдан, әр сөйлемде емес.

activities.prompt: Sort each linker by type.
activities.why: Conjunctions join clauses within a sentence; connectors (adverbs) link separate sentences.

items.s: It was expensive. Nevertheless, we bought it.
She was tired, but she kept going.
He was late, therefore we started without him.
Although it was raining, but we went.

activities.why: ‘therefore’ needs a semicolon/full stop (comma splice), and don't pair ‘although’ with ‘but’.

alts: consequently
as a result
thus
activities.why: a formal result connector after a semicolon → therefore,

activities.after: , it's close to the station. (addition connector)
alts: Furthermore
In addition
Besides
activities.why: adding a further point → Moreover,

alts: yet
activities.why: a contrast conjunction joining clauses → but.
‘however’ in mid-position, set off by commas.

activities.q: Which correctly links the two clauses?
options: Sales fell, consequently we cut costs.
Sales fell; consequently, we cut costs.
Sales fell consequently we cut costs.
why: a comma splice — ‘consequently’ needs a stronger break.
Correct — a semicolon before the connector, comma after.
runs the clauses together with no punctuation.

activities.q: Choose the best link: “The prototype failed twice. ___, the team refined the design and tried again.”
why: signals an example, which doesn't fit.
Correct — ‘Nevertheless’ signals contrast/concession (despite failures).
‘in other words’ reformulates, not the meaning here.

activities.prompt: Sort each connector by what it signals.
activities.why: furthermore = addition; nevertheless/on the other hand = contrast; consequently = result.
‘despite’ takes no ‘of’ → “Despite the setbacks…”.

activities.instruction: Rewrite as two sentences using a result connector:
activities.prompt: The road was flooded, so the bus was diverted.
activities.answer: the road was flooded. consequently, the bus was diverted
alts: The road was flooded. Consequently, the bus was diverted
The road was flooded; consequently, the bus was diverted
The road was flooded. Therefore, the bus was diverted
activities.why: use a full stop/semicolon + a result connector → Consequently/Therefore, the bus was diverted.

steps.npc: Does this sentence read well: ‘Costs rose, however profits held steady’?

options.t: Almost — make it ‘Costs rose; however, profits held steady.’
options.why: ‘however’ needs a semicolon/full stop, not a comma splice.

options.t: It's fine as it is — ‘however’ works like ‘but’ here.
options.why: ‘however’ is a connector, so it needs a semicolon or full stop, not a comma.

steps.npc: And the next line adds a supporting point.

options.t: Then open it with ‘Moreover,’ to signal addition.
options.why: ‘Moreover’ correctly signals an additional supporting point.

options.t: Then open it with ‘Nevertheless,’ to signal addition.
options.why: ‘Nevertheless’ signals contrast, not addition; use ‘Moreover/Furthermore’.

activities.why: Distinguish conjunctions (join clauses, comma) from connectors (link sentences, need a full stop/semicolon), avoid comma splices, and choose connectors by function for cohesion.

learn.title: Position vs movement
learn.html: Position (static) prepositions say where something is (at, on, in, above, below, beside, between); movement (dynamic) prepositions say where something goes (to, into, onto, out of, towards, through, across, along, past). Many pairs contrast static/dynamic: in vs into, on vs onto.

learn.title: at / on / in for place
learn.html: at = a point (at the corner, at the door, at the station); on = a surface/line (on the wall, on the coast, on the second floor); in = an enclosed space/area (in the box, in the city, in the garden). These are the core, with many fixed collocations.

learn.title: into/onto vs in/on; out of
learn.html: Movement into a space uses into/onto; being there uses in/on: She walked into the room (movement) vs She was in the room (position). Get onto the bus / out of the car. After verbs of throwing/putting, ‘in/on’ can also work informally.

learn.title: through / across / along / over / past
learn.html: across = from one side to the other of a surface; through = within a 3-D space (through the tunnel, through the forest); along = following a line (along the river); over = above/across the top (over the bridge); past = beyond (walk past the shop).

learn.title: Figurative uses
learn.html: These prepositions extend to abstract meanings: in difficulty, on a diet, at risk, under pressure, beyond belief, into trouble, out of work. The physical logic (containment, contact, point) often carries over metaphorically — a key to guessing correct usage.

learn.title: Advanced learner errors
learn.html:
✗ She got in the bus. (large vehicle) → ✓ She got on the bus (but ‘in the car’).

✗ arrive to the airport → ✓ arrive at the airport / in Paris.

✗ He walked in the room (movement) → often clearer walked into the room.

learn.title: In a nutshell
learn.html:
• Static: at (point), on (surface/line), in (enclosed space).
  • Dynamic: into/onto/out of/to/towards/through/across/along/past.
  • into/onto for movement into a place; in/on for being there.
  • Physical logic extends to figurative uses (under pressure, out of work).

ru.title: Положение vs движение
ru.html: Положение (статические) предлоги говорят, где нечто находится (at, on, in, above, below, beside, between); движение (динамические) говорят, куда нечто идёт (to, into, onto, out of, towards, through, across, along, past). Многие пары контрастируют: in vs into, on vs onto.

kk.title: Орын vs қозғалыс
kk.html: Орын (статикалық) предлогтар бірдеңе қайда екенін айтады (at, on, in, above, below, beside, between); қозғалыс (динамикалық) предлогтар қайда баратынын айтады (to, into, onto, out of, towards, through, across, along, past). Көп жұп статик/динамик қарама-қарсы: in vs into, on vs onto.

ru.title: at / on / in для места
ru.html: at = точка (at the corner, at the door, at the station); on = поверхность/линия (on the wall, on the coast, on the second floor); in = замкнутое пространство/область (in the box, in the city, in the garden). Основа с множеством устойчивых сочетаний.

kk.title: at / on / in орынға
kk.html: at = нүкте (at the corner, at the door, at the station); on = бет/сызық (on the wall, on the coast, on the second floor); in = жабық кеңістік/аймақ (in the box, in the city, in the garden). Бұлар негізгі, көп тұрақты тіркесіммен.

ru.title: into/onto vs in/on; out of
ru.html: Движение в пространство — into/onto; нахождение там — in/on: She walked into the room (движение) / She was in the room (положение). Get onto the bus / out of the car. После глаголов бросания/помещения ‘in/on’ тоже работает неформально.

kk.title: into/onto vs in/on; out of
kk.html: Кеңістікке қозғалыс into/onto; онда болу in/on: She walked into the room (қозғалыс) / She was in the room (орын). Get onto the bus / out of the car. Лақтыру/қою етістіктерінен кейін ‘in/on’ бейресми де жарайды.

ru.title: through / across / along / over / past
ru.html: across = с одной стороны поверхности на другую; through = внутри 3D-пространства (through the tunnel, through the forest); along = вдоль линии (along the river); over = над/через верх (over the bridge); past = мимо (walk past the shop).

kk.title: through / across / along / over / past
kk.html: across = беттің бір жағынан екіншісіне; through = 3D кеңістік ішінде (through the tunnel, through the forest); along = сызықты бойлай (along the river); over = үстінен/асып (over the bridge); past = ары қарай (walk past the shop).

ru.title: Переносные употребления
ru.html: Эти предлоги распространяются на абстрактные значения: in difficulty, on a diet, at risk, under pressure, beyond belief, into trouble, out of work. Физическая логика (вмещение, контакт, точка) часто переносится метафорически — ключ к угадыванию верного употребления.

kk.title: Ауыспалы қолданыстар
kk.html: Бұл предлогтар абстракт мағынаға таралады: in difficulty, on a diet, at risk, under pressure, beyond belief, into trouble, out of work. Физикалық логика (қамту, жанасу, нүкте) жиі метафоралық түрде ауысады — дұрыс қолданысты болжаудың кілті.

ru.title: Ошибки продвинутого уровня
ru.html: ✗ She got in the bus (большой транспорт) → ✓ She got on the bus (но ‘in the car’). ✗ arrive to the airport → ✓ arrive at the airport / in Paris. ✗ He walked in the room (движение) → часто яснее walked into the room.

kk.title: Жоғары деңгей қателері
kk.html: ✗ She got in the bus (үлкен көлік) → ✓ She got on the bus (бірақ ‘in the car’). ✗ arrive to the airport → ✓ arrive at the airport / in Paris. ✗ He walked in the room (қозғалыс) → жиі анығырақ walked into the room.

ru.title: Кратко
ru.html:
• Статические: at (точка), on (поверхность/линия), in (замкнутое пространство).
  • Динамические: into/onto/out of/to/towards/through/across/along/past.
  • into/onto для движения в место; in/on для нахождения там.
  • Физическая логика распространяется на переносные (under pressure, out of work).

kk.title: Қысқаша
kk.html:
• Статик: at (нүкте), on (бет/сызық), in (жабық кеңістік).
  • Динамик: into/onto/out of/to/towards/through/across/along/past.
  • Кеңістікке қозғалысқа into/onto; онда болуға in/on.
  • Физикалық логика ауыспалы қолданысқа таралады (under pressure, out of work).

activities.prompt: Sort each place phrase.
activities.why: at marks a point, on a surface/line, in an enclosed space.

items.s: They live on the third floor.
We arrived at the station at noon.
She got in the bus quickly.
He walked in the room and sat down.

activities.why: you get ‘on’ a bus, and ‘walked into the room’ is clearer for the movement.
movement into an enclosed space → into the box.
a specific point → at the entrance.

activities.before: With three deadlines this week, she's really
activities.why: a figurative extension → under pressure.
‘through’ (within a 3-D space) then ‘across’ (from side to side).

activities.q: Complete: “We walked ___ the forest for hours.”
why: ‘across’ suits a flat surface (across the field).
Correct — ‘through’ = within a 3-D space like a forest.
‘along’ follows a line, not moving among the trees.

activities.q: Which sentence describes movement (not position)?
options: The keys are on the table.
She threw the keys onto the table.
The picture hangs on the wall.
why: static position (on).
Correct — ‘onto’ marks movement to a surface.
static position again.

activities.prompt: Sort each preposition.
activities.why: beside/between describe position; towards/out of describe movement.
‘arrive’ takes ‘at’ (a place) or ‘in’ (a town) → “arrived at the hotel.”

activities.instruction: Rewrite to make the movement into the space explicit:
activities.prompt: The children ran in the playground. (they entered it)
activities.answer: the children ran into the playground
alts: The children ran into the playground
the children ran into the playground.
activities.why: movement into an area → ran into the playground.

steps.npc: How do I get to the gallery from the station?

options.t: Go along the river, then across the footbridge.
options.why: ‘along’ (follow a line) then ‘across’ (from side to side).

options.t: Go across the river, then along the footbridge.
options.why: you go ‘along the river’ (following it) and ‘across the footbridge’ (from one side to the other).

steps.npc: And is it easy to spot?

options.t: Yes — it's the tall building at the corner of the square.
options.why: ‘at the corner’ marks a point.

options.t: Yes — it's the tall building in the corner of the square.
options.why: for a point like a street corner, use ‘at the corner’ (‘in the corner’ is for inside a room).

activities.why: Use at/on/in for static position, into/onto/through/across/along for movement, and extend the physical logic to figurative uses.

learn.title: The core distinction
learn.html: between relates something to separate, individually-seen things (typically two, but more if considered individually); among relates it to a group/mass seen collectively (three or more, not individualised): a valley between two mountains · a house among the trees.

learn.title: between with more than two
learn.html: Use between for more than two when the items are distinct and considered separately: a treaty between five nations · the differences between the four options · She divided it between the three children (each gets a share). ‘between two’ is the classic case, but the number isn't the rule — individuality is.

learn.title: among for a mass or collective
learn.html: Use among when the surrounding items form an undifferentiated group: among friends, among the crowd, popular among students, hidden among the papers. You can't easily separate them one by one. amongst is a more formal/British variant.

learn.title: Sharing, choosing & relationships
learn.html: between for two-way relationships and choices: the link between cause and effect · choose between A and B · between you and me. Note the object pronoun: between you and me (✗ between you and I).

learn.title: Register & fixed phrases
learn.html: Fixed: between ourselves, between jobs, between meals, in between; among other things, among the best. The between/among choice is subtle — many native speakers use ‘between’ broadly, but careful writing keeps ‘among’ for undifferentiated groups.

learn.title: Advanced learner errors
learn.html:
✗ a secret among you and me → ✓ between you and me.

✗ between the crowd → ✓ among the crowd.

✗ between you and I → ✓ between you and me (object pronoun).

learn.title: In a nutshell
learn.html:
• between = separate, individually-seen things (often two, but more if distinct).
  • among = a group/mass seen collectively (three or more, undifferentiated).
  • between for two-way relationships/choices; use the object pronoun (between you and me).
  • amongst is a formal variant; careful writing preserves the distinction.

ru.title: Ключевое различие
ru.html: between связывает нечто с отдельными, индивидуально видимыми вещами (обычно двумя, но больше, если рассматриваются по отдельности); among — с группой/массой, видимой в целом (три или более, не индивидуализированы): a valley between two mountains · a house among the trees.

kk.title: Негізгі айырма
kk.html: between бірдеңені жеке, дара көрінетін заттармен байланыстырады (әдетте екеу, бірақ дара қаралса көбірек те); among оны ұжым/масса ретінде көрінетін топпен байланыстырады (үш не одан көп, даралмаған): a valley between two mountains · a house among the trees.

ru.title: between с более чем двумя
ru.html: Используй between для более чем двух, когда предметы различимы и рассматриваются отдельно: a treaty between five nations · the differences between the four options · She divided it between the three children (каждому доля). ‘between two’ — классика, но правило — не число, а индивидуальность.

kk.title: between екіден көппен
kk.html: Заттар дара әрі бөлек қаралса, екіден көпке between: a treaty between five nations · the differences between the four options · She divided it between the three children (әрқайсысына үлес). ‘between two’ — классикалық жағдай, бірақ ереже — сан емес, даралық.

ru.title: among для массы или коллектива
ru.html: Используй among, когда окружающие предметы образуют недифференцированную группу: among friends, among the crowd, popular among students, hidden among the papers. Их нельзя легко разделить по одному. amongst — более формальный/британский вариант.

kk.title: among массаға не ұжымға
kk.html: Айналадағы заттар ажыратылмаған топ құраса, among: among friends, among the crowd, popular among students, hidden among the papers. Оларды бір-бірлеп бөлу оңай емес. amongst — ресмилеу/британдық нұсқа.

ru.title: Деление, выбор, отношения
ru.html: between для двусторонних отношений и выбора: the link between cause and effect · choose between A and B · between you and me. Заметь объектное местоимение: between you and me (✗ between you and I).

kk.title: Бөлісу, таңдау, қатынастар
kk.html: between екіжақты қатынас пен таңдауға: the link between cause and effect · choose between A and B · between you and me. Объект есімдігін ескер: between you and me (✗ between you and I).

ru.title: Регистр и устойчивые фразы
ru.html: Устойчивые: between ourselves, between jobs, between meals, in between; among other things, among the best. Выбор between/among тонок — многие носители широко используют ‘between’, но тщательное письмо сохраняет ‘among’ для недифференцированных групп.

kk.title: Регистр және тұрақты тіркестер
kk.html: Тұрақты: between ourselves, between jobs, between meals, in between; among other things, among the best. between/among таңдауы нәзік — көп ана тілінде ‘between’-ды кең қолданады, бірақ мұқият жазу ‘among’-ды ажыратылмаған топқа сақтайды.

ru.title: Ошибки продвинутого уровня
ru.html: ✗ a secret among you and me → ✓ between you and me. ✗ between the crowd → ✓ among the crowd. ✗ between you and I → ✓ between you and me (объектное местоимение).

kk.title: Жоғары деңгей қателері
kk.html: ✗ a secret among you and me → ✓ between you and me. ✗ between the crowd → ✓ among the crowd. ✗ between you and I → ✓ between you and me (объект есімдігі).

ru.title: Кратко
ru.html:
• between = отдельные, индивидуально видимые вещи (часто две, но больше, если различимы).
  • among = группа/масса в целом (три или более, недифференцированы).
  • between для двусторонних отношений/выбора; объектное местоимение (between you and me).
  • amongst — формальный вариант; тщательное письмо сохраняет различие.

kk.title: Қысқаша
kk.html:
• between = жеке, дара көрінетін заттар (жиі екеу, бірақ дара болса көбірек).
  • among = ұжым/масса ретінде көрінетін топ (үш не көп, даралмаған).
  • between екіжақты қатынас/таңдауға; объект есімдігін қолдан (between you and me).
  • amongst — ресми нұсқа; мұқият жазу айырманы сақтайды.

activities.prompt: Sort each phrase.
activities.why: between relates separate, individual items (even several); among relates something to an undifferentiated group.

items.s: The talks between the four parties collapsed.
She felt at ease among friends.
He was standing between the crowd.
This is just between you and I.

activities.why: a crowd is a collective (among the crowd), and the pronoun is objective (between you and me).
each finalist gets a distinct share → between the five finalists.

alts: amongst
activities.why: an undifferentiated mass → among the population.

activities.before: Keep this quiet — it's between you and
activities.why: the object of ‘between’ is an object pronoun → you and me.
a two-way relationship → between.

activities.q: Why is ‘between’ correct in ‘an agreement between the three governments’?
options: because the governments are seen as separate parties
why: there are three, not two.
